title: Lesson 2: Outbound Sales KPIs
body:
To ensure success in outbound sales, it’s essential to track progress through measurable Key Performance Indicators (KPIs). In this lesson, we’ll cover the KPIs you need to achieve weekly and monthly goals, explain what a Sales Qualified Lead (SQL) is, and introduce the tools for tracking your progress.


What is an SQL?
A Sales Qualified Lead (SQL) is a prospect who has:

1.    Engaged with your outreach (via LinkedIn or email).    

2.    Expressed interest in learning more about our solution or services.    

3.    Been identified as meeting our ideal customer profile and is ready to move into the next phase of the sales process.


In short, an SQL is a lead with high potential to convert into a customer. Your outreach efforts aim to identify and nurture these leads effectively.


Weekly KPIs
Each week, your goal is to:
    •    Contact 150 people using two outreach channels: LinkedIn (via LinkedHelper campaigns) and Email (via HubSpot sequences).

This means that the same contact must be enrolled in:
    •    A LinkedHelper campaign for LinkedIn outreach.    

    •    A HubSpot email sequence for follow-up communication.


The weekly average of 150 contacts is designed to produce 2 Sales Qualified Leads (SQLs) per week.


Monthly KPIs
By the end of each month, your KPIs will be:
    •    600 contacts enrolled in both LinkedHelper and HubSpot sequences.    

    •    8 SQLs produced.


Tracking Progress
To monitor performance and share findings with the Sales Team, we use a weekly Excel spreadsheet. This spreadsheet must be updated every Monday morning with the results from the previous week.


The date range for each reporting period is Monday through Sunday to maintain consistency across all submissions. For example, if you’re reporting on Monday, December 18, the data entered should cover the period from Monday, December 11, through Sunday, December 17.


Link to spreadsheet: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1ZGyQudAa37avfioulXYJS_0eZ53jf-ElXPCcVyiKFE4/edit?gid=1816152512#gid=1816152512


Why These KPIs and Processes Matter
These KPIs and the tracking process are based on proven methods for achieving consistent results. They ensure alignment with our sales strategy and provide a clear pathway to meet—and exceed—our outbound sales goals.


As we progress through this module, you’ll learn more about identifying prospects, creating effective outreach, and converting contacts into SQLs. Stay consistent with these KPIs, and you’ll set yourself up for success!

title: TikTok Shop Introduction
body:
What is TikTok Shop?

TikTok Shop is an e-commerce feature integrated within the TikTok platform, enabling users to discover and purchase products directly through the app. It combines TikTok’s strengths in short-form video content, influencer marketing, and user engagement with a shopping experience. This allows businesses, creators, and consumers to interact and conduct transactions without leaving the TikTok ecosystem.

How TikTok Shop Works

  1. In-App Shopping Experience: TikTok Shop allows brands, retailers, and creators to display and sell products directly on TikTok. Users can browse through products while watching videos, during live streams, or by visiting product links in profiles. Transactions, including payments, are handled directly in the app.

  2. Shop Tab: TikTok has a dedicated "Shop" tab, where users can explore curated products, browse collections, and make purchases. This functions similarly to a traditional e-commerce platform but is deeply integrated with TikTok’s unique content experience.

  3. Product Tags: TikTok Shop utilizes shoppable content, where creators and brands can tag products in their videos. Users can click on these tags to view product details and make purchases. These tags often appear in short videos, reviews, tutorials, and live streams.

  4. Live Shopping: Live shopping events are a significant part of TikTok Shop. Influencers and brands can host live streams where they showcase and sell products in real-time. Viewers can purchase products during the live stream by clicking on displayed product links.

  5. Integration with Creators: TikTok Shop encourages collaboration between brands and influencers. Influencers can create sponsored content or affiliate content where they promote products. They often earn commissions on sales generated through their content, making TikTok Shop an attractive option for content creators to monetize their following.

  6. Personalized Shopping: TikTok’s algorithm, known for its personalized content recommendations, also powers TikTok Shop. It tailors product recommendations based on a user’s behavior, interests, and interactions within the app. This personalized experience enhances product discovery.

Key Features of TikTok Shop

  • Shoppable Videos: Creators can directly link products in their content, making videos more interactive and driving direct sales.

  • Product Listings: Brands can set up a storefront within TikTok, listing products with descriptions, prices, and images for easy shopping.

  • Checkout Process: Users can complete transactions directly within TikTok without needing to navigate to an external site, making the shopping experience seamless.

  • Seller Center: TikTok provides a backend tool called "Seller Center" for businesses to manage their product listings, orders, and inventory. This tool allows businesses to oversee their operations within the app.

  • Campaign Tools: TikTok offers businesses tools to run marketing campaigns and promotions, including special discounts, flash sales, and influencer marketing campaigns.

  • Affiliate Program: TikTok Shop includes an affiliate program that allows creators to promote products and earn commissions. This enhances the collaboration between influencers and brands, driving more sales through user-generated content.

Benefits of TikTok Shop

  1. Engagement-Driven Commerce: TikTok’s content-centric platform encourages user engagement, making it a powerful tool for discovery-driven commerce. Users are likely to engage with products they see in the context of creative, entertaining content.

  2. Seamless Integration: The shopping experience is fully integrated into the app, reducing friction between product discovery and purchase. This seamless flow encourages impulse buying and makes it easier for users to convert from viewers to customers.

  3. Influencer and Content-Driven Marketing: The platform’s reliance on influencers and user-generated content creates authentic marketing opportunities. Users trust the creators they follow, making them more likely to purchase recommended products.

  4. Personalized Recommendations: TikTok’s algorithm enhances product discovery by offering personalized product recommendations based on user preferences and behavior. This targeted approach increases the likelihood of conversions.

  5. Expanding Market Reach: TikTok Shop enables brands to tap into TikTok’s vast global audience. The platform is particularly popular with younger demographics, making it a valuable tool for brands targeting Gen Z and Millennial consumers.

Challenges and Considerations

  1. Competition: TikTok Shop faces competition from established e-commerce platforms like Amazon, Shopify, and Instagram Shopping. While TikTok’s content-first approach offers a unique experience, it must compete with platforms that have more established logistics and trust among consumers.

  2. Content Moderation: Like other social platforms, TikTok faces challenges in moderating content to prevent the spread of misinformation, counterfeit goods, or inappropriate material in its Shop.

  3. Transaction Fees and Policies: Sellers must comply with TikTok’s transaction fees, return policies, and other terms, which may differ from traditional e-commerce platforms. Understanding and navigating these policies is crucial for businesses.

  4. Logistics and Fulfillment: TikTok Shop currently relies on sellers to manage their logistics, which can vary in quality and speed. As the platform grows, it may need to invest in logistics solutions to compete with faster and more reliable e-commerce options.

  5. Regulatory and Privacy Issues: Operating in multiple countries means TikTok must comply with various e-commerce regulations, consumer protection laws, and privacy policies, which can be challenging, particularly in regions with stringent laws like the EU.

Future Outlook

TikTok Shop is part of TikTok’s broader strategy to diversify its revenue streams beyond advertising. The rise of social commerce—where shopping happens directly within social media platforms—is expected to grow rapidly, and TikTok is well-positioned to capitalize on this trend.

By continuing to refine its shopping features, expanding logistics support, and building strong relationships with creators and brands, TikTok Shop is likely to become a major player in the e-commerce space. Brands that effectively leverage the power of TikTok’s engagement-driven environment are poised to see significant benefits from the platform’s unique approach to commerce.

In summary, TikTok Shop is a dynamic blend of content creation, entertainment, and e-commerce, providing users with a novel way to discover and purchase products while engaging with the TikTok community.

title: Confidentiality
body:
At Brandsom, the protection of company and client information is of utmost importance. Our commitment to confidentiality ensures that sensitive information is handled with the highest level of care and integrity. This policy outlines the guidelines for maintaining confidentiality within our organization.

1. Protection of Confidential Information

All employees are expected to protect the confidentiality of all company and client information. This includes, but is not limited to, business strategies, financial data, client lists, proprietary processes, and any other information that is not publicly available.

2. Handling Confidential Information

  • Access: Confidential information should only be accessed by authorized personnel who need it to perform their job duties.
  • Storage: Confidential information must be stored securely, in a digital form, to prevent unauthorized access.
  • Transmission: When transmitting confidential information, ensure it is done through secure and approved channels.

3. Disclosure of Information

  • Internal Disclosure: Share confidential information within the company only on a need-to-know basis.
  • External Disclosure: Do not disclose any confidential information to external parties unless expressly authorized to do so and with appropriate non-disclosure agreements in place.

4. Handling Client Information

  • Client Data: Treat all client information with the same level of confidentiality as company information.
  • Client Communications: Ensure that any communication containing confidential client information is conducted through secure methods.

5. Data Protection and Privacy

  • Compliance: Adhere to all relevant data protection laws and regulations, ensuring the privacy of personal data.
  • Personal Data: Handle personal data in accordance with our data privacy policy, ensuring it is used and stored responsibly.

6. Breach of Confidentiality

  • Reporting: Immediately report any suspected or actual breach of confidentiality to your manager and/or HR.
  • Investigation: All reported breaches will be investigated promptly and thoroughly, with appropriate measures taken to mitigate any harm and prevent future occurrences.

7. Consequences of Breach

Violations of this confidentiality policy may result in disciplinary action, up to and including termination of employment. In cases of severe breaches, legal action may also be pursued. The specific consequences are explained in detail in your employee contract.

8. Employee Responsibilities

  • Understanding: Ensure you understand and comply with this confidentiality policy.
  • Training: Participate in any required training on confidentiality and data protection.
  • Vigilance: Always be vigilant in protecting confidential information and report any concerns promptly.

By adhering to this policy, we uphold the trust placed in us by our clients and partners, and we maintain the integrity and reputation of Brandsom. If you have any questions or require further clarification on this policy, please contact your supervisor.

title: Amazon Advertising Strategies
body:
Amazon Advertising Strategies

Over time we have developed some strategies that are applicable to all of our clients. These campaigns should always be created since they have a very high success rate. In this academy, we will tell you more about these strategies and how to set them up in Amazon.

Low Bid campaign (Catch-All)

A 'Low Bid Campaign' is a campaign that should always be implemented for all of our clients. The idea is very simple:

  • Sponsored Products campaign with all products in 1 campaign
  • Automatic Keyword targeting
  • Start with a bid of €0,05 - If the campaign is performing really well you can try to increase by 1 cent per time. 

Using this strategy we have seen amazing results for our clients, almost always with a ROAS of over 10, meaning an ACOS of lower than 10%.

To summarize, use the following settings:

  • Automatic Campaign
  • Add all products (up to 1000 products)
  • Bidding Strategy: Up and Down
  • Set bid of €0,05
  • Budget - €50


Brand Protection Strategy

Another strategy that works for most of our clients is to set 'Brand Protection' campaigns. This can be achieved via different campaigns. 

  • Sponsored Products, Keyword Targeting
    • By targeting your own brand its keywords, you can prevent competitors from 'stealing' clients that are already looking for your brand.
    • You can use the '+' sign (it is called a modifier) to tell Amazon that it should always include the term. For example: '+Petrol +Industries' should always have both terms in the search term before the ad shows.
    • Use Broad match with identifiers to include just your brand, and Phrase match for more specific search terms (Petrol Industries Slim Fit Jeans)
    • You can use a high bid, since conversion normally is high on your own branded keywords

To summarize, use the following settings:

  • Manual Campaign - Sponsored Products, Keywords
  • Add Broad Match the brand name with modifier
  • Add Phrase Match for all branded, more specific search terms
  • Bidding Strategy: Fixed Bids
  • Set bid of €2
  • Budget - €50


  • Sponsored Products, Product Targeting
    • The idea here is the same, protect your PDP from competitors that are looking to steal your customers.
    • Add the products to the campaign, it is advised to differentiate based on the product category. Use the same products you use to advertise with, as a target for your campaign
    • The same can be applied for Sponsored Display ads, it is advised to first test the performance via Sponsored Products before creating a display campaign

To summarize, use the following settings:

  • Manual Campaign - Sponsored Products, Product Targeting
  • Add products you want to advertise, differentiated per category
  • Add the same products as targets
  • Bidding Strategy: Dynamic Up and Down
  • Budget - €50

title: Fulfilment by Bol.com (FBB)
body:
Fulfilment by Bol.com (FBB) is a service provided by Bol.com where they manage your logistics and inventory; they handle your offers, orders, and stock replenishment for you. It benefits the customers by increasing speed and performance, while also freeing up your capacity to focus on your core business of selling products. More information on this service is given through the bullet points below:

  • The entire fulfilment process is taken over by Bol.com. This includes order processing, packaging, shipping, and returns.
  • FBB is available in the Netherlands and Belgium from it's distribution centre in Waalwijk, making Bol.com's reach quite large.
  • Products sold through FBB automatically become Select items. This is very attractive for Select customers because this means that they don't have to pay shipping costs for the product and can have returns collected by Bol.com. FBB, therefore, makes it much more appealing for customers to purchase from a certain seller.
  • According to Bol.com data, FBB delivers up to 30% more conversion. This could be due to the fact that customers benefit much more from products sold through FBB, as they are considered Select and order processing is completely taken over by Bol.com.
  • FBB's rates depend on the weight and dimensions of the parcel. It is not always easy to determine this. You can make a calculation for this on the Bol.com site. Prices include pick & pack costs, shipping, customer service, and returns.
  • In order to use FBB and benefit from all its advantages, products need to meet certain conditions. The following items are eligible for Fulfilment by Bol.com:
  1. Only new products
  2. Dimensions should be no larger than 72 x 50 x 41 cm
  3. Not weighing more than 16 kg and having a volume of 70 litres
  4. Perishable items (can be kept for at least 100 days after the date of sale)
  5. No hazardous substances

 

LVB (FBB) for Brandsom Clients

  • Clients that work we Brandsom can easily get access to LVB by requesting it via our Partner Manager. Please request this via your manager with the following information:
    • Seller ID
    • Name of the Seller
    • Inventory level (voorraadplafond) > Usually starting around 500/750 units

If you want to increase the amount of units for existing clients, this is done the same way as requesting it. Sometimes the algorithm doesn't scale fast enough, or new assortment is onboarded. In this case you can request a manual increase via our agency manager, sending the same information as mentioned above.

title: IT Policies
body:
Internet Use and Mobile Phones

Brandsom allows the use of mobile phones and personal use of the business internet connection, provided it does not distract from work or cause a nuisance. Please follow these simple rules:

  • Use your mobile phone to benefit your work (business calls, productivity apps, calendars).
  • Keep personal calls brief and make them in an empty meeting room or common area to avoid disturbing colleagues.
  • Do not record confidential information with your phone.
  • Avoid downloading or uploading inappropriate, illegal, or obscene material using the business internet connection.
  • Do not visit potentially dangerous websites that could compromise network security.
  • Unauthorized or illegal actions, such as hacking or buying/selling illegal goods, are prohibited.

title: Product Information Management (PIM)
subcategory: WIP - Marketplace and E-commerce IT set-up 101
body:
A Product information management solution provides a single place to collect, manage, and enrich your product information, create a product catalog, and distribute it to your sales and e-commerce channels. There are 2 types of PIM systems: 

Stand-alone PIM systems

Stand-alone PIM systems can work without other systems. Examples are 'Informatica', and 'Inriver'.

Integrated PIM systems

An integrated PIM can communicate with other critical systems to automatically show a complete, accurate, up-to-date picture of what's for sale. E-commerce websites, web portals, web services, electronic product catalogs and other electronic systems can automatically retrieve the product information from the PIM and display it where it needs to be displayed. An example for this is 'Akeneo', which is linked to 'Magento'.

Benefits of working with PIM systems:

  • PIM systems offer enhanced features to manage content
  • PIM systems are often flexible on both the import and outbound feed structure
  • Onboard products and brands to your online store with ease
  • Improved customer satisfaction

PIM systems and marketplaces

  • PIM systems can be used to establish a direct content interface for marketplace integrators 
  • Some PIM systems offer direct content integrators with marketplaces. This is often not a favorable integration option because orders and shipments are not included.

title: Social Media

Not Permitted:

Consider the impact of your message on the company. If unsure, consult your supervisor. Report any factual inaccuracies about Brandsom found online to your supervisor.

title: MerchantSpring - The basics
body:
In this module you will find the basics of MerchantSpring and how the tool can be used.

Resources that may be helpful:

 MS Academy

  1. How to Add a Channel (marketplace)

 Knowledge Base

MerchantSpring and Advertising

title: Amazon AI Content Creation
body:
AI Bullet Point Creation for Amazon

To keep the content process as efficient as possible we have created an AI tool that can help you create content for your client. For now, it is only possible to create Bullet Points via the AI tool. Other content still has to be created via the builders (Titles, Search Terms).

If you want to create AI Bullet Points, kindly submit the information below:

Description: {Description} 

Context: "Bluetooth Speaker" 

Keywords (comma separated): [Insert keywords here]

Example Output:

  • SIZES UP TO 3XL: Available in XS to 3XL, our Men's Modern fit tapered Scrub Trousers are designed for durability, style, and comfort, catering to all sizes.

  • ODOR FIGHTING, LONG WEARING: Featuring antimicrobial and moisture-wicking technologies, these Scrub Trousers stay wrinkle and stain-free, ensuring you remain fresh throughout your workday.

  • EXCEPTIONAL QUALITY: Crafted from a blend of 95% fabric and 5% Elastane poplin, our Trousers offer stretchability and lightweight comfort for medical professionals.

  • 6 POCKETS: Equipped with six convenient pockets, including a double cargo pocket with a bungee loop and a back patch pocket, to keep essential items secure.

  • FUNCTION AND FIT: Designed for medical professionals, these Cherokee Scrub Trousers include a fly zip with drawstring waistband, rib-knit interior cell phone pocket, and side vents for optimal functionality and fit."


===============================================================

List of definitions:

  • Description: Input is the description of the client

  • Context: What kind of product is it, for example “Bluetooth Speaker” or “Airfryer” (mostly client category)

  • Keywords (comma separated): Important keywords you want the tool to use in the content. For example; “Waterproof” or synonyms. 

  • Example Output: Create 1 example output the AI can use. The AI will follow this structure. If you have multiple categories it is best to split this up into various templates to make sure you highlight the most important information per category.

Submit your request via Imco@brandsom.nl with the information provided.

title: Working abroad (remote working)
body: At Brandsom, we believe in providing our employees the flexibility to work remotely when feasible. Each employee is eligible to work remotely for up to one month per calendar year. To ensure that everyone can responsibly and effectively work remotely, we have established guidelines detailed in our "Company Specific Policies - Remote Working" module. Please review this module thoroughly before applying for remote work.

title: Requesting Extra Budget (Hours) for Clients
body:
Introduction

As a consultant at Brandsom, you are responsible for managing your clients within the allocated budgeted hours each month. However, there may be instances where you require additional hours to meet client demands or because you see opportunities in the market. This module outlines the process for requesting extra budget (hours) and the key considerations involved.

Understanding Client Budgets

Each client has a monthly budget of hours allocated based on financial revenue forecasts and contract specifications. The finance department sets these budgets, ensuring that the services provided remain financially viable for the company.

Example Budget Calculation

For a client with the following financial details:

  • Revenue: €10,000 per month
  • Gross Margin Value (GMV) Fee: 6% of revenue (€600)
  • Monthly Fixed Fee: €1,000
  • Total Budget: €600 + €1,000 = €1,600
  • Hourly Rate for Profitability: €85/hour
  • Monthly Budgeted Hours: €1,600 ÷ €85 = 18.8 hours

The consultant is expected to manage client work within the allocated 18.8 hours for the upcoming month.

When to Request Extra Budget

A consultant may need more hours due to various reasons, such as:

  • A big integration project requiring additional work.
  • Onboarding new products for the client.
  • Rectifying mistakes made by the team.
  • Identifying opportunities to generate more revenue (goal: ensure every extra hour spent increases the average hourly revenue to €85 or higher).

Process for Tracking and Requesting Additional Hours

Tracking Budgeted Hours

  • Team logs hours in Asana, which is linked to Everhour where the budget is monitored.
  • Automated emails notify consultants when the budget is close to being reached.
  • Consultants proactively monitor their hours and identify when additional budget is required.

How to Request Additional Hours

To request additional hours, consultants must send an email to the COO. The email should include:

  • Why extra hours are needed (e.g., new integration, revenue opportunity, rectification, etc.).
  • What tasks will be completed with the extra hours.
  • Whether these hours will generate extra income and how much.
  • Potential impact on the average price per hour (year-to-date).

Possible justifications for extra hours:

  • Using saved hours from a previous month.
  • Anticipating increased income from an investment in additional work.
  • Compensating for rectification efforts by reducing hours in a future month.

Next Steps

  • The COO will approve or deny the request via email.
  • The COO will update the budget in Everhour (no one else is authorized to make these adjustments).
  • Once approved, consultants can immediately begin logging extra hours.
  • The impact of extra hours on the average price per hour will be discussed in bi-weekly/monthly meetings.

Conclusion

By following this structured approach, consultants can efficiently manage client budgets while ensuring profitability. Requesting additional hours should always align with company goals, maintaining financial sustainability and maximizing revenue opportunities.

title: Amazon Content Phase 2 (optimisation phase)
body:
Amazon Content Optimization (Phase 2)

Before you start

The proces of content optimization is a time consuming task, but is definitely worth it when done right. Because this task is so time consuming, one should be very selective about the products it focuses on. Some useful selection criteria can be:

  • Product is showing sales without extensive optimization done

  • Product is new and has a good price point in the market

  • Product is being pushed via promotions and advertising


Before you start optimizing, it is important to do some research regarding the selected product(s). Some things to keep in mind:

  • Is the price we are selling for in line with the market? Or is it expensive?

  • Does the product have good reviews? Or is the score below average (<3 stars)

  • Will there be sufficient stock to build the product ranking? Or is the product End Of Life (EOL)?


Data points you can use to optimize your product(s)

In general you want to increase the amount of keywords your product is found on, combined with an increased sales rank equals increased GMV. There are multiple data points that can be used to identify the most important keywords, these data points will be explained below.


Helium10

Lars input vragen


Brand Analytics

Amazon Brand Analytics is a tool that can be used by brand owners to get additional insights in the performance of your products. It gives an extensive overview of your performance on keyword level.  The most important overview within Brand Analytics is: Search Analytics > Search Query Performance > ASIN View


This overview shows you the performance of of your ASIN on keyword level. It shows the search queries used by the customers and the most important metrics such as; Impressions, Clicks, Basket adds and conversion. 


You can use this overview to identify gain valuable insights from first party data, such as more long tail keywords which is used by customers to find the product you are optimizing for. You can use these keywords in the content and put focus on these keywords in the advertising as well to gain sales rank on specific keywords to increase longterm sales.


Advertising data

Within our advertising strategy we always utilize automatic campaigns to gain sights in more long tail keywords which are not identified via our initial keyword research. These insights can be used to optimize the product content. You can request these datareports from your advertising specialist or download them yourself via the advertising portal; Campaign Manager > Reports > Search Term report

title: Amazon Campaign Naming Convention
body:
Amazon Campaign Naming Convention

Naming conventions are super important in advertising because they keep everything organized and easy to find. When everyone uses the same names for campaigns and files, it’s a lot simpler to track what’s going on and see what’s working. It helps teams work together better since everyone knows exactly what’s being talked about. Plus, it keeps the brand looking consistent across different ads and platforms. Clear naming also helps avoid mistakes like using the wrong assets or misplacing budgets. Overall, good naming conventions make advertising more efficient and effective.

Amazon campaign naming convention


You can use this naming convention template to make it more efficient!

title: Introduction
body:
One of the most important parts of working at Brandsom is fulfilling our client's needs. This is simply because they are the reason we get to work. Therefore, you want to build a strong working relationship with the client. To achieve this,  good communication is necessary to figure out their goals and needs and to let them know what we can do to help. After all, you want to keep the client satisfied. How do we do this? And what exactly is good communication and how do we keep it businesslike?

One way of communicating is by e-mail. Another frequently used option is calling the client by phone. It is very important to write and talk in a formal way at the early stages of the business relationship.


When to call, e-mail or plan a Google Meet?

We believe that a periodic meeting structure is the core of good communication with the customer. Besides this, we use the Voys (internet phone) and e-mail.

  • Try to discuss all topics in the (bi)weekly, monthly and quarterly meetings. If you receive e-mails in the meantime discuss the also during these meetings.
  • Second way of communication method is e-mail because it's clearer, faster and more reliable for the customer.
  • Third way of communicating is calling the customer using Voys. Sometimes it's quicker to just call the customer, if you call the customer please use Voys.
  • The customer can call the generic phone number (please check the site for the latest number) and re-routed if needed.


Tone of voice 

When you communicate with a client, it is important to use a formal tone of voice. This indicates the way and the tone in which you write or speak to a client. Your tone of voice should be professional and friendly. This way of communicating will be explained in detail in this module.

title: Services
body:
Brandsom services 

Familiarising yourself with Brandom's services is probably one of the most important things you should learn about as a Sales intern. Just spend some hours learning about the two types of services and what is included in each. 

Really try to understand when which service is most convenient for the customer. Often, you can also offer to do a trial project on an hourly basis first and then switch to managed. The cost of the trial can then be taken out of the setup fee for managed. In this trial, the client can find out the impact of Brandsom's marketplace services.

Extra tip 

As an additional tip; write out the sales pitch completely for yourself and start practicing it. This way you will develop your own sales story. 

Consultants

in addition, it is good to sit down once with a consultant and ask what he/she does for a client. If you understand the consultant's work well, you can also bring this back into your sales meetings with potential clients.

title: Amazon Advertising SOP: Forecasting Key KPIs
body:
SOP for Forecasting Key KPIs

This SOP is created to assist in forecasting the main KPIs for clients. Starting with an already active client, using this SOP and the Master Sheet Calculation, you can define:

  • The actual AdSales impact on total Sales (Section 1)

  • The market size and dimension for the product category (Section 2)

  • Identification of the largest Amazon EU countries for this category (Section 2)

  • Estimation of which country will contribute most towards a specific sales goal set by the client (Section 3)

  • Forecasting of key KPIs such as AdSpend and TACOS, given a specific sales target.

Below is an overview of each section with brief instructions on what to complete in each one. Please use the following link to access the Master Sheet: Master Sheet KPI Analysis / Forecast


DUPLICATE THE SPREADSHEET BEFORE USING IT

The file contains five main sections:

  1. Section 1 - Client Performance

  2. Section 2 - Market Dimension Check

  3. Section 3 - Sales Goals & Country Contribution

  4. Section 4 - Current KPIs Status

  5. Section 5 - Forecast by Country

Note: The file contains formulas, and altering cells may disrupt the tabs and related calculations. Only cells highlighted in yellow should be edited. 

Editable Cells: Only the yellow cells in this section should be filled.

Section 1 - Past Client Performance

The goal of this section is to review the client’s past performance in terms of AdSpend, AdSales, ACoS, Total Sales, and AdSales% Ratio (this ratio shows the impact of AdSales on total sales, crucial for forecasting ACoS’s effect on TACOS). 

By knowing AdSales' impact on total sales (Organic Sales + AdSales), we can better understand how ACoS affects TACOS (providing insight into AdSpend’s impact on total sales).

  • Relevant Marketplaces: The template includes DE and NL marketplaces as starting points; however, fill cells for relevant marketplaces.

  • Data Source: Merchant Spring. Choose either the last 30 days or calculate a monthly average over the past year for greater statistical relevance.

Section 2 - Market Dimension Check

The purpose of this section is to conduct a preliminary analysis of market size per country. Amazon + Helium 10 can provide valuable insights.

  • Steps:

    • Search a product-related keyword on Amazon.

    • Use the Helium 10 Google Chrome extension to access additional data related to that keyword.

For example, if the client sells headphones on amazon.de, searching “headphones” will provide metrics such as 30-day revenue, units sold, average price, and Page 1 breakdown by product or brand. (Refer to Picture 1 for an example.)

  • Filling the Section 2 Tabs:

    • Perform the same analysis for the key Amazon countries relevant to the target, and input 30-day revenue data (translate the keyword by locale).

    • Repeat for primary keywords.

Note: The "Total" row in Section 2 is not the total market size for that category; rather, it calculates each marketplace’s average weight within the total market.

Why is this data important?

These insights help identify the countries that are most significant for the client’s product category. This understanding allows us to set realistic expectations with the client and prioritize launches in specific markets accordingly. Additionally, if a client sets an annual or monthly goal, these insights enable us to evaluate its feasibility and educate the client on realistic goal-setting.

With Helium 10, we also gain insights into brand competition and revenue share. 

For instance, if Client X, who sells headphones, wants to achieve a monthly revenue of €400,000 on Amazon DE, our research can show that reaching this target would require a 16-17% market share and a position among the top 4 brands (Refer to Picture 2 for an example.). Analyzing these top brands provides insights on average pricing and required content standards for top rankings (e.g., content benchmarking for titles, A+ content, and images) which will be crucial before the launch.

Picture 1


Picture 2

Section 3 - Sales Goals & Country Contribution

Section 3 is goal-oriented, used to determine the sales targets by marketplace based on the client’s overall revenue goal. 

By filling the Total Yearly Sales Goal in the designated cell, the formula will automatically distribute it by month and country. This breakdown serves as an average; however, a monthly ramp-up plan should be developed to account for seasonal trends and peak events in the category.

Section 4 - Current KPIs Status

This section provides a snapshot of current KPIs. By entering actual data from the last 30 days (or the monthly average from yearly performance), we can derive metrics like CPC and Conversion Rate, which will be applied in the forecast.

  • Data Source: Amazon AdConsole

Section 5 - Forecast by Country

The primary section of the Master Sheet, Section 5, provides a forecast based on the KPIs tracked in the previous sections and market dimension assumptions from Helium 10 data. 

This forecast, organized by country, has two methods:

Forecast 1 - Autofilled

This forecast is automatically generated, so no data input is needed. It calculates key KPIs based on:

  • Total Monthly Sales Goal: The client’s total sales goal (organic + ad sales), weighted by country impact from Section 2 (Market Check)

  • Actual AdSales Ratio (vs. Organic): The actual AdSales-to-Organic Sales ratio calculated in Section 1

  • Expected AdSales: Monthly target sales generated by Ad Sales, given the client’s goal and the current AdSales Ratio

  • AOV: Current Average Order Value (AOV) from Section 4

  • Orders: Estimated target orders, based on the AdSales target and AOV (AdSales Target / AOV)

  • Actual CVR: Current conversion rate from Section 4

  • Clicks Needed (based on CVR): Number of clicks needed to reach the target order volume, calculated by dividing the order target by the CVR

  • Current CPC: Current client CPC from Section 4

  • AdSpend (based on current CPC): Total expected AdSpend, calculated using the Sales Goal, current CVR, and CPC (CPC * Clicks)

  • Expected TACOS: Expected TACOS based on the country-specific Sales Target, calculated by dividing the Expected AdSpend by the Total Monthly Sales Goal

Forecast 2 - Manually Filled

Forecast 2 has the same structure as Forecast 1 but requires manual input rather than autofilled data from Sections 1-4. This forecast allows for adjustments based on industry benchmarks, rather than actual client performance, by inputting optimal AdSales Ratios, CVR, and CPC values.

  • AdSales Ratio Optimal: Fill with an ideal AdSales-to-Organic Sales ratio based on goals or other clients within the same category.


  • CVR Optimization – To calculate optimal CVR, we can reference data from other clients in the same category to establish a category benchmark or use tools like Helium 10 and Amazon Opportunity Explorer.

To calculate CVR, we need clicks and generated sales. In Amazon Opportunity Explorer on Seller Central, we can look up main competitor ASINs and obtain clicks generated in the last 30 days (Picture 3). Then, by finding these ASINs on Amazon and checking their product pages, we can identify sales generated over the last 30 days via Helium 10 (Picture 4).

The CVR calculation is straightforward: divide Orders by Clicks.

Here, both tools serve as sources of insight, though judgment is essential, as data may not be fully accurate.


  • Benchmark CPC: Use Amazon’s suggested bid or Helium 10’s recommendation for main keywords as a benchmark for CPC. Expect a month-over-month increase in CPC as part of general market trends observed over the past five years.


The suggestion for Section 5 is to focus on the marketplaces with the largest weight in the total market size, as identified in Section 2.


Picture 3


Picture 4

Conclusion

This SOP provides a clear process to forecast KPIs and align with client sales goals effectively. Focus on high-impact markets as identified in Section 2 for the best results. For any questions, please reach out to Andrea.

title: ChannelEngine: Repricer module PART 1
body:
The repricing task refers to reprice your products on several marketplaces based on competition prices. There are several ways to target your repricing:

  • Target lowest competitor.
  • Target buy box.
  • Target fee reductions. 

Lowest competitor: You can compete with marketplaces using fulfillment services, buy box winners or to specific groups based on the seller rating or shipping times. You can also include or exclude specific shops. 

Target buy box: Only available if the marketplace provides information about the buy box winner. Product’s price isn’t the only factor: the delivery times and ratings are important as well.  

Fee reduction: If enabled, the reprice price is lowered to the channel’s maximum price for a fee reduction. This option cannot be combined with targeting the buy box or with targeting the lower competitor. 


 1. Prerequisites

There are some general prerequisites that we have to consider, as well as marketplace specific: 

  1. Make sure of the list of products you want to reprice: the complete offer or a subset (for example, a specific brand or product group)? 
  2. It is recommended to offer purchase prices in the product feed, shop or ERP system. Without them, it is hard to reprice on low margin products without risk to sell below the purchase price. 
  3. For Bol.com
  • You need the sales number (Settings  Account)
  • An OpenApi key.
  1. For Amazon
  • You need to be in the top 20 for products you add to the repricer. 


 2. Managing reprice rules

Add new rule

  1. Go to ChannelEngine dashboard.
  2. Pricing  Reprice rules.
  3. Add.
  4. Save.
  5. Preview. This is useful to see the projected changes in price.
  6. Active. We check this tab to export the actual outcome of the price rule to the marketplace. 

 



Edit a price rule:

  • Select the pencil icon on the desired listing row in the Reprice rule overview.

Delete a price rule:

  • Select the trashcan icon on the desired listing roe in the Reprice rule overview. 


 3. Repricer rule settings

When we click the “Add” tab to create a new reprice rules, we’ll find the following information that must be filled in: 

  • Marketplace: to which channel the rule applies to. 
  • Name: of your reprice rule. Use a descriptive name.
  • Listing: product selection listing for which you want to apply the repricing to. For multiple product selection listings to reprice all your included products, you need to set up various reprice rules.
  • Filter by: if you want the repricing applied to the last number of products, you can set a minimum stock amount from which the rule will be applied.
  • Min. price: select the price attribute that is used as the base value for the minimum price that is used by the repricer.

  • The increase / decrease buttons: found next to Min. Price, Max. Price and repricing with the symbol +/-
    • Two fields to increase or decrease the price.
    • If you enter the information “15” in the percentage (%) one, the input price value will be increased by 15%.
    • If you enter the information “2” in the fixed value (€) one, the input price value will be increased by 2€.
    • If you want to decrease prices instead of increasing, add a minus in front of the numbers in the correspondent tabs (% or €).
    • Example: Price = 10, % = 10 and € = -0,50 will result in the calculation
      • 10 + (0,1* 10 – 0,50) = 10,50 as the final price.
  • Max. Price: just as the Min. Price, we select the price attribute used as the base value for the maximum price that is used by the repricer.
    • We do also find the x / - buttons here, so we can increase / decrease this price.
    • There are also two options: Price or Catalog Price.
      • Price: if we have price rules for this listing, we can use the output from this price rule as the maximum price input.
      • Catalog price: if available. 

  • Repricing: the actual input where you set both percentages as the fixed value you want your price to be below or above the competitor with the lowest price.
    • Not compatible with “Target Buy Box” or “Target fee reduction”.
    • You can, again (just like in Min. Price or Max Price) use the + / - buttons to increase or decrease a specific percentage or fixed amount to the price of the lowest competitor.
    • If the resulted price in this section is lower than the Min. Price stablished before, the Min. Price will be exported. If not, this repricing price will be exported. 


*Click on the next module for part 2 of 'ChannelEngine: Repricer module'

title: Relevant Terms
body:
On this page, you can find some relevant terms that will help you to understand advertising better. We will cover some terms and abbreviations to help you get up to speed with advertising. 

ACoS

Advertising Cost of Sales gives the ratio between what is spent in ads in contrast to how many sales have been made. An ACoS of 25% means that for every euro in sales, 25 cents has been spent on the ad. The lower the ACoS, the more profit is being made. It simply means you spend less on an ad.

Cost-Per-Click (CPC)

CPC is a metric that determines how much advertisers pay for the ads they place on websites or social media, based on the number of clicks the ad receives. It is important for marketers to consider because it measures the price of a brand's paid advertising campaign. With CPC ads like Sponsored Products, you bid the maximum amount that you're willing to pay when a shopper clicks an ad for your product. The more competitive your bid, the more likely your ad is to be displayed when it matches a customer's shopping query. Sponsored Brands also use CPC to determine how much advertisers need to pay, based on the clicks of customers.


Pay-Per-Click (PPC)

Amazon PPC is known as their advertising platform and allows sellers to amplify their product sales online by having their products show up in related searches. It is an auction-style system where advertisers bid on keywords. When an Amazon customer searches for a product, the sellers with the highest bids on relevant keywords win the auction, meaning that their product ads get listed in their chosen placement.

With the PPC model, advertisers only pay Amazon when shoppers click on the ad. Sellers that are running Amazon PPC campaigns will not have to pay for impressions. Optimizing and managing a PPC campaign well can significantly boost a product's visibility and sales. It will also boost the product's organic ranking, ensuring long-term success. 


CTR 

Click-through-rate. The number of clicks on an ad is divided by the number of times your ad has been shown. A high CTR is a good indication that your ad is seen as useful/relevant by customers. By looking at the CTR you can determine which ads and keywords are effective. 

ROAS 

Return on Ad Spend. De omzet van je advertenties gedeeld door de kosten van je advertenties.

title: Disallow over budget policy
body:
New Budget Rule: Disallow Over Budget

What does the new rule entail?

A new budget rule is now in place for clients with a set budget: the "disallow over budget" option. This means:

  • No hours over budget: No additional hours can be recorded beyond the noted budget.

  • Budget review process: Budgets will be discussed during a meeting with finance, the lead, and the consultant. This is the same meeting where forecasts are adjusted or reviewed.

Note: This rule does not apply to project-based clients, such as Onitsuka Tiger.

How does it work?

  • Automatic prevention of hours over budget: Once the budget is reached, additional hours are automatically blocked.

  • Option to justify: Going over budget is not prohibited but requires justification:

    • The consultant needing extra hours must report this to their lead.

    • Upon approval, the lead can adjust the budget to allow extra hours to be recorded.

    • If extra budgeted hours are accepted, they must be added to the Forecast/Actuals sheet under the row labeled "Adjusted Target Hour Budget". Include a brief comment in the corresponding cell to explain the reason for the budget change.

  • Insight into profitability: The additional budgeted hours will be displayed in Looker Studio, offering a clear view of the extra hours invested. This will help evaluate the impact of these hours on the client’s profitability in the coming months.

Important to remember:

  • Budget monitoring helps us work efficiently and transparently.

  • Providing justification for extra hours allows us to consistently make better planning decisions and uphold budget agreements.

title: Amazon Plan Of Action - Pre-Fulfilment cancellation rate
body:
Below you will find a POA to reinstate an Amazon account which is blocked due to Pre-Fulfilmentcancellation rate. Please customize the plan and make adjustments where needed, DO NOT use this plan 1:1.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A Plan of Action that includes:

The root cause(s) of confirming the cancellation of orders, which impacted your Pre-Fulfilment cancellation rate.

More details about the actions you have taken to resolve higher than expected cancellations.

More details about the steps you have taken to prevent higher-than-expected cancellations in the future.

The root cause of the issue
What was the primary cause for your Pre-Fulfilment cancellation rate to exceed 2,5% over 7 days?

The primary reason for the high cancellation rate was due to failing stock updates, which meant that customers would order products that were unfortunately out of stock. Our inventory levels were not automatically updated in Seller Central, causing discrepancies in the stock levels shown on Amazon and in our warehouse. We ourselves failed to keep a closer eye on this and identify this issue before it caused problems.

The actions that you have taken to resolve the issue
 More details about the actions you have taken to resolve higher than expected cancellations.

  • We have contracted a fulfillment partner, Monta packing, which will help us maintaining correct inventory levels. Meaning our products inventory levels should now always be up-to-date due to advanced systems.
  • We have contracted the services of an agency assisting is with the day-to-day account management of Amazon, also helping us creating up-to-date product content. Meaning we have more resources available internally to manage the marketplace.
  • We partnered with an integrator, named ChannelEngine. This tool will help us automatically sync our stock levels between our fulfillment partner and Amazon. 
  • In addition to automatically syncing our stock levels and partnering up with a fulfillment partner, we have implemented a safety measure which automatically deducts about 10 units of our inventory. This measure will make sure we have sufficient inventory if for example multiple orders are placed on the same time. 
  • The company now has more experience selling on various marketplaces, where we always meet the SLA. We have invested tremendously in our operational processes and have seen great improvements.
  • We have updated our shipping policy to ensure clear communication towards the customer, this will help managing customer expectations and reduce cancellations due to misunderstandings or miscommunications.

 

The steps that you have taken to prevent the issue going forward
 More details about the steps you have taken to prevent higher-than-expected cancellations in the future.

  • We now have a fulfillment partner to help us manage our inventory. Due to their advanced systems, we have accurate inventory level management.
  • We have ordered our fulfillment partner to prioritize Amazon orders, so they are shipped first.
  • Automated stock synchronization with our integrator, ChannelEngine.
  • Safety measure implemented which automatically deducts our inventory pushed towards Amazon with about 10 units.
  • Increased resources managing the Amazon marketplace, as well internally as externally via a specialized agency.
  • 15-minute inventory update to make sure inventory levels are always updated towards Amazon.
  • Also, in the future we will keep looking for possibilities to improve our operational processes to avoid these sorts of things from happening. With multiple checks we can identify the pain points in an early stage and therefore resolve them before it causes any trouble.

To summarize, we have invested to streamline our operational processes and will prioritize Amazon orders to avoid a high Pre-Fulfilment cancellation rate. Above processes are already in place and therefore we are more ready than we have ever been.

title: What causes error 6024?
subcategory: Amazon Error Solving SOP Overview - internal
body:
What causes error 6024?

This error occurs when seller is not authorized to list products under a brand name in a product line or category. Seller must meet additional qualifications to sell certain brands on Amazon.

Sample:

ResultMessageCode: 6024


ResultDescription: You are not authorized to list products under this brand. To sell products under this brand, please try adding one of your SKUs under this brand through the 'Add a Product' page and follow the application instructions.

How to solve and prevent error 6024?

  1. To solve this error, follow below procedure:

    1. In Seller Central, click the inventory link and select ‘Add a Product’.

    2. Run a search for the item you wish to sell.

    3. In the search results, click the ‘Listing limitations apply’ link for that item.

    4. Click the Request Approval button to begin the application process.


To check the status of an application, return to the ‘Add a Product’ tool and click the Selling application status link located toward the top of the page. For more details go to https://sellercentral.amazon.co.uk/gp/help/200333160

title: Distributed Order Management (DOM)
subcategory: WIP - Marketplace and E-commerce IT set-up 101
body:
Distributed Order Management (DOM) lets you collect orders from any channel and use rules to dynamically decide how they should be sourced and shipped. Manage, monitor, and configure orders from placement to fulfilment.

DOM is the central hub that connects all your other systems and enables you to manage the life cycle of sales orders. It can be used to serve information to your other channels keeping them all in sync, providing a seamless multichannel experience for your business users.

Brands with a more complex distribution network often use a DOM system. DOM systems could be offered as stand-alone or as plug-in to an ERP system.

DOM and marketplaces

  • Brands with multiple stock locations (retail stores, warehouses, brand stores, outlets, showrooms) can benefit from a DOM system to manage marketplace orders.
  • Advanced distribution strategies (e.g. based on postal code) can be achieved with DOM systems - for example, Zalando's connected retail.

title: Mission, Vision and Values
body:
At Brandsom, we are driven by a clear vision and mission. Our vision provides high-level guidance, while our mission concretely defines our actions and objectives.

Vision

Becoming Europe's leading performance-based marketplace agency that connects brands to online customers.

Mission

By helping brands build their brand on marketplaces.

Core Values

At Brandsom, our core values—freedom and responsibility—guide us in how we work and collaborate.

Practical Examples of Freedom & Responsibility

  • Take the freedom to work from home, but ensure you know what’s expected and deliver on your responsibilities.
  • Work remotely (in line with company policy), but make sure you hit your targets. If targets are missed or customers are dissatisfied, it’s clear that customers come first, and remote work may no longer be an option.

Decision-Making Made Easy

If you're ever unsure about how to balance freedom and responsibility in a situation, here’s your go-to advice:
Just ask Thijs. He’ll help you clarify the values and make the right decision.

title: Type of campagnes and KPI’s
body:
This academy is specifically focused on Amazon Advertisement. A large part of it can be generalized to other platforms, but always pay attention to the different specs of the platforms and advertisements. Do the bidding systems work the same, for example? Furthermore, this piece goes quickly through the core concepts of Amazon Advertising. For a more detailed explanation, please visit the Amazon Advertising Academy.


The main types of campaigns offered by Amazon are:
Sponsored Products: You promote specific products on the search page or product page of other products. You target search terms or products.
Sponsored Brands: This is a banner that you promote on specific search terms. It appears at the top of the search screen and you can promote multiple products in it.
Sponsored Displays: These are visual advertisements that are displayed on the side of various pages. They link to one specific product. It is also possible to use a video display for this. 


There are also a few KPIs to keep an eye on:
Impressions: how often is an ad shown?
Clicks: How often is an ad clicked on?
CPC: Average cost per click
ACOS: Advertising Cost of Sale. Advertising costs are divided by paid sales.
Search Term Impression Share: How often is your ad shown out of all the times that a certain search term is searched for.
Orders: How many times has your ad been bought.
NTB Orders: How often you are bought by someone who has not bought from you in the last 12 months.


These are the most important KPIs for monitoring and adjusting the campaigns from this framework. Remember that here too, customization per account is involved.

title: Amazon Vouchers
body:
Vouchers are a great way to promote products. By adding a voucher to a product, customers get a discount of a fixed amount/percentage of a certain product. Different clients need different types of vouchers. A checklist including the exact settings each client wants for their vouchers can be found here.

The products that need a voucher created for them are in an Excel file that you will receive in Asana. In this file, you can find the product ASIN and names. Voucher percentages differ per product and are easy to mix up in the file. Therefore, the first thing you should do is sort cases A-Z in Excel to prevent any mistakes.

First log in to ASC (select the LastPass credentials of the client you are working for)→ Click: 'Advertising' → Click: 'Vouchers' or 'Coupons'. Then, to start adding vouchers on Amazon Seller Central follow the steps below:

  1. Click: 'Create a new voucher' (Top right corner)

  2. Select the ASIN in the Excel file and paste it in the search box on ASC: 'Search by SKU or ASIN'

  3. Click: 'Add to coupon' → Then click: 'Continue to next step' (Top right corner)

  4. The next steps depend on the type of voucher the client wants. Go to the checklist and find out what the correct settings are

  5. Select: 'Money off' or 'Percentage off' and fill in the amount that you found in the Excel file → Then select: 'Yes, limit redemption to one per customer' → Then on the right side fill in the budget that you found in the Excel file → Then click: 'Continue to next step' (Top right corner)

  6. Select the title of the product in the Excel file and paste it in the 'Save .. on' textbox → Then select target customers as instructed in the Excel file

  7. Under schedule fill out the 'start date' and the 'end date' as instructed in the checklist → Then click: 'Continue to next step' (Top right corner)

  8. Quality check: check if everything is correct according to the checklist → Then click: 'Submit coupon' (Top right corner) and mark the product ASIN green in the Excel file

  9. Click: 'Go to coupon dashboard' and repeat the steps until you created a voucher for all products in the Excel file


Quality sample

After creating 50 coupons, randomly select 1 coupon in the submitted coupons column and check if everything is filled out correctly. If you find a mistake, investigate the remaining vouchers you just created.

Mistakes

If you make or find a mistake: click: 'Submitted coupons' → Then click: 'Product name' → Then click: 'Deactivate' → Then create a new coupon for this product
Note: Fix your mistakes right away! If the coupon already went through, you can find it under 'Running coupons'

Bulk voucher upload

In ASC vouchers can be uploaded in bulk by importing a file. If you need to create a lot of vouchers this is a lot quicker than manual voucher creation.

First log in at ASC (select the LastPass credentials of the client you are working for)→ Click: 'Advertising' → Click: 'Vouchers' or 'Coupons'. Then to start adding vouchers on Amazon Seller Central follow the steps below:

  1. Click: 'Create in bulk' (Top right corner)


2. Next, click 'Download template file'


3. Open the file 'SPC Template.xlsx'


4. Read the instructions given by Amazon at the top of the template file


5. On column A - ASIN list - in the template file, place the ASIN that you selected from the Excel file.


6. On column B - Discount type - in the template file select either: '% off' or '$ off' based on the client wishes.


7. On column C - Voucher discount - in the template file input how much % off the voucher is for, example: '10%'


8. On column D - Voucher discount - in the template file input how much $ off the voucher is for, example: ‘$ 5’ 


9. On column E - Voucher title - in the template file input the product title:


10. On column F - Voucher budget - in the template file input the vouchet budget:


11. On column G - Voucher start date - in the template file input the voucher start date:


12. On column H - Voucher end date - in the template file input the voucher end date:


13. On column I - Limit redemption - in the template file input: 'Yes' unless the client file instructs you otherwise.



14. On column J - Target Segment - in the template file input: 'All customers' unless the client file instructs you otherwise.


15.  After filling the template with the products you want to create a voucher for, the template can be uploaded to Amazon. Click ‘Choose file’ on the page where you downloaded the template file.


16. Now select the template file, but before uploading change the name of the file to the following formate: [date]_vouchers[Country]


17. After uploading the template a status report will be generated. This status report is the same template file you uploaded with 2 new columns added to it. In these new columns K & L you can find if the voucher went through or if an error occurred.


18. After looking into the errors and changing the values you can upload the file again to make the vouchers that failed, repeat the process to resolve errors.

Examples

If you are unsure about any of the values that go into the template file, you can download any of the previous status reports on the following page: 

' Advertising' → ' Vouchers' or ' Coupons'→ ' Create in bulk’ and then click ‘ Status File'
