title: Lukewarm acquisition
body:
A lukewarm acquisition is an acquisition that follows a previous contact with the prospect. The difference with cold acquisition is that there has been a contact moment beforehand. This contact moment can have taken place in various ways: by letter, e-mail, LinkedIn, or networking.

 

For Brandsom this contact moment will have been on the website. We can see on Leadinfo which companies visited our site and which pages they visited (more information about this can be found in the Leadinfo module).

 

When a company is qualified as 'not interesting' in leadinfo, it will not be an interesting company to approach.

When the company falls within 1 of the 4 pipelines, it is interesting to approach. Look mainly at what pages the interesting company has looked at, you can anticipate this.

title: Amazon Assortment Analysis
body:
Amazon Assortment Analysis

The assortment analysis is mainly used to determine the products that have to biggest potential to succeed on Amazon. Before you start, you should discuss with the client if they have products they think have potential, for example via historical sales on a different marketplace are their own webshop.


In the assortment analysis you want to determine:

  • Is there sufficient search volume on the most important keywords?
  • Does the price point of the client fits with the market average?

Also it is important to discuss with the client a potential discount during launch phase. This drastically increases the potential success of the product launch.


We will discuss the following tools to analyse the potential of a product:

  • Product Opportunity Explorer (Amazon tool, use different client to analyse if this is not yet available in current clients account)
  • Helium10

Before we begin, make sure to document your findings!! This helps you present it to your client.


Product Opportunity Explorer

The overview can be found via Menu > Growth > Product Opportunity Explore


In this overview you can search for the keywords that are most relevant to the product you are looking into. You can start with more highover keywords and deep-dive into a specific niche later on. Let’s take “airfryer” as an example. 


Start with searching for “airfryer” and it will show you already some niches with some important metrics:

  • Search volume
  • Average Price
  • Price Range


Of course, airfryer is maybe a bit too generic. Since they come in all sizes and shapes. Therefore you want to do some deepdiving to really get an understanding of the market you are looking to sell in. 


Click on the niche that is closest to your product, in this case we look into “heißluftfritteuse”, this will show you an overview of the best-selling products. You can download this overview if you want to make your own selection, but it gives you a better understanding of what products are selling on the market and what the price is. You can compare this with the client's product.


Search Terms overview

The search terms overview can be used to dive a little deeper into the category and the customer behavior. For example, if we were selling an "airfryer XXL", we can now see who are our competitors and for what price they are offering their product. If we can go a bit below their price for example, this increases the possibility of success.


Helium10

Make sure you have the Helium10 plugin installed, you will need this if you are doing your research via Helium10.


Let’s stick with the airfryer, if you have installed the plugin and have logged to Helium10, you can just search for “airfryer” in the search bar and the plugin will show an extensive overview of keywords.

This already gives an overview of the market size for the product you are looking into. You can press “See all related keywords” to get even more keywords that might be a fit for your product. For a first overview, this might be a bit too much, but if needed it can come in handy. 


Once you have the search volumes, you want to know the average price. You can now press enter and search for “airfryer”, you will now see the overview below:


Here you will see the average price of the products on the first page and the price range. In this case you might want to specify a bit further, since “airfryer” might be a bit too generic to get a clear overview. For example, you could search for “airfryer xxl” or “airfryer 12L” to get a bit more specific results.


Keep an overview of your work

As mentioned at the very beginning of this academy, it is important to log what you are doing. This can be as simple as creating a table where you note down

  • The search volume for the top 3 most important keywords 

  • The average price of the competitors

  • Most important competitors price (if you have this information)


Based on this you can advise your client, about what you think the discount during the launch phase should be and what the introduction price should be.

title: Title builder
body:
When you have to create product titles using the title builder in excel follow these steps:

  1. The first step is for the consultant to fill in the necessary data which the consultant has received from the client. This should have been placed in the sheet called “Brondata”. This data file will contain, hopefully, most of the data you will need for creating the bullets. Look through the “Brondata” to get to know the products you will be working with a bit better.


  2. In the Master datasheet, you first make sure that SKU - Parent SKU - EAN are filled in correctly. Next you enter the corresponding data/information that you want to have in your title, be it brand, gender, color etc.
    Quality check: make sure you know in advance which set-up (which information is required in the title) the marketplace requires. Also, enter the identifiers or USP (Unique Selling Points) if you want to use them for your title.

     
  3. (If you don't need to translate anything you can skip this step)  Open the sheet with the name "Translation [language]". Then use the function "=UNIQUE()", with this function you will get all unique values from your specified range below each other. Next, you can put the correct translation in 'Optimised'
    You do this not only for standard characteristics but also for the USPs, i.e. everything you want in your title but ought to be translated first.


  4. Next, go to one of the sheets called "Builder [language]", depending on the language your titles have to be in. Make sure the SKUs and Parent SKUs are filled in, you will need this data later to retrieve the correct data for your title.


  5. Then, check whether every set of data you will use for your titles has a column. Now use " =VERT.ZOEKEN()" to get the corresponding data from your translation sheets (or “Master data” if you haven’t translated anything). Your formula will look something like this: "=ALS.FOUT(VERT.ZOEKEN(G7;'VERTALINGEN DE'!D$3:F; 3;ONWAAR))". 


  6. Now that you have all the necessary data in your builder, you can build your titles with the help of "TEXT.SAMENVOEGEN()". You will create titles for PARENT, CHILD, and PARENT + CHILD. Child title often include the whole parent title, with some additional information. So carefully assess what is supposed to be in the parent title, and what ought to be in the child title.
    Quality check: Check your title for the number of characters using "LENGTE()". Easiest is to create a new column next to the respective titles of which you want to know the number of characters of.


  7. Lastly, you need to deduplicate the title and check it again for the number of characters. Deduplicate using "=REMOVE_DUPLICATES_RANGE()". This way you can also see when double words are removed. In the case that double words are removed, check whether the title still makes sense (quality check).
    (This formula is made especially for Brandsom, sometimes it can happen that this formula does not work in your google sheets file. Please ask your supervisor about this!) 


Example of the title builder:

title: Step-by-step plan for Photoshop Batch
subcategory: Photoshop: Step-by-step plan for Photoshop Batch with Excel
body:
This step-by-step plan was made while working on a Soft Touch project - they wanted new images for their products.

Step 1:

Store all the images that you need in a clear and organized way (folders & subfolders)

Note: 

  • Make sure that you have a clear 'Padnaam', which indicates the order your folders are in (see image below).
  • *Click to enlarge
    All images should be PNG
  • Image dimensions should correspond with those in the template. For example, if your images should be 300x300 pixels in the template, then make sure your images are in this correct format*

    *There is a quick and simple way to adjust this for all images. A step-by-step guide on how to do this will be given on the next page of this tutorial.

Step 2: 

The next step is to put all the data, such as SKU, title, texts, and images you need in an excel document as shown below: 

*Click to enlarge

Note: 

  • Make sure the images are saved under the same name as how you saved them on your PC and then copy/paste the padnaam of that image into your excel document. This is quickly done in excel by using the 'merge text' formula.
  • Make sure the headings have a clear title
  • If you want to import a text that consists of commas, make sure that in the excel file the commas are indicated in this way: ",". The reason for this is that if you later save the file as .csv, there will also be commas as the separator. Photoshop gets confused if there are commas for a reason other than separating data - you'll get an error.

Important:

It is best to put the SKU or title of the product at the beginning of your excel document. If you don't want this to be visible in your photoshop file, make an invisible text box somewhere in the document.

If you later upload all files as different files, it will automatically upload all files with the name you gave in the first row of your excel document. This way, you know exactly which photoshop file belongs to each SKU.

*Click to enlarge

Step 3:

Create a separate excel file with a few test products. In this file, you put the same info as in the main excel file.

Step 4: 

Save your final document as .csv file.

Step 5:

Open the csv file through a text editor and replace all semicolons (;) to commas (,). You may also find that there are 4 or 5 commas after each product (,,,,) as shown on the image below. Remove these as well.

*Click to enlarge

Note: You can easily replace all semicolons (;) with commas (,) by typing CTRL+F and searching for semicolons. This way, you don't have to modify them all manually. See image below:

*Click to enlarge

Step 6: 

Save this file.

Step 7:

Open Photoshop and open the template or an empty file (depends on what you need). Create a text area or layer in the spot where an image/text should be, as shown on the image below:

*Click to enlarge

Note: Give this layer the exact same name as how you have it in the excel file:

*Click to enlarge

Step 8:

Next, go to 'Images', then 'Variables' and then to 'Define'
(NL: 'Afbeeldingen' - 'Variabelen' - 'Definiëren') 

Step 9: 

Select the layer in which you want to add your text/image.

Step 10:

In this layer, check 'pixel replacement' ('Pixelvervanging') and give it the exact same name as to how it is in your excel file. See image:*Click to enlarge

Step 11:

Once you've done this for all the layers you want to fill, click 'Next'.

Step 12:

The next step is to click on 'Import'. This will import the csv file as shown in the image below. After doing this click 'Ok'.

*Click to enlarge

Step 13:

If everything went well, the images should be in the right place and the texts will be filled in as well. You can check this by clicking on the arrows next to 'Data set' ('Gegevensset'). Make sure that you have checked 'preview'. By clicking on the arrows you can see the different product images. If everything is correct click on 'OK'.

What if this doesn't work?
- It could be that you accidentally made a spelling mistake in the layer or in the title of your excel file. If this is the case, it will not accept the data and your csv file cannot be imported. This will usually trigger a pop-up. The message in the image below means that the layer 'Main image' is checked in Photoshop (as you had to do in the previous step), but that it's not in your excel file.

*Click to enlarge

- It is also possible that you have a semicolon instead of a comma somewhere. Make sure to always check for this carefully and then save the document again.


After going through all of these steps, you want to test if you are able to save all the images at once. To do this, we will use the Batch tool.


Step 14:

Go to 'File' - 'Export' - 'Data set as files'
(NL: 'Bestand' - 'Exporteren' - 'Gegevensset als bestanden')

Step 15:

Click the folder to which you want to export the PSD(PhotoShop Document) files. (Look at the image below to make sure you're on the right track)

In the first column, fill in 'Name dataset' to have the name of your file be the same as your products. You will also see an example of what it will look like above the column. 

*Click to enlarge

Step 16:

PhotoShop should now save all files as psd files in the folder. If this is the case, you can now import your excel file with all products in exactly the same way as you did with the file that you used for testing.

What if this doesn't work?
You may receive an error message during the export process. Usually, the message tells you that the data set was not found. If this is the case, there is probably an error in the excel file. The error message usually informs you in which row the mistake is located. This makes it easier to find the error. Find the row in your excel document and go through it until you find an error. Next, save the document again as a csv file and follow the previously explained steps from Step 5.

Another error that could occur is a 'disk error' message. If this is the case, you most likely included a / in the name of your excel file. Photoshop doesn't recognize this symbol so it's better to use a dash (-).


These are the most common reasons for an error to show up:

  • Typing error in excel
  • Wrong 'padnaam' of an image
  • Forgetting .png or .jpg after an image name
  • Putting one period (.) too many in the text
  • Incorrectly copied folder name in excel
  • Entering a 'padnaam' with an image that doesn't exist
    - Leaving a text box empty will prompt an error message. Therefore, always fill in something, such as a dash (-)
  • A name in the excel file contains a "/". Change the / to - to fix this error.
    - If you don't have an image for a particular product, create a blank image in Photoshop. Make sure it is a transparent background and save it as a png. For the products that don't require an image, enter the 'padnaam' of the blank image. As you can see on the images below, the blank image has become transparent and only the background of the template is shown:

*Click to enlarge

Tip:
If you find it difficult to find a mistake in your padnaam, there is an easy way to check if it is correct:
For Mac users, go to 'Finder', use the shortcut CMND+Shift+G, put in your padnaam and press enter. If this leads you to the correct image without any errors, the padnaam works. Now you can go back to checking other columns in your excel file.
If 'Finder' does not find the padnaam, this indicates that there is something wrong with it and you should carefully look where the error is located. Usually, a hyphen (-) is in the wrong position or an uppercase letter that should be a lowercase letter or vice versa. So always make sure to double-check this.

title: Economic sustainability
body:
Another pillar is economic sustainability. This pillar focuses on improving living standards via a healthy economic growth. So if we apply it to the business context it refers to using several assets to maintain companies’ profitability over time. 

In a nutshell:

Economic sustainability implies stable levels of economic growth taking into account sustainable development as a core factor. The quality of growth matters as well as the quantity. Economic growth is therefore challenged by having to take into account continual growth and maintaining balanced conditions for ecological and human systems (Benn et al., 2014).


From a business perspective point of view: Companies can improve their environmental sustainability by, for example, reducing their carbon footprint or wasteful practices.

title: Sustainability and its pillars
body:
Sustainability

The term sustainability is broadly used to indicate programs, initiatives and actions aimed at the preservation of a particular resource. However, it actually refers to four distinct areas: human, social, economic and environmental – known as the four pillars of sustainability.

Main pillars of sustainability

Sustainability is currently one rising topic within fashion and e-commerce. For this reason, learning more about what it means and how to apply it within our field can be helpful.

Sustainability and its pillars 

This term is used to indicate actions of different nature and across several fields that have as a main goal to preserve a particular resource. To make the goals more concrete there are four pillars: human, social, economic and environmental.

title: Sales Manager Job Profile
subtitle: The Sales Manager at Brandsom is responsible for new business development, driving lead generation, customer acquisition, and pipeline management.
body:
1. The Function

Job Title: Sales Manager

Role Description:

The Sales Manager at Brandsom is responsible for new business development, driving lead generation, customer acquisition, and pipeline management. This role focuses on outbound sales, ensuring a structured and efficient approach to converting leads into long-term clients. Additionally, the Sales Manager is responsible for hunt list creation, sales process improvements, and working with partners to generate sales.

Key KPIs include:

  • Outbound lead generation
  • New business revenue target

Reporting Line:

The Sales Manager reports directly to the CEO. While there is no dedicated team reporting into this role, the Sales Manager collaborates with:

  • Marketing for lead generation
  • Consultants for sales analysis
  • Finance for contract structuring
  • COO for pricing strategies

2. Your Responsibilities

High-Level Responsibilities

  1. New Business Development

    • Proactively identify and engage potential clients through outbound efforts.
    • Develop and execute outbound sales strategies to generate qualified leads.
    • Work with marketing to optimize lead generation efforts and inbound sales conversion.

  2. Lead Nurturing & Pipeline Management

    • Build and maintain a structured sales pipeline with a steady flow of opportunities.
    • Leverage AI to personalize and automate lead nurturing through structured follow-ups, insights, and value-driven engagement.
    • Utilize AI-driven tools for lead scoring, outreach automation, and predictive analytics. Leverage HubSpot for CRM management, sales automation, and structured follow-up processes.
    • Continuously refine pipeline processes to increase efficiency and forecasting accuracy.

  3. Sales Execution & Deal Closing

    • Conduct discovery calls, demos, and proposal presentations tailored to client needs.
    • Negotiate contracts, working with finance and the COO on pricing strategies.
    • Secure long-term agreements that align with Brandsom’s business model.

  4. Collaboration & Partner Sales

    • Work with tech partners and marketplaces to generate joint sales opportunities.
    • Align with consultants, marketing, and finance to streamline the sales approach.

3. How to Make Impact

On the Customer Side

  • Understand client needs and position Brandsom’s marketplace solutions effectively.
  • Build trust with potential customers through tailored sales approaches.
  • Maintain a structured follow-up process using AI-driven insights to improve engagement and conversion rates.
  • Have a clear understanding of the phase the prospect/lead is in and adjust the approach accordingly (e.g., awareness, consideration, or decision phase).

On the Process Side

  • Optimize outbound sales workflows and improve the lead generation process.
  • Implement AI-powered tools within HubSpot to streamline prospecting, automate follow-ups, and enhance CRM insights.
  • Use AI-driven predictive analytics for better forecasting and efficiency in pipeline management.
  • Ensure collaboration between sales, marketing, finance, and consultants for a seamless customer journey.

On the Growth Side

  • Identify and capitalize on new market opportunities.
  • Leverage insights from AI-driven lead interactions to refine the sales strategy.
  • Strengthen partnerships with tech providers and marketplaces to create new revenue streams.

4. Your Competencies

Core Knowledge & Skills

  • Strong expertise in outbound sales, lead generation, and pipeline management.
  • Solid understanding of AI-driven sales tools and their impact on sales efficiency.
  • Experience in HubSpot CRM for lead tracking, automation, and sales pipeline management.
  • Ability to assess and adjust sales strategies based on the prospect’s phase in the buying journey.
  • Experience with CRM systems, sales automation, and analytics tools.

Leadership & Management Skills

  • Excellent communication and negotiation skills to close deals effectively.
  • Ability to collaborate cross-functionally with marketing, consultants, and finance.
  • Self-starter with a proactive and results-driven mindset.

Analytical & Innovation Abilities

  • AI-oriented and process-minded approach to sales efficiency.
  • Strong data analysis skills to improve lead conversion and sales strategy.
  • Continuous improvement mindset, leveraging automation and feedback loops to optimize performance.

5. Your Experience

  • 5+ years of experience in B2B sales, preferably within marketplaces, e-commerce, SaaS, or digital services.
  • Proven track record in new business development, outbound sales, and lead generation.
  • Experience working with AI-driven sales tools, HubSpot CRM, and sales automation.
  • Strong background in managing sales pipelines, nurturing leads, and optimizing conversion rates.
  • Ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams, including marketing, consultants, and finance.
  • Experience with strategic partnerships, preferably with tech providers or marketplaces.

title: COO Job Profile
body:
1. The Function

COO
Brandsom’s COO holds a pivotal role within our organization, driving both strategic and operational initiatives that directly impact the consultancy teams and client success. This includes responsibility for customer retention, upsells, and renewals, while also owning core KPIs such as gross margin, average hourly rate, hours logged, and billability. Although this is a C-level position, Brandsom’s current size means that the COO will also handle operational tasks to structurally improve processes tied to these performance indicators.

Reporting Line
 The COO reports into the CEO and collaborates closely with the consultancy teams and the finance controller to ensure targets are met and exceeded.

2. Your Responsibilities

The COO plays a crucial role in overseeing the consultancy teams and ensuring customer success. By managing recruitment, development, and performance within these teams, the COO is directly responsible for meeting key organizational KPIs. While strategic service development is primarily owned by the Head of Marketplace & Digital Strategy, the COO makes sure internal processes and team structures support the effective delivery of those services.

High-Level Responsibilities

  • Consultancy Teams: Oversee recruitment, onboarding, and professional development of consultants, ensuring alignment between job profiles and actual business needs.
  • Customer Success: Support account performance and client satisfaction, tracking renewals, upsells, and overall retention.
  • Team Development: Continuously evaluate roles, responsibilities, and job profiles to keep them up to date; manage performance by identifying gaps and creating action plans for improvement.
  • KPI Management: Drive and monitor key performance indicators (gross margin = 50%, average hourly rate = €85, hours logged = 90%, billability = 85%) and implement structural changes to maintain or exceed these targets.

Service Development

Although the creation of new service offerings is led by the Head of Marketplace & Digital Strategy, the COO ensures that the existing team capabilities, operational processes, and internal structures fully support the ongoing delivery and enhancement of those services.

Team Development & Support

  • Right People, Right Roles: Ensure the recruitment of top talent and a clear understanding of each individual’s responsibilities and success metrics.
  • Performance Management: Implement performance improvement plans for underperforming individuals, provide growth opportunities for strong performers, and transition out employees who consistently fail to meet expectations.
  • Culture & Growth: Foster a culture of accountability, learning, and collaboration to drive both individual and team success.

3. How to Make Impact

  • On the customer side
     Maintain a clear overview of client needs and collaborate with the Head of Marketplaces & Digital Strategy to ensure Brandsom’s products and services deliver real value to key accounts. Balance client satisfaction with profitability by monitoring allocated hours and maintaining the target average hourly rate.

  • On the process side
     Structure teams for maximum efficiency—ensuring the right talent mix, clear responsibilities, and well-defined roles. This helps streamline workflows, reduce bottlenecks, and promote accountability.

  • On the quality side
     Continuously identify efficiency bottlenecks and leverage customer feedback to stay ahead of market trends. By actively seeking out areas for improvement and acting on real-world insights, the COO drives a culture of innovation and operational excellence.

4. Your Competencies

  • Core Knowledge & Skills
    • Expertise in managing people and structuring teams, including setting and monitoring KPIs.
    • Solid understanding of digital agency operations and the corresponding performance metrics.
    • Strong planning abilities, with a focus on effective scheduling and resource allocation.
  • Leadership & Management Skills
    • Coaching mindset, dedicated to educating and mentoring consultants on HR and leadership topics.
    • Exceptional communication skills to align cross-functional teams and stakeholders.
    • Proven capacity to inspire trust, foster collaboration, and lead by example.
  • Analytical & Innovation Abilities
    • AI-oriented and process-minded approach to identify opportunities for efficiency and automation.
    • Proficiency in data-driven decision-making, particularly in resource and hourly planning.
    • Continuous improvement outlook, leveraging technology and feedback loops to optimize performance.

5. Your Experience

  • Minimum 10 years of work experience, with at least 5 years in a management team (MT) or leadership role.
  • Proven track record in scale-ups, digital agencies, or e-commerce environments.
  • Demonstrated ability to communicate effectively and lead cross-functional teams in a dynamic, fast-paced setting.

title: Finance controller Job profile
subtitle: The Finance Controller at Brandsom plays a critical role in shaping the financial strategy, reporting, and operational efficiency of the company.
body:
Finance Controller Job Profile

1. The Function

Job Title: Finance Controller

Role Description:

The Finance Controller at Brandsom plays a critical role in shaping the financial strategy, reporting, and operational efficiency of the company. This role ensures financial oversight, budgeting, and forecasting, while also identifying opportunities for cost control and efficiency improvements. Additionally, the Finance Controller leads financial operations across multiple teams, providing insights to optimize revenue and profitability.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Financial planning, budgeting, and forecasting
  • Team P&L and financial performance monitoring
  • Sales operations support and reporting
  • Process optimization and efficiency improvements
  • Overseeing payroll, invoicing, and financial administration
  • Developing financial insights to support strategic decision-making
  • Implementing AI-driven financial tools and automation where applicable

Reporting Line:

The Finance Controller reports directly to the CEO and collaborates with key stakeholders across teams, including the COO, consultants, and sales.

2. Your Responsibilities

High-Level Responsibilities

  1. Financial Oversight & Strategic Leadership

    • Oversee all aspects of financial management, including budgeting, forecasting, and cost control.
    • Provide financial insights and recommendations to the CEO and senior leadership.
    • Ensure compliance with financial regulations and internal policies.
    • Leverage AI-driven financial tools to enhance reporting, forecasting accuracy, and decision-making.

  2. Operational & Team Management

    • Lead financial operations within vertical teams, ensuring alignment with company objectives.
    • Support team sales operations, targets, and performance monitoring.
    • Develop and implement financial training and best practices for internal teams.
    • Introduce AI-powered automation where beneficial for financial reporting and analytics.

  3. Financial Reporting & Analysis

    • Manage monthly financial reporting, P&L analysis, and performance tracking.
    • Identify cost-saving opportunities and efficiency improvements.
    • Develop financial dashboards and KPIs to streamline decision-making.
    • Ensure AI-driven analytics are used where applicable to improve accuracy and efficiency.

  4. Financial Administration & Compliance

    • Oversee payroll, invoicing, and financial follow-ups on outstanding payments.
    • Maintain accurate financial records and compliance with accounting standards.
    • Manage financial tasks such as contract creation, bookkeeping, and invoice tagging.
    • Explore automation opportunities for financial administration to reduce manual workload.

3. How to Make Impact

On the Financial Strategy Side

  • Provide financial insights and forecasting to help steer company growth.
  • Ensure financial stability and compliance by maintaining accurate financial tracking and reporting.
  • Use AI-driven financial analytics to optimize forecasting and budget planning.

On the Operational Side

  • Streamline financial workflows for greater efficiency in invoicing, budgeting, and payroll management.
  • Support sales teams and operations with financial insights for revenue optimization.
  • Implement automation tools where applicable to improve reporting and workflow efficiency.

On the Compliance & Quality Side

  • Maintain compliance with financial regulations and ensure proper documentation.
  • Conduct regular financial performance reviews and recommend improvements.
  • Ensure AI is applied responsibly in financial processes to improve accuracy and compliance.

4. Your Competencies

Core Knowledge & Skills

  • Strong background in financial planning, budgeting, and analysis.
  • Proficiency in accounting and ERP software.
  • Experience in sales operations and financial reporting.
  • Ability to optimize financial processes and identify cost-saving opportunities.
  • Knowledge of AI applications in finance and automation is a plus.

Leadership & Management Skills

  • Proven experience in managing financial operations within a fast-growing company.
  • Strong communication and stakeholder management skills.
  • Ability to provide financial mentorship and training to internal teams.

Analytical & Innovation Abilities

  • Ability to analyze financial data and make strategic recommendations.
  • Strong problem-solving mindset with a focus on efficiency.
  • Experience with AI-driven analytics and automation tools for financial management is an advantage.

5. Your Experience

  • 2+ years of experience in financial management, preferably in e-commerce or a fast-growing industry.
  • Proven experience in financial reporting, forecasting, and budgeting.
  • Strong background in ERP and financial software tools.
  • Experience implementing financial automation solutions is a plus.
  • Excellent stakeholder management and cross-functional collaboration skills.

title: Digital Asset Management (DAM)
subcategory: WIP - Marketplace and E-commerce IT set-up 101
body:
Digital asset management (DAM) is a system that stores, shares and organizes digital assets in a central location. It amplifies the benefits of creative files such as images, videos and other media. Ultimately, DAM is a company's content sharing and storage solution. An example of DAM software is 'Bynder'.

DAM increases the security, organization and searchability of digital assets (videos, photos, documents and other media) and expands content storage while upholding brand standards. Addressing how you manage digital assets will certainly affect the success of your product. An abundance of products, images, and other files, combined with inefficient systems, leads to slow market time and reduced productivity - all of which are avoidable. 

Marketplaces and DAM

  • Marketplaces sometimes require extended and specific image requirements. DAM systems help to meet these requirements

title: Enriched Images - How to request
body:
Enriched Images - How to request

Since we only have limited capacity to create enriched images, there is a strict process how to request them for your client. Please follow the instructions below:

Gather all information

As instructed in the previous lesson of this academy, there is specific information required to make this process as efficient as possible. This comes down to:

  • You gathered all the information from the client
    • Access to drive / location where images are stored
    • Textual information available
  • Content has been fully optimized, meaning no big changes will be made soon
  • It is a high-potential product, meaning that the enriched images will actually contribute to the success of the product
  • If the client is in the launch phase, you can create enriched images for the 5-10 focus products

If your product listings check all the above boxes, create an Asana task with all the information. Now you can move on to the next step!

Request hours

Again, capacity is limited and for now, we do not have a dedicated designer yet. We have about 16-24 per week available to create enriched images. Estimations for enriched images for 1 product would be:

  • Completely new clients, no enriched images previously made
    • Creating template - 4 hours
    • Creating images per product - 2-3 hours per product
  • New enriched images, template already created
    • 2-3 hours per product

Always communicate the estimate. At this time the design capacity is available in the Home & Garden team, therefore enriched images should be requested with the Team Lead and Consultant, most recent information can be found in Hubspot.

title: Fulfilment by Octopia (Cdiscount)
body:
The French marketplace Cdiscount is launching a new business called Octopia. This entity helps companies of all sizes and backgrounds to access the power of e-commerce by providing a turnkey marketplace. It provides access to numerous services to facilitate your e-commerce development in France and on an international scale. Many of the services, such as the fulfilment logistics service and the support for your business are operated by Octopia. With this, you can follow the progress of your different packaging from the seller space. Once the products have been placed in storage, they will take care of everything for each of your orders, from preparing your orders to delivery, and even customer relations if required.

More information on Cdiscount Marketplace and Fulfilment by Octopia is listed below:

  • Cdiscount is a French marketplace and is known for its favourable prices.
  • Cdiscount is active in France, Belgium, Germany, Italy and Spain.
  • Storage, packaging and shipping of products, management of returns and customer relations, and management of Cdiscount orders are handled by Octopia. This allows for the range of products to be increased, as storage space no longer needs to be purchased.
  • Lower shipping costs and faster shipping - in many cases, shipping via Cdiscount is cheaper and faster for the customer, which makes it much more appealing to order from Cdiscount over other marketplaces.
  • Customer service - with fulfilment by Octopia, customers can simply contact their customer service, which is available 7 days a week in the local language. This eliminates the need to employ different people for various languages.
  • Time-saving - staff no longer has to spend time on packing and shipping or handling returns.
  • Flexibility - with Fulfilment by Octopia, you can be sure that products will always be shipped, even if you or your staff are temporarily unavailable.

title: Introduction & Content
subcategory: TBR - Channel Added Value marketplace strategy framework
body:
Welcome to the Brandsom Academy course and strategy framework: 'Channel Added Value'. With this strategy framework, you will be able to determine the added value per channel in the online distribution strategy of the brand. 

We will use 'Casall' (a Swedish sportswear brand) as an example. Let's imagine Casall wants to increase its brand awareness on the German market. How can they achieve this?

With this course, you will learn which channels are suitable to increase brand awareness per region, and how you can operate on these platforms using the different Brandsom pillars: strategy, integration, operation, content and traffic. 

Download:

Feedback >> academy@brandsom.nl

title: Overview of specific campaigns
body:
To keep all advertising possibilities a bit clearer, a distinction is made between a launch phase and a growth phase. 

The launch phase gives you a good starting point with campaigns that you can create when a client starts advertising. These campaigns are also the most generalizable to any client (again, some customization will always be necessary). In Brand Protection you will find the most necessary campaigns, in Profitable Growth, you will find campaigns to generate keywords, and to increase Brand Awareness you can launch an exposure campaign. You will see that the Profitable Growth campaigns, in particular, need more steering in the beginning and stabilize over time.


Click on the image to enlarge


In the growth phase, you probably have a better idea of which keywords and product targets work. You also have an idea of what your best-sellers are. Now you can use the advertising budget in a more targeted way. You can also create separate campaigns for products that convert well in terms of clicks per order but are not profitable enough to capitalize on this. Brand Protection can be expanded with Sponsored Brand campaigns and a budget can be freed up to do more awareness and competitor targeting. The growth phase requires more customization because every customer is different. These campaigns are more intended as tools to give you an idea of what is possible. Feel free to make adjustments here, as long as you always ask yourself the question What will my KPIs look like if this campaign runs well and what will I be able to do to adjust them? 


Click on the image to enlarge

title: Lesson 4: Identifying the Ideal Customer Profile (ICP) and Building Hunting Lists
body:
In outbound sales, success hinges on targeting the right companies and individuals—those who align closely with our Ideal Customer Profile (ICP). This lesson outlines the criteria for identifying our ICP, the industries to target, and the tools you’ll use to build effective hunting lists.


Target Industries (Verticals)
We focus on companies within the following verticals:
    •    Fashion    

    •    Home and Garden   

    •    Consumer Products    

    •    Home Appliances    

    •    Personal Wellness and Beauty


Company Criteria
    1.    Revenue Range:    

           •    Target companies with annual revenues between €5–100 million.    

           •    Our sweet spot lies in companies between €10–50 million.    

   2.    Geography:    

           •    Companies should already be located within Europe.


Hunting Lists
Hunting lists are curated groups of companies that meet the above criteria. These lists will be provided, and we’ll update them as needed to align with our sales goals.

Hunting Lists: TBD


Ideal Customer Profile (ICP) Roles
We target decision-makers and specialists whose roles align with the following functions:
    1.    Marketplace Roles    

           •    Marketplace Director, Head of Marketplaces, Marketplace Coordinator,                             Marketplace Manager, Marketplace Specialist    

    2.    Ecommerce Roles    

            •    Ecommerce Director, Head of Ecommerce, Ecommerce Coordinator,                               Ecommerce Manager, Ecommerce Specialist    

     3.    Digital Roles    

           •    Digital Director, Head of Digital, Digital Coordinator, Digital Manager, Digital  m.                Specialist    

     4.    Sales and Other Relevant Roles    

           •    Chief Digital/Ecommerce Officer, Head of Online Sales, Sales Director


These individuals are most likely to be involved in decisions about marketplace operations and ecommerce strategies, making them prime contacts for outreach.


How to Find the ICP
Identifying the right individuals can be done in three ways:
    1.    Check Lead Info First    

           •    Always start by checking our existing system to see if we already have the lead               information available.   

    2.    Use LinkedIn Sales Navigator    

           •    This tool allows you to search for individuals by job title, location, and company               criteria.    

3.    Use Lusha    

          •    Lusha provides verified contact information for potential leads.


Tutorials
To ensure you are confident in using these tools, please watch the following tutorials:
    1.    How to Search for ICP in Lead Info   

    2.    Using LinkedIn Sales Navigator for ICP Searches

    3.    Finding ICP with Lusha


By the end of this lesson, you should feel equipped to identify target companies and decision-makers effectively. These efforts form the foundation of a strong outbound sales strategy.

title: Formula Vertical Lookup Explained
body:
In addition to the video about Excel's VLOOKUP function, we've created a step-by-step guide to help you get familiar with this useful formula in Excel.

Example of a situation in which you could use this function: 

Suppose Thijs has given each employee a number when he signed their contract and is now trying to figure out which number belongs to each employee.


Step-by-step guide

If the names, displayed in the image above, are in a different order, it is useful for Thijs to be able to VLOOKUP (name of Excel formula). This is done as follows:

Step 1: Select which information you want to use for a vertical lookup (VERT.ZOEKEN or VLOOKUP), in this case, the names shown in column A. Select one cell as shown in the image below:


Step 2: Select in which column(s) you want to search for the additional information vertically. In this case, these are columns A and B of the sheet 'Namen'. Make sure to select the entire column.



Step 3: Put in Excel how many columns you want it to search for the information - in this case, 2.



Step 4: Put in Excel that you want it to only show the exact corresponding values. This is done by entering "ONWAAR" or "FALSE" in the formula.



Step 5: Type the close bracket; ' ) ' and press enter. You will see the employee number next to, in this case, Rana. You can get the other employee numbers by dragging down the created formula.



 Copying the information

If you want to copy the above-mentioned information to another field, you'll see that the created formula will remain. If you only want to copy the values (in this case the employee numbers), do as follows:

Step 1: Copy the entire column


Step 2: In another column, click 'Plakken speciaal' and then 'Waarden'

You will see that if you now click on cell B1, it will show a formula at the top. However, if you click on cell C1, you will only see Rana's employee number.

title: What Causes errors 8542?
subcategory: Amazon Error Solving SOP Overview - internal
body:
What Causes errors 8542?

This error is commonly referred to as a "multiple matching error" and occurs when your product ID (UPC, EAN, JAN, ISBN, ASIN etc.) is currently associated with two or more ASINs, but the other attributes you have provided in your inventory template (title, brand, color, size, etc.) do not match any of these ASINs.

Sample:


ResultMessageCode: 8542


ResultDescription: The SKU data provided conflicts with the Amazon catalog. The standard_product_id value(s) provided correspond to the ASIN BXXXXXXXXX, but some information contradicts with the Amazon catalog. The following are the attribute value(s) that are conflicting: size (Merchant: [Merchant value]/ Amazon: [Amazon value]). If your product is this ASIN, then modify your data to reflect the Amazon catalog values. Else, check your value(s) for standard_product_id are correct. 

How to solve errors 8542?

You should create an interface in your solution where the sellers can see the details of the products with such errors along with the conflicting data i.e. Amazon value and Merchant value. Please note that your solution should be able to pick both the values from error report.

With this interface, a seller should be able to continue with the following process:


  1. Check if the product is the same as they actually intended to sell.

    1. If it is the same product, you need to use the ASIN from Amazon instead of the EAN code the seller provided.

    2. If it is not the same, the seller must check their own data and correct anything that is wrong.

    3. If the seller is sure that his/her data is correct, you should direct them to contact Seller Support and report an error with the product data on Amazon.

    4. If you have used ASIN while uploading the product, either change the conflicting data or only use SKU and ASIN with no other product information.


You can also implement a preventive measure by checking the EANs of the products provided by the sellers using the Amazon Products API before you upload the Sellers’ catalogs. This way you can show potential conflicts to the seller beforehand.

title: Master Data Management (MDM)
subcategory: WIP - Marketplace and E-commerce IT set-up 101
body:
Master data management (MDM) is a technology-enabled discipline in which business and information technology work together to ensure the uniformity, accuracy, stewardship, semantic consistency and accountability of the enterprise's official shared master data assets.

MDM arose out of the necessity for businesses to improve the consistency and quality of their key data assets, such as product data, asset data, customer data and location data.

The need for accurate, timely information is acute and as sources of data increase, managing it consistently and keeping data definitions up to date so all parts of a business use the same information is a never-ending challenge. To meet these challenges, businesses turn to MDM.

title: Amazon Launch Plan Instructions - Content
body:
Amazon Launch Plan Instructions - Content

In this part we will dive more into the content strategy that we will use for the client, meaning:

  • What search behavior do we see on the marketplace? (Keywords)
  • Are we missing any information from the client? For example, specific images
  • What should we focus on with the products?

You can also take this moment to discuss with the client why there is a price difference for example. If the product of the client is relatively on the high side of the market, there usually is a good reason for this. This should then also be highlighted in the content to inform potential buyers.

Search Term Analysis on the Marketplace

Before the kick-off meeting, the client will submit a list of potential products and the USPs. The idea is to match and confirm the potential of these products on the marketplace. Perform keyword research to determine the search behavior, are the USPs matching with what the consumer is looking for? For example:

  • Global Glass sells plastic glasses that are nearly unbreakable. This is also shown in the search results. Consumers are also looking for 'Camping glasses' meaning we should focus on this.
  • M Line sells a pillow that stays cool during summer nights, this is a clear USP of the product. In the keyword research, we see consumers are searching for a pillow with this specific USP.

Do we have all the information we need?

It is very important to match the content we create with the visuals we have available. Once we have decided the content strategy for the product, we also need to visuals to support this in the enriched images. Therefore it is good to discuss with the client what they have available and if we need any new images created. For example:

  • For Global Glass we decided to focus on camping and outdoor, the image they had was a fancy dining table. Which is a mismatch with the strategy we want to follow in our content. We requested them to create images in a more outdoor and camping setting.
  • For M Line the focus was on the cooling aspect of the pillow, but we didn't have any assets supporting this. We requested M Line to create images to make it more visual to clients.

title: What causes error 90202?
subcategory: Amazon Error Solving SOP Overview - internal
body:
What causes error 90202?

This error occurs when there is a restriction in effect at the item level. Amazon has restrictions on certain ASINs that prevent sellers from listing against them without prior approval. Attempting to list with condition-type “Used-Like-New” against an ASIN with condition-type “New” is a common cause for error 90202.

Sample:


ResultMessageCode: 90202


ResultDescription: There is a restriction in effect at the item level (because of Item-Level gatings on an offer).

How to solve and prevent error 90202?

Once you detect this error on the feed for a seller, check with the seller to ensure that the condition type mentioned in the file is correct. If the condition type was wrong, correct the same and resubmit the data.


If the issue does not get resolved, instruct the seller to contact Seller Support from their account in Seller Central to understand why these products are restricted and if it is possible for them to sell those products on Amazon. Your application should block such SKUs with these errors from uploading until the seller confirms that they have received a good to go from the Seller Support to upload these products.

title: Default marketplace IT setup
subcategory: WIP - Marketplace and E-commerce IT set-up 101
body:
There are 2 common IT set-ups that most brands use:

ERP integration

Set-up

With the implementation of ERP software, your e-commerce website is more manageable and easily organized with a streamlined order process. With an ERP solution, your e-commerce platform can integrate a variety of software programs and applications so they run together seamlessly.

Advantages

  • No dependency on e-commerce platform
  • No dependency on e-commerce product selection
  • Less latency and delay in the interfaces


Disadvantages

  • This integration option is often more expensive 



E-commerce platform integration

Set-up

In this setup, the brand uses an e-commerce system or platform and prefers to connect directly to the ERP system. An e-commerce platform only has reporting capabilities on sales and marketing. In order to integrate the data with other systems, you would need IT intervention or have employees manually re-key the information, which is an inefficient and error-prone process.

Advantages

  • Often the cheapest integration option

Disadvantages

  • Integrator plug-ins for Magento, Lightspeed, and WooCommerce often have limited features regarding the processing of orders, content and images

title: Introduction
body:
One of the most important parts of working at Brandsom is fulfilling our client's needs. This is simply because they are the reason we get to work. Therefore, you want to build a strong working relationship with the client. To achieve this,  good communication is necessary to figure out their goals and needs and to let them know what we can do to help. After all, you want to keep the client satisfied. How do we do this? And what exactly is good communication and how do we keep it businesslike?

One way of communicating is by e-mail. Another frequently used option is calling the client by phone. It is very important to write and talk in a formal way at the early stages of the business relationship.


When to call, e-mail or plan a Google Meet?

We believe that a periodic meeting structure is the core of good communication with the customer. Besides this, we use the Voys (internet phone) and e-mail.

  • Try to discuss all topics in the (bi)weekly, monthly and quarterly meetings. If you receive e-mails in the meantime discuss the also during these meetings.
  • Second way of communication method is e-mail because it's clearer, faster and more reliable for the customer.
  • Third way of communicating is calling the customer using Voys. Sometimes it's quicker to just call the customer, if you call the customer please use Voys.
  • The customer can call the generic phone number (please check the site for the latest number) and re-routed if needed.


Tone of voice 

When you communicate with a client, it is important to use a formal tone of voice. This indicates the way and the tone in which you write or speak to a client. Your tone of voice should be professional and friendly. This way of communicating will be explained in detail in this module.

title: General ruling regarding Swiss brands
body:
•  Switzerland is not a member of the EU, and the rules and regulations for the sale of goods and services are different from those of the EU. 
  •  Swiss companies still have the opportunity to sell goods on EU platforms, but they must comply with EU legislation and register for VAT purposes in the EU countries where they sell.
  • If a Swiss company sells products to EU consumers, it must register for VAT purposes in the country where the consumer is located.

title: Equal Opportunity, Inclusion and Diversity
body:
At Brandsom, we are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive work environment where all employees have equal opportunities to thrive. We ensure that all employment decisions, including hiring, promotions, and benefits, are based on merit, qualifications, and abilities. Discrimination based on age, race, religion, gender, sexual orientation, disability, or any other protected characteristic is strictly prohibited.

We aim to maintain a workplace free from threats and harassment, where everyone is treated fairly and has the chance to succeed. We value respect and expect all employees to treat each other with dignity, upholding good manners and confidentiality. Employees should act as ambassadors of our company, demonstrating professionalism in all interactions. Threatening, intimidating, or discriminatory behavior, as well as offensive or inappropriate language, will not be tolerated.

Our goal is to create an environment where all employees feel valued and supported. If you have any concerns about equal opportunities or experience any form of discrimination, please reach out to your supervisor or HR for assistance.

title: Channel types explained
subcategory: TBR - Channel Added Value marketplace strategy framework
body:
We have already introduced the 4 channel types with an image earlier on in this module. Now it is time to go more in-depth about the different channel types. As a reminder, the 4 different channel types are:

  • Demand creation
  • Demand fulfilment 
  • Category specialist
  • Discounters

Depending on your strategy, channels can be used in multiple buckets. Zalando and Amazon, for example, can be used as a demand creation channel, as well as a fulfilment channel. We will get into this more in the next slides.

Demand creation

Demand creation channels are used to create demand within a specific region. These platforms can be used to introduce products and brands in new regions. Demand creation is about educating customers about why they need a product or service from your company. 

Zalando is often used as a demand creation platform. With their powerful marketing tools and organic audience, they are able to create demand in the markets they are active in. 



Demand fulfilment

Demand fulfilment channels are used to 'fulfil' the demand for a certain product or brand in a specific region. This demand (for example brand awareness) has been created outside of the platform.

Amazon could be used as a demand fulfilment platform. The Amazon audience has a high buy intent and often already has their mind made up on a specific product they want to buy. Demand does not have to be created here, as it is already there. 

Category specialist

Category specialists are channels that offer their audience a niche category of products. Examples are lingerie, sustainable wear or premium clothing brands. 

Breuninger for example only sells fashion brands in a luxe and high price segment. This makes Breuninger a good example of a category specialist in the premium segment. 


Another example of a category specialist is Bever, a store that specialises in/is well-known for their wide assortment of Outdoor products. 


There are also category specialists that specialise in offering sustainable items. A great example of this is Avocadostore. 

Discounters

Afound is an example of a discounter. They are part of the H&M group and offer discounts on products from all brands that also belong to the H&M group.

title: Minimum Cost Price Template

Feel free to fill in this sheet yourself with the information you received from the client, or let the client fill this in. 


The last columns are the most important columns. This will display the net profitability and margins of the client. 


Keep an eye out on the values for VAT. These are product and country specific. Further information can be found in the template, by hovering over the subject and a note will appear.

The Template can be found here.

title: Discount channel
subcategory: TBR - Channel Added Value marketplace strategy framework
body:
Afound from Sweden, a daughter company of the H&M group, is an example of a discount channel. Only offering discounted items and deals, this channel could have a specific function in your strategy.

. 

Characteristics of a Discount channel

We will classify every channel type using the topics below. These topics are roughly the same as the Brandsom marketplace pillars (strategy, operation, integration, content and traffic). Integration is not included because this framework only focuses on marketing and sales.

  • Audience (part of the strategy)
  • Assortment (part of the strategy)
  • Marketing (part of traffic)
  • Content
  • Pricing (part of the strategy)
  • Operations


Audience

  • Closed platform

  • The platform has an organic audience (looking for discount)

  • Direct relation with the consumer (user account)

  • End of funnel


Assortment

  • Old season

  • Overstock


Marketing

  • Vouchers

  • Special deals and offers


Content

  • Product detail page


Goals of content:

  • Revenue


Pricing

Operations

title: Zalando Return Rate Analysis
body:
Which factors thrive the return rate?

Which factors influence the returns analysis?

You can analyze your return rates based on each country and product details such as size, color, or style. It’s helpful to understand that there are specific patterns seen across countries, categories, customer gender and price

points, and you should therefore expect this to be reflected when you review and compare return rates in these groups

Country 

  • In Germany, Austria and Switzerland (DACH region), Zalndo observe the highest Return Rates, ranging from 20-25 percentage points above the average seen across all Zalando markets.

  • The lowest Return Rates are often seen in Central and Southern Europe (France, Italy and Spain), where you should expect 15-20 percentage points below the average across all markets. The same trend is observed in Ireland.

  • The UK and Norway also register lower return numbers versus the average, on a scale of around 10 percentage points lower.

  • The remaining markets (Netherlands, Belgium, Finland, Sweden, Poland, Denmark, Czech Republic) show fairly similar behaviors, coming in at around the overall market average.

Gender 

  • Female customers tend to return items more frequently than male customers

Price

  • There are higher returns on articles with higher price points.


How to solve a high return rate?

If you notice an item has a high return rate, you can investigate the product content, sizing, and pricing. 

Product content

Check your content and attributes to make sure they all line up.

Sizing

This can be a common reason for returns, so make sure your size advice gives people a realistic expectation and guides them to the right purchase. If you use a model in your photos, it can be helpful to state a point of reference, such as the model’s height and size worn by the model.

Pricing 

Customers are sensitive to pricing when it comes to deciding whether to return an item or not. Generally, there are higher returns on articles with higher price points. Similarly, we also see that overall returns decrease when there are higher discounts offered to the customer

Promotion

You can also promote these items with additional advertising. 

If you still see no improvement after taking remedial action, it is probably better to remove the item from Zalando.

title: Manual for lead inbox management
body:
USE THE MANUAL: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1GoniJhiM8TMJD8iPgb5uHsdspSk7HtA-orxNZo-YMdU/edit


The procedure is that all messages will be picked up 2x per day for 30 minutes: In the morning and in the afternoon. All messages will be answered from the contact@brandsom.nl mailbox. Messages can be replied with your own name or a pseudonym if preferred + role: Sales Agent. 

For example:

 

Kind regards,

Joris

Sales agent at Brandsom

 

Once you click a message (and it becomes ‘’read’’) it becomes your responsibility to answer that specific message. 

Do you not have enough time to finish the message = put the message back to ‘unread’.

All messages that you pick up need to be tagged. The following tags can be used:

 

Consultancy -  High priority tag for warm and high-potential leads.  These messages need to be prioritized and should receive special attention. Thus it may not be desirable to send an Academy template message but a more direct email initiating a meeting.

 

Academy - Low priority tag for no potential clients. These messages can be answered with an automated response/template response + information about the customer is saved in Academy. (Deals amount = €1000) 

Note: After receiving the first reply, the customer could move to Potential Lead. 

 

Potential lead - These are all the messages that fall between academy and consultancy. These messages can be answered through a template.

 

 Tone of voice

English:

  • You can use ‘dear’, ‘good morning or ‘good afternoon’ to address the customer. 

  • In case the name is not visible: Dear sir/madam

  • Extra: If you encounter British customers that use a more formal way of communication, then please adjust accordingly. 


Nederlands:

  • Normaal gesproken kan je de klant altijd benaderen met ‘je’, de enige uitzonderingen zijn: 

  • Als de klant uit vlaams België komt

  • Als de klant zelf al met U ons heeft benaderd

  • Je kan de klant adresseren met ‘Beste’, ‘Goedemorgen’ of ‘Goedemiddag’. 

  • Je kan altijd de voornaam gebruiken van de klant behalve als de klant zelf eindigd met meneer/mevrouw…

 

Tags


Once you have opened a message you need to verify whether the message comes from a high potential client (Consultancy), potential lead, or no lead (Academy). Go through all three of the following steps, they will help you determine which tag belongs to your customer.


  1. Look up the customer on Linkedin: 

  1.  Does the customer have a LinkedIn presence and more than 10 employees = Consultancy

  2. Does the customer have a LinkedIn presence and has 5-10 employees = Potential lead  

  3. The customer has a LinkedIn presence and has 1-4 employees = Academy


 2. Does the first message have a yearly revenue indication? = Consultancy


3. Customer’s website: 

  • Does the customer have a website? 

  • Does it sell more than 5 products?

  • Does it include a webshop?

  • Does the company have registered retail stores? 

  • Does the company exist for 3+ years? 


If the answer is yes to:

  • All 5 questions =  Consultancy

  • 3-4 questions = Potential lead

  • Less than 3 = Academy

title: Dual business models
subcategory: TBR - Channel Added Value marketplace strategy framework
body:
To prevent things from getting confusing, we will use the definitions from the previous page. In the following section, we will differentiate between two business models:

  • The marketplace commission-based business model
  • The wholesale margin-based business model, also known as the 'Pure Player' 


Marketplace commission-based business model

Marketplaces operate on a commission-based business model. In this case, the marketplace is not the legal seller of the goods but charges a selling fee (commission) to the brand (often between 5% and 25%). The products are displayed and sold on the marketplace while the brand is still the owner and legal seller of these products. This also means that the brand has full control over the price, assortment and stock. For brands, this can be one of the main advantages of selling on marketplaces.


Wholesale margin-based business model

Pure players operate on a Wholesale margin-based business model. In this case, the channel purchases the goods and is the legal owner and seller of these goods. When the channel has purchased the products, they can use and sell them as they like. They can, for example, resell the products or change the selling price. In the EU and UK, brands are not allowed to dictate a selling price to retailers like pure players (this is called: vertical price-fixing). In the US, brands have more possibilities to arrange this with the use of so-called 'distribution contracts'. 


Dual business models

As shown on the visual at the top of this page, channels can offer both wholesale models and marketplace commission-based models. Well-known examples of channels that offer both options are Amazon and Zalando.

title: Amazon Launch Plan Instructions - Traffic
body:
Amazon Launch Plan Instructions - Traffic

As you may know, advertising is an important aspect of the success on marketplaces. Without a good advertising strategy, it is very hard to drive sales. Managing the client's expectations is very important and for this reason, we should always communicate how their advertising budget will be spent. For us, it goes without saying that there will be high spending in the early days of the launch, which will lower over time. For the client, this is not always clear.

Advertising Strategy

The advertising strategy is fully in line with the content strategy. The keywords in the content will also be the focus of the advertising. The template provides some slides to also show the structure of the advertising and the process of optimization. This can help to paint the picture for the client.

TACOS Development

As previously mentioned, we have to inform the client regarding the TACOS development. Meaning that we will spend a high percentage if the incoming revenue in the advertising of the products. Over time, this will balance out and be in line with targets discussed during sales (if any).

title: What causes error 8016?
subcategory: Amazon Error Solving SOP Overview - internal
body:
What causes error 8016?

This error occurs when you do not provide enough product data to create a variation (parent/child) relationship between products. To create a variation (parent/child) relationship, you must provide a Variation Theme value for both the parent and the child product. For each child product, you must also provide the variation data that corresponds to the Variation Theme that you indicated.


The most common causes for Error 8016 are:


  • Submitting a different Variation Theme for the parent SKU and the child SKUs within the same variation family.

  • The variation attributes associated to your variation theme are missing for the child SKUs.

    • For example, if you designate a Variation Theme value of Size Color for your parent product and child products, you must also provide a specific size and color for each child product.

Sample:

The product data provided was insufficient for creating a variation (parent/child) relationship for SKU: <SKU Number >

     

Ensure that the parent and child share the same variation theme and that there is a submission for variation theme attributes (Ex. size and color for size_colour variation theme).

How to solve and prevent error 8016?

You application should validate the feeds before uploading them to Amazon in order to ensure that:


  1. Both the parent and the child products of the same family have the same variation theme.

  2. Ensure that all the attributes associated to the variation theme are filled for every child product

  3. on the feed.

    1. The most common attributes associated to the variation theme are:

Note: It is enough that one of these errors happens only in one product of a family, and it will prevent the whole product family from being created correctly on the seller’s catalog on Amazon.

title: Amazon Content Phase 3 (Business as usual)
body:
Amazon Content Optimization (Phase 3)

Optimize for the long term

In Phase 3, which is the last phase of the initial content set-up, we are going to optimize the listing for the long term. Please keep in mind that:

  • The listing is never finished, there is always something to be done. But eventually it is good to accept the outcome of your efforts.

  • Periodically it is good to review your listing to see if you are still focussing on the correct keywords.


Now that the product listings should have a decent sales rank on a variety on keywords, we can start focussing on more high volume keywords. If possible, try to keep the longtail keywords somewhere in the product content (can also be back-end) to make sure the product keeps its ranking. This will also be supported via advertising.

    5. Uitnodiging afscheidsevent
    6. Toon dankbaarheid

Ontslag 

  1. Wees zorgvuldig en transparant, vertel kort wat de situatie is.
    1. We hebben het contract niet verlengt 
    2. [Naam] zal per direct Brandsom verlaten 
  2. Vertel zo min mogelijk over de inhoud


Voorbeelden 

Eigen keuze 

Hi Brandsom Team, 

Via deze weg informeer ik jullie dat [naam medewerker] Brandsom op [vertrekdatum] verlaat. [naam medewerker] vertrekt naar [beschrijf de reden van vertrek in maximaal 10 woorden als je hiervoor toestemming hebt; zo niet, schrijf deze zin dan niet op].
Nadat [naam medewerker] is vertrokken, richt je alle communicatie die je normaal gesproken naar [hem/haar] zou sturen, naar [naam contactpersoon]. Zodra we een vervanger hebben aangenomen voor de functie van [naam werknemer], zal ik jullie opnieuw informeren
/  Op dit moment hebben we geen plannen om een vervanger aan te stellen. 
Voordat [naam medewerker] vertrekt, nodigen we je uit om om [tijd] op [datum] op [locatie] aanwezig te zijn voor een afscheidsevenement. 
Wij bedanken [naam medewerker] voor [zijn/haar] inzet en betrokkenheid en wensen [hem/haar] alle succes met de voortzetting van [zijn/haar] loopbaan.

Via deze weg informeer ik jullie dat [naam medewerker] Brandsom op [vertrekdatum] verlaat. We hebben besloten om [zijn/haar] contract niet te verlengen.
