title: What causes error 4400?
subcategory: Amazon Error Solving SOP Overview - internal
body:
What causes error 4400?

This error occurs when the Merchant is only authorized to sell products using Amazon Fulfilment Network (AFN). If the merchant is trying to sell using Merchant Fulfilment Network (MFN), offer will be un-buyable.

Sample:

ResultMessageCode: 4400


ResultDescription: Your seller account is not approved to offer seller-fulfilled products in this category at this time. For more information, please see our Help page

How to solve and prevent error 4400?

To resolve this error, create a list of such products and then you can use the inventory file to Switch fulfillment to AFN and start the inbound process.


Note: You can also encounter this error if your seller does not have approval to sell in MFN and your solution is updating quantities for AFN products. This switches products from AFN to MFN landing up in this error. Hence, your solution should also not upload quantities to products that are tagged under AFN.

title: Introduction of integrators
subcategory: How to pick the right integrator?
body:
E-commerce integration is the coordination between a business' e-commerce websites and marketplaces and its back-end systems, such as an ERP or CRM system. Integrators play an important part in a total back-end organization, and can help marketplaces and E-commerce businesses become successful, automated and scalable.

Choosing the right integrator is important, as switching costs can be high. At Brandsom, we work with various integrators:

ChannelEngine

ChannelEngine's story began in 2013 when it became undeniable that marketplaces were going to be one of the most important elements of the global E-commerce market. We saw an opportunity to make these powerful platforms available to any business. Our 15+ years of E-commerce experience helped us build the most complete marketplace management suite and now, 8 years later, we facilitate the sale of over 3 million products from 1500 brands to over 100 marketplaces.
Source: https://www.channelengine.com/company/about-us/ 

Tradebyte

Tradebyte is an international SaaS (Software-as-a-Service) company with a rapidly growing customer base across Europe. By providing an uncomplicated, digital connection of supply and demand between the big players in the E-commerce industry, we enable seamless direct-to-consumer experiences for brands and manufacturers.
Source: https://www.tradebyte.com/en/about-us/ 

Channable

Channable’s integrated API connections enable you to push product listings to all major marketplaces. With an order connection, everything is in real-time. Meaning you don’t have to wait for scheduled updates to receive your orders. Your product data will be automatically updated and you’ll receive immediate feedback from the marketplace highlighting errors.
Source: https://www.channable.com/how-channable-works 

ChannelAdvisor

Since 2001, ChannelAdvisor has been the industry’s most trusted E-commerce solution, helping global brands and retailers solve their marketplaces, digital marketing, direct-to-consumer, first-party retail, drop-ship and fulfilment needs — all in a single, centralized platform.
Source: https://www.channeladvisor.com/ 

Vendiro

"With smart software and well-researched advice, we help you make more profit." Vendiro is the marketplace expert with more than 10 years of experience. With one integration, more than 50 marketplaces in Europe are at your disposal.
Source: https://vendiro.nl/nl 

ProductFlow

Link all marketplaces and manage your orders, offers and content for all marketplaces and webshops in one place. ProductFlow makes scaling up to multiple marketplaces a piece of cake. "Inventing a new marketplace is superfluous because we have already done that for you." All marketplaces data models are integrated into ProductFlow.
Source: https://www.productflow.com/

title: How to increase cost control?
body:
How to increase cost control for Amazon Advertising?

What we usually see is that most of the accounts have automatic campaigns running. There is to options here:

  • Automatic Campaign
  • Extended Product Targetting

Since Amazon's algorithm is always looking for new potential targets the budget will most likely be spend as much as possible. This makes it difficult to control the costs for the campaigns on Amazon.

Now, how to solve this?

The answer is fairly easy, for the Product targeting campaigns, make sure you are always focussing on the "exact" product match. 

For automatic campaigns, filter the keywords that are actually performing well and turn them into a manual campaign. Here it is important to keep in mind that "exact match" has the best cost control, depending on the situation you would also be able to use "phrase match". When trying to control the cost, it is never advised to use "Broad Match Type", since this will also be spending on less-relevant keywords.

title: Company strategy and descion makers
body:
First some theories...

A successful strategy with impact.

How do we make sure that the marketing / strategy plan doesn’t end up in a drawer of someone’s desk to never be looked at again? The success formula is to:

1) Identify the Critical Business Issues of the company.

2) The plan should add positive value or solve the CBI.

3) Let the client quantify the value of the plan.


Critical Business Issues. A critical business issue can be thought of as “A problem, a goal, or an opportunity that is critical to the overall success of the organization”. Usually CBI’s are part of the top or senior management strategy and all departments of the company add positive value or make sure that the CBI isn’t negatively impacted. Usually CBI are commercial objectives such as turnover, profit/loss, ReturnOnInvestment (ROI) or an organizational issue. But, for a nutrition company health and safety can be a top CBI.


“Charlie”.

Why do we start with knowing the CBI? Well, there’s always one person, or multiple people, who ultimately can say ‘Yes’ or ‘No’ to your plan! Usually this is the CEO or the board/management of the company. Let’s call this decision maker: Charlie. As explained in the paragraph above: Charlie’s main concerns are CBI’s of the company. So, although you create a marketing plan for a sales director and the goals of the sales director, it’s ultimately Charlie that gives your sales director a yes or no to your plan! And of course you want to have the full company on board to see the value of the plan…


The big 3

  • Determine the Client’s company goals (KPIs) and the CBI of the company. Usually the goals of your contact person and the management goals.

  • Develop a strategy on how to reach this goal.

  • Develop tactics and techniques to execute your strategy successfully. 


Value is only value if it’s relevant for the client and its company or brand! 

Create value by meeting client’s expectations or even better: excel them! Help your client to address the CBI. Let the client quantify the value, and do not fill this in for them! 2 advantages: 1. The client thinks about the value itself and therefore perceives the value even higher. 2. You’ll never have a discussion about a target when the client has set the goal.

title: Dynamic Seller pricing
body:
Products on marketplaces often have multiple sellers that compete for the sale. Sometimes this is visible to the public (bol.com and Amazon), while other marketplaces such as Zalando use a closed system in which a product can have multiple sellers. Customers, however, do not see the competing sellers on Zalando's platform.

 

Picture 1: This Under Armour t-shirt has 55 sellers on Amazon.


Picture 2: This Under Armour t-shirt on Zalando could have multiple sellers, but the platform only shows one.


Dynamic Seller re-pricing

Dynamic seller pricing is a method to optimise your listing price (the price of the product that you are offering) to compete with other sellers. This process is often automated by tools and can be done up to 100 times a day. 

Retailers (resellers of products) are often more focused on re-pricing. For brand owners, the price is often part of their branding strategy. Brand owners don't want to devalue their products by reducing prices. Reducing prices is often the outcome of dynamic re-pricing. 


Tools

There are 3 types of tools available to automate dynamic seller pricing:

  1. Stand-alone:
  2. Integrated with the integrator:
  3. Offered by the marketplace (for example Amazon).


1. Stand-alone:

Stand-alone tools work independently from other tools (like integrators). There are different stand-alone tools mainly focused on Amazon. An example is:

@Bonnie: Bron: https://www.repricerexpress.com/


2. Integrated with the integrator:

Do you want to know what an integrator is? Please see the academy course about integrators.

The most common place to automate pricing is to do this within an integrator. Integrators with advanced pricing options are:

  • Vendiro
  • ChannelEngine
  • ChannelAdvisor



3. Offered by the marketplace:

@Bonnie: graag deze informatie hier neerzetten (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ipbQTLMR9CI&ab_channel=AmazonSellerUniversity)

title: Job descriptions
body:
At Brandsom, we distinguish between overhead and non-overhead roles to ensure efficient and effective operations. Overhead roles include essential functions such as sales, finance, HR, and the management team, which provide critical support and strategic direction.

Non-overhead roles are organized into vertical teams focused on specific market segments like fashion, home and living, consumable products, and consumer electronics. Each vertical team is led by a consultant and includes a consultant trainee and marketplace specialists in integration, content, and advertisement. These teams are managed by vertical leads, ensuring specialized attention and expertise in each area to drive our mission forward.

For more information about our job descriptions, you can visit the job description Google drive folder or reach out to HR

title: Enriched Images - Before you Start
body:
Enriched Images - Before You Start

Creating enriched images is a time-consuming task, which makes it even more important that everything is in place before we start to create the images. Make sure the following information is in order before you hand it over to the specialist.

Full image gallery

Make sure the client sends over as many images as possible. In the Launch Plan, you should already find out what assets you will need, if these are not available you can ask your client to create them. Ideally, we have access to either a drive or a DAM (Digital Asset Manager) to make sure we have all the images. If the client sends over the images via WeTransfer, make sure to add them to our own drive in the client folder so everyone can have access if needed.

Product information

To make sure our specialists work as efficiently as possible, send over all product information you have available. Meaning:

  • Descriptions
  • Attributes
  • Any USPs discussed with the client
  • Ask your client for 3-4 USPs of the products
  • Brand book (Not always available)

The more complete your briefing is towards the specialist, the more efficient but also the better the images will turn out.

Last but not least!!

  1. Make sure the content is fully optimized before you request the images. This is because the images will reflect what is mentioned in the content. If you change this shortly after, the images must also be updated.
  2. Since the creation of enriched images is time-consuming, we are unable to create this for all products and all clients. Be selective, and focus on the top 10% of products that drive the most revenue.
  3. If the client has any previous Enriched Images and is able to share the Photoshop files with us, this can also be extremely helpful.

title: Sustainability at Zalando
body:
Zalando is one of the main marketplaces within fashion, has focused more and more on sustainability and has made available requirements for Brands that want to be part of their marketplace. 


Changes in our approach to Product Sustainability Q4 2022

To better serve customer demand and ensure compliance with EU legislation, we are introducing new data requirements and launching an updated customer experience in Q4 2022, designed to help our customers understand items that have sustainability attributes better and make more informed fashion choices. This will involve the removal of our ‘sustainability flag’ and the introduction of more specific information across the customer experience. The Product Sustainability at Zalando: Full Guide (attached below) has been updated with the new criteria updates that will come into effect as of Q4 2022. We have also outlined the updates to Sustainability Criteria for Products and the updated steps that you must follow to ensure that you fulfil our requirements for product sustainability in the article Changes in our approach to Product Sustainability Q4 2022. 



For Zalando as a company, and for the fashion industry as a whole, we are at the beginning of our sustainability journey. Adoption of industry initiatives and close collaboration with brand partners is key to affect lasting change.


Zalando is aware that the fashion industry needs to find new ways to produce, sell and consume fashion, today and for generations to come. As a platform, many of the commitments they set out in their sustainability strategy, do.MORE, affect their partners. Zalando’s sustainability requirements cover topics such as compliance with policies, setting carbon reduction targets or filling out an assessment. 

 

In order to sell on our platform, all partners are required to meet Zalando’s Sustainability Requirements. These include ensuring that all articles and content provided from the partner complies with current European standards and legislation and with Zalando’s Ethical Standards as outlined in the following: 


  • Zalando’s Code of Conduct

  • Zalando’s Product and Content Guidelines

  • Animal Welfare Policy

  • Sustainable Sourcing Policy 


All information is also available on Zalando’s corporate website here.


Brands, in addition, must meet the following minimum sustainability requirements and ethical guidelines:


  • Have your own Code of Conduct, 

  • Have Tier 1 supply chain transparency.

  • Have a person responsible for sustainability in your company. Partners must share the contact information of this person with Zalando here.


Complete an annual brand assessment (don’t worry, data will be treated confidentially). 

Potentially setting science-based targets, if required. Partners that surpass a certain threshold of CO2 emissions will be required to set science-based targets by the end of 2025. 


Please note that the above applies to brands only. We define brands here as companies who produce or manufacture their own products (this includes private labels), as opposed to retailers, distributors or service providers, who only sell third-party brands' products.


That being said, all partners are encouraged to offer sustainability assortments that match our criteria. 


We all need to take action in our supply chains and we believe that the above requirements can help you on your sustainability journey. We are launching an annual review process from 2022 onwards to identify partner challenges and how we can support them. Ultimately however, if a brand does not engage or demonstrate progress, an escalation process will be triggered. 


https://partnerportal.zalando.com/partners/s/article/Sustainability-Requirements-for-Brands-on-Zalando

title: AI Overview
body:
What is AI and LLM?


Let’s unravel the mystery of AI. Imagine if your computer suddenly became super smart and could do things that usually require human intelligence—like understanding language, spotting patterns, solving problems, and making decisions. That’s Artificial Intelligence (AI).


Now, meet the rock stars of AI: Large Language Models (LLMs). These are AI systems trained on tons of text data, enabling them to understand and generate human-like text. They can write essays, chat with you, translate languages, and even draft emails. Think of them as incredibly knowledgeable and articulate digital assistants.

How Can AI and LLMs Be Used in the Workplace?

Wondering how these AI marvels can help you at work? Here are some practical and exciting ways they can make your job easier and more productive:

  • Content Creation: Struggling to find the right words? LLMs can generate blog posts, marketing copy, and social media content, helping you save time and boost productivity.

  • Data Analysis: AI loves crunching numbers. It can analyze large datasets to identify trends, make predictions, and support your decision-making process, turning data into actionable insights.

  • Personal Assistance: Virtual assistants powered by AI can schedule meetings, manage emails, and organize tasks, allowing you to focus on more strategic work.

  • Language Translation: Breaking down language barriers has never been easier. AI can instantly translate documents and communications, making international business smoother.

How to Write Effective Prompts for AI

To get the best results from your AI assistant, it’s important to write clear and specific prompts. Here’s how to do it:

  • Be Specific: Clearly define what you want the AI to do. Instead of asking, “Tell me about AI,” try, “Explain how AI can be used in customer service.”

  • Provide Context: Give the AI as much information as possible. For example, “Write a friendly email to a client thanking them for their purchase and offering a discount on their next order.”

  • Use Examples: If possible, provide examples of what you’re looking for. This helps the AI understand your expectations.

  • Iterate and Refine: You might not get the perfect result on the first try. Adjust your prompt based on the AI’s responses and try again.

Additional Tips for Using AI in the Workplace

  • Stay Updated: AI technology is rapidly evolving. Keep up with the latest developments to take full advantage of new capabilities.

  • Security and Ethics: Be mindful of data privacy and ethical considerations when using AI. Ensure your use of AI aligns with our company policies and legal regulations.

  • Collaborate with AI: View AI as a collaborative tool rather than a replacement. It’s here to enhance your work, not take over.

title: Zalando - CXM template- DHL delays
body:
This Template can help with Zalando CXM that and can be customized and help when there are for example DHL delays and so on.

Zalando CXM template - DHL delays  

Good afternoon Zalando,


We are emailing you because we’ve just received the CXM report [Enter date]. The results are:

  • BE reimbursement on target: 84.85% (should be higher or eq to 95%);

  • DE reimbursement on target: 75.73% (should be higher or equivalent to 95%)


The reason is a bad score for [Enter Country and % indication] is a delay related to DHL couriers. 


Situation & Timeline

Since [DATE] DHL is not processing the returns within the required timeframe which causes returns to be returned later than the ideal times, causing an automatic impact of Zalando’s CXM KPI’s. In this case on the reimbursement target %. 

As soon as we saw this, we have been researching what caused this issue and how to prevent it. 

Improvements

With this said, these delays are currently inevitable and out of our client’s control. 


[Insert possible solution and eventual improvements that could be requested] 


We will monitor the daily updated CXM in Z-direct to spot issues like this sooner and keep you informed about the eventual developments.


With regards to this issue, we would also like to ask you, have you seen or recently experienced this same issue among other Zalando partners? And if so, do you have any recommendations? 


Thank you in advance for your response and we look forward to hearing from you.

title: Conversion Rate Optimisation
body:
Conversion rate optimization (CRO) is the process of increasing the percentage of users or website visitors to take the desired action, such as buying a product or leaving contact details. CRO typically involves generating ideas for elements on your site or app that can be improved and then validating those hypotheses through testing.



Conversion rate optimization is important because it allows you to lower your customer acquisition costs by getting more value from the visitors and users you already have. By optimizing your conversion rate, you can increase revenue per visitor and acquire more customers, allowing your business to grow. In addition, CRO will help you to learn more about your customers and it will boost your Google rankings as user experience is a huge factor Google considers when it comes to ranking websites.


Key benefits of CRO

Conversion rate optimization brings unique benefits when it comes to SEO. Some of the benefits of CRO include:

  • Increased user engagement: Conversion rate optimization works to optimize your website and campaigns for better engagement and, ultimately, conversions. An increase in engagement metrics can provide valuable insights into how your campaigns are performing and what makes users want to interact with your content.
  • Valuable user insights: The process of CRO requires a better understanding of your audience. You will be better prepared to reach the right types of customers with the right messaging at the right time.
  • Scalability: CRO allows you to make the most of your existing audience/traffic to attract new customers. By improving the conversion rate, you will be able to scale your business without running out of potential customers.
  • Enhanced customer trust: CRO helps you build customer trust and leaves a positive impression on potential customers.

Establishing conversion metrics

CRO begins by first identifying what the conversion goals are. The success metrics of a website or mobile app will depend on the type of business you're in, and what your goals are.

If you sell products online, for example, a conversion may be purchases or the number of website visitors that add a product to their shopping cart. If you sell products or services to businesses, you might be measuring the number of leads your website collects. Once you have established the conversion metrics for your digital interactions with your audience, you can begin to try to improve your digital customer experiences through conversion rate optimization. Examples of common conversion goals are:

  • E-commerce: Product sales, add-to-carts, shopping cart completion rate, e-mail newsletter sign-ups
  • B2B: Leads generated, deals closed


Identifying areas to optimize

The next step, after identifying your conversion metrics, is to identify which part of your conversion funnel you want to optimize. Typically, you want to begin optimizing the portion of your conversion funnel that receives the greatest amount of traffic or generates the greatest number of conversions. By focusing on these pages you will be able to see the results of your changes faster and have a larger impact on your business. Other potential places to start include your highest-value pages or pages that are underperforming compared to the rest of your site.


How websites benefit from CRO

There are multiple areas of a website that have the potential to largely benefit from conversion rate optimization. Let's see what these areas are and how they can be optimized. 

Homepage

Homepages are prime candidates for CRO. In addition to making a first impression on visitors, the homepage is also an opportunity to retain those visitors and guide them further into your website. You can do this by emphasizing links to product information, offering a free signup button, or even incorporating a chatbot that solicits questions from visitors at any point during their browsing experience.


Pricing page

A website's pricing page can be the make-or-break point for many website visitors. CRO can help a pricing page convert visitors into customers by modifying the pricing intervals (e.g. price-per-year vs. price-per-month), describing the product features associated with each price, including a phone number for visitors to call for a price quote, or adding a simple pop-up form.


Blog

A blog is a great conversion opportunity for a website. In addition to publishing thoughtful and helpful content about your industry, a blog can use CRO to convert readers into leads. This process often includes adding calls-to-action (CTA) throughout an article or inviting readers to learn more about a topic by submitting their email addresses in exchange for an ebook or industry report.


Landing pages

A landing page is a web page that serves as the entry point for a website or a particular section of a website. Since landing pages are inherently designed for people to take an action, it makes sense that they have the highest average conversion rate of all signup forms. A landing page that's offering a free resource can be optimized with preview content from that resource to encourage visitors to download it.


CRO marketing strategies

In this chapter, we'll go over some applicable conversion rate optimization marketing strategies.

Add lead flows to your blog

A lead flow is a CRO element you can include on your website. Lead flows are high-converting pop-ups designed to attract attention and offer value. Depending on your offer, you can choose between a slide-in box, drop-down banner, or pop-up box.

Run tests on your landing pages

Landing pages are an important part of the modern marketer's toolkit and, as mentioned earlier, integral to conversion rate optimization. That's because a landing page is where a website visitor becomes a lead or an existing lead engages more deeply with your brand. To optimize a landing page, run A/B tests to identify your best design and content features. With this method, you can quickly and easily test different versions of your website copy, content offers, images, form questions, and web pages to determine what target audience and leads respond to best.

Optimize high-performing blog posts

As we mentioned before, publishing blog articles opens the door to a big opportunity for conversions. To get started with optimizing your blog content, identify your posts with the highest levels of web traffic but low conversion rates. Possible causes of this issue may be related to SEO, the content offer you're promoting, or your CTA.

Additionally, look at your blog posts with high conversion rates. You want to drive more qualified website traffic to those posts and you can do so by optimizing the content for the search engine results page (SERP) or updating it as needed to ensure it's fresh and relevant.

title: Client check
body:
Mail client

  • Consultant emails client to share EN/NL written content and asks for quality check. 

  • The client can determine how detailed they will check it. 

  • The client gives feedback or a GO

*Ask your client when it will be done - you can then plan translations


Feedback 

  • Consultant receives feedback, creates a new Asana task and informs CS of new feedback.

  • Consultant sits down with CS to process the feedback and ensure content is good to go for translation. 


GO

If both EN/NL content has been approved and feedback has been implemented: It’s time for translations.

If translations aren’t needed: Content can be pushed.



Mailing template for content delivery

Hi [name client],
Please find attached the content written by our specialists. To make sure that the content complies with the corporate identity of [brand], we ask you to briefly check the content on the following points:

  •  Is the writing style in line with the house style of [brand]?
  • Are the claims made in line with the legal guidelines? [NOTE, CLIENT-SPECIFIC]
  • Have the USPs been properly incorporated?


Once we have received an agreement on the content, we will schedule the following work:

  • Our team will translate the content 
  • We will incorporate the content into the feed and push it to the marketplace
  • We will schedule a front-end check to verify that the content has gone through properly to the marketplace


When the content has gone through properly, we will confirm this with you. If there are any questions or remarks, please let me know.

title: Amazon - KYC Process Instructions
body:
Amazon KYC Instructions

After a certain amount of revenue, you are obligated to submit certain documents for all relevant companies and Ultimate Beneficial Owners (UBO). This is due to EU law. 

Please keep in mind that the documents that are allowed by Amazon tend to change relatively often. Therefore it is important to always double-check if the document you are submitting is still allowed. Also, follow the instructions by the letter. These instructions are found in the Performance Notifications overview and are also communicated to the main email address. For example;

  • Amazon requires you to submit an organizational chart, showing the entire structure of the company.

  • This document should have a company stamp, and the date when it was signed and needs to be signed by the CEO (or any other legal person)

  • It should also show the exact shares every holding or UBO has.

If ANY of this information is missing, Amazon will reject the document. They will not tell you what exactly is missing or incorrect, but you will receive the same instructions for this document.

Last but not least, the KYC is a process that is usually done in steps. Amazon requests new information based on the previously submitted documents. Below you will find the documents that are ALWAYS required, per holding and UBO:

Company Level documents:

  • Organizational chart (see example below)

    • Must include the percentage of shares per holding / UBO

    • Must be printed on company letterhead or have a company stamp

    • Picture is not allowed, must be a PDF file

    • Must be dated and signed by the CEO or company representative

  • Extract of the Chamber of Commerce

  • Shareholder register (request via notary)

    • Must be a PDF file, picture is not allowed

  • Articles of association


UBO documents:

  • Copy of passport or ID

  • Utility bill (should match the address submitted in Amazon)

Simplified example of an organizational chart:

If the client has a more complex structure (for example with investment companies), please reach out to your manager. It is a fragile process which should always be handled with care

title: Amazon - Changing Business Details Checklist
body:
Amazon - Changing Business Details Checklist

When you change your business details in Amazon (can be an address, can be entity type), this will most likely trigger the KYC progress where you will have to resubmit some documents. Below, you will find a checklist that you can use to make sure all mandatory changes have been made. Not all changes might apply to your specific situation.

  • When changing your address you need to resubmit utility bills to verify the new address;

  • When the business name is changed, make sure the VAT data is updated as well;

  • Update bank account details to reflect the new business entity or address, ensuring uninterrupted payments from Amazon.

  • Update any business licenses and permits with the new address or business entity information. (EPR etc)

  • Make sure you have the documents available mentioned in the Amazon KYC Instructions academy

title: Middleware
subcategory: WIP - Marketplace and E-commerce IT set-up 101
body:
Middleware is software that enables one or more kinds of communication or connectivity between two or more applications or application components in a distributed network. By making it easier to connect applications that weren't designed to connect with one another, middleware streamlines application development and speeds time to market.

While all middleware performs communication functions, the type a company chooses to use will depend on what service is being used and what type of information needs to be communicated. This can include security authentication, transaction management, message queues, application servers, web servers, and directories.

title: Adopting an Inbound Sales Strategy
body:
There are many factors of inbound selling; from hiring salespeople, to executing an inbound sales strategy, to properly using technology. In this chapter, we are going to talk about the best practices of inbound selling for the individual salesperson. As a salesperson, one of the best things you can do is to improve the experience you provide to your buyers. To do this, you need an Inbound sales strategy.


An inbound sales strategy can be applied to every salesperson at every company. That's because an inbound sales strategy focuses on providing a personalized experience based on the buyer's context. Regardless of how big or small, and how complex or transactional your sale is, there will be four phases in your inbound sales strategy. Focusing on the experience you'll provide to your buyers during each phase, let's discuss these four phases one at a time.

The Identify phase

The first part of your inbound sales strategy. What you do here will set the trajectory for the rest of your strategy. If you're going to provide an excellent experience for the people you work with, you need to put serious effort in at the beginning of your strategy. The goal in the Identify/Connect phase is to identify and connect with leads within the large pool of available prospects.

  • Gather as much information as you can about each individual prospect so you can better understand their situation. This way, you'll be able to offer help and resources from the beginning. The key thing to keep in mind during this phase is that many buyers enter the awareness stage of the buyer's journey long before they engage with salespeople.


  • Identify the buyer's interests and priorities, and offer guidance. Show a genuine interest in helping your prospect accomplish their goals and overcome their challenges. Doing this increases the likelihood that the buyer will want to move forward with the solutions you suggest


  • Focus on providing an experience of helping your prospect make progress in a journey they've already begun. The hallmark of an inbound sales strategy is finding ways to attract people to you. How you do this depends a lot on the behaviour of the people you're trying to attract. 


  • A key part of inbound sales involves social media. Follow the thought leaders your buyers follow and share insightful comments and content. Not only do these activities help you identify active buyers, but they will also position you as a thought leader with your buyers. This will give you more authority, credibility, and trust than other salespeople. Over time, more and more buyers will reach out to you for guidance and advice, and to learn how you can help them.


  •  In your opening outreach, make an offer aligned with the awareness stage of the buyer's journey or whichever stage that particular buyer is in at that particular moment. As an inbound salesperson, you will offer content or consultations to help the buyer diagnose their situation. Then you'll be there to guide them through the rest of the buyer's journey.


The importance of the identify phase in inbound sales

As an inbound salesperson, you will approach the identify phase in a smarter, more modern way:

  1. You will start by defining your ideal customer profile and buyer personas, so you know what sort of people or organizations you should be targeting in the first place.
  2. You will then identify leads within that profile who have already started the research process. And every time you find a new lead, you will enrich the information you have with context and research.

If you use these inbound techniques, you’ll spend less time talking to people who are probably not going to buy from you and spend more time helping people who need and want your help. And ultimately, you'll be spending your time with prospects who have a greater potential of being good fits for your solution.

Use this checklist during the identify phase to define a lead is active or passive.


The Explore phase

The experience you want to provide here is expert consultation.

  • Instead of launching directly into a canned presentation, use your buyer's initial interest and your own credibility as thought leaders to probe deeper into the buyer's specific goals and challenges. You're the expert here, so you can assess whether you can help the buyer more efficiently and thoroughly than they can help themselves.


  • Through proper positioning of value and the right questioning process during the explore phase, you will guide your prospects to the right conclusions. And it's important to note that the right conclusion will sometimes be not to buy from your company. However, by using a consultative, inbound approach, you'll be able to identify unqualified leads quickly so you don't spend your time working with people who are never going to buy from you. In the end, that will give you more time to focus on the people who want your help.

In the explore phase, you can use this sales slide deck to help with discovering relevant information.


The Advise phase

The advise phase is the final part of your inbound sales strategy – the grand finale of the experience you’ve been providing. The experience you want to provide here at the end of the sale is one of connecting the dots and identifying a clear path from where they are to where they want to be, and how your offering is uniquely positioned to take them there.

  • By uncovering the buyer’s context and tailoring the presentation accordingly, you’ll add tremendous value to the buyer's journey beyond the information available online. You’ll serve as a translator between the broad messaging found on your company’s website and the unique situation of the buyer.


  • It’s important to remember that your buyers don’t need you to provide them with general information at this point. They can get all of that from your website. Your buyers are drowning in a flood of information overload. Focus on helping them connect your company's broad positioning to their specific challenges.

In the advise phase, you can use this sales slide deck to help with discovering relevant information.

title: Data Protection
body:
At Brandsom, we are committed to protecting the personal data of our employees, clients, and partners in accordance with Dutch law, including the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). This chapter outlines our data protection policies and procedures to ensure that personal data is handled responsibly and securely.

1. Introduction to Data Protection

Data protection is a fundamental right under Dutch law and the GDPR. It ensures that personal data is processed lawfully, transparently, and for a specific purpose. We take the protection of personal data seriously and implement strict measures to safeguard it.

2. Personal Data We Collect

We collect and process personal data necessary for employment and business operations. This may include:

  • Employee Data: Name, address, contact details, date of birth, bank details, social security number, employment history, performance records, and other HR-related information.
  • Client Data: Name, contact information, contractual details, and any other information necessary for business relations.
  • Partner Data: Information required for collaboration and business partnerships.

3. Purpose of Data Processing

Personal data is processed for the following purposes:

  • Employment Management: Recruitment, payroll, performance evaluation, benefits administration, and compliance with legal obligations.
  • Business Operations: Client and partner relationship management, service delivery, and communication.
  • Legal Compliance: Adhering to Dutch laws and regulations, including tax, employment, and data protection laws.

4. Data Protection Principles

We adhere to the following data protection principles:

  • Lawfulness, Fairness, and Transparency: Data is processed lawfully, fairly, and in a transparent manner.
  • Purpose Limitation: Data is collected for specified, explicit, and legitimate purposes and not further processed in a manner incompatible with those purposes.
  • Data Minimization: Data collected is adequate, relevant, and limited to what is necessary in relation to the purposes for which it is processed.
  • Accuracy: Data is accurate and kept up to date. Inaccurate data is corrected or deleted without delay.
  • Storage Limitation: Data is kept in a form that permits identification of individuals for no longer than necessary.
  • Integrity and Confidentiality: Data is processed securely to protect against unauthorized or unlawful processing, accidental loss, destruction, or damage.

5. Data Subject Rights

Employees, clients, and partners have the following rights regarding their personal data:

  • Right to Access: Request access to personal data held about them.
  • Right to Rectification: Request correction of inaccurate or incomplete data.
  • Right to Erasure: Request deletion of data when it is no longer necessary or if they withdraw consent.
  • Right to Restriction: Request restriction of data processing under certain conditions.
  • Right to Data Portability: Request transfer of their data to another organization.
  • Right to Object: Object to data processing based on legitimate interests or direct marketing.

By adhering to these policies and procedures, Brandsom ensures the responsible and secure handling of personal data, in compliance with Dutch law and GDPR.

title: Amazon Performance Metrics
body:
In this lesson, we'll discuss how to track your seller performance on Amazon Seller Central. When you sell on Amazon, addressing feedback and monitoring performance is essential to your success. It is impossible to be successful on the platform if you don't know your metrics and are not aware of how your products or ads are doing. 


Keeping track of your Amazon Performance Metrics ensures the profitability of your Amazon account. Doing this on a daily basis will result in more sales, and ultimately more profit. If your sales or ad campaign is not successful, there is a chance that, based on your performance metrics, Amazon will suspend your account. The platform wants you to actually sell products, rather than use it as a means to generate more reach for your brand or product.


Note: To track your seller metrics, you need to log into your Amazon Seller Central account.

Amazon Advertising and metrics explained

When creating a campaign by using Amazon Advertising, you need to make sure it is profitable. Reading and correctly interpreting the data your campaigns are generating on a daily basis is crucial. The success of your campaign can be determined in a series of Amazon PPC (Pay-per-click) metrics. These include the following:


Advertising Cost of Sale

For most advertisers, the advertising cost of sale (ACoS) is the most important variable of all Amazon metrics. It is measured as a percentage showing you how much a sale costs you in advertising money. The lower the ACoS, the better an ad is performing. Theoretically, an ACoS of 0 means that you spend nothing on advertising and still make sales. Its importance lies in the ability to show at a glance whether an ad is profitable or not.

Formula: ACoS = € spent on advertising / € made in sales



Impressions

Impressions indicate how many times your ad has been displayed on the platform. If this metric is too low, your bid may be too low and you're getting outbid by competitors. It could also mean that you are using the wrong keywords for your target audience. Maybe the keywords you're using are too specific or the selection is too small. It could also indicate that the keywords are not matching the search queries of your audience. A solution could be to add keywords that are more widely used.


Clicks

Clicks show how many times people have clicked on your advertisement. When it comes to clicks and impressions, it is important to be aware of two things:

  1. A high click rate with low sales indicates a problem on the product page. Your ad gets people to the product page, but something is causing them to click away from this page without buying anything. Most likely you are targeting your ad to the wrong people, or you need to improve your product page.

  2. A low click rate with many impressions indicates a problem with your targeting: many people view the ad but have no further interest in your product. This is due to a poor selection of keywords: the words you are using do not match the intent of the viewer of your ad. In this case, re-evaluate your keywords.


Click-through rate

The Click-through rate (CTR) is the ratio of clicks on ads to the number of impressions. Obviously, you want a high CTR, because that means viewers find your ad captivating enough to view your product page. A lower CTR means that your ad is not very appealing. In this case, you could improve your ad text, product image, keywords or the position of your ad. There is also the possibility to compare your ad with those of competitors to find out how you could optimize it.

Formula: CTR = Clicks / Impressions


Total spend

Total spend is the total amount of advertising money you have spent on your campaigns. To improve your profits, you should spend as little as possible on this variable. But if the spend is too low, there is a chance that Amazon will not display your ad. This can be just as much of a problem as an unprofitable campaign, so you need to examine your targeting and keywords for ways to increase your campaigns’ impressions.



Total revenue

The total revenue consists of the sales you generate through organic (sales that are not generated as a result of someone clicking on your ad) and paid sales (through ads). A healthy ratio between the two is 6:4. This means that 6 of the total advertising sales made were organic while 4 were paid for.



Cost per click

Cost per click (CPC) is about the amount you have to spend every time someone clicks on your ad. A product that you sell at a profit of €1 cannot afford a high CPC, but a product selling for €100 can. Always keep in mind how much you expect to make from a sale when optimising your ads. 

To lower the CPC, choose the right keywords, optimize the product listings and keep monitoring the campaign's performance to see if what you're doing is working.


Conversion rate

The conversion rate (CVR) is the percentage of website visitors that have clicked on an ad and then continue to buy the product. This percentage ranges from 4 to 50%. Some market studies show that the average CVR on Amazon PPC is 9.6%, while for e-commerce it is 1.33%.

Amazon calls this metric the 'Order Session Percentage'. It can be calculated by dividing the number of orders by the number of times people visited your listing including return visitors ('Sessions') to get a percentage.

Formula: Order Session Percentage = Number of orders / Number of visits

If people click on your ad but don't purchase your product, this probably means that you need to take another look at your product page and identify areas to optimize.
Amazon looks at your conversion rate when determining your ad’s ranking. This plays a part in the decision of how often your ad will be displayed.



Keeping track of your Amazon Seller Metrics

The Account Health page on Amazon provides an overview of your selling account's adherence to the performance targets and policies required to sell on the platform. To ensure that Amazon is delivering a great experience for their customers, it might take action on these metrics if they do not comply with the targets. Customer satisfaction is one of the most important performance measures Amazon uses to determine how well sellers are doing on their platform.


Amazon regularly reviews the performance of all sellers and notifies them when they are off-target. The Account Health page provides you with visibility into your selling account’s adherence to the targets for various product policies as well as the below performance metrics:

Order Defect Rate

The Order Defect Rate (ODR) is a key measure of your ability to provide a good customer experience. It includes all orders with one or more defects represented as a percentage of total orders during a given 60-day time period. 

An order is considered defective by Amazon if the customer:

  • Makes an A-to-Z Guarantee claim
  • Makes a chargeback
  • Leaves negative feedback (1- or 2-stars)

Amazon's policy is that sellers maintain an ODR under 1% in order to sell on Amazon. An ODR above 1% may result in account deactivation.


Reviews

Positive reviews from buyers make your account grow and increase the position of your products in the feed.


Cancellation Rate

The Cancellation Rate (CR) is all seller-cancelled orders represented as a percentage of total orders during a given 7-day time period. CR only applies to seller-fulfilled orders.

This metric includes all order and item cancellations initiated by the seller, with the exception of those that are requested by the customer using the order-cancellation options in their Amazon account. Pending orders or items that are cancelled by the customer directly on Amazon are not included.

Amazon's policy is that sellers maintain a CR under 2.5% in order to sell on Amazon. A CR above 2.5% may result in account deactivation.


Late Shipping Rate

The late shipment rate (LSR) represents orders ship confirmed after the expected ship date as a percentage of total orders, over both a 10-day or a 30-day period. LSR only applies to seller fulfilled orders.

Amazon's policy is that sellers should maintain an LSR under 4% in order to sell on the platform. An LSR above 4% may result in account deactivation.


Valid Tracking Rate

Valid tracking rate (VTR) measures how often you use valid tracking information for your packages. VTR measures the percentage of merchant fulfilled packages that have a valid tracking number. Amazon customers depend on tracking numbers to find out where their orders are and when they can expect to receive them. VTR is a performance metric that reflects those expectations.

Sellers must maintain a VTR greater than or equal to 95% at a product category level. VTR below 95% in a product category may result in:

  • Restrictions on your ability to sell seller-fulfilled items within that category or categories, without affecting your seller-fulfilled offers in product categories that meet the VTR requirements, nor will these restrictions affect your FBA offers.
  • Negative impacts to your eligibility to participate in premium shipping and guaranteed delivery.

title: Introduction
body:
One of the most important parts of working at Brandsom is fulfilling our client's needs. This is simply because they are the reason we get to work. Therefore, you want to build a strong working relationship with the client. To achieve this,  good communication is necessary to figure out their goals and needs and to let them know what we can do to help. After all, you want to keep the client satisfied. How do we do this? And what exactly is good communication and how do we keep it businesslike?

One way of communicating is by e-mail. Another frequently used option is calling the client by phone. It is very important to write and talk in a formal way at the early stages of the business relationship.


When to call, e-mail or plan a Google Meet?

We believe that a periodic meeting structure is the core of good communication with the customer. Besides this, we use the Voys (internet phone) and e-mail.

  • Try to discuss all topics in the (bi)weekly, monthly and quarterly meetings. If you receive e-mails in the meantime discuss the also during these meetings.
  • Second way of communication method is e-mail because it's clearer, faster and more reliable for the customer.
  • Third way of communicating is calling the customer using Voys. Sometimes it's quicker to just call the customer, if you call the customer please use Voys.
  • The customer can call the generic phone number (please check the site for the latest number) and re-routed if needed.


Tone of voice 

When you communicate with a client, it is important to use a formal tone of voice. This indicates the way and the tone in which you write or speak to a client. Your tone of voice should be professional and friendly. This way of communicating will be explained in detail in this module.

title: The process
body:
In order to streamline the promotions process and make the most out of the promotional moments it is important that consultants follow the step by step process below for all upcoming promotional moments.


(Step 0:)

If you receive any email from a marketplace with regards to an upcoming promotional moment or template that needs to be filled in in order to participate, this should be shared with the vertical leads as soon as possible. Marketplaces are not always clear about their promotional moments and emails aren't always send to all partners, so we need everybody to help us get the best overview of whats coming as possible. 


Step 1:

If we have a promotional moment coming up you will receive an email from the senior advertising specialist. Included in that email is a link to a checklist document which will be your guide during this promotion. The checklist includes the promotion timeline as well as the different tasks and responsibilities you have during this event. 

This checklist can also be found in the promotional template. 


Step 2:

The upcoming promotion should be discussed with all brandsom customers. Don't assume that a customer might not want to participate, or if they are not allowed to participate, they don't want to use regular discounts in order to make the most out of the extra marketplace traffic. During this step it would be wise to also discuss extra budget available to run ads on the discounted products. 

This presentation can be used in client meetings in order to convince them to participate by showing the possible uplift from promotions. 


Step 3:

Once a promotion is discussed the consultant is responsible for updating the checklist document receive via email. This includes filling in if participation is discussed, how customers are participating or if they chose to abstain. If customers are participating it is also important to share the product list with the advertising team, so they can create extra ads to push these products. 


Step 4: 

After the promotion has ended it is the responsibility of the consultant to analyse and report on how well the promotion has run based on the product KPI's (sales, conversion, ctr etc.). Depending on the marketplace this can be done in multiple ways. For bol.com you can check the promotion data, or track products via marktmentor. For amazon.com you can check the deals or coupons/vouchers page to see how well the promotion performed. After the promotion you should discuss the results with the customer.

title: Sick leave
body:
This chapter provides detailed guidance on how employees should report sickness and recovery, including the necessary steps to follow during this process. If you have any questions about the absence protocol, please consult your supervisor.

Calling in Sick

Employee:

  • You must personally notify your supervisor (in case of absence of supervisor - notify HR) of your sickness at least half an hour before your work begins, which is by 8:30 am. If you are unable to report sickness yourself, someone else can do it for you. If you fall ill during working hours, report to your immediate supervisor/HR in person.
  • When calling in sick, provide the following information:
    • The expected duration of your absence
    • Your contact information
    • Any appointments you have and any important work-related matters
  • Your supervisor/HR will agree with you on how and how often you will maintain contact, and decide who will initiate contact.
  • After reporting sick, you must remain reachable by phone during working hours.
  • You are not required to provide medical information to your supervisor at any time during your illness.

HR:

  • Register the sickness report in Nmbrs with the following information:
    • The telephone number of the sick employee
    • The expected duration of the absence
    • The sick employee’s appointments and pending work
  • Agree on how and how often you will keep in touch with the sick employee and who will take the initiative.
  • Do not inquire about any medical information from the employee.
  • Always inform the person responsible for planning about the sick employee.
  • If the absence lasts longer than three days, send a card to the employee.

Reporting sickness in Nmbrs

1. Visit https://bunnig.nmbrs.nl/ and log in to your Brandsom account.
Don't have an account? Send an email to team@brandsom.nl

2. Go to 'Medewerkers' (Employees) 

3. Select the employee's name

4. Click on the + icon at 'Verzuim' (Absence)


5. Next, fill in the pop-up window

Absence Regulations

Frequent Absence

If an employee is absent three or more times within a year or a shorter period, a meeting between the employee and HR will be arranged. The purpose of this meeting is to discuss ways to prevent frequent absences and to clarify the roles and responsibilities of both the employee and Brandsom in managing attendance.

Continued Payment

At Brandsom, there are no waiting days for sickness. This means employees will be paid from the first day of their illness. Employees with min/max contracts will receive 70% of their scheduled hours. If an employee is not scheduled, they will be paid 70% of their guaranteed hours.

title: Risk table
body:
Marketplace Model Selection Guide

Objective: Use this guide to select the appropriate model for working with a client based on their revenue stability, market position, and other strategic factors.

Step 1: Assess Revenue Stability

  • Question: Does the client have a history of stable and consistent revenue?
    • Yes → Consider a GMV-Contract model.
    • No → Proceed to Step 2.

Step 2: Check for Resellers

  • Question: Does the client operate with resellers?
    • Yes → Choose the Fixed/Hourly model.
    • No → Proceed to Step 3.

Step 3: Evaluate Market Leadership

  • Question: Is the client a strong market leader with minimal competition?
    • Yes → Opt for a GMV/Fixed model.
    • No → Proceed to Step 4.

Step 4: Investment Willingness

  • Question: Is the client willing to invest in pricing, promotions, and other strategies?
    • Yes → Opt for a GMV/Fixed model.
    • No → Choose the Fixed/Hourly model.

Step 5: Verify Brand Readiness

This is a separate step that should be conducted after the above decisions.

  • Question: Is the brand ready to proceed?
    • Yes → Proceed with the model selected in the previous steps.
    • No → Recommend No contract or an hourly-based model only.

Notes/Definitions:

  • Revenue Stability: Evaluates the client's revenue consistency, ranging from stable (low risk) to highly volatile (high risk).

  • Market Position: Assesses the client's competitive standing in the market, from strong leadership (low risk) to weak positioning with intense competition (high risk).

  • Brand Readiness: Determines if the client is prepared to launch on the marketplace, including account setup and logistics.

  • Willingness to Invest: Examines the client’s commitment to investing in marketplace activities like pricing and promotions, impacting potential for growth and competitiveness.

  • Presence of Resellers: Identifies if resellers of the client’s brand are present on the marketplace, with the presence indicating a higher risk level due to potential brand and price competition.

  • GMV-Contract Model: Ideal for clients with stable revenue and no resellers, offering a performance-based structure.
  • Fixed/Hourly Model: Suitable for clients working with resellers, with less market dominance, or with limited willingness to invest in market strategies.
  • GMV/Fixed Model: Recommended for market leaders or those willing to invest in aggressive growth strategies.

title: Logging hours
body:
In this module, we'll discuss how you should log your hours, which process you need to use and how many hours you need to try and log. This academy described where and when to log your hours. 

Where do you log your hours?

Most hours are logged on the client's project or your team's Asana board. Below is explained where to log which hours. 

Customer

Hours logged on the customer's project are tasks performed for the customer and include:

  • Meetings with the customer
  • Answering emails from and to the customer
  • Checking client's work together with a colleague in a meet
  • Internal explanation of a task via meet or mail (customer-specific)
  • Creating tasks related to the client's project 
  • Planning the customer's project 

Tasks that you do not log:

  • Training about a general subject (e.g. Amazon account management). 
    Example:
    If you are learning about the general introduction regarding account management, you log this at training on your team Asana project. If you are learning about the customer's account management, you log this on the customers Asana project

Team

All hours that do not belong to customer tasks are logged on the project of your own team. This includes, among other things:

  • Team meets
  • Other internal tasks
  • Training

Within your team, you use the standard tasks. You do not create a separate task for each meeting. Is something missing? Then first consider whether there isn't already another place for it. 

  • Are you working for the academy? > Register hours at Academy 2.0
  • Are you working on the website? > Register hours at Brandsom.nl

 If it is not listed, please contact HR.

When do you log your hours?

You log your hours immediately after completing the task. This way we can guarantee more precision. Of course, you can also use the timer in Everhour, but don't forget to turn it off! 

How many hours do you really need to have logged

We understand that it is sometimes challenging to log all hours, especially when something ad hoc comes up. However, it is important that you log as many hours as possible. Therefore, we adhere to the following rule: 80% of your work must be logged. If you work 40 hours, you log 32 hours; if you work 32 hours, you log 25 hours, etc.

title: Remote Working Addendum
body:
Extra information - working abroad (Addendum)

In the working abroad addendum, the following will be discussed:

1. Time Zone Restrictions.

A) Employee agrees to work only in countries where the time difference with the Netherlands is a maximum of 2 hours. Employees will consider time zone differences and ensure they stay within the specified limit. The employee will work according to Dutch working hours, from 9 AM to 5 PM.

2. Professional Attire

Employees will maintain a professional appearance at all times during working hours, both in personal and virtual interactions. Employees shall not wear inappropriate clothing, such as the absence of a shirt or other inappropriate garments.

3. Coworking Spaces

For meetings and work-related appointments, should the Employee's remote workspace prove inadequate (e.g., lacking a stable internet connection), it is required that the Employee utilize designated coworking spaces in the foreign location. While the Employer offers facilities to accommodate such needs, in circumstances where the Employee's personal workspace does not meet professional standards, the responsibility to secure and finance a suitable coworking space falls solely on the Employee.

4. Background Music

An employee is not allowed to have background music during professional conversations, meetings, or other work-related activities. This could disrupt communication and be distracting to others.

5. Return at own Expense

Employee understands and agrees that if working abroad becomes infeasible for any reason, he/she is obligated to return to the Netherlands at their own expense and as soon as possible. Employer is not responsible for any costs or delays resulting from failure to comply with this obligation.

6. Liability Outside Working Hours

The employer is not liable for accidents, theft, or injuries occurring outside regular working hours.

7. Insurance and Responsibility

The employee is responsible for obtaining all necessary insurance required for the country in which he/she will be working. It's also mandatory to always work with a secure network, where the use of unsecured/public wifi networks is not allowed.  

8. Other Provisions

This Addendum supplements the existing employment agreement between Employer and Employee. All other provisions of the employment agreement remain in full force unless expressly stated otherwise in this Addendum.

9. Applicable Law and Dispute Resolution

This Addendum is governed by Dutch law. In the event of disputes arising from or related to this Addendum, Employer and Employee will attempt to resolve them amicably. If such a resolution is not feasible, disputes will be submitted to the competent court in the Netherlands. Employee acknowledges having read, understood, and agreed to this Addendum.

title: TikTok Advertising
body:
What is TikTok Advertising?

TikTok Advertising is a suite of tools and services provided by TikTok that allows businesses, brands, and organizations to promote their products or services through paid advertising on the platform. TikTok’s advertising options are designed to leverage the platform’s strengths—short-form video content, creative engagement, and a powerful recommendation algorithm—to reach a highly engaged and often younger audience. TikTok Ads can appear in various formats across the platform, targeting users based on their behavior, interests, and demographics.

Why TikTok Advertising?

TikTok’s explosive growth and global user base, particularly among Gen Z and Millennials, make it an attractive platform for advertisers. Unlike more traditional social media platforms, TikTok emphasizes creativity and authenticity, meaning that ads on TikTok often blend seamlessly with user-generated content, making them more engaging and less intrusive.

TikTok Ads are effective for both brand awareness campaigns and direct response marketing. The platform’s algorithm, known for its precision in content recommendation, also plays a critical role in ad targeting, helping brands reach users who are likely to be interested in their products or services.

TikTok Advertising Formats

TikTok offers a variety of advertising formats designed to fit different marketing objectives and budgets:

  1. In-Feed Ads:

    • Description: In-feed ads are short video ads that appear in the user’s "For You" feed as they scroll through content. These ads look similar to organic TikToks, which helps them blend into the user experience.
    • Benefits: These ads support calls to action (CTAs) like app downloads, website visits, or e-commerce conversions. Users can like, share, comment, or engage with the ad just like any other TikTok content.
    • Ad Placement: Typically appear between organic content as users scroll.

  2. TopView Ads:

    • Description: TopView ads are full-screen video ads that appear when a user first opens the TikTok app. They take over the screen for a few seconds, offering high visibility.
    • Benefits: This format guarantees exposure and is ideal for brand awareness. It provides up to 60 seconds of full-screen video, with sound enabled.
    • Ad Placement: First video that appears when the app is opened.

  3. Branded Hashtag Challenges:

    • Description: A Branded Hashtag Challenge encourages users to create content around a specific hashtag promoted by the brand. These challenges often go viral, as users are invited to participate and create their own spin on the challenge.
    • Benefits: These challenges drive massive engagement and user-generated content (UGC). They are effective for building brand awareness and community engagement.
    • Ad Placement: Promoted in the "Discover" section, and users engage by creating content under the hashtag.

  4. Branded Effects:

    • Description: Branded Effects allow brands to create custom stickers, filters, and augmented reality (AR) experiences that users can add to their TikTok videos.
    • Benefits: These interactive elements enhance engagement by encouraging users to incorporate branded elements into their content. This is a powerful tool for building brand affinity and viral content.
    • Ad Placement: Available in the TikTok effects section for users to add to their videos.

  5. Branded Takeover:

    • Description: A Branded Takeover ad is a full-screen static or video ad that appears immediately when the user opens the TikTok app. These ads are exclusive to one advertiser per day.
    • Benefits: This ad format ensures high visibility and is ideal for brands looking for maximum reach and impact within a short time frame.
    • Ad Placement: Full-screen ad upon opening the app, followed by the option to click through to a landing page or hashtag challenge.

  6. Spark Ads:

    • Description: Spark Ads allow brands to boost organic posts created either by the brand itself or by users, turning them into ads. This format leverages existing engagement and social proof, making the ad appear more authentic.
    • Benefits: These ads allow brands to capitalize on content that is already performing well organically. Spark Ads maintain the original format of the post, including user comments and interactions.
    • Ad Placement: These appear in the user's For You feed as promoted content but retain their organic look and feel.

Targeting Options in TikTok Advertising

TikTok offers robust targeting capabilities to help advertisers reach the right audience. These include:

  1. Demographic Targeting:

    • Age, gender, location, language, etc.

  2. Interest Targeting:

    • Categories such as fashion, beauty, travel, sports, and more, based on user behavior and interests.

  3. Behavioral Targeting:

    • Based on recent activity, such as the types of videos users have interacted with, liked, shared, or commented on.

  4. Custom Audiences:

    • Advertisers can upload their own data, such as customer lists or website visitors, to create custom audience segments.

  5. Lookalike Audiences:

    • TikTok’s algorithm can find users similar to your existing customers or audience, expanding your reach to potential new customers.

  6. Automated Targeting:

    • TikTok offers automated targeting where the platform’s AI optimizes who sees the ad, focusing on the users most likely to engage or convert.

TikTok Ads Manager

TikTok Ads Manager is the self-serve platform that allows advertisers to create, manage, and analyze their campaigns. It provides tools for:

  • Campaign Creation: Step-by-step guidance on creating campaigns, setting goals (e.g., conversions, app installs, brand awareness), and choosing ad formats.
  • Budget and Bidding: Options for setting daily and total campaign budgets, as well as choosing bidding strategies (e.g., cost-per-click (CPC), cost-per-mille (CPM), cost-per-acquisition (CPA)).
  • Ad Creation: Tools to design ads using TikTok’s video creation kit, which includes templates, music, and special effects.
  • Performance Tracking: Detailed analytics and reporting on campaign performance, including metrics like impressions, clicks, engagement rates, and conversions.

TikTok Creator Marketplace

The TikTok Creator Marketplace is a platform that connects brands with TikTok creators for sponsored content and influencer marketing campaigns. This tool allows brands to find creators based on their audience demographics, engagement rates, and niche, making it easier to collaborate with influencers who align with their brand values and objectives.

Advantages of TikTok Advertising

  1. High Engagement: TikTok users are known for their high levels of engagement. The platform encourages creativity and interaction, which means ads that align with this style are more likely to resonate with users.

  2. Creative Flexibility: TikTok ads allow brands to experiment with different formats, storytelling methods, and interactive elements. Brands can create content that feels less like traditional advertising and more like entertainment.

  3. Viral Potential: The viral nature of TikTok content offers a unique opportunity for ads to reach a broader audience through shares, trends, and challenges. A well-executed ad can generate organic engagement far beyond its initial reach.

  4. Access to Younger Audiences: TikTok is especially popular among Gen Z and younger Millennials, demographics that can be harder to reach on other platforms. This makes it a valuable channel for brands targeting younger consumers.

  5. Data-Driven Optimization: TikTok’s analytics tools allow advertisers to track performance in real-time and adjust campaigns based on what’s working, ensuring that marketing efforts are optimized for better results.

  6. Global Reach: TikTok’s massive global audience means brands can reach users across different regions, expanding their market reach with a single campaign.

Challenges of TikTok Advertising

  1. Creative Demands: TikTok’s emphasis on creativity and authenticity means that ads need to be highly engaging to stand out. Brands need to invest in creative content that resonates with the platform’s audience, which may require a different approach than traditional advertising.

  2. Short Attention Span: TikTok users consume content quickly, meaning ads need to capture attention within the first few seconds. This fast-paced environment can be challenging for brands that are used to longer-form advertising.

  3. Ad Fatigue: As more brands flock to TikTok, users may become desensitized to ads, making it harder for brands to break through the noise. Continuous innovation in ad formats and messaging will be necessary to stay relevant.

  4. Ad Measurement: While TikTok provides analytics and performance tracking, measuring the long-term impact of TikTok ads (e.g., brand lift, customer loyalty) can be complex, especially for brands accustomed to more established advertising platforms with more mature metrics.

  5. Competition: As TikTok’s advertising platform grows, competition for ad space increases, potentially driving up costs, especially for premium ad formats like TopView or Branded Takeovers.

Future of TikTok Advertising

TikTok is continuously evolving its advertising offerings, expanding into new markets, and refining its tools to make the platform more accessible and effective for advertisers. As social commerce grows, TikTok is expected to integrate more seamless shopping features, further merging content and commerce.

Looking ahead, TikTok’s focus on community engagement, combined with advances in AI and machine learning, will likely lead to even more personalized and interactive ad experiences. Brands that stay ahead of the curve by embracing TikTok’s creative possibilities and understanding the platform’s unique culture will be well-positioned to succeed.

In summary, TikTok Advertising is a powerful tool for reaching a young, highly engaged audience through creative and interactive ad formats. It offers a mix of high-impact brand awareness campaigns and performance-driven marketing opportunities, all within the context of TikTok’s dynamic content environment.
