title: Ads
body:
Advertisements are visible at various places on the marketplaces. All these advertisements work with a bidding system. Sellers can indicate how much they want to spend on an advertisement and they only pay when someone clicks on the advertisement to visit the product page. This module gives a short explanation of the different advertisements within a marketplace.


Sponsored Products
With Sponsored Products, you can promote products to customers while they browse online and search for certain items to buy. Sponsored Product ads target keywords or products and appear between search results and on a product page, on both desktop and mobile (app and web).
As a seller, you can bid on keywords or on products. If you choose to bid by keyword, as a seller you specify how much you want to bid on a particular keyword. For example, You as a seller would like to sell bicycle bells. It can be useful to make a good bid on the keyword 'bicycle bell or bicycle bell accessories' so that customers searching for bicycle bells will see your ad. 


Sponsored Brands

Sponsored Brands drive awareness and discovery of a brand and product portfolio with customizable ads that appear in shopping results. They help build brand awareness by increasing visibility and deciding how sellers want to present their brand to customers through an immersive environment with custom headlines. In addition, they connect shoppers with products as they're taken to a store or custom landing page when clicking on a brand's logo. 

More established brands might use Sponsored Brands to stay top of mind with shoppers and increase brand loyalty.


Automatic or Manual 

When creating adverts, you usually have the choice of going for an automatic campaign or a manual campaign. 

When selecting an automatic targeting campaign, the marketplace will automatically find keywords for an advertisement. It targets all relevant keywords based on a product’s category, keywords from the product description, and keywords in related products. This option will help sellers find long-tail keywords that they otherwise would never find, and can then figure out what search terms people actually use. Search terms are the exact words customers fill in, in the search bar. The keywords discovered in the automatic campaign can then be used for a manual campaign.


A manual targeting campaign is much more fragile than an automatic targeting campaign. A seller handpicks keywords corresponding with the products they sell. Your ad will only come up if the customer is using a search term that matches the keywords the seller put in, depending on the match type.

To make this campaign a success, every keyword needs to be carefully selected. This will make your campaign more precise. Only showing up for the specific keywords that are relevant to the product that is being targeted. Yet, this is time-consuming and you are at risk of paying for keywords that are not relevant to your product and possibly lead to poor conversion. For this reason, it is always wise to run an automatic campaign next to your manual campaign, so you can see what keywords are automatically being found and used, and which ones work. It is labour intensive because every keyword is added manually. 


Naast de keuze om te gaan voor een automatische of handmatige selectie van keywords, bieden sommige marketplaces ook nog de optie om voor een automatische selectie van producten te gaan of een automatische biedstrategie. 

If you choose "Manual Bidding", you set an amount per bid in advance. If you choose "Automatic Bidding", you do not choose an amount, but your budget is calculated based on ACOS. 
Each advertising platform is always looking for the highest possible bid to win an ad auction.

title: Consultancy 3: Weekly team planning (Leads/consultants)
body:
Each week, ideally on the same day/time, each vertical team will have their plan meeting. The planning meeting includes all team members (also both the consultant and team lead). The meeting can be run by the lead or consultant. The goal of the meeting is to:

- Create a clear task overview per client project

- To assign individuals to each task that doesn't have an assignee (every planned task MUST have an assignee)

- To create an hour estimate overview per employee (using the plan meeting Asana template)

- To discuss important tasks/projects for the upcoming week


*** A- Prior to this meeting the consultant/lead must have sat together to go over the tasks, assign specialists to the different tasks and fill in the estimates. - Below described under steps 2 and 3

[Recommendation - plan this meeting the day before your plan meet, structurally each week]

Team planning process:

Step 1 (Consultant): Create all tasks for next week (as shown in modules Consultancy 1 and Consultancy 2). Including: 

- Fields: Paid fields + other fields

- Estimation: Filled in the task (where you also log your hours)

- Comment section: Fill in any relevant information + documentation

- Using the standard task templates

- Place tasks in the correct 'section' (e.g. General or content)

Step 2 (Consultant & Lead): Assign specialists to all tasks that don't have an assignee (Done by Consultant/Lead: go through each client project to assign all tasks - Explained under A - here above)

Step 3 (Consultant & Lead): Create an hour estimation for EACH task that has someone assigned

Step 4 (Consultant): Create an Asana vertical plan overview (See module: Creating an Asana vertical plan overview)

Step 5 (The whole team): Use the planning meeting to go over this plan overview and highlight the importance of different tasks but also to show the project overview of each client. 

During the plan meeting, there are two ways to show the planning: 

- On project level (Sort: Project)

This filter will show you all projects of your vertical and who is planned for each task. This can be useful to show highover who is working on what and what the goals are for next week regarding a specifc client.

- On employee level: (Sort: Assignee)

This filter will give you a clear overview of all the tasks that are assigned to a specific person for the following week. If estimations are filled in correctly, this will show you the hours someone is planned and thus for how many hours they are still available to be planned. 

*If there are tasks where there are already hours logged, then you can use those estimates to give you an indication of the work that may need to be completed next week. 

E.g. Lea has been assigned 2 tasks with an estimation of 12 hours for this week - if the tasks aren't completed yet, Lea still has worked 9/12 hours on the tasks and thus needs to be scheduled in for an extra 3 hours for next week. This is a perfect example of tasks that you can discuss during the plan meet and adjust accordingly, for instance if Lea indicates that she needs more or less time for the task.

title: How To Structure Seasonal advertising?
body:
How to structure advertising for clients that have a strong season?

Let's first dive a little bit deeper what type of clients this would be relevant for. For example:

  • Living plants or plant bulbs
  • Shade Clotches 
  • Outdoor Camping Articles
  • And many, many more..

Typical for these type of products is that they have a very strong season, for example during spring and summer time. In this academy, we will dive deeper into the best practices on how to set-up ads for these type of clients.

Why is this important?

To explain the importance of this structure, we will divide the year into three phases:

  • Pre-Season - This is 1-2 months before the season starts
    • Here we want to generate traffic and sales, so we increase ad spend
    • Generating sales early in the season can give you a head start compared to the competition
    • ACOS is relatively high during this phase, this is completely normal
  • In-Season - The moment when traffic and conversion are at its highest
    • Traffic and sales are increasing in this phase
    • Since CVR is higher, advertising is more efficient. ACOS should be relatively low during this phase.
  • After-Season - The part where the season slows down and products go out of season.
    • Volume and CVR are dropping and sometimes best sellers are out of stock
    • During this phase, it is important to control advertising spend and keep it relatively low

Campaign Structure

Please be aware this academy is diving into the generic set-up, therefore Top Of Search Campaigns are not discussed. 

The campaign structure is structured as follows:

  • BR - Manual - Monstera - Keyword - Multi
    • Monstera - Broad Match
      • Keyword 1
      • Keyword 2
      • Keyword 3
    • Monstera - Phrase Match
      • Keyword 1
      • Keyword 2
      • Keyword 3
    • Monstera - Exact Match
      • Keyword 1
      • Keyword 2
      • Keyword 3

The keywords can be identical in each ad group. Always make sure to put high-performing keywords, identified via "Broad Match", to the Phrase and Exact ad group. 

How to manage seasonal campaigns?

To keep it structured, we are sticking to the same phases as discussed earlier in this academy. 

  • Pre-Season - The goal is to increase visibility and drive conversion
    • During this phase, the Phrase Match and Exact Match ad groups should be activated
    • Depending on performance, you can activate the Broad Phrase ad group
    • Play around with the bids based on performance, this varies per client
  • In-Season - Focus on conversion, this is where the money is earned
    • All ad groups are running during this phase
    • Bids increase during this phase due to more competitors entering the market
    • Manual campaigns can be supported by automatic campaigns since CVR is at its highest point during this phase
  • After-Season - The goal is to keep ACOS targets in check
    • First, lower the bids during this phase
    • Once performance drops even further, turn off the Broad Match ad group, once performance keep dropping you can also turn off the Phrase Match ad group
    • At the very end you can keep lowering bids, to make sure performance is in line with discussed client targets

title: Sending an e-mail to a lead
body:
If you are going to send an email to a lead then it is important to send the right content. The goal, of course, is to create a conversation. You have to trigger the reader in some way. Below you can read what to look out for when you send an e-mail; 

collecting permission:
You need to have consent from the person you're emailing to send them e-mails. The user took an action to grant consent to you and your business.  The lead informed you in this way that he wants information about your company. This will result in a conversation between you and the lead. 

Contact lists:
It is very important to know what the source of your contact list is. Have they permitted you? And are they expecting an e-mail from you? The reason why this is important is when you have permission the lead will trust you. 
Contact lists that you bought from the internet are not the most workable lead lists. The lead will get more of these ''spam'' emails from other companies. So you need to know what the source of your lead list is to make it successful. 


  • Focus on a segment- Change the language and content and focus it on the lead.
  • Use the right content for the lead and end with a call-to-action
  • Make sure the lists are up-to-date. This way you avoid sending emails to companies that are no longer interesting. 

After sending the email:
Track positive engagement (the lead opens, reads, and responds to the email) and
negative engagement (when there is no response).


Next, analyze how it could be that there was no response. Perhaps a wrong source or the lead had a different expectation.

title: Amazon Brand Store
body:
In this course, we will go over the benefits of having a Brand Store on Amazon. In short; A brand store is a shop-in-shop that can only be utilized by Brand Owners. Please find some examples below:

  • BOSKA
  • ToExplore
  • AirMax

What are the benefits of a Brand Store on Amazon?

Having a Brand Store on Amazon has a variety of benefits, such as:

  • Enhanced brand visibility and credibility
  • Direct customer engagement and storytelling
  • Improved product discovery and cross-selling opportunities
  • Analytics and insights on customer behavior

Amazon Brand Store Best Practices

Seasonal Brand Store

If the client has seasonal products, it is always a good idea to reflect this in your brand store. You can do themes based on actual seasons, such as; Summer, Winter, Spring, and Autumn or you can do them based on holidays, for example; Valentine's, Christmas etc.

Make sure the Brand Store is always updated, you can save on 'generic' brand store which you can always rotate back to if the season ends. You don't want to have Christmas visuals during summer.

Tell the brand's story

There are endless possibilities for how to design your Amazon Brand Store, one thing you should always consider is to tell the brand story. Do not just highlight products in the brand store, but add visuals that tell more about the brand, what it stands for and what the USPs are.

Use advertising to drive traffic to the brand store

You can use Sponsored Brands to drive traffic to the Brand Store. Make sure the ads you create are always relevant to the keywords you are advertising in. This means you need to create very specific ads with specific visuals so you can target as focused as possible.

Useful URLs

  • Official Amazon Brand Store Page
  • Step-by-step guide on how to create a brand store

title: First sales meeting
body:
Which data should be obtained in the first sales call?

The target of this meeting:

- Show expertise to the potential client (Knowledge about marketplaces/integrations)
- Brandsom's way of working explained
- Brandsom managed explained
- Introduction to integrators (more information about the integrators can be found here)
- Asking all questions related to the client (see template notes)

How to:

It is important to collect some information from the lead in the first meeting. This is important to best help the potential customer.
The easiest way to collect this data is:

1. Start typing #ExploreCall after which a pop-up will appear. Click on the pop-up, this put's the template in your note.

2. During the sales meeting get answers to the questions and write them down directly in the notes, otherwise there is a good chance you will forget important information. Also, try to tell the lead why this information is important: this is obviously to better help the lead.

3. If needed, adjust your notes after the meeting.

4. During the first conversation, make follow-up appointments right away. That way you keep in touch with the lead. 


After the first meeting 

After the first meeting, it is important that you follow up right away. You send the information that was requested and set out further tasks as needed. 

 (Explore meeting + information after first meet) - template mail

title: Amazon Error Stranded Inventory
subcategory: Amazon Error Solving SOP Overview - internal
body:
Resolution of Stranded Products on Amazon

Error Description:

Amazon Flagged items as stranded due to multiple distribution methods (FBA and Fulfilled by Amazon) being applied on the backend for the product in question.

Amazon Error code: 

Steps taken to resolve:

  • Downloaded the inventory loader file from: [Here]
  • Fill in the following important fields in the file:
    • SKU
    • Price
    • Product ID
    • Product ID type
    • Price
    • Item Condition
    • Item quantity
    • Add delete (Indicate with an 'a' those listings you wish to update the distribution method in order to fix the stranded items)
    • Fulfillment-center-id (If its merchant fulfilled leave empty, if its FBA write AMAZON_EU or the fulfillment center of the region of the seller)
  • Finally, save and upload the file to Amazon via the add products via upload option under 
  • After approximately one hour, the issue regarding stranded products must be resolved.
  • It is imperative to ensure that the products are devoid of any additional errors for this solution to be effective.

title: Sponsored brands campaign
body:
To set up a sponsored brands campaign follow these steps:

  1. Log in on ASC (for the client you are working for) → Click: ‘advertising’ → ‘campaign manager’. 
  2. Perform a generic search term analysis. Check the client scan for extra information about previous search term analysis for this client and extra information about their products.
        -   Automatic campaigns: pay attention to the number of clicks relative to  
            sales and ACOS. (module 4.4)
        -   Helium 10: Pay attention to search volume and IQ score. (module 4.2)

  3. Update the client scan with important (or negative) search terms you found

  4. Make sure the campaign manager is set to the right country (you can find/change this in the top right corner of the screen)
  5. Click: ‘create campaign’ and choose ‘sponsored brands’

  6. Make sure the settings of the campaign are according to the settings checklist.

  7. Add the search terms you found in the search terms analysis, add them as:
       -   Broad, (This offers the widest traffic exposure. The advertisement may 
           appear when a customer searches for your keyword in any order, including
           close variations)
       -   Phrase, (The advertisement may appear when a shopper searches for either
           the exact phrase of search terms or sequence of words in your search
           terms) 
       -   Exact, (In order for the advertisement to show, a shopper’s search term must
           match the search term exactly)

  8. Quality check: Make sure everything is set correctly →Click: 'submit for review' in the top right corner.

title: Cleaning Up and Restructuring Amazon Ad Campaigns
body:
Cleaning Up and Restructuring Amazon Ad Campaigns


Objective: To optimise and restructure existing Amazon ad campaigns by leveraging historical data, refining campaign structures, and ensuring future scalability without causing a decline in sales.

Key Steps:

Understanding Historical Data:

  • Utilise the historical performance of existing campaigns to inform restructuring decisions.

  • Avoid pausing well-performing campaigns to prevent a decline in sales.

Campaign Structure Analysis:

  • Identify and maintain campaigns with good structures, renaming them for clarity.

  • For campaigns with poor structures but good performance, plan gradual restructuring while keeping them active until new campaigns perform well.

Defining Bad Structure:

  • Campaigns advertising completely different products or listings should not be mixed.

  • Avoid having too many ad groups under one campaign; consolidate and archive non-performing ad groups.

Data Review Process:

  • Analyse the last 30 days of data focusing on spending and orders.

  • Archive ad groups and targets that do not generate sales.

  • Sort by orders and prioritise high-performing keywords and targets.

Optimization Strategy:

  • For ad groups with mixed product listings, keep only relevant targets.

  • Pause high ACoS (Advertising Cost of Sales) keywords that do not contribute significantly to sales.

  • Maintain a balance between pausing non-performing elements and ensuring continued sales performance.

Campaign Renaming and Notes:

  • Rename campaigns for better clarity (e.g., product type, match type, performance indicators).

  • Document notes about the campaign’s history, changes made, and future plans for easy reference.

Keyword and Target Management:

  • Focus on keeping high-performing keywords and targets.

  • Plan to create new campaigns with refined targets and pause old ones once new campaigns gain traction.

  • Ensure future campaigns do not mix high-volume keywords with lower visibility ones to maximise potential.

Ongoing Monitoring and Adjustment:

  • Continuously monitor performance, making incremental changes based on data.

  • Maintain detailed notes and mappings of campaign changes for transparency and tracking.

Final Implementation Steps:

  • Systematically go through each campaign and ad group to remove non-sales generating keywords and high-cost targets.

  • Rename campaigns to reflect their new structure and purpose.

  • Ensure proper placement and documentation in portfolios for organised management.

Recommendations:

  • Structured Approach: Use a systematic approach to analyse, rename, and document campaigns.

  • Gradual Transition: Avoid abrupt changes; instead, transition gradually to prevent sales decline.

  • Detailed Documentation: Keep detailed notes and mappings for every change to maintain clarity and track progress.

  • Focus on Scalability: Aim for a scalable structure that avoids mixing different product listings and maintains clear target focus.

title: What causes error 8572?
subcategory: Amazon Error Solving SOP Overview - internal
body:
What causes error 8572?

This error occurs when the Brand associated with the EAN conflicts with the Brand used to upload i.e. the EAN used to list the product is not found to be valid for the brand name used.

For Ex: Seller linked ‘XXXXX1X3XX7X’ EAN with ‘ABC’ brand name while purchasing Barcodes and associated the same EAN with a different brand name ‘XYZ’ while listing on Amazon.

Sample:

ResultCode: Error


ResultMessageCode: 8572


ResultDescription: You are using UPCs, EANs, ISBNs, ASINs, or JAN codes that do not match the products you are trying to list. Using incorrect UPCs, EANs, ISBNs, ASINs or JAN codes is prohibited and it can result in your ASIN creation privileges being suspended or permanently removed. Please ensure you are always using the appropriate UPC, EAN, ISBN, ASIN or JAN code when listing a product. If you have reached this message in error, please contact Seller Support using the following link: https://sellercentral.amazon.co.uk/hz/contact-us. For more information, refer to the ASIN Creation Policy Help Page - https://sellercentral.amazon.co.uk/gp/help/201844590

How to solve and prevent error 8572?

To resolve this error, your solution should list such products along with EANs and provide it to the seller. Based on the list provided, seller has to reach out to Seller Support team with their GS1 certification of the barcodes. Amazon will then examine the documents and whitelist the barcodes.


To prevent this error, ensure that you buy EANs directly from GS1 rather than buying it from a third party vendor. This will avoid any conflict in the data.


Note: This error has an associative error with it i.e. 8566. Hence you should ignore 8566 error associated with the SKUs erred out with 8572.

However, don’t make promises. You’ll never have a discussion about a target when the client has set the goal themselves and they can’t “blame” you for it.

title: Specialist: Asana Way of Working - How to work with a task
body:
We want to introduce a systematic approach that ensures everyone uses Asana in the same way. 

 Task overview

There are two overviews you could access to get a summary of your tasks

  1. The ‘My task’ overview - with our new way of working, the 'my task' overview should look like the picture below. This overview is especially useful to sort your task on ‘Due Date’, to easily see which tasks need priority and should be picked up first (unless told otherwise by your manager/consultant). 

Note: Always pick up the task with the shortest due date unless explicitly stated by the consultant.

*Click to enlarge


  1. The ‘Client Team’ overview gives you a clear summary of all the tasks related to a specific client. Besides being able to see your own tasks, you can also see other specialists who are currently working on that client. The new overview should look something like the picture below:

Receiving a task

All newly assigned tasks will be visible in the ‘My Task’ overview in Asana. How to pick up a newly assigned task:

1. The task

Each task is set up in 3 phases and there is a minimum amount of time to be spent by a specialist on each phase. The 3 phases are: 

  1. The preparation: reading through the client scan and the academy
  2. The task: the actual work to be done
  3. The check: to ensure that you read through your own work before handing it in. Once done, the task should not be put on 'done', but on 'needs check'.

2. Comments
The comment section must be used for the following:

  • If you can’t finish a task in the same shift - status report on how far you’ve gotten with the task (even if you're the next person that will pick up the task).

  • Any major difficulties/questions or doubts you have related to the task.

  • Any improvements and suggestions you may have for this task (especially regarding recurring tasks).

  • Communication between you and other colleagues that are assigned to the task. 

3. Finishing a task  - Change the status from ‘in progress’ to ‘Needs check ’.

* Important: Do not click the ‘mark completed’ box as this will be used by consultants as a quality check. 


Note: If you exceed the set estimated time to complete a task by more than 1 hour, make sure to let a consultant know to check if doing so is allowed.

title: Creating an Asana vertical plan (weekly) overview.
body:
In Asana you can create a 'task search' that will give you a weekly overview of all planned personnel in your vertical for each of the projects. This will allow you to create a clear overview that can be used in your planning meeting. In order to create this overview, please follow the follow steps: 

1. Click on 'Search' In the top bar in Asana and click on 'Advanced Search'

2. Add filters to your search

- Includes: 'Any'

- Completion: 'Completed'

- Due Date: Depends on when you do your planning but as an example: '7days' (You can also filter to Monday-Friday next week)

- Add filter 'In any of these teams': Add your vertical: E.g. Home & Garden.

- If recurring tasks are set and don't need to be assigned then you can also use the filter 'one time' for type of tasks. 

Eventually, the filter overview will look like this: 


3. The overview:

- After filling in the filters, you will get a custom overview of all your projects, tasks and who is assigned to them. The easiest way to create a per-project overview is to use the project filter (Sort: Project): 


4. Using the overview in your plan meeting:

- Use this overview to:

 - Assign open tasks to the specialists

 - To create a clear overview that shows how each task is assigned to someone and has a clear overview. 

 - You can change the 'sort' filter to 'employee', and add the estimates to create an overview to show how many hours someone is planned next week. 


IMPORTANT: THIS ONLY WORKS IF EACH TASK HAS A DUE DATE + ESTIMATE


In Summary:

The overview can be used for:

1. The consultant/lead meeting to plan and assign specialists to tasks + estimations

2. For the weekly plan meet with the team to:

 - Show per individual what they are planned for and how many hours are estimated for the work planned. 

 - Show per client/project, who is working on which tasks.

title: Front-End check Bol.com
body:
Front-End check Bol.com

For Bol.com, there are unfortunately no tools available to scrape the front-end. Therefore we have to check on a different way. 


Check in Bol.com Back-End

First we will check if the content is denied in the back-end of Bol.com. You can do this via: Artikelen > Gewijzigde Productinformatie > Mijn wijzigingen > Afgewezen


If the client has brand registry in place for Bol.com, all changes should be pushed.


Front-End check Bol.com

Since no tool is available, we have to check the content on the front-end ourselves. You can do this via sample:

  • You should check 10% of the products

  • Make sure to check various products (different categories / parents etc)

  • Search for the EAN on Bol.com, this way you know that you are checking the correct products


What to do if content is not updated correctly?

  • Check if the content is in the back-end, if this is not the case you have to change the content here. Also check why the content is not being pushed via the integrator.

  • If the client has brand registry in place and the content is not updated, you need to create a case via Bol.com


- Use sampling size of ~5%

title: Keyword Research Template Amazon
body:
Full video can be found here


The file needs input from different data sources, where one of them is optional:

  • Helium10 xRay

  • Amazon Brand Analytics (optional)

  • Helium10 Cerebro export


First we will walk through how to find the correct exports and how to implement them in the Keyword Research Template. At the end we will explain how the actual analysis tab works and how to identify the correct keywords depending on the phase the product is in.


To start this research we head to Amazon Brand Analytics > Top Search Terms. We will use this tool to identify the highest volume keyword which we will use to search competing products.

  • Select filter “Monthly” or “Quarterly”, this should have enough data

  • Use a search term which identifies the product, for example “airfryer”

  • Select the keyword with the highest search volume and use this keyword as a search query on Amazon (depending on country you are doing research for)


Helium10 xRay

To download the correct file, follow these steps:

  • Use the search term you identified in the beginning of this academy

  • Click on the Helium10 plugin, select xRay and export the data

  • Go to your Keyword Research file, Select column A1 in the xRay tab and import the file.


Amazon Brand Analytics

This step is optional, but recommended to get the most clear overview possible. This export requires you to:

  • Select all product in xRay that are equal to your product, this avoids any pollution. 

  • Go to Amazon Brand Analytics > Top Search Terms > Paste all the asins in the search field

  • This will give you an overview of all keywords where any of the imported ASINs has a Top 3 position in the search results.

  • Now export the file and import it, same concept as previous export, but this time in the “BA Analytics” tab


Helium10 Cerebro Export

Now for the last export:

  • Filter the xRay overview from highest to lowest based on Sales.

  •  Select the 10 most relevant products that match the product you are doing research for and press ‘Run Cerebro’

  • Make sure to remove the ABA Total Click Share and ABA Total Conversion Share columns via "Customize"


Now that you have the Cerebro overview, you can start manipulating the data before exporting is. Some settings that are advised:

  • Set the minimum search volume to 100

  • Set “Match Type” to “Organic”

  • Click apply filters and export the file

  • Import in the the correct tab, same way as the other exports.

And it contains 5 different sections (Example check: New Edition project)

  • Operational
    - Tasks here include: Operational management, Account management and specific error-solving tasks. 

  • Content

    * Some projects have the extra section: 'Timeline' - This is used to create a Gantt Chart-like overview of certain high-over projects.


Fixed tasks: 

Use the fixed tasks created in the template tasks form as much as possible!

Examples are:

  • Planning: For project planning and weekly planning 
  • Meeting [Customer name] <> Brandsom | Marketplace specialists : For all client meetings
  • Account management 
  • Operations Management: This task can be used for example to log internal meetings. Note that it is important to record in the task what the meeting was about
  • Correction: This may only be filled in by the vertical lead. This task is meant to correct excess hours in the invoice


For a new client, you can use the template Asana project. You can find it here and it is called [Template] Managed customer or  [Template] Project customer.

Click on 'Use Template" and follow the steps in the pop-up 


The way of working for Consultants

  1. Besides the use of the basic template, consultants can add ‘projects’ based on the specific requirements a client has. *Important - the standard attributes will remain the same, these must always be filled in.  (e.g. onboarding of new client)

  1. We want to keep all work (tasks) in one team per client.  

  1. For every Task created and any newly created tasks from here onwards, at least the following attributes must be filled in by the consultant:

    • Estimated hours to complete: To do this, download the extension Everhour for your browser. This extension will show up in Asana and looks like this:
        
    • Status: All open tasks without status should be put on ‘new’ (Factory will receive instructions to put on in progress/done).
    • Service: Consultancy or Specialist. 

    • Standard task: What type group task does this belong to: content, ads, integration, etc.

    • Recurring: Is it a one-time task or is it repeated (weekly, monthly etc.)? 

    • Paid: Non-billable, Fixed (project) or Managed. 


Marking tasks as 'in progress' and 'Needs Check'

Specialists have the responsibility to change a newly created task to 'in progress' when they have started a task and to 'Needs Check' when they have finished the task. If you notice that this doesn't happen in your team then make sure you address the responsible specialist. 

*The consultant is responsible for their Asana teams! 

This way, we can ensure everyone follows the way of working and we keep a clean and organised Asana.

title: Grievance Procedures
body:
Grievance Procedure

At Brandsom, we are committed to providing a fair and supportive work environment. Employees are encouraged to raise concerns or complaints about their work environment, treatment by colleagues or supervisors, or any other issues affecting their employment through the following grievance procedure, in compliance with Dutch law:

Types of Grievances:

  • Work conditions
  • Bullying or harassment
  • Discrimination
  • Unfair treatment
  • Any other work-related issues

Grievance Process:

  • Informal Resolution: Employees are encouraged to first discuss their concerns directly with the person involved or their immediate supervisor to seek an informal resolution.
  • Formal Grievance Submission: If the issue is not resolved informally, employees can submit a formal grievance in writing to HR.
  • Grievance Meeting: A meeting will be arranged between the employee, their supervisor, and HR to discuss the grievance and seek a resolution.
  • Investigation: HR will conduct a thorough investigation of the grievance, including interviews with relevant parties and review of any evidence.
  • Resolution and Outcome: HR will communicate the findings and any proposed actions to the employee. Possible outcomes may include mediation, disciplinary action against others, changes in work conditions, or other appropriate measures.
  • Appeal: If the employee is not satisfied with the outcome, they can appeal the decision to senior management or HR. This appeal process will be conducted in accordance with Dutch labour law, ensuring a fair and impartial review.

  • Employee Rights:

    • Employees have the right to a fair and impartial investigation of their grievance.
    • Employees have the right to be accompanied by a colleague or representative during formal grievance meetings.
    • Employees have the right to confidentiality throughout the grievance process.
    • Employees have the right to appeal any decision made as a result of the grievance procedure.

By clearly defining these procedures, Brandsom ensures a fair, transparent, and consistent approach to managing disciplinary actions and addressing employee grievances, in accordance with Dutch law. For more information on these procedures, please contact HR.

title: How to define the right integration method?
subcategory: How to pick the right integrator?
body:
Integrators often offer different integration options:

  • Stand alone
  • API
  • Plugins
  • Middleware

Each integration option has pro's and con's. With the method below you can define which integration is suitable for the brand.


Which information needs to be transferred over the interfaces?

An interface is an abstract term to describe the process between to systems. For example between the plug-in and the e-commerce platform. Or between the e-commerce platform and the WMS.

Every marketplace, system or stakeholder requires information which needs to be transferred over the different interfaces.

Examples:

  • Zalando (marketplaces) requires an outbound and return tracking code. Since these codes are often created in the logistical process, all interfaces between the logistical process and Zalando should be able to transfer these code.
  • ERP systems require a channel code to do a correct revenue allocation. This means that the order interface between the integrator and the ERP should contain information about the channel.
  • Stakeholders: finance could ask for the sales commission per product. To comply with this request, sales commission data per order, product line should be available in the order.

How can we define which information needs to be transferred over the interfaces?

  1. Ask which marketplaces the brand want to sell on? Analyse the marketplace requirements.
  2. Ask which logistical set-up the brand prefers? Analyse the logistical requirements from the marketplaces.
  3. Set-up a meeting with stakeholders from finance, marketing, content, logistics, customer service and management to make sure everybody is aligned.


Example questions could be:

  • Which marketplaces do you want to sell on?
  • Which fulfilment methodes do you want to use?
  • Which countries do you want to sell in?
  • How does you return process look?
  • Do you want to sell on Zalando or other fashion marketplaces using 'own logistics'?
  • In case of fashion: are you able to provide a return label in the box?
  • In case of fashion: are you able to capture the return tracking code in the box?

title: Amazon Launch Budget Esti
body:
Amazon Launch - Budget Estimation 

TEMPLATE - Launch Budget / TACOS Estimation


Before we begin, some notes:

  • The template is based on estimates, always communicate this to the client
  • This is based on 1 month of sales, you can create multiple templates for example to cover more months.
  • Only change the metrics in the top 5 rows to keep the sheet working
  • If you are looking into multiple categories, my advice would be to highlight 2-3 categories instead of combining them all into one.
  • Keep in mind that the first month 100% PPC sales is very common. 

Launch budget calculation sheet

The Launch Budget estimation is used to give an overview of the investment that is needed to build a sales rank on Amazon. Calculations are done based on the following metrics, we will discuss how to determine them below:

  • Monthly Unit Sold Target
  • Monthly Organic Sales
  • Monthly PPC Sales
  • Average CPC
  • Average Selling Price

Monthly Sales (PPC and organic)

The best way to calculate the estimations for the monthly sales, is to look at the search volume of the keywords you will be focussing on. You can look at the average CTR via the Product Opportunity Explorer to calculate the visits. With the average CVR you can now calculate the estimated sales. 


Average CPC

To find out what the average CPC for the client is, you can simply add the keywords when you try to create a sponsored products campaign. Since we want to target Top Of Search (TOS), take the higher end of the estimates Amazon is giving you. Follow these steps:

  • Take the most important keywords for your client
  • Go to Seller Central > Campaign Manager and create a Sponsored Products campaign
  • Here you need to select a product, after this you can add the keywords to the campaign.
  • Add them as “exact match”, Amazon will now show you the CPC estimates


IMPORTANT: CPC is based on multiple factors, one of them being sales. Once you can more sales on a specific keyword, CPC will drop. If you are less relevant for a keyword, CPC will increase.


Average Selling Price

If the client is selling multiple products in the category you are presenting, you can use an average price of the combined products. Try to avoid adding high-price items with low-price items, this will have a negative impact on the overview. If it is only one product you can of course use the price of the product to calculate.

title: Maternity/Paternity Leave
body:
Maternity and Paternity Leave

At Brandsom, we comply with Dutch law regarding maternity and paternity leave to support our employees during these significant life events.

Maternity Leave
Expectant mothers are entitled to a total of 16 weeks of maternity leave. This includes 6 weeks of leave before the expected due date (pregnancy leave) and at least 10 weeks after childbirth (maternity leave). If the baby is born later than the due date, the maternity leave is extended to ensure a full 16 weeks. During this period, employees will receive 100% of their salary.

Paternity Leave
Partners are entitled to 1 week of paid paternity leave to be taken within 4 weeks of the birth of the child. In addition, partners can take up to 5 weeks of additional paternity leave within the first 6 months after the birth. During these additional weeks, employees are entitled to receive 70% of their salary from the Dutch government.

For more detailed information or assistance with applying for maternity or paternity leave, please contact HR.

title: MarktMentor - Keyword Tracking
body:
Keyword Tracking

MarktMentor automatically tracks the performance of all products on all keywords they rank on. Why is this important:

  • You can report to the client how their products are performing on their most important keywords
  • Makes it able to track the impact of content optimizations on more than just GMV. For example, because you rank on extra keywords

Via Winkel Analyseren > Mijn Rankings you will find an overview of all the products that are currently in the Bol backend. When you click the arrow on the very right of a product, it shows you all the keywords the product is ranking on.

Via the toggle you can tell MarktMentor to stop tracking specific keywords. This way you can narrow it down to the most important keywords per product. You are also able to do this in bulk if you are managing a high amount of products. To do this, click on "Zoektermen wijzigen" and follow the instructions provided by MarktMentor.

How to report this to your clients?

You can use this to report on a monthly basis to show the progress that is made on the most important keywords. Use a screenshot and add this to your presentation. 

You can also report on the impact of content optimization. For new keywords you will see a ranking available on 7 days or 30 days, but not on 90 days for example. You can show this to the client and report on the extra search volume we are now potentially reaching.

title: FBA Shipping Template
body:
Creating an FBA plan for clients on Amazon can be a daunting task, but it shouldn’t be. It’s pretty straightforward if you have the correct information from the client. It so happens that clients do not provide the necessary information to ship products to FBA, for example: carton sizes, palletized shipment or non palletized, etc. 

For this scenario, we will use a FBA Shipping Template. This template can be used to either ask your client for the missing information, or copy this template, save it in your clients folder and let the client fill this in themselves. 

This will ensure a smooth process when creating shipments and avoid back and forth communication with the client to request information. Make sure you understand and explain the difference between shipping box and unit packaging. 

  • The shipping box is the box with all the units inside of it. Usually this box contains a label from the shipping carrier on it

  • The unit packaging is the box that is around the unit. The first layer of packaging around the product. It usually has a nice design on it. 


The Template can be found here

title: What causes error 8560?
subcategory: Amazon Error Solving SOP Overview - internal
body:
What causes error 8560?

This error occurs when the product data provided in the SKU do not meet the requirements for ASIN creation. The most common causes for this error are:


  • The values entered for a specific attribute is invalid.

  • The required fields are missing in the feed submitted by you.

  • The product ID does not match any ASINs.

Samples:

ResultMessageCode: 8560


ResultDescription:SKU XXXXXX, Missing Attributes product_type. SKU XXXXXX does not match any ASINs. Make sure that all standard product ids (such as UPC, ISBN, EAN, or JAN codes) are correct. To create a new ASIN, include the following attributes: product_type. Feed ID: 0. For more troubleshooting help, see http://sellercentral.amazon.co.uk/gp/errorcode/200692370

How to solve and prevent error 8560?

Your solution should validate the following on the feed:

  1. Make sure that the product ID is correct.

    • Check character requirements. For example, UPCs have 12 digits, EANs have 13 digits, and ISBNs have 10.

    • Check for barcode validation.

    • Update your data and resubmit.

  2.  Make sure all required fields are complete.

    • Use the correct inventory file template/ feed for your product category.

    • Required attributes vary from category to category (For example product_type) Hence check 'Data Definition' tab of specific category in flat files to determine required attributes and populate the same in feeds. (Download category specific template from here.

    • Update your data and resubmit.


Either fix an incorrect product ID, or create a new ASIN using a new product ID.

title: Integration job profile
body:
Marketplace Integration Specialist (Junior / Medior / Senior)


  1. The Function
    The Marketplace Integration Specialist plays an important role in ensuring seamless connections between business systems (e.g., PIM, ERP, WMS) and online marketplaces. This role focuses on automation, AI-driven processes, and data management to minimize manual efforts in product onboarding, error resolution, and marketplace optimization.

    This position is critical in streamlining marketplace connections, enhancing efficiency, and driving scalability. Responsibilities include managing integrations, improving automation workflows, and leveraging AI-driven insights to optimize marketplace performance.

    Reporting Line
    The Marketplace Integration Specialist reports to the team consultant.

Your Responsibilities
The Marketplace Integration Specialist is responsible for ensuring smooth marketplace connections and enhancing automation workflows to improve efficiency regarding marketplace integrations. The role evolves from foundational execution to strategic automation and leadership responsibilities as experience grows.


High level responsibilities

  • Marketplace Integrations: Ensure seamless integration of product data, pricing, shipping, and categorization across multiple marketplaces. 
  • Automation & AI-Driven Efficiency: Leverage AI tools and automation scripts (E.g. ChatGPT and V-lookup) to streamline repetitive processes and error resolution.
  • Error Resolution & Optimization: Identify patterns in integration errors and implement solutions that reduce manual intervention.
  •  Data Management & Reporting: Utilize structured data (using Google Sheets/Excel) and visualisation tools to track integration health and performance. 
  • Process Improvement: Continuously enhance marketplace workflows, driving efficiency through scalable automation solutions.

Role-Specific Responsibilities

Junior Integration Specialist

  • Gain familiarity with 1-2 integration tools (e.g., ChannelEngine, Tradebyte, Channable) and execute foundational tasks such as account setup, product onboarding, and error resolution.
  • Assist in marketplace health checks and reporting.
  • Support senior specialists/consultants with promotions and replenishment processes.
  • Begin learning automation tools to reduce manual work.

Medior Integration Specialist

  • Execute Junior-level responsibilities with full efficiency and proficiency.
  • Independently manage marketplace integrations and troubleshoot issues efficiently.
  • Take ownership of automation implementation and contribute to AI-driven enhancements.
  • Identify and address recurring integration errors, working towards scalable automation solutions.
  • Support consultants by improving integration workflows and driving efficiency.
  • Create automated data feeds and implement AI-powered solutions for optimizing marketplace performance.

Senior Integration Specialist

  • Strategically lead marketplace integrations, ensuring automation-first workflows.
  • Collaborate with developers and automation specialists to enhance integration tools.
  • Develop best practices and process documentation for knowledge sharing and training.
  • Drive AI-powered optimization strategies for marketplace success.
  • Provide operational leadership and mentorship to the integration team.

  1. How to Make Impact

On the customer side

Ensure that integrations align with marketplace requirements, optimizing product listings and maintaining data consistency to drive performance. Support consultants in maximizing marketplace potential for clients through automation and AI-driven insights.

On the process side

Structure and streamline integration workflows to enhance efficiency. Implement automation tools that reduce repetitive tasks, freeing up time e.g. for strategic initiatives.



On the quality side

Leverage AI and automation to proactively monitor and resolve integration errors. Continuously identify areas for improvement, ensuring scalable, high-quality marketplace operations.

  1. Your Competencies

Core Knowledge & Skills

  • Expertise in managing marketplace integrations and ensuring data accuracy.
  • Strong understanding of automation tools and AI-driven process improvements.
  • Proficiency in structured data management and visualization (Google Sheets, Excel, reporting tools).

Analytical & Innovation Abilities

  • AI-oriented mindset to enhance automation workflows and reduce manual interventions.
  • Proficiency in identifying patterns in error resolution and implementing scalable fixes.
  • Continuous improvement approach, staying ahead of industry trends in AI and automation.

Leadership & Management Skills (Senior Level)

  • Proven ability to drive automation-first strategies and mentor junior team members.
  • Strong communication skills to align cross-functional teams and improve marketplace operations.
  • Ability to translate technical integration challenges into scalable business solutions.

  1. Your Experience

Junior Level

  • Experience with Google Sheets and/or Excel required.
  • Basic understanding of marketplaces and e-commerce is a plus but not required.
  • Strong motivation to learn automation and marketplace integration tools.
  • Bachelor’s degree (HBO level) or equivalent work experience preferred.
  • Pre: Affinity with AI tools and automation tools.

Medior Level

  • At least 2 years of experience in marketplace integrations or automation.
  • Proven ability to manage integration tasks independently and support process improvements.
  • Exposure to automation tools, AI-driven optimizations, or scripting (Python, SQL is a plus).
  • Experience working in fast-paced, deadline-driven environments.

Senior Level

  • At least 2-3 years of experience on a medior level in marketplace integrations and automation leadership.
  • Strong automation scripting skills (Python, SQL, AI-based categorization, or API management).
  • Experience in project management and operational leadership.
  • Demonstrated ability to develop and implement scalable automation solutions.

title: Brandsom Marketplace Escalation Ladder
body:
1. Marketplace, Integrator, or Brandsom Academy Resources

Start by attempting to resolve the issue using available resources such as the Marketplace, Integrator, or Brandsom Academy (e.g., ChannelEngine Support). These platforms often have comprehensive guides and FAQs on most common errors and issues. 


2. Direct Colleagues

If the issue remains unresolved, consult with your direct colleagues within your team or with colleagues from other teams in a similar role to yours. 


3. Support Email

If further assistance is needed, reach out to the integrator support team via email (support@marketplace/integrator) or for an even quicker response, call them.


Important numbers:

Channelengine support: +31 715 288 795

Channable support: +31 (0)30 260 08 21

Vendiro support:  +31 (0)71 203 25 00


4. Consultant

Should the issue persist, escalate to a designated consultant. Ensure to include all relevant information and any guidance received from previous steps.


5. CSM or Contact Person at [Marketplace/Integrator]

If the consultant is unable to resolve the issue, contact your Customer Success Manager (CSM) in case of ChannelEngine or designated contact person at the marketplace or integrator. Provide a comprehensive report of the situation, including all previous communications and attempted solutions.


6. Lead

For issues that remain unresolved after following the previous steps, escalate to the lead.


7. Imco

As a last resort, if the issue is critical and has not been resolved through the above channels, escalate to Imco. This step should only be taken when all other avenues have been exhausted. If he can't solve it, nobody can. 


Important Notes

Always document each step of the escalation process, including dates, times, and individuals involved.

Urgent matters that have a significant impact on business operations may warrant a direct escalation to higher levels, bypassing certain steps as necessary.

title: What causes error 8032?
subcategory: Amazon Error Solving SOP Overview - internal
body:
What causes error 8032?

This error occurs when you are trying to associate the same child SKU to multiple parent SKUs on the feed.

Sample:

Invalid variation relationship: The child variation is already assigned to a parent SKU. Please delete the old relationship before submitting a new relationship.

How to solve and prevent error 8032?

Your solution should validate before uploading the feeds to Amazon or alternatively prevent the seller from assigning the same child SKU to multiple parent from your interface. To submit a delete feed, enter Delete in the Update/Delete field in your data file for the SKUs that you would like to reassign variation parent/child relationships for and upload the template to Seller Central. Once the SKUs are deleted from your inventory, remove Delete from the Update/Delete field in your data file, update the relationship data as desired and upload the file containing the new relationship data to Seller Central.


Note: It is enough that one of these errors happens only in one product of a family, and it will prevent the whole product family from being created correctly on the seller’s catalog on Amazon.

title: TradeByte How to upload a CSV document using Libre Office
body:
In this module, I will show you how to upload a CSV bulk upload to TradeByte using LibreOffice.


*IMPORTANT* ALWAYS TAKE INTO ACCOUNT THE FOLLOWING RULES:

  1. CHECK TRADEBYTE GUIDE 4B  IN THE SOLUTION CENTER FOR SPECIFICS

  2. CHECK YOUR WORK AFTER UPLOADING

  3. NEVER UPLOAD END OF DAY OR ON FRIDAY  

  4. PLEASE TAKE INTO ACCOUNT WHEN UPLOADING COMPONENTS: DISABLE EXPORT SETTINGS FOR THE NON RELEVANT CHANNELS

  5. COMPONENTS AND TAGS (ATTRIBUTES) SHOULD BE PROCESSED ON PRODUCT LEVEL, EXCEPT COLOUR AND SIZE.


Save the file:

  1. Download Libre office

  2. Reference guide 4b of the general TB manuals for text formatting

  3. Check the content:

    • Check the headers for spaces and correct import keys, both in the guide as well as in TradeByte

    • Check individual cells for empty spaces and errors (#N/B)

    • If changing “color” and or “size”, always upload both even if only changing one

    • When uploading prices, check admin ->  import -> general settings

    • Remove all rows & columns you don’t want to upload 

    • Check if all cells are formatted as text (see below)

      1. select all data

      2. right mouse click

      3. format cells

      4. click tekst

      5. click Ok

  4. Save the file:

    • save as tekst CSV

    • Use correct naming (article_, product_, panda_ → see guide 4b)

    • when asked check the CSV import settings at TB admin -> import ->CSV Import

  5. Upload the file at data exchange -> validators-> csv

  6. If accepted, upload the file at   data exchange -> validators -> input

  7. Check errors and output

  8. Do a quality check by manually checking 5 products if the information is chanced correctly

  9. Do a quantitative check by exporting and V-looking for changes

  10. Check the error lists  in the solution channel if changes resulted in new errors.

title: ChannelEngine: Repricer module PART 2
body:
PART 2

4. Targeting buy box

A buy box is the most prominent space within the product page in a marketplace. It is used to indicate to the consumer a good seller option, among those who offer the product, to deliver the desired goods.

In a marketplace, the same product can be offered by several sellers. Therefore, the buy box is a way that marketplaces use to indicate the best offer available to the buyer.

In ChannelEngine, the “Targeting Buy Box” option is only available if the marketplace can supply us with information about the buybox winner. Remember that not only the product’s price is important, but the delivery times and ratings. 

  • Decrease price by: price decreases to get the Buy box. This tactic assumes applying regular repricing rules first, proceeding to the gradual price decrease until you get the Buy Box or until the minimum price is reached. 
    • This depends on the price dynamic and Buy box change by the marketplace.
  • Increase price by: price increase while maintaining the Buy box. Enable this option if you set the value to increase the price with each step. Good chance to lose the buy box, so you must place a threshold to trigger the increase:
  • Threshold: it is the change between the current reprice by the regular repricing (before the buy box targeting), and the previous reprice by regular repricing (before the buy box targeting) as a percentage. If the threshold isn’t met, other changes will trigger an increase in your price:
    • Fulfillment status.
    • Buy box winner status.
    • Shipping time status.
    • 10% increase / decrease in price, including shipping.
    • 10% increase / decrease in seller rating. 

 5. Possible scenarios

You can find this tab right under the “Target buy box”:

  • If lowest below min. price: in regular repricing, if the current cheapest competitor is offering a price lower than your minimum, you can select what to do, either set your minimum price or do no repricing. 
  • If lowest above max. price: if your lowest competitor is offering a higher price than your maximum price, you can either set your maximum price or do no repricing.
  • If no competition: there’s no competitor price to reprice on. Three possibilities:
    • Export your max. price as the price for this product.
    • No repricing (the regular price with price rules will be exported).
    • No repricing unless below min. price (export the minimum price if your regular price is below that). This prevents selling items at a very low price. 
  • If buy box below min. price / if buy box winner: the label and the options change in this case depending on if you selected “Target buy box” or not:
    • Target buy box selected:
      • Min. Price: minimum price is exported.
      • No repricing: regular price with price rules applied is exported.
    • Target buy box is not selected:
      • Reprice: the exported price will increase / decrease with the configured elements selected before.
      • Keep the current price unless below Min. Price: price remains the same unless the product is currently offered for a price lower than the one we set for the minimum.


 6. Choosing your competition

The last information we should include for the repricer is related to the competition. You can find this under the “scenarios” tab. 


  • Seller groups: the ones you want to compete with. Select from the list. 
  • Max. shipping time: you want to compete with (to exclude sellers that have a very long shipping time, for example). 
    • Out of stock: click this button to enable competing with sellers that are currently having a listing on your product but not available stock.
  • Min. seller rating: sellers with a lower price but a worse rating (you might not want to compete with them and lower your price). 
  • Min. seller rating count: exclude sellers that only have a few ratings.
  • Sellers: exclude specific sellers. 

 

When you’re finished, change the “preview” mode to “active”. Save it and the repricer will be activated.

title: Zalando Brand Home
body:
Brand Homes allow brands to tell their brand story and increase the reach of their content. The concept allows you to showcase your brand and products and helps shoppers learn your brand story and discover your products. A key benefit of having a brand home is that the consumer can 'follow' your brand on Zalando. This way, the consumer will receive an email when you are launching a new collection. 

Some Brand Home examples are:

  • Travelin
  • Bamboo Basics
  • Petrol Industries

Key benefits of Zalando Brand Home

  • Brand Homes are free - we believe all brands should be able to connect with their customers, share content and stories, and build their brand value on Zalando

  • It increases brand visibility and customer reach - visibility goes beyond the dedicated Brand Homes page (to Zalando's home page, app push notifications for brand followers and beyond)

  • Brand Homes have a premium app experience - this makes it easier for customers to discover brands as they browse

  • Brands have access to performance insights - giving them data and insights to monitor engagement and sales KPIs and react to their performance

What to keep in mind when creating a Brand Home

Over time, lots of learnings have been made regarding Brand Homes on Zalando. Please find them below:

  • If you update the Brand Home to match a season, for example Christmas, make sure to switch back to a 'regular' brand store once the season is over.
  • When the brand is pushing a specific campaign outside of marketplaces, you want to reflect this on the Zalando Brand Home.
  • All text (also on images) has to be translated into target language (e.g. German in Germany, Dutch in the Netherlands etc)
  • The order of SKUs is important. The first 15 articles in your SKU list will be shown in that order in the collection carousel, visible underneath the header image. 

Important URL's

  • Introduction to Brand Homes
  • Brand Home Website
  • How-To video's

title: Zalando Retagging
body:
TABLE OF CONTENTS

2. What is Retagging in Zalando

2.1. General Information

2.2. Seasons for Retagging

2.3. Deadlines for Retagging

3. How to do Retagging

3.1. Requirements to Consider

3.2. Through Retag Request

3.3. Through Discounting

4. Additional Information for Clients of Brandsom


  1. What is Retagging in Zalando

  1. General Information 

Retagging is the process of updating an article’s designated season, e.g. an AW22 article can be retagged to AW23. It offers partners the opportunity to keep the products in their collection to be marketed and sold for an additional season at full price. We say full price, because discounting is one of the two methods of retagging. The other one is through discounting of the price (see chapter 3 for more information). 

 

  1. Seasons for Retagging

Zalando follows a seasonal timeline consisting of two sales seasons:

  • Autumn Winter (AW) starts in May and ends in February (ten months)

  • Spring Summer (SS) starts in November and ends in August (ten months)



In addition to seasonal articles, throughout the year you can also onboard your articles as:

  • Year_Round_Basics (YRB), 

  • Spring Summer_Basics (SS_Basics) 

or 

  • Autumn Winter Basics (AW_Basics)

YRB articles are suitable for all seasons and exhibit a consistent customer demand all year round. On the other hand, SS/AW_Basics articles are in high demand across multiple summer or winter seasons.

For example: A white t-shirt would be considered a YRB, while a bikini would be specific to SS seasons, and a black turtleneck sweater would be specific to AW seasons. 


Basics or NOS articles require ongoing stock replenishment to meet customer needs. They don’t need to be discounted or retagged when a season ends. We have implemented a monthly review process to monitor stock levels and customer demand for these articles. You can find out more details here. 

  1. Deadlines for Retagging

Retagging will only be possible in a dedicated window around the season switch.

  • SS retagging is possible from January to April

  • AW retagging is possible from July to October

 

The deadlines for submitting the requests for 2024 are:

 Please also be aware that Zalando reserves the right to refuse retagging based on safety concerns, content violations, or fashionability concerns as determined by Zalando’s assortment steering teams.


  1. How to do Retagging

  1. Requirements to Consider


In order for the retag process to be successful and within the wanted time frame, some requirements need to be taken under consideration.

To ensure a desirable and fresh assortment for our customers, we assess your retagging requests based on Size Availability Rate (SAR), Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) and article age. 


SAR threshold (percentage of available -with stock- sizes)

Articles to be retagged to seasonal, i.e SS or AW, should have a minimum SAR of 75% in the past four weeks.

Articles to be retagged to YRB/SS_Basics/AW_Bascis should maintain a SAR constantly above 85% throughout the year. 


GMV threshold:

Articles must have generated at least €1,000 GMV after cancellations and returns in the past 12 months to be eligible for retagging.


Article age:

Articles can be retagged a maximum of two times. For example, from SS22 to SS23 or AW22 to AW23. For further extension, the articles would have to retag to YRB/SS_Bascis/AW_Basics, upon meeting the SAR and GMV thresholds mentioned above.


  1. Through Retag Request

The retag request requires us to populate a template from Zalando regarding the products and the seasons requested. The template can be found in the link bellow:


https://partnerportal.zalando.com/partners/s/article/Season-Retagging?t=1704892145718


Populate the template and send it through a ticket to Zalando


  1. Through Discounting

Once a sales season ends, the articles associated with the previous season are categorised as old season articles. These articles can only remain online in the sale section, subject to meeting a minimum discount rate.(for more information on the exact discount rates per market, have a look at the table provided below).

There is a regular process in place to check if the discount requirement has been fulfilled. Old season articles without sufficient discount will be deactivated, and flagged as ZABLO_01: Old Season Cleaning in the Article Listing in zDirect. 

Once we detect that the discount requirement is fulfilled, the articles will be reactivated and made available for sale again. 



  1. Additional Information for Clients of Brandsom


How long does the process take?

Zalando usually asks for about two weeks to consider and complete the retagging process on request.


What is a Season Switch?

A season switch is the process of transitioning from the current sales season to a new season in the catalogs. During this transition, articles from the previous season are categorized as old and shifted to the sales section, subject to a minimum discount rate. This is to ensure a fresh, seasonally appropriate assortment for customers on the platform. 

Season switch takes place twice a year:

  • AW changes to SS around February 

  • SS changes to AW around August 

The actual dates of each season switch differ per market and are decided based on factors such as market needs, weather conditions etc. We will always ensure to  inform partners of the finalized dates through the ZPU and via email.

 

Below are the season switch per market dates from SS23 to AW23:
 

 

  1. You can either take full advantage of the season switch by adapting your prices to the minimum discount before the official switch date listed on the above table per market. 

  2. Or you simply pick the highest minimum discount rate across all the markets 

 

Article journey after the season switch per market date onwards: 

  • AW23 articles will become the main season in the catalogs and be actively marketed to customers. 

  • SS23 articles will be considered as old and relocated to the sale section. To remain online, they must offer a minimum discount rate of either 10% or 15% depending on the market mentioned above. They can stay online for a maximum of one year from the switch date.

  • SS22 articles will be completely offboarded and no longer for sale. 

Important note: If you still want to actively market your articles and sell them at full price, you have the opportunity to keep them in the catalogs by retagging. You can find out more about the retagging process here.

title: What causes error 90122?
subcategory: Amazon Error Solving SOP Overview - internal
body:
What causes error 90122?

This error occurs when there is HTML content on the product description that is in the product data feeds. HTML content is not allowed on the product description on Amazon.

Sample:


ResultMessageCode:90122


ResultDescription: The Message/Product/DescriptionData/Description field contains an invalid value: < br /& gt; The ARCTIC F PWM series outperforms other 80mm/92mm/120mm fans in the market. The patented PWM Sharing Technology (PST) regulates the speed of up to five fans via BIOS. With efficient central cooling, the ARCTIC F PWM series offers excellent cooling performance for the system. Lt; /p> < /table&gt. HTML content is not allowed in this field. 

How to solve and prevent errors 90122?

You have two options to tackle this type error:


  1. Parse the descriptions from the seller, remove any HTML characters or tags, and then upload the fixed descriptions.


    Caution: This sometimes may cause the descriptions to lose their formatting.


  1. Identify if there are any HTML characters in the products descriptions before uploading the feed form the seller and prevent the upload until the seller has fixed this.


We recommend that you use the first method and additionally show a warning to the sellers identifying which product descriptions have been modified so they can fix them if they are not showing correctly.
