title: AI - Example Uses of ChatGPT
body:
Example Uses of Chatgpt

Client Communication and Email Drafting

  • Example Prompt: "Can you help me draft an email to a client summarizing their monthly sales performance and suggesting strategies for the next quarter?"

  • Example Output: Subject: Monthly Sales Performance and Strategy Recommendations
    Dear [Client's Name],
    I hope this message finds you well. I wanted to share a brief overview of your store's performance over the past month. Your sales have shown a steady increase, with a significant uptick in [specific product category]. This suggests growing customer interest in these items.
    To capitalize on this momentum, I recommend focusing our marketing efforts on these popular products. Additionally, enhancing product descriptions and images could further boost conversions.
    Let's discuss these strategies in our next call. Looking forward to your thoughts.
    Best regards,
    [Your Name]

Meeting Summaries and Action Items

  • Example Prompt: "We just had a meeting with a client about optimizing their product listings. Can you summarize the discussion and list the agreed action items?"

  • Example Output: Summary of Meeting on Product Listing Optimization
    Participants: [Your Name], [Client's Name]
    Date: [Meeting Date]
    Key Discussion Points:

    1. Reviewed current product listings and identified key areas for improvement.

    2. Agreed on enhancing SEO keywords and updating product descriptions for clarity.

    3. Discussed implementing A/B testing for product images.

  • Action Items:

    1. [Your Name] to draft updated product descriptions by [Date].

    2. [Client's Name] to provide feedback on proposed changes by [Date].

    3. Schedule follow-up meeting to review changes and analyze performance metrics.

Task Management and Prioritization for Multiple Clients

  • Example Prompt: "Here are the tasks for our top clients today: optimize ads, update product descriptions, and analyze sales data. Can you help prioritize these tasks?"

  • Example Output: Task Prioritization for [Date]

    1. Update Product Descriptions: Essential for improving conversion rates and SEO. Target completion by noon.

    2. Optimize Ads: Focus on adjusting targeting and budget allocation. Aim to complete by end of day.

    3. Analyze Sales Data: Review performance metrics for strategic planning. Can be done by end of week.

Market Analysis and Report Drafting

  • Example Prompt: "I need to draft a market analysis report for a client focusing on their main competitors and potential growth opportunities. Can you outline the report?"

  • Example Output: 

  • Market Analysis Report Outline
    Introduction

    • Overview of Client's Market Position

    • Purpose of the Analysis

  • Competitor Analysis

    • Key Competitors Overview

    • SWOT Analysis for Each Competitor

    • Market Share Comparison

  • Market Trends

    • Current Trends in the Industry

    • Consumer Behavior Insights

    • Emerging Opportunities

  • Growth Opportunities

    • Potential Areas for Expansion

    • Recommendations for Product Diversification

    • Strategic Marketing Initiatives

  • Conclusion

    • Summary of Key Findings

    • Next Steps and Strategic Recommendations

Brainstorming Marketing Strategies

  • Example Prompt: "Can you suggest some marketing strategies for boosting the visibility of a client's new product line in a highly competitive market?"

  • Example Output: Marketing Strategies for New Product Line

    1. Influencer Partnerships: Collaborate with influencers relevant to the target audience to showcase the product.

    2. Content Marketing: Create engaging blog posts and videos highlighting the unique features and benefits of the product.

    3. Social Media Campaigns: Run targeted ads and promotions on platforms popular with the target demographic.

    4. Limited-Time Offers: Launch introductory discounts or bundle deals to encourage initial purchases.

    5. Email Marketing: Utilize your email list to inform existing customers about the new product and offer exclusive deals.

Researching Market Trends and Insights

  • Example Prompt: "Can you provide a brief overview of the latest trends in online shopping behavior, particularly during holiday seasons?"

  • Example Output: Overview of Online Shopping Trends During Holiday Seasons

    • Increased Mobile Shopping: A significant rise in mobile device usage for shopping, emphasizing the need for mobile-optimized websites.

    • Early Shopping: Consumers are starting their holiday shopping earlier, driven by early sales and promotions.

    • Social Commerce: Growing trend of shopping directly through social media platforms, making it crucial to enhance social media presence.

    • Personalized Shopping Experiences: Shoppers are increasingly expecting personalized recommendations and offers.

    • Eco-Friendly Products: A noticeable shift towards sustainable and eco-friendly products, aligning with consumer values.

Creating Templates and Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)

  • Example Prompt: "Can you create a checklist for new client onboarding that covers all necessary steps from initial consultation to setting up their online store?"

  • Example Output: New Client Onboarding Checklist
    Initial Consultation

    • Schedule and conduct initial consultation call

    • Gather client requirements and expectations

    • Discuss contract and terms of service

  • Account Setup

    • Create client profile and account in the system

    • Set up communication channels and project management tools

  • Marketplace Integration

    • Link client's online store to relevant marketplaces

    • Verify product listings and inventory integration

  • Branding and Design

    • Review and refine brand assets (logos, banners)

    • Customize store layout and design elements

  • Marketing and Promotion Setup

    • Develop initial marketing strategy and campaigns

    • Set up tracking and analytics tools

  • Final Review and Launch

    • Conduct a comprehensive review of the setup

    • Schedule a launch date and promotional activities

    • Provide client with training and support resources

Content Optimization and Writing Assistance

  • Example Prompt: "I need to rewrite a product description to make it more engaging and SEO-friendly. Can you help improve the language and keywords?"

  • Example Output: Original Description: "This is a comfortable chair, perfect for home or office use. It comes in several colors and has a modern design."
    Revised Description: "Upgrade your home or office space with our stylish and comfortable ergonomic chair. Designed for all-day comfort, this chair features a sleek modern design and is available in a variety of vibrant colors. Ideal for enhancing your workspace, it offers both functionality and a contemporary look. Shop now and experience the perfect blend of style and comfort."

Scheduling Promotions and Campaigns

  • Example Prompt: "Can you help me plan a promotional schedule for a client's upcoming sale event, including email marketing and social media posts?"

  • Example Output: Promotional Schedule for Upcoming Sale Event
    Week 1: Pre-Sale Teasers

    • Email Campaign: Announce upcoming sale, highlight key products

    • Social Media Teasers: Share sneak peeks and countdown posts

  • Week 2: Sale Launch

    • Email Blast: Officially announce sale start, offer exclusive discounts

    • Social Media Posts: Share promo codes, highlight best deals, and engage with followers

  • Week 3: Mid-Sale Boost

    • Email Reminder: Emphasize limited-time offers and ending soon deals

    • Social Media Ads: Run targeted ads to boost visibility and reach

  • Week 4: Last Chance & Post-Sale Follow-Up

    • Email: "Last Chance" message to encourage final purchases

    • Social Media: Post testimonials and encourage sharing of purchases

    • Post-Sale Email: Thank customers and offer a discount on next purchase

Creating and Editing Visual Content Descriptions

  • Example Prompt: "We need engaging descriptions for new product images to be used on a client's website. Can you suggest some creative and appealing captions?"

  • Example Output: Product Image Captions

    1. "Discover comfort and style with our latest ergonomic chair, perfect for any workspace."

    2. "Unveil your inner chef with our premium kitchenware, designed for perfection."

    3. "Elevate your home décor with our elegant and timeless lighting collection."

    4. "Step into luxury with our soft and durable area rugs, available in stunning designs."

    5. "Find your style with our versatile and chic fashion accessories, crafted for every occasion."

Writing Excel/Google Sheets Formulas

  • Example Prompt: "I need a formula to calculate the average monthly sales growth rate in Google Sheets, based on a column of monthly sales figures. Can you provide the formula?"

  • Example Output: Formula to Calculate Average Monthly Sales Growth Rate:
    If your monthly sales figures are in column B, starting from cell B2 to B13, you can use the following formula:
    =AVERAGE((B3:B13-B2

    )/B2
    )
    This formula calculates the month-over-month growth rate for each month, and then takes the average of these rates.

Creating Custom GPTs to Automate Repetitive Tasks

  • Example Prompt: "How can I set up a custom GPT to automatically categorize new products based on their descriptions and attributes?"

  • Example Output: Setting Up a Custom GPT for Product Categorization:

    1. Define Categories and Attributes:

      • Identify the main categories (e.g., Electronics, Clothing, Home Decor) and key attributes (e.g., color, size, material) relevant to your products.

    2. Gather Training Data:

      • Collect a dataset of product descriptions with labeled categories and attributes. Ensure diversity and accuracy in the data to improve model performance.

    3. Train the Custom GPT:

      • Use a GPT platform to train a model using the dataset. Focus on tuning the model to recognize patterns and keywords associated with each category.

    4. Integration and Testing:

      • Integrate the trained GPT with your e-commerce platform or CMS. Test the model with new product data to ensure accurate categorization.

    5. Continuous Improvement:

      • Regularly update the training data with new products and adjust the model to accommodate changes in product lines or categorization criteria.

  • This custom GPT can streamline the process of categorizing products, ensuring consistency and saving time for your team.

title: Strategies explained
subcategory: TBR - Channel Added Value marketplace strategy framework
body:
In the next section, we will classify the following 4 strategies: brand protection, brand awareness, profitable revenue, and size opportunity.

Brand protection

You can protect your brand against competitors and substitute products by using brand-protection strategies. In a nutshell, a brand protection strategy safeguards a company's intellectual property against counterfeiters, pirates, and infringers. This is particularly useful for markets in which the brand has high brand awareness.


Brand awareness

To achieve becoming a well-known name in the market or region that you are selling in, brands can use brand awareness strategies. Brand awareness is the extent to which a consumer can correctly identify your brand based on visual indicators such as a logo and colours. 


Profitable revenue

This strategy can be used by brands that want profitable revenue on a specific channel or in a specific region.


Size opportunity

Brands can also make use of opportunity sizing strategies to create or find opportunities in foreign markets.

title: Zwangerschapsverlof en bevallingsverlof
body:
Zwangerschapsverlof

Wanneer: Het begin van je verlof bereken je door vanaf de dag na jouw uitgerekende bevallingsdatum 6 weken terug te tellen.

Je moet uiterlijk 4 weken voor de dag na je uitgerekende bevallingsdatum met zwangerschapsverlof. Wat je minder aan 6 weken zwangerschapsverlof opneemt, tel je op bij je bevallingsverlof.

Duur: 6 weken 

Salaris: De werkgever betaalt het salaris door tijdens het verlof.

Bevallingsverlof

Wanneer: Je bevallingsverlof gaat in op de dag na je bevalling. Het verlof duurt minstens 10 weken. Ook als je baby later dan de uitgerekende datum wordt geboren.

Sinds 1 januari 2015 kun je de laatste periode van je bevallingsverlof in delen opnemen. Het gaat om het verlof dat overblijft na 6 weken na de datum van de bevalling. Dit deel van het verlof kun je gespreid opnemen over een periode van maximaal 30 weken.

Duur: 10 weken 

Salaris: De werkgever betaalt het salaris door tijdens het verlof.

title: (Online) price strategies
body:
A pricing strategy is a model or method used to establish the best price for a product or service. It helps you choose prices to maximise profits and shareholder value while considering consumer and market demand. Having a price strategy is crucial. Without it, you risk putting your price too high, resulting in fewer sales. Putting your price too low, on the other hand, might result in your business not making any profit. Ultimately, getting your pricing right is the best way to improve your sales conversions. Knowing the optimum price you can charge for your product will ensure you maximise sales and help you to stay ahead of your competition. 

To help you get familiar with the different kinds of pricing strategies, we have listed some definitions of various online pricing strategies below.

Premium price
Premium pricing involves setting the price of a product or service higher than similar products on the market. This pricing strategy can be used to improve brand identity in particular markets because the high price signals to consumers that the product is of high quality. Premium pricing is not generally used when there is direct competition for a product because they tend to undercut prices. For this reason, premium pricing is often only a short-term strategy. Many products start out at premium prices, but the price is cut once competitors appear in the marketplace.

Low price (Low-cost strategy)
A pricing method in which a company offers a relatively low price to stimulate demand and gain higher market share. It is used when you're trying to undercut the competition with a low price and acquire customers quickly. 


Premium price, high discount (High-low strategy)
The brand/seller offers the product for a premium price with a permanent high discount. This pricing strategy is outdated and should only be used in very specific cases.


Competitor based pricing
A pricing method in which a company sets the price for its products after observing the competition. The term can be used in a broad sense to include any competitive strategy, including product design and marketing decisions, such as targeting niches where competition is low or creating a new product designed to compete with specific competitors.


Cost price based pricing
A pricing method that is based on the cost of production, manufacturing, and distribution of a product. Essentially, the price of a product is determined by adding a percentage of the manufacturing costs to the selling price to make a profit.


Dumping
Selling the product for an exceptionally low price to lose overstock. The general idea of this strategy is to sell exported products for less than a 'normal value'. The primary goal of this practice is to gain a competitive advantage over producers in foreign countries.

Entry strategies

The premise is to offer very low up-front prices to attract customers from competitors or in the open market. Often, short-term profit is limited, as your goal is to build a customer base.  Low entry price strategy (Market penetration strategy)
Penetration pricing is the pricing technique of setting a relatively low initial entry price, often lower than the eventual market price, to attract new customers. The strategy works on the expectation that customers will switch to the new brand because of the lower price.


High introduction, high discount
When entering a new market(place), you have to stand out from competitors as the brand is not yet known. This can be achieved by offering a high discount on the product.

title: Consultancy 2: Asana tasks
body:
In Asana, under 'Template' you can find the first template tasks for a.o.: search terms, bullets, titles, and ads (Missing a standard task? Let us know!).

The reason for this is that it takes less time for you to prepare a task, and in this way, we want to increase the quality that is delivered. All you have to do is copy and paste them and add the right files that are needed.

Once a task is on "needs check", it becomes your responsibility, as a consultant, to check these at random. 

When using these tasks, note the following:

  • Also, include the title of the task. It is important to fill it out correctly. This is important for reporting purposes.
  • If you are using a builder make sure you also copy the builder and edit the link. This way the builder template remains intact. 
  • Fill out all fields 
  • Check the estimation!

Recurring tasks:

Some tasks in Asana can be recurring tasks. A recurring task is something that needs to be done weekly or monthly.

 A perfect recurring task must have at least the following tags:

- An Assignee
- A repeating due date
- A recurring tag
- An estimate!
- 'Paid' field filled in


To make a task recurring follow these steps: 

  1. Click the task you want to make recurring 

  2. Click on 'due date' and click the ‘set to repeat button’

  3. Decide how often you want your task to be recurring and on what days (e.g. every Wednesday)

  1. If a recurring task has been completed, click the ‘mark complete’  button.

5. The task will be marked ‘completed’ and automatically a new task will be created. This will be assigned to the same factory specialist with the repeated due date (and with the same tags).

title: ChannelEngine Notification settings
body:
Channel Engine Notification Settings

Channel Engine has recently introduced new notification settings, allowing you to customize how you receive important updates in your client's account. To take necessary action for our clients, it is important that Brandsom effectively utilizes these tools on Channel Engine. For all clients using Channel Engine, a trainee consultant or integration specialist should review these settings and collaborate with the lead consultant to implement them.

To activate or modify these notifications, navigate to your account settings and select "Notification Settings."



Available Notification Categories

There are 14 different notification categories you can manage:

  1. Orders: notifications for failed order, backorders, late unshipped orders

  2. Products: notification for failed export/import product data/offer

  3. Returns: notification new return, refunds, return exports 

  4. Shipments: notifications for failed shipment export & import

  5. General: notifications from CE support, translations, messages

  6. Feed: notifications for failed feed import, new feeds 

  7. Plugin: notifications for plugin settings, early token authorization, validations

  8. Fulfilment: notifications for export & import fulfilment 

  9. Cancellations: notification for order cancellation export & import

  10. Stock: notification for failed stock update

  11. Custom VAT Rates

  12. Purchase Orders

  13. Purchase Order Shipments

  14. Purchase Order Invoices


Notification Delivery Options

With the new settings, you can choose from three options to receive notifications:

  1. Daily Digest via Email - Receive a summary of all notifications once per day.

  2. One Email per Notification - Get an individual email for each notification.

  3. Show in Notification Center - View notifications directly in your Channel Engine dashboard.

For critical notifications, we recommend selecting "One Email per Notification." For less urgent updates, the "Daily Digest" option is a good choice. The "Notification Center" is suitable for the least important notifications.


Customizing Notifications Based on Client Needs

The importance of each notification will vary depending on your clients’ needs, the services you provide, and the capabilities of their teams. We suggest advising your clients to activate relevant notifications in their accounts. For instance, if a client uses their own fulfillment services, it’s crucial for them to enable notifications related to fulfillment, stock, and orders.

Recommendations for Brandsom Teams 

For the services Brandsom provides, it's essential to have at least one team member receive notifications for each client concerning Feeds and Plugins. We specifically recommend using the "One Email per Notification" option for critical updates, such as plugin deactivation or expiration of plugin authorization. Please see the recommended settings for Feed and Plugin notifications below.


Final Steps

Remember to update the notification settings in each client's tenant according to their specific needs. Don’t forget to save your settings once you’ve made the necessary adjustments.

title: Employee Review Process (Probation, 6 months review and 12 months review)
body:
Employee Review Process at Brandsom

At Brandsom, we believe in a structured and transparent review process that ensures both individual and company growth. Our review process consists of three key stages: the Probation Period, the 6-month Review, and the 12-month Review. Each stage has its unique purpose and structure to help employees understand their progress, receive feedback, and align their personal and professional goals with the company’s expectations.

1. Probation Period (First Month)

Purpose

The probation period is the first month of an individual’s contract, designed as a mutual assessment period for both the employee and the company. This time allows both parties to evaluate whether there is a strong fit regarding the role, responsibilities, and company culture.

Key Elements

  • Adaptation & Learning: The employee familiarizes themselves with company processes, team dynamics, and role expectations.
  • Performance & Engagement: The company observes the employee’s engagement, skills, and ability to integrate into the team.
  • Feedback & Decision Making: At the end of the probation period, a review meeting is held to discuss the employee’s experience, performance, and whether both parties wish to continue working together.

2. 6-Month Review

Purpose

The 6-month review serves as a checkpoint to assess the employee’s progress and provide guidance on areas that require improvement before the 12-month review. Unlike the annual review, this is not a promotion discussion but rather an opportunity to reflect on progress and make necessary adjustments.

Key Elements

  • Function Profile & Responsibilities: Evaluation of the employees’ current performance based on their job role and competencies.
  • Personal Development Plan: Review of any professional development goals and necessary action steps.
  • Employee Satisfaction & Feedback: Open discussion about job satisfaction, challenges faced, and any support needed from the company.
  • Informal Setting: This meeting is conducted in a relaxed manner to encourage honest and constructive dialogue.

3. 12-Month Review

Purpose

The 12-month review is a formal evaluation of an employee’s yearly performance, contract status, and potential promotion. This review determines the next steps for career growth, salary adjustments, and continued employment.

Key Elements

  • Performance Evaluation: A detailed analysis of the employee’s contributions, achievements, and impact within the company.
  • 360-Degree Feedback: The manager collects input from peers and other team members to provide a well-rounded assessment.
  • Contract & Salary Discussion: Evaluation of potential contract renewal and possible salary adjustments.
  • Promotion Consideration: If applicable, discuss career advancement opportunities within the company.
  • Senior Management Involvement: The COO often joins this meeting to discuss strategic career planning and financial considerations.

Conclusion

At Brandsom, we value transparency, personal development, and mutual growth. Our structured review process ensures that employees receive clear guidance, constructive feedback, and opportunities for advancement. By engaging in these regular evaluations, we create a work environment where individuals can thrive, develop, and contribute meaningfully to the success of the company.

title: Amazon Main Image Optimization
body:
Amazon - Optimizing Product Content

Amazon Main Image Optimization

Main images are important for the Click-Through-Rate, which is the amount of people that click on your product after a search query. You want to grab the customer's attention by standing out in the search results. 

Amazon guidelines and main images

When optimizing main images on Amazon, you have to keep in mind their guidelines. There is quite a grey area which we can operate in, when optimizations are done right. Since it is a grey area, there is no rule set to follow. But in general:

  • If the optimization is done on the packing, or on the product, it usually passes

  • People are visually oriented, so you should use visuals as consumers process them faster.

Please don’t reinvent the wheel, there are usually a lot of competitor images you can use as your starting point.

What should be included in the main image?

 

Some basic examples you can use:

If you have color variations, you can include these in your image. Example:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

If you have size variations, you can try some of the following examples:

 

You can also be innovative, for example:

The first image shows the 'out of packing' product and uses a 'cup handle' to show the USPs of the product. In the second image, the brand has enlarged the label and combined them for two products into one single label, making it able to leave more information.

We have ran multiple tests on main images and noticed customers prefer to have informative main images. The main images can highlight product benefits or USPs, which should result in higher CTR and possibly also conversion. From the test you can also see that 'boring' main images are definitely not the way to go.

 

External assets

  • Main Image Optimization SOP by MyAmazonGuy
  • Youtube Video

title: Fulfilment by About You
body:
If you choose Fulfilment by About You, you remain the owner of your products and can still decide which products you offer and which you don't. The difference lies in the storage and logistical handling. Should you decide to use Fulfilment by About You, you store your products in the About You warehouse. The webshop takes care of all the logistics and any returns.

We have listed the advantages of Fulfilment by About You:

  • Lower storage costs - although there are expenses associated with Fulfilment by About You, in many cases, it can also save you money. By storing (part of) the products in About You's warehouse, you save money on storage fees.
  • Reduced shipping costs - choosing About You's fulfilment services can lead to reduced shipping costs. Due to the fact that About You is such a large organisation that offers large volumes, it has various agreements with transporters and parcel delivery companies, resulting in lower shipping rates. Having to pay less for shipping makes buying from About You appealing for customers.
  • Time-saving - stock management, packing and shipping of products, and handling of returns are taken care of by About You.
  • Space-saving - by storing your products in their warehouse, you can offer more products or even expand your range of products. You can store more products while needing less storage space.
  • Flexibility - peaks in the number of orders, during the holiday season, for example, are more easily dealt with.
  • Customer service - customers can make use of About You's customer service, which is available 7 days a week. This way, customers always have the possibility to get in contact with someone with questions or complaints about their order.
  • Knowledge and experience - efficient management of logistical order processing can be complicated. With Fulfilment by About You, this process is carried out by a company with the resources and knowledge to make this process run seamlessly.

title: Employment contract types
body:
At Brandsom, there are two types of employment contracts offered, being the full-time employment contract and the freelance contract. A full-time employment contract is a formal agreement between an employee and an employer, typically involving a set number of working hours per week (usually 36-40 hours), providing job security, a fixed salary, benefits such as paid leave and pension.

In contrast, a freelance contract is a more flexible arrangement where individuals work as independent contractors, often providing specific services for multiple clients without the long-term commitments of traditional employment. Freelancers have more control over their work schedule and projects but are responsible for their own taxes, insurance, and benefits.

title: Social sustainability
body:
Social sustainability has a focus on society and communities as a whole, with the addition that it considers the current activities and the impact they may have on future generations. Thus, it entails acknowledging that what we do now has an impact on others and improving social quality by focusing on relationships amongst people and how to improve them.

In a nutshell:

The principle of sustainable development addresses social and economic improvement that protects the environment and supports equality, and therefore the economy and society and the ecological system are mutually dependent (Diesendorf, 2000).

title: Zalando regulation
body:
• Zalando is a deemed supplier in the two cases only:
  •  A) If the Partner is not established in the EU and has no fixed establishment for VAT purposes in the EU and is fulfilling orders from an EU warehouse/stock location to an EU customer.
  • B) If the Partner is shipping goods from outside the EU directly to an EU customer based on a certain value. Please bear in mind that this sales model is not supported by Zalando at the moment.
  • I am a non-EU established partner and have a VAT registration in an EU country - does this mean I have a fixed establishment in the EU?
  • Not necessarily, it is possible to have a VAT registration and no fixed establishment, or place of business, in a country.

title: Extra information - working abroad (Addendum)
body:
In the working abroad addendum, the following will be discussed:


1. Time Zone Restrictions.

A) Employee agrees to work only in countries where the time difference with the Netherlands is a maximum of 2 hours. Employees will consider time zone differences and ensure they stay within the specified limit. The employee will work according to Dutch working hours, from 9 AM to 5 PM.

2. Professional Attire

Employees will maintain a professional appearance at all times during working hours, both in personal and virtual interactions. Employees shall not wear inappropriate clothing, such as the absence of a shirt or other inappropriate garments.

3. Coworking Spaces

For meetings and work-related appointments, should the Employee's remote workspace prove inadequate (e.g., lacking a stable internet connection), it is required that the Employee utilize designated coworking spaces in the foreign location. While the Employer offers facilities to accommodate such needs, in circumstances where the Employee's personal workspace does not meet professional standards, the responsibility to secure and finance a suitable coworking space falls solely on the Employee.

4. Background Music

An employee is not allowed to have background music during professional conversations, meetings, or other work-related activities. This could disrupt communication and be distracting to others.

5. Return at own Expense

Employee understands and agrees that if working abroad becomes infeasible for any reason, he/she is obligated to return to the Netherlands at their own expense and as soon as possible. Employer is not responsible for any costs or delays resulting from failure to comply with this obligation.

6. Liability Outside Working Hours

The employer is not liable for accidents, theft, or injuries occurring outside regular working hours.

7. Insurance and Responsibility

The employee is responsible for obtaining all necessary insurance required for the country in which he/she will be working. It's also mandatory to always work with a secure network, where the use of unsecured/public wifi networks is not allowed.  

8. Other Provisions

This Addendum supplements the existing employment agreement between Employer and Employee. All other provisions of the employment agreement remain in full force unless expressly stated otherwise in this Addendum.

9. Applicable Law and Dispute Resolution

This Addendum is governed by Dutch law. In the event of disputes arising from or related to this Addendum, Employer and Employee will attempt to resolve them amicably. If such a resolution is not feasible, disputes will be submitted to the competent court in the Netherlands. Employee acknowledges having read, understood, and agreed to this Addendum.

title: Marketing Plan
body:
At Brandsom we work for brands and retailers and set up and execute their online strategy. We apply the Digital Marketing Strategy into the Strategy plan of the company.

1. Company goals and strategy gathering

  • A) Define and collect: Company KPI’s and Critical Business Issues.
  • B) Collect or subdivide in goals per department or region: marketing / brand / sales strategy and goals for country X.

This is the starting point. Our job is to translate these company goals to the actual channels and markets we manage for the client. 

For example Boldking’s company goal is to: “Become the biggest subscription razors brand in Europe by having X% market share in x years”. Next step would be to potentially subdivide this into regional markets if they have seperate market goals. For example for Boldking they want to grow with X% in Germany. Finally we - Brandsom - set up their local channel goals: e.g. for Zalando DE.

Now, include the following into the strategy plan, and collect the answers from the client:

  1. Which region (and possible channels) is the brand interested in and focussing on?

  2. Which products and categories does the brand offer and in what categories is their growth potential?

  3. What’s the brand's sales and product strategy?

  4. What’s the main brand message they want to communicate to their end-consumers?

title: Lesson 5: HubSpot - Maximizing Efficiency in Outbound Sales
body:
HubSpot is a key tool for managing email outreach and automating follow-ups, which makes it indispensable in our outbound process. Here’s how to use HubSpot effectively to create, manage, and monitor email sequences for optimal results.


Creating and Enrolling Contacts in Email Sequences
    1.    Email Sequences    

           •    HubSpot allows you to set up email sequences, which are a series of automated                  emails designed to engage prospects over time.    

           •    These sequences handle follow-ups automatically, ensuring no leads are                              missed.    

    2.    Enrollment Process    

           •    Contacts can be enrolled into these sequences, triggering the automated emails                  according to the schedule.    

           •    Livia has created pre-built sequences you can start using right away. You can                      find them here: https://app.hubspot.com/sequences/5694025/?page=1&folder=77188427

If you are unable to see or use the sequences, please inform Livia so she     can adjust your user settings in HubSpot.


Monitoring and Evaluating Sequences
    1.    Performance Metrics    

           •    A sequence needs at least 200 enrolments to provide enough data to assess its                  effectiveness.    

           •    Key metrics to monitor include: reply rate, open rate, click rate

    2.    Reviewing Results    

           •    By clicking on a sequence in HubSpot, you can access a detailed page showing                  performance metrics.    

           •    These metrics are critical for understanding the campaign’s success and must                       be included in your weekly outbound Excel spreadsheet.


Weekly Tracking and Optimization
    •    At the end of each week, analyze your sequence results:    

         •    Are reply rates meeting expectations?    

         •    Is engagement improving with follow-ups?    

         •    Use these insights to refine your messaging and sequence strategy.


HubSpot Academy Resources
For further learning, we recommend the following HubSpot Academy courses:
    1.    Introduction to Email Sequences    

    2.    Understanding Email Engagement Metrics

    3.    How to Enrol a Contact


These materials provide advanced strategies and insights for improving your email outreach campaigns.


By leveraging HubSpot sequences effectively, you’ll be able to automate outreach, monitor success, and align your results with the weekly KPIs. If you have any questions or encounter issues with access, don’t hesitate to reach out to Livia.

title: From Profile to plan
body:
When you are aware of the possible DISC profile of your client or contact person, you'll be able to communicate better to their needs and motives and therefore keep them happier and meet their expectations. You’ll be able to evaluate the needed style and skills for the specific client. 


Imagine yourself writing a marketing strategy for a contact person with a “Stable S”: You’ll have to work step by step and very gradually, create trust and be sincere and structure the plan and conversations very clearly. Working with a “Controlling C” would require some space and room to fill in by the receiver, be very factual and not personal. A “Dominant D” needs a short, simple, powerful and full of confidence plan that is focused on results and goals. Ultimately the “Influencing I” doesn't like too many details, wishes to be liked, be positive and wants to be involved in the plan. 

Adjusting your plan to the profile of the receiver, and the profile of “Charlie” in the company, and including the CBI has the greatest impact and chance to succeed.

title: Cyber Security
body:
Cyber security is important because it protects all categories of data from theft and damage. To protect Brandsom's sensitive data and intellectual property from malicious people, we have made some easy-to-follow guidelines concerning cyber security.


Regularly perform software updates

Do not postpone updating your system (Windows/Mac) or your browser (Google Chrome). With updates, you fix vulnerabilities in your software.


Only use the following apps on your Brandsom laptop

  • Asana
  • Google Apps
  • Everhour
  • Hubspot
  • Pandadoc
  • Microsoft Office
  • Google Chrome (no other browsers)
  • Photoshop, Indesign
  • WeFact
  • Slack

If you currently have another tool, app or program on your Brandsom laptop, ask your supervisor for permission. All private and/or unnecessary apps should be removed immediately and not be used anymore.


Be wary of phishing mails
Do not just open any link in your email. Always check the sender. Stay alert and double-check everything, even when you're in a hurry.


Use secure passwords
Choose unique and difficult passwords. Use a password generator (e.g. LastPass). If you have your own LastPass account, use it. This way, you don't have to remember all the passwords as they are all secured in your LastPass vault. All you have to remember is the master password. Lastpass will be explained later on in module 3 of the Academy.


Set up two-step verification everywhere you can
Setting up two-step verification adds an extra layer of security to your accounts. Many client accounts already have this but it is also important to add it to personal accounts on Google applications and LastPass.


Use the Flextops
Working on a private laptop? Use a Flextop! They can be found in the office and can be used by anyone working for Brandsom.

title: Procces for applying
subcategory: How to apply for a German Vat (Dutch customers)
body:
Takes approximately 6-8 weeks. 
IMPORTANT: Brandsom is not a tax advisor nor do we work for a tax-authorised agency. Make sure you always clearly state this to any client BEFORE giving them any tax advice whatsoever!

In order to apply for a German Vat, for Dutch customers, you need to reach out to the Kleve tax office in Germany. Unlike in the Netherlands, where we have one KvK office, in Germany, these are spread out over different offices in different cities - for the Netherlands, this is Finanzamt Kleve

Postal address:

https://www.ag-kleve.nrw.de/kontakt/index.php

The process

In order to start the process you need to go to the Kleve tax website and apply, you can do that here: 

https://www.finanzamt.nrw.de/umsatzsteuer-fuer-niederlaendische-unternehmer

Based on your type of entity (Bv, VOF, eenmanszaak etc) you have different processes you need to follow. On the page, you can choose your own scenario and follow the steps. FOr example, for most of our customer option 2  - an online retailer from the Netherlands - will be the best choice.


Tips: 

- Sent all documents through physical mail NOT email

- Try to answer everything in German - Replying in English/Dutch can slow down the process a lot. 

- Make sure you fill in everything 100% correctly without any spelling mistakes or the process can take a lot longer

- Be polite and serious with the German tax office!

- Almost everything will be happening through physical mail, in case you have any urgent questions call them at Telefon: +49 2821 803-2427 (don't do this too often)

title: TradeByte Value mapping
body:
How does value mapping work?
The properties that you assign to products in the form of components and attributes, such as 'size' or 'gender', have certain values. Value mapping is assigning values to the properties. You can find a lot of detailed information about this process in the TB manual. You can find the manual under the menu top left → Infocenter → click 'Login' → click 'Manuals'. 

You begin with folders: 

  1. You select the channel you are mapping for, e.g. Zalando.be (main channel). Click on value mapping.  

  2. You will see two tabs, one with "Mapping" and one with "History". If you click on the History tab, you can find out how the values were mapped last time. It's useful to check this if you're not sure how certain properties are mapped, or if there's an error and you want to find out where it went wrong. Under the tab 'Mapping' you will do the value mapping.

  3. This is how you complete the list. Below you can see that property 'agegroup' is a mandatory field. That means that you ALWAYS have to map it! Now select the desired component or attribute and enter it correctly. Then click on 'save'. Important: you click 'save' after every change. This is how you complete the list.

title: Exploring Goals and Challenges
body:
Once you've set the tone and agenda for your exploratory sales call, you'll be able to use the CGP, TCI & BA framework to guide you through the rest of your call. In this chapter, we will talk about just the first part of it: CGP - challenges, goals, and plans.



First up, challenges. People generally don't make changes unless they have a challenge that obstructs their progress.

Many prospects will not respond well to things like:
"Tell me more about your pain points" or "Share all of your problems with me."
However, most prospects are open to sharing their challenges. A small but powerful wording adjustment can make all the difference. It's best to find multiple challenges for every prospect. It helps to make a list of all the challenges you solve most frequently for each of the personas, industries, or roles you sell to. This way, you can prompt your prospects. Here is an example of how you can bring these types of challenges to life proactively:

"When I hear someone say they have X issue, that often means they're frustrated with [challenge]. How about you?"


When you start probing for challenges, it's critical to employ active listening. This is a four-step process to not only ensure you've listened but also make sure that your prospect feels heard and understood.

The four steps to active listening are:

  1. Truly listen to the prospect
  2. Repeat the content and the feeling of the prospect's words
  3. Confirm you heard the prospect correctly
  4. Ask a relevant follow-up question to further clarify your understanding of their situation


At some point, you need to switch from talking about challenges to talking about goals. Most people love talking about their goals. If you've uncovered a handful of challenges, you can easily change that to talking about goals.

For example, you could ask:

"You've shared with me that X, Y, and Z challenges are most important. What goals are they preventing you from achieving?"


However you uncover goals, it's important to understand which challenges are preventing them from achieving which goals.

For example, ask something like:

"I'm wondering how much you think challenge Z is preventing you from achieving goal Z?"

The reason you need to talk about the goals you can help them achieve and challenges you can help them overcome is to get them thinking about the quantifiable business value you can provide. If you don't talk about goals and challenges, you won't express your value in that way and they may not directly tell you these things right away. Once the prospect has shared an important goal, quantify it.


The next step in CGP is for Plans. Once you have a good understanding of the prospect's most important goals, it's time to switch over to their plans. Before you start suggesting solutions to their challenges, it's important to know how they're already thinking of doing things now. Cover both the past and the future. 

For example, you could ask:

"How have you tried to overcome challenge X"
"Why didn't that work?"
"How do you plan to overcome challenge X in the future?"

At some point, you want your prospect to acknowledge that they don't have a good solution to their challenges and that they're afraid they won't achieve some of their goals as a result. Ask something like:

"Are you 100% confident that implementing this plan will help you achieve your goals?"

Then you want to ask them if they're open to new ways of approaching their situation that might be better by saying something like:

"Sounds like you have some doubt. Would you be open to new ways of solving your challenges?"


To recap, you need to help your buyers discuss their challenges, goals, and plans in that order specifically. Once you have this information, you'll be well-positioned to encourage them to take action.

title: Amazon Content Phase 1 (onboarding phase)
body:
Amazon Content Creation (Phase 1)

Introduction to Amazon content

Once you start creating Amazon content you must have a good understanding of the theory behind it. This way, you can make better decisions when you start writing the content for one of our brands. 


First, it is important to know the difference between indexing and ranking.

  • Indexing: When the product shows up in the search results (top 300 products)

  • Ranking: Your position in the search results for a specific keyword


Next, you should know the impact of the content and how this can be measured. All content elements support the findability of the product, but they impact different eCommerce metrics:

  • Product Title: Good product titles increase conversion rates. Consumers tend the click the product title that match their search behaviour. Therefore it is important to be selective in the order of the keywords in the title.

  • Bullet Points and Description: These content elements impact the conversion rate of the productpage. Therefore it is important to highlight the benefit of the customer. For example:

    • Reduce neck and back pain (clear benefit for the client)

    • Physiotherapeutically recommended (Expert opinion proves the benefit of the product)


Last but not least it is good to be aware of the various type of keywords we separate:

  • High Volume Keywords: High volume keywords that are generic and often highly competitive, for example: Cheese Slicer, Vacuum cleaner, Sweater

  • Long Tail Keywords: Keywords that are more specific and describing, often have less competition on them, for example: staubsauger beutellos 1000 bis 2000 watt / brotbackautomat ohne loch im brot


Amazon Honeymoon Phase

Sellers refer to the time right after a product launch on Amazon as the honeymoon period. New product listings can easily rank higher than normal for keywords on Amazon's search results during this period. Sellers who capitalize on the honeymoon period can get more visibility in front of shoppers during their product launch, boost sales, get reviews for their new listings, and eventually position their product for long-term success.


During this phase it is important to rank on very specific keywords so you can gain and maintain a high sales rank for these keywords. This is often supported with advertising or promotions, which you can read more about in the Amazon Launch Plan academy. 


The goal during this phase is to have a high conversion rate, this is done via longtail keywords which very specifically describe the product


Keyword Placement

There are a couple of placements within Amazon where you can put keywords, in this part of the academy we will walk you through all the different placements and the guidelines to keep in mind per content element. This academy covers the following elements:

  • Product Title

  • Bullet Points

  • Back-end Search Terms

  • Description

  • A+ Content


Product title

Amazon Title Guidelines can be found here.


An Amazon product title can contain a maximum of 200 characters, the best practice is to use as much of this space as possible. Using multiple search terms in the product title will increase the findability of the product. You want to put your most important keywords in the product title. More information regarding Amazon product titles can be found in this academy.


Bullet Points

Amazon bullet points guidelines can be found here.


In the bullet points you want to highlight the benefits of the product to the clients. The content in the bullet points is also being indexed by Amazon, therefore you should try to combine to express the benefits of your product with bullets points that include keywords. It is also important to focus on the benefit as mentioned earlier in the academy. More information regarding Amazon product titles can be found in this academy.


Back-end Search Terms

Amazon search term guidelines can be found here


Back-end keywords are not visible for the customers shopping on Amazon, but are used for indexation and ranking. Since they are not visible for the customers, you can use this field to add Polish or English keywords for example. Also keywords that are related to your product, but are not necessarily an exact match for your product can added to the search terms field. More information regarding Amazon search terms can be found in this academy.


Product descriptions

Amazon product description guidelines can be found here.


Product description are the least important if we look at the content elements from a ranking perspective. Therefore the product description is a great place to use keywords that are ment for indexing, and not as much for ranking. This are usually the keywords that have lesser search volume but are still highly relevant and competitive for example. More information regarding Amazon product descriptions can be found in this academy.


Amazon A+ Content

Amazon A+ content guidelines can be found here.


Amazon A+ content enables to brand owners to add enriched content to their product pages, highlighting the benefits of the product and the possibility to tell the consumer more about the brand. Within A+ content there is two options to place keywords:

  • In a text module, similar to a product description

  • In the alt-text of the images that are added to the A+ content


Since the text modules are showing, the keywords should also be relevant to the product. For alt-text this is not the case and are therefore similar to back-end search terms.


How to create a product listing that is ready for a product launch?

Now that you know everything about the placements and the theory behind Amaonz content, it is time to create the products listings and make sure they are ready for an amazing launch phase. 


Product Title

Step 1:

First, we need to have our focus products ready. These focus products should be determined by the consultant and should be discussed with the client. Once we have the list, we can start our research. This is starts with briefly reading through the descriptions of the products, this way you will be familiar with the products that you are going to optimize. You don’t have to remember everything, but make sure you scan and try to filter out the important information.


Step 2:

The moment is there, we are going to do our keyword research for the selected products. This is done in a couple in steps: 

  • First, you want to search for products that are similiair to the product we are creating content for. This can be done with a variety of search queries within Amazon itself

  • Select the product that are equal to your product and is performing well on Amazon. Put these ASINs in Cerebro

  • Now, for the first phase we want to select “Tier 1” keywords that are

    • Highly relevant for the product

    • Ideally have medium search volume 

    • Have low title density (<30)


Step 3:

Now that we have selected the most important keywords for our product title, we can start creating our first piece of content based on the best practices of Amazon Product Titles.

Bullet Points

The next step is to create converting bullet points for our products. As mentioned previously in this academy, bullet points are mainly focussed on conversion but are also an important placement to add keywords. Therefore we are going to select more keywords (tier 2) which we will use to write our bullet points. When creating bullet points, keep in mind the following:

  • Stick with a common theme, make sure that you translate the keywords which you are focussing on in the title into your bullets points

  • Write content that tells the client what it is solving for them, not just factual bullet points


Step 1:

First we have to decide on the most important features of the product, this is something that can also be discovered during the keyword research. Try to find keywords such as:

  • Vacuum cleaner for animal hair

  • Nonstick Frying Pan Dishwasher and Oven Safe


Try to focus your listing on the specific keywords ‘pain’ you are taking away from the customer.


Step 2:

Now that we have determined the focus of our product listing and the most important USP’s, we can start searching for more keywords that can be used in the bullet points. Hopefully, some of them were found in the proces as mentioned above. Try to aim for about 15-20 keywords in total which can be used to create bullet points.


Step 3:

Time to write the bullet points! And remember, try to solve the issue the customer is facing. You can check if you have used (almost) all keywords that you wanted to used with the Helium 10 Tool - ff uitzoeken (scribbles?). 

If any of the important keywords you have selected is still missing in the content, try to include them in the bullet points by re-writing your bullet points.



Back-End Search Terms

In the back-end search terms you can place the following keywords (when relevant)

  • Any miss-spellings you found during your keyword research

  • Any foreign languages such as English, Dutch or Polish. 

  • Other keywords that have not been used in the front-end content


When adding keywords make sure you avoid using double words. Also, the back-end search terms is supposed to be one long string of text so avoid the usage of any special characters such as comma or dashes.

title: Amazon EU Browse nodes
body:
To help customers find products, Amazon developed a detailed product hierarchy, known as the browse-tree structure. Customers refine their search by category links until they narrow it down to the desired product.


Amazon uses Recommended Browse Nodes, Product Description, Bullet Points and other detail page elements to classify your products under the correct categories. Amazon may assign multiple categories to an item, but only require one Recommended Browse Node from sellers.


An example of the browse-tree category structure on Amazon that is used for search navigation can be seen below.
Electronics > Electronics Bags & Cases > Laptop Computer Briefcases


An overview of the browse nodes can be found: Here

title: Creating a ticket in hubspot
body:
Every incoming lead needs to be registered and documented. We do this in Hubspot through the deals ticketing system. 


Go to contacts  > contacts to access the ‘all contacts overview’. Here you can register all new contacts and leads; Consultancy, potential leads, and Academy. 


Try to fill out the ticket as completely as possible. You will likely experience that with a new ticket you will be able to fill out limited information but as you make contact with the client, you will learn more about the company. It’s the idea that this ticket will be constantly updated when new information becomes available. 


Click on the top right corner on ‘create contact’ and fill out at least the following:


  1. Deal name (company name):

  2. Brandsom vertical:

  3. Deal owner

  4. Customer profile (amount of employees): 

  5. Lead Description: First question the lead writes to us. 

  6. Deal from: (likely website)

  7. Service: type of lead

  8. Deal Type: New/existing

  9. Customer phase:




Once contact is created in Hubspot  - sending reminders

In order to send out reminders, you need to access the new ‘deal’ profile. You can do this by clicking sales>deals> click the specific name of the company. e.g:


by clicking on the name ‘’pinkroses.nl - new deal’’:

You will then access this window: 



On the top left, you will see a menu that allows you to make notes and send out reminders.

To send out reminders click the ‘task button’ and click on the due date tab and click on  ‘1 week’ to send out a reminder.

title: Image Resizing
body:
Examples are for Zalando. If your work is for a different marketplace, please check relevant guidelines.

  1. open photoshop
  2. open a new file and set it as follows: 
    1. Format: best-practice ratio
    2. Resolution: minimal 72dpi
    3. Colourmode: RGB 8 bit 
    4. Colour profile:not important
    5. pixel ratio: square
  3. When this is done click →  cntr+o → click all your images and it creates tabs → then cntr a + cntr c + ctrl v to Zalando best practice
  4. You can now enlarge a photo if it does not fit (cntr t, or free transformation) but be careful the image may become blurred.
  5. → Save as (cntrl alt S). Save as jpg (no transparent background)


If the background is not the right colour, you must remove it, as follows 

  1. Remove the image from the lock
  2. Select the magic wand link is the menu (step 1)
  3. Set the tolerance (step 2). 255 is MAX and 0 and MIN, for an even background 30 - 50 should be sufficient.
  4. Select the background (step 3)
  5. Then in step 4, check that the edge of the object is still sharp  
  6. Click on Delete, now the background is removed  


Now you can add a background of the correct colour 
Choose a colour from the menu on the left, click on the coloured square. At RGB for example you fill in 255,255,255 (that is white).

  1. Click on the + at the bottom right 
  2. Select the bottom layer
  3. Then select the bucket on the left.
     
  4. Click anywhere in the image and now your background is white

title: List of questions for interviews
body:
It could be difficult to really understand a candidates previous experience or motivations. Especially for medior and senior candidates it's super important to select the right people.

Motivation to work in e-commerce:

  • What motivated you to pursue a career in e-commerce, and what do you find most interesting about it?
  • What do you think are the biggest challenges facing e-commerce businesses, and how do you think you can help them overcome these challenges?
  • What do you think are the most important skills for a successful e-commerce consultant, and how do you rate yourself on these skills?
  • How do you stay up-to-date with the latest e-commerce trends and technologies, and how do you apply this knowledge to your work as a consultant?
  • What do you think are the key metrics that e-commerce businesses should focus on, and how do you help your clients track and improve these metrics?
  • Can you describe your approach to developing and implementing an e-commerce strategy for a new client? What are the most important steps in the process, in your opinion?


Project management

  • Can you describe your project management experience in the e-commerce industry?
  • What is your approach to project planning and scheduling in an e-commerce context?
  • How do you monitor and track project progress, and what tools or methods do you use?
  • Can you provide an example of a successful e-commerce project that you have managed, and what role did you play in its success?
  • What challenges have you faced while managing e-commerce projects, and how did you overcome them?
  • How do you ensure that projects are delivered on time, within budget, and to the client's satisfaction?
  • Can you describe your process for identifying project risks and developing mitigation strategies?
  • How do you manage stakeholder expectations and communicate project status and progress?
  • How do you ensure that project deliverables meet quality standards and comply with best practices in the e-commerce industry?

Team management

  • Can you describe your experience managing e-commerce teams, and what is your approach to team management?
  • Can you provide an example of a team that you have managed and how you helped them achieve success?
  • How do you handle conflict or performance issues within your team?
  • How do you foster a positive team culture and ensure that team members are aligned with the company's mission and values?
  • How do you delegate tasks and responsibilities to team members, and ensure that they are properly trained and prepared for the work?
  • How do you manage teams with remote or distributed members, and what challenges have you faced in this context?
  • How do you manage cross-functional teams and ensure that different teams are working together effectively to deliver projects?


Amazon hard skills:

  • Can you describe your experience with Amazon Seller Central, and how long have you been working with the platform?
  • What is your process for setting up an Amazon Seller Central account for a new client?
  • How do you optimize product listings on Amazon Seller Central to improve search visibility and sales?
  • Can you provide an example of a successful Amazon Seller Central campaign that you have managed, and how did you measure its success?
  • How do you manage inventory and pricing on Amazon Seller Central, and what tools do you use to track this information?
  • Can you explain how you handle customer service inquiries and reviews on Amazon Seller Central, and what metrics do you use to measure customer satisfaction?
  • Can you describe a situation where you had to troubleshoot an issue with an Amazon Seller Central account, and how you resolved the issue?
  • How do you keep up-to-date with changes and updates to Amazon Seller Central, and what sources do you use to stay informed?
  • How do you collaborate with clients and other stakeholders when managing Amazon Seller Central accounts, and how do you ensure that everyone is aligned with the project goals and strategies?

title: Environmental sustainability
body:
Last but not least: environmental sustainability. This pillar has as its main goal to improve human conditions by preserving as much as possible natural resources (e.g. land, air, water, minerals etc.). Environmental sustainability has as main purpose to develop  Initiatives and programs to meet the needs of the current population without compromising future generations. 

In a nutshell:

Environmental sustainability, as described by Dunphy, Benveniste, Griffiths and Sutton (2000), places emphasis on how business can achieve positive economic outcomes without doing any harm, in the short- or long-term, to the environment. According to Dunphy et al. (2000) an environmentally sustainable business seeks to integrate all four sustainability pillars, and to reach this aim each one needs to be treated equally.

title: Extra information - working abroad
body:
At Brandsom, we believe in providing flexibility to our employees. That's why we allow our team members to work abroad for up to 30 days per calendar year. To ensure a smooth experience, we have established a few guidelines and requirements that must be met before you embark on your overseas work journey.

1. Eligibility to work abroad

To work remotely from abroad, employees must follow these steps:

  • Consult with Your Manager:

    • Email your plans to your manager, covering the following details:
      • Timing: When do you plan to go abroad?
      • Destination: Where do you plan to work from?
      • Duration: How long do you intend to stay abroad?

  • Manager and Team Lead Approval:

    • Managers should share the discussed information with their team lead. Together, they will evaluate:
      • Timing Suitability: Is it a good time for the employee to go abroad considering promotions, major events, new clients, new team members, or other team members on leave?
      • Requirement Compliance: Does the employee meet all the requirements outlined in the addendum?
      • Performance Review: Is the employee performing according to expectations?
      • Time Logging: Has the employee been accurately logging their hours?

2. Pre-departure requirements

Before you can work abroad, ensure the following:

  • Sign the Addendum: An addendum detailing the terms and conditions must be signed (see the next module for details).
  • Agreement: There must be a mutual agreement between the lead, manager, and employee regarding the remote work arrangement.
  • Time at the company: You must be employed for a minimum of 6 months at Brandsom before being entitled to work abroad.

By adhering to these guidelines, we aim to provide a structured and efficient process for our employees to enjoy the benefits of working abroad while maintaining productivity and team cohesion.

For any further queries, please refer to the addendum or contact Joris. Happy travels!

subcategory: What can Brandsom do for its clients?
body:
Considering all these guidelines provided by the main marketplaces we work with can be a good starting point to offer insightful advice to our customers and spread awareness on how they can become more conscious brands and therefore step up their game within the current fashion industry. 


To sum it all up Consultants can keep the following steps in mind when it comes to starting a more sustainable journey with current and new potential clients:

  • Bear in mind what sustainability means and the main pillars that characterize it.
  • Inform yourself, via several publications within the e-commerce industry, about the new developments and challenges the fashion industry is facing in order to provide our clients with solid updates and reasons why they should be more sustainable.
  • Stay up to date with the latest trends on marketplaces themselves and new ways to make our brands stand out among all the products available on marketplaces (e.g. check new Zalando and/or Bol sustainability criteria etc)
  • Include sustainability intentions and goals into the clients’ year plan (e.g. acquiring a new label by March 2023). 
  • Set into Asana as a standard task requesting to make visible sustainability labels visible on Zalando and send requests.
  • Bol packaging

title: Company culture
body:
You can find our office at (second floor of the Synderella building):

Vliegend Hertlaan 21
Rooms 210/211
3525 KT Utrecht
The Netherlands


Office opening hours
The office is open between 7 AM and 7 PM.


Workplaces
All workstations, except those in the ‘210/211’, are flexible workstations. An external screen, mouse, keyboard, and laptop stand are available for everyone. Try to keep these sets together as much as possible. Please tidy and clean the workspace after usage so the next person can use it. 


Meeting rooms
The office has two rooms for meetings: ‘210’ and ‘211’. These spaces can be reserved via Google calendar. There is also the option to work downstairs, in the boardroom on the second floor, the call booth or the shared space outside our office. 


Noise
If there is no meeting room available, please use headphones during calls. Try to make as little noise as possible in the office. Short work-related requests are always allowed, but for long (telephone) calls, please use the rooms outside of the office.


Groceries
Grocery shopping for the office is arranged by the office manager (Paula). You can let this person know if you have any special requests or when something is no longer available in the office.


Working from home

At Brandsom, it is possible to work from home. If the employee wishes to do so, they have to comply with the following:

  • The employee must ensure that they can be contacted by telephone, e-mail, and/or Slack while working from home.
  • The employee takes sufficient measures at home to minimise security and privacy risks. 
  • The work at home days have been discussed and agreed by your manager. 

Brandsom provides resources such as a mouse and keyboard to ensure a good workplace. It may happen that certain work cannot be done at home. Make clear arrangements about this with your supervisor.


Working hours

A working week is from Monday till Friday. At Brandsom, we mainly work between 9 AM and 5 PM. It is possible to work flexible hours by working in the evening or weekend. However, in consultation with the manager, the working hours should be determined for the optimal occupation of the department. The department must be staffed between 9 AM and 5 PM.


Monthly update

The Brandsom Monthly Update will take place every first Friday of the month. During this presentation, employees are informed about all developments within Brandsom. Attendance is mandatory for anyone that's working during those times, it takes place from 1630-1700. If you are working from home, you can also follow the presentation online.


Social activities

Achieving good results goes hand in hand with a pleasant and collegial working environment. Therefore, we organise social activities such as:

  • Drinks after the monthly update.
  • A fun activity for employees once a month.


Code of conduct

We follow a code of conduct within Brandsom. More information on our code of conduct can be found in the extra information on 'Code of conduct' at the end of this handbook.


Sanctions

By signing your employment contract, you also agree with the conditions of this handbook and what is included in the extra information at the end. We trust you will comply with our conditions in order to work for Brandsom in a pleasant way. If this is not the case, we are forced to impose sanctions. This could give reason to proceed to suspension and even instant dismissal. The violation of one of the above conditions gives immediate cause to assume that there are serious grounds for suspending the employee and even dismissing them with immediate effect.
