title: What can we do?
body:
It is clear that to reduce the impact of this industry it is necessary to take concrete steps and embody sustainability within the current fashion scene. Explore some of the many dimensions of sustainability in the fashion industry, and learn how people use fashion to inspire and affect positive social change.


In more practical terms,  these are some of the main principles we can keep when we talk about more sustainable fashion and the processes that entail it:

  • Avoiding  harm to people by guaranteeing better working conditions to the workers and using higher quality materials that are healthier in the long run for both the environment and consumers.
  • Avoiding harm to the planet by using less natural resources, creating more durable products, making products more circular. 
  • Enhancing the well-being of people who interact with it
  • Enhancing the well-being of the environment in which it is developed and used

title: Chapter 2: GPT-01 – The Lightweight AI Assistant
body:
What is GPT-01?

GPT-01 is a smaller, more streamlined model designed for efficiency. While it’s still intelligent, it’s optimized for handling simpler requests quickly.

What Type of Requests Work Best?

  • Quick Information Retrieval: Need a fast response on best practices for Amazon SEO? GPT-01 can deliver straightforward answers.
  • Short-Form Copywriting: Ideal for product descriptions, email subject lines, or brief social media captions.
  • Basic Summarization: If you have a long article and just need a key takeaway, this model works well.
  • Workflow Automation Assistance: It can help with Zapier automation logic, offering guidance without deep technical complexity.

Limitations

  • Less nuanced than ChatGPT-4o when handling deep, complex queries.
  • Doesn’t perform as well with advanced reasoning or long contextual discussions.

title: Chapter 3: GPT Operator – The Custom AI Engine
body:
What is GPT Operator?

GPT Operator is a custom AI model fine-tuned for specific business needs. It can be tailored to internal documentation, SOPs, and proprietary data, making it highly specialized for a consultancy agencies.

What Type of Requests Work Best?

  • Internal SOPs & Training: Colleagues can ask it company-specific questions, like "What’s our standard process for onboarding a new marketplace client?"
  • Automation & Workflow Execution: If integrated into Slack or Zapier, it can trigger actions like sending emails or fetching reports.
  • Highly Specialized Insights: Unlike ChatGPT-4o, which relies on public data, a GPT Operator can answer based on company knowledge.

Limitations

  • Requires setup and training with company data.
  • It’s not a general AI like ChatGPT-4o—it’s only as good as the data it has been trained on.

Final Thoughts

Each model has its own strengths and ideal use cases:

✅ ChatGPT-4o – Best for advanced reasoning, brainstorming, and in-depth content.
✅ GPT-01 – Best for quick responses and simple requests.
✅ GPT Operator – Best for company-specific knowledge and automating internal processes.

By understanding these differences, your team can use AI more effectively and optimize daily workflows.

title: Marketing & Branding
body:
Although often mentioned interchangeably, marketing and branding are two separate things. Branding precedes marketing. Think of marketing as the actions you take to connect with your customers and to get them to buy your products or services. Branding, on the other hand, is more internalized. It is the marketing practice of actively shaping your brand. Branding is about defining who you are as a company. Defining your brand is paramount to the success of your marketing efforts and your business in general. Marketing gets a customer's attention, branding is a way to keep their attention.


Marketing and branding both aim to attract customers to a business to ultimately grow that business. They are two different components of a business. However, they both rely on each other and both contribute to the success of the other when applied correctly. You need strategies for both and they have different goals and different results. 



Branding and pull strategy

Again, branding is about defining who you are as a company. It's your mission, your values, and what makes you special and unique. It's your key brand elements, like your logo, your website, and your brand style guidelines. It gives your marketing content and context. Branding needs to be clear, concise, and consistent. Staying consistent in all aspects of your brand will allow for recognition and makes it easier to build a solid reputation. With branding, you try to make people fans of your company.


A pull strategy is based on creating value for the customers. The logic of a pull strategy states that if you create enough value for a product or brand, the customers will come to buy the product themselves. They will choose the product above competitors because they see more value in the product.


Using a pull strategy, the producer directs its marketing activities (primarily advertising and consumer promotion) toward final customers to persuade them to buy the product. It is a strategy aimed at the consumer. The goal is to create loyal customers by providing marketing materials that showcase what they're looking for. The intention is for the consumer to demand that a product be available in the distribution channel. It is about promoting a product directly to consumers to stimulate strong consumer demand, which 'pulls' the product into the stores.


Marketing and push strategy

Marketing makes up the collection of activities and tactics brands use to place their messages into the market with the goal of influencing the audience to buy. It is more focused on sales than branding is, and is based on a shorter timeframe. It almost always has a call to action to buy or to step towards a buy. The primary goal of marketing is to generate interest in the product or service. Marketing is the act of promoting a brand in the market to drive sales.


A push strategy, also called a push promotional strategy, refers to a strategy in which a company attempts to take its products to consumers - to 'push' them onto consumers. The goal is to use various marketing techniques to push their products to be seen by as many consumers as possible. Push strategies are commonly used to gain and increase product exposure and rely mainly on traditional advertising and marketing. Again, the primary goal is simply to make as many consumers as possible aware of the product and its benefits. Push refers to the fact that the company that sells the product is continually pushing it into the potential customer's scope.

title: HubSpot Introduction
body:
HubSpot introduction

HubSpot is a very extensive CRM system, which we use for multiple purposes. Such as:

  • It hosts our website
  • All sales leads are centralized here
  • It contains all client information

Of course, not everything is relevant for everyone. Therefore, in this academy, we dive into the most important functionalities. 

HubSpot Dashboard

You can find the dashboards via Reporting & Data > Dashboards. Every team has their own dashboard, which includes:

  • Overview of all team members, including their position
  • Client overview, with current consultant and integrator
  • Promotion Calendar - So you are always up-to-date regarding the most important events
  • Looker Studio Reporting - This enables you to dive into your clients' performance.

Contacts

This overview can be found via CRM > Contacts. Here you will find three views:

  • All Contacts
    • This shows an overview of all the contacts we have ever been in touch with. This overview will most likely not be relevant for you
  • Brandsom Employees
    • Overview of everyone that is working at Brandsom, including their position and contact details
  • Brandsom Partners
    • Here you can find the contact persons for our partners. For example, our Zalando account manager, or the contact person for Staxxer if you need to reach out for a VAT / EPR question.

You can add views via 'Add View' just above the table on the right side.

Companies 

This overview can be found via CRM > Companies. Here you will find multiple views:

  • All companies
    • All companies we have ever been in touch with. This is not relevant.
  • All Brandsom Clients
    • Overview of all active Brandsom Clients
  • Overview per team
    • There is also a tab per team, where you will find a list of all active clients per team. 

 All views are updated automatically. Therefore it is important to keep the information for your clients up-to-date.

Lists

You can find this overview via CRM > Lists. You will find multiple lists, which can be easily found via 'Folders' > Team Folders on the very right. Lists that are currently set:

  • Lists based on integrator
  • Lists based on key marketplaces (Amazon, Zalando, Bol)
  • All Brandsom Clients list.

These lists are also used to create bulk tasks, which is something currently done by the leads within Brandsom. This is to make sure everything works correctly before we implement it for everyone. 

If you have any questions or feedback related to Hubspot, please reach out to imco@brandsom.nl so we can keep improving!

title: MerchantSpring - Quiz
body: Error

title: What causes error 99010?
subcategory: Amazon Error Solving SOP Overview - internal
body:
What causes error 99010?

This error occurs when there are missing or conflicting values in one of the conditionally required columns. For example, the three following columns are a group and are conditionally required to be meaningful: 1) sale_price 2) sale_from_date 3) sale_end_date. When the value of at least one attribute in a group is missing, the record is rejected.

Samples:

ResultMessageCode:99010


ResultDescription: A value is missing from one or more required columns from this group: [Message/Price/Sale/SalePrice = "1868.00"], [Message/Price/Sale/StartDate = "0014-11-17T00:00:00Z"], [Message/Price/Sale/EndDate = "0014-11-17T00:00:00Z"]. 

How to solve and prevent error 99010?

To resolve this issue, ensure all attributes of a conditionally required group are populated with valid values.


To prevent this error, your solution should check ‘Data Definition’ and ‘valid values’ tab of category specific flat files and add such checks before uploading.


A few examples of conditionally required fields include:

  1. If you are provide Sale Price, you also must provide Sale start date and sale end date. -

  2. If you provide sale price along with sale start date and end date, but your date format is incorrect (i.e. DD_MM_YYYY), you’ll receive an error

  3. If you are provide product_id, you must also provide product_id_type

  4. If you provide ‘Parentage’, you must also provide ‘variation theme’.

title: Zalando Error Analysis
body:
For error analysis on Zalando we use an Excel file in which all products are listed and the analysis is made. The structure of this file can be found here: 
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-Pyr8oVgHXVdzq99gnqAViYVu49jWU7S5XMvCfmwZO0/edit#gid=0

  • By downloading this each time as Excel and saving it in the google drive of the customer, you also have the possibility to keep the old overviews.
  • Create a separate folder per season in the customer folder.
  • Put the most recent Excel in here 
  • Move the old file to a separate 'Old' folder

How to perform Error analysis?

Always start the analysis with the Zalando errors and then work backwards in the process (so if a product is not on ZDirect, check if there is an error in TB, or if there is already something known about missing pictures/information).

1. Download all errors from the main channel of the customer on Zdirect (filter for example on Germany).

2. Then update the following columns in the Analysis document in the tab: EAN status update
- Move the column new Zalando status to "old zalando status"

- Then update the Zalando status using a V-lookup with the EANs 

  • Remember to save this column as text (otherwise the data might dissapear when you open the document again)
  • Put a filter on the new Zalando status, Everything with a #NB status is not visible on Zdirect. This means there is something else goiing on with the EAN, you need to check for errors in the integrator. 
  • Add a status for these #NB products (For ASICS this is "stuck in TB"). This way you keep your overview clear.

- The column solutions will update itself (if you leave the V-lookup)

  • Solutions are already in there with a formula to another tab, here are the learnings per error (so for this error you need to contact zalando, change something in the materials etc.)

- Update the Zalando SKU's (with V-Lookup). 

  • You do this because not all products will get a SKU assigned right away because of possible errors.
  •  If you want to see which SKU's are new, create a new column and do a V-lookup for the SKU's. By using a filter you can see which SKU's were "0" or "#NB" last week, and have a SKU this week.

3.Then update the pivot table in the Zalando Update tab. 

  • If you are creating the table for the first time, then put Solutions and the number of EANs on the axes. This way you also have a handy overview of the type (and number) of errors for the customer.

4.Next, create an error overview specifically for all product numbers (customer SKUs).

  • Filter the new error status, only leave the errors (you can exclude stock errors, Stuck in TB, and all live products) 
  • Copy the Client SKU's, Zalando SKU's and new error status to a new tab (or replacre the data in the last product number overview.
  • Deduplicate the data
  • Now you can compare the current errors with the google sheet error file, and add the new errors to make sure that these will be solved.

5. If there are products which are not yet visible on Zalando (i.e. #N/B appears when you did the V-lookup for Zalando errors) then download the error overview from the TB solution centre (or a similar overview from another integrator). See if there is an error stopping this product from being pushed to Zalando. 

6. If you can't find any errors in the integrator, go to the product itself and check everything:

  • Check that all necessary data has been added/mapped
  • Check if the product (and articles) have been activated for Zalando
  • Check if the price is added

title: Touch, Tell, Sell, Care model
body:
Define the marketing strategy and customer journey based on the traditional or reversed touch, tell, sell, care framework. 

Answer these steps first:

  1. What is the main goal of the brand or retailer? And what is the current situation? How far away from the goal are we? For example:

    1. The customer currently sells 100k and wants to grow to 110k next year.

    2. The customer has 0% market share and wants to be a top 5 player within 3 years.

    3. The customer currently has 500k revenue and wants to grow to 5 mln in 3 years.

> Don’t forget to make your goals SMART.

  1. Does the brand or retailer already use the (max) potential of PPC/Conversion (for example sponsored products) in the current ads? To answer this question, analyze the current media assets and results.

  2. In general, the more ambitious, the higher the budget/investment in amongst others marketing tools. However, be aware that investment in technique, stock and assortiment or logistics can be crucial. What’s the current development stage for your brand?

Later, we refer to budgeting. 

  1. The phase the brand or retailer is already in. The most common approach is to start at the sell phase, to gain traffic, revenue and customer insights.  When you have solid revenue and notice that PPC ads are becoming less efficient if you increase budget and bids, it’s time to move up in the funnel (to tell or touch) to increase the reach. Where is the brand or retailer now and what phases are we focusing on?

  2. The assortment to start with. Starting with a wide assortment creates the advantage to collect more data, however requires more budget in the beginning. Focussing on select best selling articles will make sure to create highly likely successful ads right away. Or a combination of both. What’s the strategy, and do we make a proposal or in consultation with the client?


Collect all information: Company Goals and KPIs, channels, assortment, marketing potential,, brand message… before you start filling out the actual plan.

title: What causes error 6039?
subcategory: Amazon Error Solving SOP Overview - internal
body:
What causes error 6039?

This error occurs when you are not authorized to sell products under this restricted product group. The restricted product class for the item being offered is not open for selling under this condition.

Sample:


ResultMessageCode: 6039


ResultDescription: Merchant is not authorized to sell products under this restricted product group.

How to solve and prevent error 6039?

Your solution should create list of restricted products and provide it to the seller. The seller can then login to Seller Central to request approval to sell in the specified category. To request approval, either contact Seller Support or follow the steps below:


  1. In Seller Central, click the inventory link and select ‘Add a Product’.

  2. Run a search for the item you wish to sell.

  3. In the search results, click the Listing limitations apply link across from the item.

  4. Click the Request Approval button to begin the application process.


To check the status of an application, return to the Add a Product tool and click the Selling application status link located toward the top of the page. For more details go to https://sellercentral.amazon.co.uk/gp/help/200333160.

title: Ouderschapsverlof
body:
Wanneer: Je kan ouderschapsverlof opnemen voor ieder kind tot 8 jaar. Bij een tweeling heb je recht op 2 keer ouderschapsverlof. Je mag voor meerdere kinderen tegelijkertijd ouderschapsverlof opnemen. 

Duur:  26 x aantal werkuren per week per kind.
Je kan meenemen van oude werkgever naar nieuwe. Indien werkuren veranderen dan moet er opnieuw berekend worden op hoeveel recht je hebt. Deze berekening wordt hier uitgelegd. 

Salaris: Je werkgever hoeft het salaris niet door te betalen tijdens ouderschapsverlof. 

Let op! Vanaf 2 augustus 2022 kan je tijdens het eerste levensjaar van je kind een UWV-uitkering krijgen van 9 weken. Dit is 70% van je dagloon. 


Bunnig: Er is geen mogelijkheid om dit als extra soort verlof in Nmbrs te zetten. Mocht hier ooit gebruik van gemaakt worden dan moet dit aan Bunnig doorgegeven worden. Zij maken dan de benodigde aanpassingen

title: Video concept Brandsom
body:
Videos have been a new addition to our marketing plan. It is important that whoever is in charge of this process knows what is expected. We at Brandsom mainly focus on distributing our videos on Youtube and LinkedIn. It is important to analyze which videos are suitable for these platforms. It is up to you to research what is useful for Brandsom, what could lead to conversion, and what is trending within the market. Once you have composed an idea for a video, you can start with the process of how to create such a video!

 

Firstly it is important to actually create the video prior to distributing it. We advise you to download a free copy of Davinci Resolve 17. This is the easiest and most well-featured software for beginners and does not require a lot of time to learn. If in case you are more familiar with editing videos feel free to use any other software of your choice! To get started with Davinci please watch the tutorials mentioned at the beginning of setting up the application on your laptop, or visit YouTube and watch some beginner tutorials.

 

Now that you have become a bit more familiar with the software, you can start editing your videos! Initially, most of the videos you will need to edit in Davinci require little to no experience for them to be edited for distribution. All you have to do is add the pre-made animations on top of the video and you can already export the video! 

 

As you are in charge of the YouTube and LinkedIn distribution you will need to schedule weekly meetings with our Visual creator Timo. In this meeting you will discuss strategy, and which animations would be suitable for the video! In most cases, you will obtain a rough version of the video from whoever recorded it and he or she will send it to you for final editing and distribution.

 

To summarise you are basically going to just add the animation created by Timo on top of the video in Davinci and export it!

 

Now that your video is rendered let's look at how it should be distributed.

title: LinkedIn

title: New content assignement
body:
Content is one of the most important aspects of marketplaces and it's important to manage the content process precisely. Once there is a new content assignment, follow this academy to make sure everything is covered. 


Gathering information from a client

Once there is a new assignment, we need to request some basic information from the client. They always need to provide us with some basic information, such as:

  • Title

  • Description

  • Most important attributes

  • EAN code and/or SKU


To make sure the content is in line with the style of the brand, you can also request the following documents:

  • Most important brand USP’s

  • Brand Book / Content style guide

  • What writing style does the brand use?


The more information we can gather regarding the brand and its content, the better we can create content that meets the needs of the brand.

Template information request

Hi [name client],
As already discussed, we will be writing content for the agreed products. To ensure that the content we write fits well with the image of [brand], I would like to ask you to answer the questions below:

- What kind of writing style does [brand] use? Is it formal or informal?

- Is there a Brand Book available?

- Are there any USPs that should not be missing from the content?

To start writing content, we need at least the information below:

- Basic title- Basic description

- Key attributes

- EAN / SKU


Should there be more relevant information, it never harms to share it with us. The more information we have, the better we can write the content in your house style.
Once the content is written by our specialists we will discuss this with you, this way we can be sure that the content is in line with the corporate identity of [brand] and that any changes can be made.


If there are any questions or remarks about this, please let me know.


Information received from ‘content information’ mail must be shared with the team

  • Use your weekly team meets and Asana/drive client projects to also save the information.

  • Once all information is received and processed it’s time to plan with the content specialist and inform about the specific project. (Use template Asana tasks!)

title: Fulfilment by Amazon (FBA)
body:
Fulfilment by Amazon (FBA) is a service that helps businesses grow by providing access to Amazon's logistics network. To help you understand what the service offers, we have listed some of the benefits below:

  • Stock is sent to Amazon fulfilment centres and Amazon then handles orders, shipping, customer service, refunds and returns.
  • An overview of the stock can be found in Seller Central, the web interface used by brands and merchants to market and sell their products directly to Amazon's customers. This makes keeping track of stock easy.
  • There is neither a minimum nor a maximum on the number of products eligible for FBA. Sellers can decide for themselves how many products they want to outsource for fulfilment. 
  • Since the fulfilment centres are located in the customer's country, delivery is often fast and within one day.
  • Amazon has fulfilment centres in Germany, Italy, France, Poland, Spain, the Czech Republic and the United Kingdom.
  • Shipping is free for Amazon Prime users. They receive a prime badge on the product page for FBA products.
  • A fixed rate per product unit, based on the product type, dimensions and weight. Storage fees are calculated per cubic foot per month.
  • Please find slide to present the creation of a FBA shipment here.

title: What is Collabary?

The platform discovers the perfect social media influencers for brands; these are the voices that can bring an influencer-led campaign to life, and reach an audience that will want to listen. In addition to this, Collabary tracks the results of every campaign to help brands tell more stories that matter. 

You can find an overview of Collabary tutorials & information here.

title: MerchantSpring - Onboarding your new client
body:
In this academy, we will walk you through the steps that you need to perform when a new client starts at Brandsom. 

Step 1: Adding the client to MerchantSpring

First, we need to add the client via MerchantSpring, click on "Add Channel" in the top right corner and follow the steps. Make sure to also add the advertising account for the marketplaces available. You can check the most recent overview in the MerchantSpring academy. Keep in mind the following:

  • When you connect the advertising account, it asks you if you want to create a "standalone account". This is not necessary so leave this box empty.
  • When connecting an Amazon client, you most likely try to connect the account via your personal account which has multiple Amazon accounts. Therefore always make sure you have the correct account selected in the pop-up window!
  • For Zalando Clients:
    • Make sure to connect Zalando EU, instead of all channels
    • You can still add the most important countries if you want to report specifically on these channels. 
  • Keep your client's naming convention identical to that of Asana. There is no need to add the country behind the connection.

Step 2: Create a Tag

Create a "tag" for the client you added, this makes sure you can see the reports in MerchantSpring. Tags are created via "Account" (top right corner), and then go to "Channels". Keep the Tag name identical to the client name.

Step 3: Add the sales targets per channel [More information in this academy]

Via Channels > Sales Targets you can set the discussed targets per channel. This is done per month. 

Step 4: Create standardized reporting [More information in this academy]

For every client we set the standardized reports, you will find all the information you need in the linked academy. Make sure the reports are set via customers@brandsom.nl so we can keep an overview of all clients that are receiving automated reports.

Step 5: Marketplace Specifics

  • If the client is selling on Amazon, make sure to toggle the Review Automation via "Review Management". You can adjust after how many days the request is sent, the standard is 5 days which works for most clients.

title: Bol Description Builder
body:
Bol Description Builder

For Bol we have developed an AI Tool that is able to create descriptions in bulk. There is some complexity to this, therefore we have divided a Bol description into three sections:

  • Introduction about the products
  • A list of up to 5 USPs and a paragraph explaining the USP a bit more in-depth 
  • List of specifications

The "About the Brand" part, which is always how we close Bol descriptions, should be created yourself and added at the end. This is to guarantee consistency in the brand description. 

The following information should be provided to create Bol descriptions:

  • SKU / EAN / Or any other identifier - This will make your life easier once the file is ready. You can use the identifier to vlookup
  • Descriptions - The better the description provided, the better the output will be. Not all clients have extensive descriptions, in that case we work with what we have.
  • Template for all three parts
    • Intro Template: The introduction about the product, should be about 300 characters
    • USP Template: Starts with a list of 5 USPs followed by a paragraph per USP
    • Specifications Template: List of specifications

Examples can be found in the attached template.

If you want to use the tool to create descriptions, send a request to Imco@brandsom.nl with a populated template. The template can be found in the attachments of this course.

NOTE: Do not change the headers in the template, they have to be identical for the tool to work!

title: Bol Campaign Naming Convention
body:
Bol Campaign Naming Convention

Naming conventions are super important in advertising because they keep everything organized and easy to find. When everyone uses the same names for campaigns and files, it’s a lot simpler to track what’s going on and see what’s working. It helps teams work together better since everyone knows exactly what’s being talked about. Plus, it keeps the brand looking consistent across different ads and platforms. Clear naming also helps avoid mistakes like using the wrong assets or misplacing budgets. Overall, good naming conventions make advertising more efficient and effective.

Bol naming conventions


You can use this naming convention template to make it more efficient!

body:
This academy will assist you in developing an effective meeting cycle for your customer. The aim of this academy is to create efficient and effective client communication.

MEETING CYCLE:

The Meeting Cycle with a client, we meet a client:

  • Biweekly
  • Monthly

Please click on the meeting type to open the template that you can use for this type of meeting. Please note: COPY and save into the client folder a new version of the template, to not overwrite the existing one.

title: Introduction
body:
One of the most important parts of working at Brandsom is fulfilling our client's needs. This is simply because they are the reason we get to work. Therefore, you want to build a strong working relationship with the client. To achieve this,  good communication is necessary to figure out their goals and needs and to let them know what we can do to help. After all, you want to keep the client satisfied. How do we do this? And what exactly is good communication and how do we keep it businesslike?

One way of communicating is by e-mail. Another frequently used option is calling the client by phone. It is very important to write and talk in a formal way at the early stages of the business relationship.


When to call, e-mail or plan a Google Meet?

We believe that a periodic meeting structure is the core of good communication with the customer. Besides this, we use the Voys (internet phone) and e-mail.

  • Try to discuss all topics in the (bi)weekly, monthly and quarterly meetings. If you receive e-mails in the meantime discuss the also during these meetings.
  • Second way of communication method is e-mail because it's clearer, faster and more reliable for the customer.
  • Third way of communicating is calling the customer using Voys. Sometimes it's quicker to just call the customer, if you call the customer please use Voys.
  • The customer can call the generic phone number (please check the site for the latest number) and re-routed if needed.


Tone of voice 

When you communicate with a client, it is important to use a formal tone of voice. This indicates the way and the tone in which you write or speak to a client. Your tone of voice should be professional and friendly. This way of communicating will be explained in detail in this module.

title: Sources
body:
Social sustainability has a focus on society and communities as a whole, with the addition that it considers the current activities and the impact they may have on future generations. Thus, it entails acknowledging that what we do now has an impact on others and improving social quality by focusing on relationships amongst people and how to improve them.

In a nutshell:

The principle of sustainable development addresses social and economic improvement that protects the environment and supports equality, and therefore the economy and society and the ecological system are mutually dependent (Diesendorf, 2000).

title: Zalando Fulfilment Solutions (ZFS)
body:
Zalando Fulfilment Solutions (ZFS) leverages the Zalando platform by giving Partners access to the logistics network and capabilities of Zalando. This allows for Partners to have even more impact in Zalando's Partner Program (PP), internationalise easily across markets, save on logistics costs, and meet customer delivery expectations. Customers benefit from added convenience and access to a larger assortment. 

We have listed some benefits of ZFS below:

  • Products are stored in Zalando warehouses.
  • Zalando personally handles every order from the moment of purchase. They take care of packaging, shipping, returns, and customer service in the customer's language.
  • ZFS is only accessible via the Partner Program. The Partner Program model supports Zalando's overarching strategy to be the starting point for Fashion, a destination for customers to be inspired, to discover new brands, and to create memorable fashion experiences.
  • Zalando has distribution centres in 23 countries all over Europe. This allows a company to expand its reach on the European market.
  • Products are delivered on the same day or the next day.
  • Customers receive products from Zalando and partners in one box - this gives a professional look and is environmentally friendly.

title: TradeByte Intro
body:
Intro
Tradebyte is an international SaaS (Software-as-a-Service) company with a rapidly growing customer base across Europe. By providing an uncomplicated, digital connection of supply and demand between the big players in the E-commerce industry, we enable seamless direct-to-consumer experiences for brands and manufacturers.
Source: https://www.tradebyte.com/en/about-us/

About our academy:
This academy is a guide to working with TradeByte. It is important to note that these are not hard rules that always apply to the mapping for TB, as there are a lot of specific exceptions. Therefore, this is an explanation on a conceptual level. 

What is TradeByte?

TradeByte is an integrator. An integrator is software that can link systems (for example PIM, ERP, WMS) to multiple marketplaces. On this basis, all marketplace-related matters can be tracked and optimised. The diagram below shows where the content (data) comes from before it can be seen in the front end of the marketplace.


    


Content and information in TradeByte can come from the client, from Brandsom, or an ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) software. Enterprise resource planning is a computer program used within organisations to support all processes within the company. An ERP program usually consists of small sub-programs that all support a specific task.

Start working in TradeByte:

title: Brandsom Managed Checklist
body: Content

title: Extra information - Absence protocol
body:
This chapter will explain in more detail how employees should report sickness and recovery, and what steps they need to take during this process. If you still have any questions regarding the absence protocol, make sure to ask your supervisor.


Calling in sick

Employee
You must personally call sick to HR or to your supervisor at least half an hour before work begins - 8:30 am. If you are unable to report sickness yourself, then you may have someone else do it for you. If you fall ill during working hours, you must report to your immediate supervisor/HR in person. When you call in sick, tell your supervisor/HR the following:

  • The expected duration of the absence;

  • Your contact information;

  • Any appointments you have and any important work-related matters;

  • HR will agree with you how, and with what frequency you maintain contact. In addition, agree on who will take the initiative to get in contact.

After reporting sick, HR should still be able to reach you by telephone during working hours.

You are not obliged to provide medical information to your supervisor during the entire period of illness.


HR 
If an employee reports sick, you have to register this in Nmbrs. Make sure you have the following information:

  • Telephone number of sick employee;

  • The expected duration of the absence; 

  • Sick employee’s appointments and work to be done;

  • Agree on how and with what frequency you will keep in touch and who will take the initiative.

You should never inquire about any medical information of the employee.

If the sick report concerns an employee with a min/max contract, the scheduled hours must be registered for 70% in Asana under sick hours. 

Always inform the person responsible for planning about the sick employee.

In case of an absence longer than 3 days, send a card to the employee.

Reporting sickness in Nmbrs

1. Visit https://bunnig.nmbrs.nl/ and log in to your Brandsom account.
Don't have an account? Send an email to team@brandsom.nl


2. Go to 'Medewerkers' (Employees) 

3. Select the employee's name

4. Click on the + icon at 'Verzuim' (Absence)



5. Next, fill in the pop-up window


Recovery notification

Employee
As soon as you know when you can work again, report to HR as recovered, even if it’s not your working day.

HR
As soon as the employee reports for work again, you need to register this in Nmbrs as well.

1. Visit https://bunnig.nmbrs.nl/ and log in to your Brandsom account.
Don't have an account? Send an email to hr@brandsom.nl.

2. Go to 'Medewerkers' (Employees)


3. Select the employee's name

4. Then select 'Verzuim' (Absence)


5. Next, fill in the pop-up window



Absence regulations

Frequent absence
When an employee is absent three times or more within a year or a shorter period of time, a meeting between the employee and the supervisor will take place. The aim of this meeting is to determine whether frequent absence can be prevented and what role and responsibility employees and Brandsom have in this.


Continued payment
At Brandsom, there are no waiting days. This means that if an employee becomes sick, they will be paid from day one. If the employee works under a min/max contract, they will be paid 70% of the scheduled hours. If the employee is not scheduled, they will be paid 70% of their guaranteed hours.

title: Immediate Interventions to Foster Sustainability in Fashion
subcategory: Immediate Interventions to Foster Sustainability in Fashion
body:
The fashion industry can take immediate steps to improve environmental and social conditions for its workers and the world at large.


Industry leaders should make the following evaluations based on the current waste and carbon footprint that this industry is causing:

  • Be transparent about workers conditions
  • Be fair when it comes to wages given to workers in underdeveloped countries.
  • Adjusting the profit margins and pricing structures so that a greener and more ethical supply chain can be guaranteed.
  • Educating the consumer about the environmental and social costs of new garments. 


Now let’s take a closer look at how these factors apply for fashion within marketplaces, and their impact on brands that wish to sell online. In the following module we will cover the importance of sustainability coming from two major and popular marketplaces at: Zalando & Bol.com.

title: Extra information - Vacation days
body:
In this chapter, we go over any extra information regarding vacation days. If anything is still unclear, make sure to ask a supervisor for more information.

Vacation days

Employee
Employees are entitled to 25 days of vacation per year, based on a 40-hour contract. You must submit your application digitally via Nmbrs no later than three weeks before you wish to use the vacation day(s).

  1. Visit https://bunnig.nmbrs.nl/ and log in.
    Don't have an account? Send an email to team@brandsom.nl
  2. Click on 'Verlof aanvragen' (Requesting leave) 
  3. Next, fill in the pop-up. Enter the dates, hours, and any remarks you may have.
  4. When your days have been approved, note them in the vacation overview.


Supervisor
When you receive a request via Nmbrs, you must respond within two weeks. You can only refuse the request if it causes a serious disruption of your business operations. Always check the vacation overview to prevent problems with multiple employees taking a vacation at the same time.


Regulations

Expired vacation days
Statutory vacation days that have not been taken by the employee will lapse six months after the calendar year in which they have been accrued. Statutory vacation days accrued in 2024 will therefore expire on 1 July 2025.

Non-statutory vacation days lapse five years after the last day of the calendar year in which the agreement was made.


Paying out vacation days
Statutory vacation days that have not been taken can not be paid out. This is only permitted at the end of a contract.

Employees who have not taken statutory vacation days may have them redeemed. Employees and Brandsom cannot oblige each other to do this.

title: Extra information - Declarations
body:
This chapter will explain in more detail how employees can use Nmbrs for various declarations of expenses and what steps they need to take during this process. If you still have any questions regarding declarations, make sure to ask your supervisor for more information.


Declaration of travel expenses

Employee
You receive an allowance for commuting expenses if the distance between your home address and the office is more than 10 kilometres and you use public transport or a car to get to work. The allowance is € 0.23 per km travelled with a maximum of € 300 net per month or the direct costs of public transport. The type of transport cost you can declare must be discussed prior with HR.

Submit your travel expenses around the 15th of each month to ensure you receive the declaration that same month. 


  1. Go to https://bunnig.nmbrs.nl/ and log in.
    Don't have an account? Send an email to hr@brandsom.nl.
  2. Click on 'Declaratieformulier' (Declaration form)
     
  3. Fill in the pop-up window. Make sure that you always enter a clear description, as this is important for the tax authorities. Continue and check the overview. Add a comment if necessary.

Important: Make copy and fill in the number of travelled kilometres in this Excel file and add it to your declaration form.




Declaration of expenses

Employee
If you make expenses for Brandsom, like ordering office supplies or sending a card, you can claim these through Nmbrs as well. This is done the same way as doing your travel expenses. However, for this, you don’t have to fill in the excel sheet. Follow the previously explained steps for Nmbrs.


Approving declarations

Supervisor
When an employee declares his expenses, you will receive an automated email from Nmbrs.

  1. Go to https://bunnig.nmbrs.nl/ and log in to your Brandsom account.
    Don’t have an account? Send an email to hr@brandsom.nl
  2. In ‘Mutatieformulieren’(Mutation forms) you can see which requests you still need to look at and which are waiting for the accountant’s approval.
  3. Check the declaration + attachment and approve it or ask for possible adjustments.

title: Extra information - Code of Conduct
body:
In our organisation, where so many people work together, we make common agreements to ensure pleasant cooperation for everyone at all times. We have laid down these general agreements in the Brandsom Code of Conduct.


We expect you to abide by these rules. If you have any questions, please contact your supervisor.


Dress code

In the office, we work for clients and apply guidelines for representativeness. But also when you work at home or attend a company event, we expect you to present yourself in a representative manner. The guiding principle is that everyone is free in their personal presentation.


Brandsom expects employees to dress appropriately in business attire of a casual nature. Our work environment encourages employees to dress comfortably for work. Please do not wear anything that other employees may find offensive or that may make colleagues uncomfortable.


Our goal is to provide a workplace environment that is comfortable and inclusive for all employees. We expect that your attire, while casual, will demonstrate common sense and professionalism.


Interaction

At Brandsom, we value diversity and inclusion. We strive to provide a working environment that is free from threats and physical or verbal harassment. Discrimination based on age, race, religion, gender, sexual orientation, or disability is not allowed.


We treat others the same way we treat colleagues; with respect. We expect employees to always behave like ambassadors of our company. Good manners are of great importance. We do not freely share confidential information with others. Threatening, intimidating or discriminating behaviour will not be tolerated. We also do not accept offensive or inappropriate language.


Theft

In case of suspicion of theft and/or fraud by an employee, there will always be an (internal) investigation. During this investigation, the employee who may be involved could be suspended. If it is proven that theft and/or fraud is involved, immediate dismissal follows and the matter can be reported to the police.


Do not take any action yourself, in case you suspect that a colleague is guilty of theft or fraud, but discuss this with your superior. They will handle the matter in confidence.


Social media

Many employees at Brandsom use social media. Therefore, the following guidelines apply for the use of social media by all employees:


Permitted 

  • the use of social media under your own name;

  • indicate that you work at Brandsom.


Not permitted 

  • the use of social media under Brandsom’s name;

  • spreading internal information including figures, customer information, legal information;

  • responding to complaints from customers.


When in doubt, first think about the impact your message could have on the company; a comment during a conversation is more noncommittal than content on social media that can be shared many times. If you’re not sure about the impact, always consult with your supervisor. 


If you find factual inaccuracies about Brandsom on the internet (or elsewhere), please report the website or other source to your supervisor.


Alcohol, drugs, and medication

The use or possession of alcohol and/or drugs is not permitted. Employees should report to work fit for duty and free of any adverse effects of illegal drugs or alcohol. This policy does not prohibit employees from the lawful use and possession of prescribed medications. If a meal is consumed during break time or overtime, the use of alcoholic beverages is not permitted, even if this is in your own time.


Internet use and mobile phones

Brandsom allows the use of mobile phones and personal use of the business internet connection at work, but we want to make sure that this does not distract you from your work or cause a nuisance in our workplace(s). Therefore, we ask that you follow a few simple rules:


  • Use your mobile phone in a way that benefits your work (business calls, productivity apps, calendars).

  • Keep personal calls brief and use an empty meeting room or common area to make those calls. This way, you don’t disturb your colleagues.

  • Do not use your phone to record confidential information.

  • Do not download or upload inappropriate, illegal, or obscene material using our business internet connection.

  • It is not allowed to download or upload illegal movies, music, material, or software using our business internet connection.

  • It is not allowed to visit potentially dangerous websites that could harm the security of our network and computers.

  • It is not permitted to carry out any unauthorised or illegal actions, such as hacking, fraudulent behaviour, and buying or selling illegal goods.


Office romances

  • When people work together, relationships and romances will happen, this is something we like.
  • When you're (about) to start a relationship with a colleague, please inform your manager to make sure there is no conflict of interest.
  • When you feel that an office relationship is impacting your development or work at Brandsom, reach out to your manager, Joris or Thijs personally. We will make sure we solve the matter and address your concerns. 
  • At Brandsom we do not interfere with your private life, what happens outside office hours or does not impact your work at Brandsom is private and will stay private.

title: Sales bonus - Bonus Terms and Conditions for Sales Manager 2025
subtitle: Revised Terms and Conditions:
body:
Clients acquired before 01-01-2025 will remain under the previous bonus terms unless explicitly stated. Revenue from such clients that transition into new contracts post-implementation will qualify under the new bonus system.

Definitions:

  • Calendar Year sales target: the by Brandsom management defined sales target per calendar year.
  • Contract period: the deal contract period.
  • Base bonus: the base bonus is valid over the initial contract period
  • Stretch Bonus: the stretch bonus is valid for the remaining period in which the sales manager exceeds the sales target per Calendar Year. This will be defined per calendar year.
  • Bonus percentages are subject to change and will be defined by Brandsom per calendar year.

 

1. Bonus Eligibility:

  • The bonus applies exclusively to the fixed value outlined in the contract, which includes:
    • Retainer fees
    • Setup fees
    • Monthly fixed fees
  • The following revenue components are explicitly excluded from the bonus calculation:
    • GMV (Gross Merchandise Value) performance fees
    • Other variable contract elements

2. Base Bonus (2.5% on the Fixed Revenue Within Target)

The sales manager will receive a base 2.5% bonus on all fixed revenue deal values that comply with the terms and conditions outlined in this agreement.Eligibility is subject to the defined criteria and exclusions stated in this document.

Fixed vs. Variable Revenue Ratio:

Example total Sales Target: €400,000

Fixed Revenue (80%): Based on historical company data, approximately 80% of total revenue in the first calendar year of new customers comes from fixed fees.
Fixed Revenue within Target: €400,000 × 80% = €320,000
Bonus Rate: 2.5%

3. Stretch Targets calendar year (Applicable Only to Fixed Fees):

Stretch targets apply exclusively to fixed fees above the initial target (in this example calculation €320,000).

Stretch Target Tiers (Fixed Fees Only):

  • 0% until +25% fixed revenue above target: 10% instead of 2.5%
  • +25% to +50% fixed revenue above target: 11% instead of 2.5%
  • >50% fixed revenue above target: 12% instead of 2.5%

Calculation Example:

If the sales manager achieves total revenue of €500,000, of which 80% is fixed fees:

  • Fixed Revenue: €500,000 × 80% = €400,000
  • Revenue above Fixed Target: €400,000 - €320,000 = €80,000
  • Stretch Bonus:
    • +25% Fixed Revenue above Target (€320,000 - €400,000): €80,000 × 10% = €8,000
    • Total Stretch Bonus: €8,000
  • Total Annual Bonus: €8,000 (Base Bonus) + €8,000 (Stretch Bonus) = €16,000

All calculations are based on eligible revenue as defined in the agreement and exclude VAT.

4. Quarterly Bonus Payout Example:

Bonuses are paid on a quarterly basis, with payments processed within 30 days after the end of each quarter. Calculations are based on revenue collected by the closing date of the quarter.

5. Conditions and Monitoring:

  • Calendar Year Agreement: Only revenue generated within the calendar year is eligible for the target. Any unused revenue or stretch bonuses will not roll over to the following year.
  • Revenue invoiced but not yet collected or under dispute will not count toward bonus calculations until resolved.
  • Monitoring of the Fixed vs. Variable Revenue Ratio: The 80% ratio is based on first-year data from new customers and will be reviewed annually. If the actual fixed revenue ratio deviates significantly, the bonus structure may be adjusted.
  • For GMV-only deals (only possible with prior approval from Brandsom and for clients generating more than €1 million in actual yearly GMV), the sales manager will receive a bonus based on the realised GMV amount invoiced during the contract period.
    • The bonus calculation applies the defined 'fixed vs. variable revenue ratio' to the invoiced GMV share.
    • Example: If a GMV-only deal with Nike generates an invoiced realised GMV of €250,000, the calculation would be as follows:
      • 80% of €250,000 = €200,000 × Bonus % (e.g., 2.5% base rate or stretch rate based on remaining calendar months in the target period).
  • Deal ownership: A deal is deemed owned by a sales manager if they have directly facilitated and closed the agreement and are listed as the deal owner in the company's CRM system.

6. Bonus Payment Conditions:

  • Eligibility: Bonuses will only be paid if:
    • The sales manager is actively employed by Brandsom at the time of payment in the role of sales manager.
    • If the sales manager is no longer employed by Brandsom at the time of any bonus payout, they forfeit all rights to bonus payments from that moment onward.This condition applies irrespective of whether the bonus pertains to previously achieved targets or collected revenue.
    • The customer contract remains active and revenue is collected per the agreement.
  • Calendar Year Definition: Bonus calculations align with the calendar year. The customer contract start date determines the applicable bonus period.

7. Exception Clause for Minimum Revenue Threshold:

Exceptions to the €18,000 minimum revenue threshold in the first 12 contract months may be granted at Brandsom's sole discretion. Such deviations require explicit written approval from Brandsom’s CEO or COO. Approved exceptions apply only to the specified client or scenario, without affecting other terms of this agreement.

Other:

  • Brandsom reserves the right to unilaterally amend bonus program terms, provided a minimum of one quarter's written notice is given, except in cases of legal or regulatory compliance.
  • Confidentiality Clause:
    Bonus structures and calculations are confidential and must not be shared with third parties, including other employees, unless required by law. Failure to adhere to this confidentiality clause may result in disciplinary action, including termination.
  • All prices are excluding VAT. Based on the eligible revenue as defined in this academy.
  • Bonus Disputes: Any disputes or clarifications regarding bonus calculations must be raised within 30 days of the payment date.
  • Upsell and renewals are excluded: only the original fixed contract value and period will be included in the bonus pay-out. Contract renewals and or upsell are excluded from the bonus agreement.
  • Secondary Targets: For achieving SQLs (Sales Qualified Leads), no bonus will be awarded. This target is considered a non-bonus performance metric.
  • Bonuses are only applicable if the following conditions are met for customer contracts:
    • The customer has fully signed the agreement.
    • The customer complies with payment terms and is not in default at the time of bonus calculation and open invoices have been handed over to the bailiff.
    • The fixed contract value in the first 12 contract months exceed €18.000 excluding VAT.

title: Frequent verzuim - Joris + VMOM
body:
Wanneer een medewerker drie keer of vaker binnen een tijdsbestek van een doorlopend jaar of een korter tijdvak verzuimt, zal een gesprek plaatsvinden tussen de medewerker en de leidinggevende. De insteek van het gesprek is met elkaar vaststellen of het frequent verzuim kan worden voorkomen en welke rol en verantwoordelijkheid de medewerker en Brandsom hierin hebben.

Mogelijke oorzaken

  • Gebrek aan motivatie.
  • Een tekort aan energiebronnen in het werk (creativiteit, zelfstandigheid, zelfontplooiing, waardering, feedback etc.).
  • Een slechte werk-privébalans.


Aanpak 

Bespreek het verzuim met een overzicht van alle meldingen. Evalueer niet na elk verzuim maar altijd over een periode. 

  • Bespreek aanwezigheid en meedoen essentieel is voor het bedrijf.
  • Onderzoek wat de mogelijke oorzaak is.
  • Stuur op meer werkplezier en bevlogenheid door te focussen op wat uw werknemer energie geeft.
  • Bied ruimte voor aangepast doorwerken bij klachten en zoek oplossingen buiten de ziektewet bij privéproblemen.

Let op: De volgende dingen mag je niet

  • Informeren naar de aard en oorzaak van de ziekte
  • Vastleggen wat er aan de hand is indien medewerker dit wel verteld
  • Diagnose stellen, behandeling voorstellen of waardeoordeel uitspreken over de klachten die uw werknemer ervaart.

title: Sustainability at Bol.com
body:
Bol, like Zalando has introduced new guidelines and highlighted the importance of sustainably among the stakeholders involved. 


Introduction - Sustainability importance at bol.com.

Strategy 

Bol.com wishes to be the number 1 platform to buy everything you need by offering her clients the broadest assortment and  including a more sustainable choice. Helping customers to make this choice, bol.com uses sustainability labels and shows them on the listing and product detail page. These labels provide more information per product, for example origin, production and impact on the environment.


Why is sustainability gaining momentum within bol.com?

  • Dutch (69%) and Belgians (82%) - customers - worry about climate conditions and more often reconsider their purchase accordingly. (Q4 2021).
  • Customers find packaging and production most important regarding sustainability. Marketplaces offer too few options and make too little efforts to improve these according to bol.com.
  • Manufacturers and sellers on the platform rank the subject as important, but wait for frameworks given by the marketplace. Strategy is often based on juridical frameworks, and nobody within the chain takes the lead.
  • More and more, the media holds bol.com and other marketplaces responsible for the entire chain, including the available assortment provided on the platform.
  • Bol.com wishes and has the chance to differentiate herself from (international) competitors by taking the lead in this field of interest. Bol.com sees it as a thread that competitors are stepping up on sustainability already. Even Amazon launched its first circular propositions in the US and UK with Amazon Second Chance.

Internally, bol.com wishes to make a stepchange via sellable assortment:

  • The current 6,5% “more sustainable” labeled available assortment on the platform needs to increase to 30% in 2025.

  • The current 2,1% “more sustainable” labeled items sold needs to increase to 20% share in 2025.

title: TradeByte Golden rules
body:
1. Always be as accurate and precise as possible. If you are not sure about something, try to find it out first by looking in the logbook, or in the value mapping history.  

By clicking on the 'History' tab in the value mapping, you can see how the mapping was done in the past and by whom. 

2. Never switch off a component or attribute without checking whether it is still in use for value mapping. That can be the case, just like that. Please note: the value mapping is different for different channels. This means that it is possible that you do not use a certain component for the value mapping for Bol, but you do for Zalando. It is important to keep in mind that if you disable components/attributes, you can (unintentionally) cause the value mapping in another channel to be completely messed up. 

3. Never create a new component or attribute without first double-checking that one has already been created. If you need to create a new one, follow the rules (see Creating a new component TB).

4. In the morning, check if all products are live after modifying a component/attribute/etc.

title: MerchantSpring - Sales Targets
body:
Sales targets in MerchantSpring

Please find templates you help you create the sales forecast (if you don't have one for your client yet) at the bottom of this academy.


How to add your sales targets to MerchantSpring

Sales Targets are added on channel level and (for now) have to be added manually in MerchantSpring per month. Once you have confirmed the monthly target with your client during the Year Plan, you can add them in MerchantSpring. Go to Channels > Select the channel you are looking to add > Go to Sales Targets (Under Sales Insights)

On this page, you will find the following metrics:

  • Sales Budget > The target you set at the beginning of the year
  • Sales Forecast > Adjusted target based on learnings
  • Ad Spend Budget > Target Ad Spend in €, at the time of writing it is not possible to add a percentage

For now, you can use the Sales Budget or both (Sales Forecast) since we do not make a difference internally.

Follow the steps below:

  • Select the month you are going to set the sales target for via the date range
  • Add the sales target in the corresponding field, and make sure the formatting is correct (no comma's etc). If you have an Ad Spend target, make sure to follow the same steps
    • Targets can be set including VAT, since this is also what we use for invoicing.
    • Targets do not change when you toggle including or excluding VAT in MerchantSpring
  • Press "Split Evenly" to divide the target evenly over all the days of the month


Where do you find the sales target overview

Once the sales targets are set, you can find the target in various places

  • The Sales Target overview, where you just set them
  • The Channel Overview
  • If you want an overview of all channels your client has, go to Dashboard > Sales By Channel (View all) > Add Columns > Sales Budget

When creating sales targets, keep in mind:

  • If a client is new to the marketplace, it is important to consider this when dividing the overall target across the months.
  • Most clients have a strong Q4 due to the Holiday season or "End Of Season Sale", this should also be considered when creating the monthly targets
  • You can use comparable clients to determine seasonality if needed.

Templates to help you forecast.

  • This template can be used to calculate your sales forecast. It has the option to add multiple marketplaces
  • NMV Targets: If you have NMV targets with your client, you can use this template.

title: the follow-up meetings
body:
Which data should be obtained in the follow-up meetings?

Target of the second call is to:

- Figure out the budgeting of the client
- Brand readiness check
- Roadmap marketplaces (more information about the marketplaces and the requirements can be found here)
- Indication of the feasibility of the offer

If the client is interested in Brandsom's services, several sales meetings will take place before the long-term contract is signed. It is important to gather information for the consultant in these meetings, so the consultant knows what needs to be done before the client goes live.

The easiest way to collect this data is:

1. Start typing #ExploreCall after which a pop-up will appear. Click on the pop-up, this put's the template in your note.

2. During the sales meeting get answers to the questions and write them down directly in the notes, otherwise there is a good chance you will forget important information. Also try to tell the lead why this information is important: this is obviously to better help the lead.

3. If needed, adjust your notes after the meeting.

title: Amazon Bid Management
body:
Bid Optimization

Bid vs Cost Per Click

Bid: The maximum amount you are willing to pay for a click.

CPC (Cost Per Click): The actual amount you are paying for a click.

Example: If your bid is $2, your CPC might be $1.1. The CPC is the main reference for optimization.

When to Start Optimizing?

Data

Ensure you have enough clicks to make a judgment. If your conversion rate is low, optimization is necessary.

Spend

If your expenditure is too high compared to your sales, you should optimize.

Time

Optimizing once a week is ideal. Evaluate the performance of an ad campaign over 30 days, considering a 48-hour delay in sales reporting and a 7-day sales attribution window.

Things to note regarding time:

  • There is a 48 hour delay by Amazon in reporting the sales.

  • There is a 7 days time frame after the click for sales attribution.

Sales Attribution

Sales attribution determines which ad gets credit for a conversion.

  • Click ⸺> Purchase: Sales of the clicked product or any product from the brand are attributed to the click.

  • If a shopper clicks multiple ads, the sale is attributed to the last ad clicked.


Bid Optimization for Good Performing and High ACOS Targets

If ACOS is Below Target ACOS

  • If our ACOS is below the target ACOS, we want to increase our bid by 5% in order to create more sales.

If ACOS is Above Target ACOS

  • If our ACOS is above the target ACOS we want to decrease our bid. The magnitude of the change depends on how far off we are from the target ACOS. The further we are from the target the more significant our adjustment should be.

  • Some additional things to pay attention to:

    • We don’t want to make drastic changes.

    • CPC is the reference, not the bid.

Low Impressions Targets

  • Increase the bid by 10%.

Bid Optimization for Non-Converting Targets

Relevant Search Terms

  • Lowering the bids for these terms is better than negating them.

  • If you negate them, they should be tested later as they might not be converting due to other reasons such as lack of reviews or bad content.

  • If you negate them, do it in “Exact Match.”

Irrelevant Search Terms

  • Negate immediately. For example; "blue" in the search term while your product is red.

  • Based on the term, you could negate it as “Exact Match” or “Phrase Match.”

Keep in mind these are used as rule of thumb, situation can differ per client and agreements made.

title: Zalando Error Analysis
body:
Resources:

  • Zalando Most Common Errors Troubleshooting
  • error solving template

 

For error analysis on Zalando we use an Excel file in which all products are listed and the analysis is made. The structure of this file can be found here: 
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-Pyr8oVgHXVdzq99gnqAViYVu49jWU7S5XMvCfmwZO0/edit#gid=0

  • By downloading this each time as Excel and saving it in the google drive of the customer, you also have the possibility to keep the old overviews.
  • Create a separate folder per season in the customer folder.
  • Put the most recent Excel in here 
  • Move the old file to a separate 'Old' folder

How to perform Error analysis?

Always start the analysis with the Zalando errors and then work backwards in the process (so if a product is not on ZDirect, check if there is an error in TB, or if there is already something known about missing pictures/information).

1. Download all errors from the main channel of the customer on Zdirect (filter for example on Germany).

2. Then update the following columns in the Analysis document in the tab: EAN status update
- Move the column new Zalando status to "old zalando status"

- Then update the Zalando status using a V-lookup with the EANs 

  • Remember to save this column as text (otherwise the data might dissapear when you open the document again)
  • Put a filter on the new Zalando status, Everything with a #NB status is not visible on Zdirect. This means there is something else goiing on with the EAN, you need to check for errors in the integrator. 
  • Add a status for these #NB products (For ASICS this is "stuck in TB"). This way you keep your overview clear.

- The column solutions will update itself (if you leave the V-lookup)

  • Solutions are already in there with a formula to another tab, here are the learnings per error (so for this error you need to contact zalando, change something in the materials etc.)

- Update the Zalando SKU's (with V-Lookup). 

  • You do this because not all products will get a SKU assigned right away because of possible errors.
  •  If you want to see which SKU's are new, create a new column and do a V-lookup for the SKU's. By using a filter you can see which SKU's were "0" or "#NB" last week, and have a SKU this week.

3.Then update the pivot table in the Zalando Update tab. 

  • If you are creating the table for the first time, then put Solutions and the number of EANs on the axes. This way you also have a handy overview of the type (and number) of errors for the customer.

4.Next, create an error overview specifically for all product numbers (customer SKUs).

  • Filter the new error status, only leave the errors (you can exclude stock errors, Stuck in TB, and all live products) 
  • Copy the Client SKU's, Zalando SKU's and new error status to a new tab (or replacre the data in the last product number overview.
  • Deduplicate the data
  • Now you can compare the current errors with the google sheet error file, and add the new errors to make sure that these will be solved.

5. If there are products which are not yet visible on Zalando (i.e. #N/B appears when you did the V-lookup for Zalando errors) then download the error overview from the TB solution centre (or a similar overview from another integrator). See if there is an error stopping this product from being pushed to Zalando. 

6. If you can't find any errors in the integrator, go to the product itself and check everything:

  • Check that all necessary data has been added/mapped
  • Check if the product (and articles) have been activated for Zalando
  • Check if the price is added

title: Amazon Advertising SOP: PreLaunch / Launch Phase
body:
In the Google Spreadsheet, you’ll find the structure for:


  • Pre-Ads Activities - a set of tasks to complete before the ads launch to ensure PDPs are optimized (including coupons/review settings). 
  • Ads Structure - The ads structure that I believe we should activate for each account. This structure covers all strategies and funnel levels. In this section, I’ve defined the naming conventions and implementation timing. Ideally, we should schedule each phase in the Ads calendar to ensure all steps are fully covered.

SOP PreLaunch / Launch -  [Targets Bank]

This SOP details the process of building a list of target keywords and ASINs for the pre-launch phase. The main objective is to create a strong set of keywords for product listings, advertising, and backend use. By analysing competitors, customer feedback, and keyword opportunities, the SOP aims to improve product indexing and ranking, improving visibility and sales potential as the product launches.

The targeting bank is part of the Launch Phase. For all strategies and campaign structure details, please refer to the following spreadsheet for the Ads Launch Structure - Link 

The link includes three main sections:

  1. Pre-Ads Tasks – A set of tasks to complete before the Ads launch to ensure product detail pages are fully optimized.

  2. Ads Structure – This section describes the optimal ad structure. Columns D-E-F-G provide the timeline and weekly tasks following the launch day. The structure will be adapted based on each client's situation and budget (e.g., delaying the creation of campaigns not directly tied to sales/conversions).

  3. Timing – Phases – A brief summary of each task for the individual phases



10 Steps to create the Targets Bank


At the end of each step, we will be able to identify which targets can be used in our listing and campaign. Complete each phase by saving the targets that make it to the final list.

1. Title Density Analysis

Understand the level of competition for a specific keyword.

  • Method: Use Helium 10 to check the title density for your target keyword. For example, if "on-ear headphone" has a title density of 9, it indicates that nine listings on page one use this exact phrase match.

This helps assess the difficulty or ease of ranking for a keyword, guiding your launch strategy accordingly.

2. Competitor Rank Analysis

Evaluate competitor performance based on top keyword phrases.

  • Method: Input competitor listings into tools like Listing Analyzer to examine their ranking for key phrases in your niche.

Understand where competitors rank for top keywords and assess their strengths. It provides a nice indication of the common keywords used.

3. Bundle Opportunities

Identify product bundling opportunities and additional relevant keywords.

  • Method: Analyze frequently bought-together products to discover indirectly related keywords that can be used in ads or product descriptions.

These keywords can be used as a base to develop new offers, creative indication for images, not closely related targeting. Expands keyword targeting and enhances product offerings.

4. Review Insights: Main Topic Identification

Leverage customer feedback for content optimization.

  • Method: By checking the Amazon Pdp of your main competitors and/or your own ASINs, you can easily access the Review insights thanks to the H10 extension. Analyze reviews to identify recurring themes or topics, then incorporate these into your product images, content, or bullet points.

Tip = Aligns product Images/listing with customer expectations and needs.

5. Review Insights: Pain Point Identification

Strictly connected to the point 4, the reviews analysis allows you to check the low rating review common problems. Address common customer concerns or shortcomings of competing products.

  • Method: Analyze negative feedback and use it to highlight how your product solves these pain points in the description and images.

Enhances product appeal by showing problem-solving features. The scope is to reassure, through the PdP content, that our product does not have the same pain point as the competitors. 

6. Competitor Image Analysis

Understand successful competitor Image contents.

  • Method: Analyze competitors’ image stacks and other media using tools like Helium 10 Media Comparison to identify trends and effective strategies.

Use insights to refine the product's graphic and marketing strategies.

7. Keyword List Preparation

Create a total comprehensive keyword list.

  • Steps:

    • Top Keywords: Extract 15 top-performing keywords from 5-10 competitors.

    • Opportunity Keywords: Identify keywords with high rankings for only one or two competitors.

    • Brand Analytics: Use BSR charts to find peak sales days and identify top keywords during these periods.

    • SEO Optimization: Refine product content based on relevant keywords.

8. Reverse Engineering PPC Strategy from Competitors

  • Method: Use tools like Cerebro to analyze competitors' ASINs and sponsored rank averages. Set search parameters to find high-volume, high-performing keywords.

Helps identify keywords that competitors are investing in for paid advertising, allowing you to strategically plan your own campaign.

9. Amazon Product Opportunity Explorer

Discover additional keyword opportunities.

  • Method: Use the Amazon Product Opportunity Explorer tool to find relevant and trending keywords for your niche.

Expands the list of potential keywords to target for increased visibility.

10. Keyword List Refinement with Frankenstein

At this point we have a keyword list with a big number of targets. The scope here is to streamline and optimize keyword lists.

  • Method: Use Helium10 Frankenstein to reduce keyword list size by removing duplicates and extracting unique words from keyword phrases.

Creates a refined and efficient keyword list for indexing and ranking.


Steps for the Target Bank Optimization:

  • Use Listing Builder to input your main keyword phrases (10-30) and individual keywords (50-300). Ensure your keywords are not just inserted as phrases but also broken down into their roots.

  • Title Optimization: Start with the title, making sure it’s optimized with your main keywords and canonical URL. Incorporate emotional trigger points from customer reviews into your content.

  • Multiple Keywords: If you have multiple top keywords with similar roots, ensure they are included in the title for maximum visibility.

  • A+ Content and Translation: Activate translations for relevant markets, ensuring that A+ content appears properly when the user changes the language setting.

  • Check Indexing: Regularly verify the indexing status of all keywords using available tools. For relevant keywords, use the Special Features field to boost indexing.

Extra: Prioritization for Indexing and Ranking

To ensure proper indexing and ranking of your product, specific fields must be optimized using the keywords derived from the analysis. Follow this prioritization scheme for accurate optimization:

Indexing Fields:

  1. Special Features (ensure it is update for all the products)

  2. Title

  3. Subject Matter

  4. Generic Keywords

  5. Bullet Points

  6. Description

Ranking Fields:

  1. Title

  2. Subject Matter

  3. Generic Keywords

  4. Bullet Points

  5. Description

  6. Special Features


Launch document Spreadsheet.

  • [Google Spreadsheet - Amazon ADS - Launch]

The templates can also be found in the Template Gallery. Create the document in the corresponding client folder.
