title: Onboarding Checklist for New Client
body:
New Onboarding Process

The onboarding process involves three key parties: Sales, Finance, and the Vertical Consultant/Team. Here’s a step-by-step guide to ensure a smooth onboarding experience:

Sales: Pre-Contract Steps

Step 1: Gather Necessary Information

Before the client signs a contract, Sales needs to collect the following details and input them in HubSpot via a note and the applicable properties in Hubspot:

  • Company Situation

  • Company Challenges

  • Key Contacts: Identify the people involved and their roles.

  • Current Marketplaces: List the current marketplaces and their countries.

  • Additional Marketplaces: Identify additional marketplaces and their countries.

  • Logistics: Outline the logistics setup.

  • WIP Project Scope: Provide an overview of the analysis, scope, and timeline of the project. Presentation Template is here.

  • Systems: Identify any integrators and systems in use.

  • SKU Count & Categories: Determine the number of SKUs and categories involved.

  • Ad/Marketing Spend: Note any applicable ad or marketing budgets.

  • Targets: Identify any specific targets if applicable.

Step 2: Initiate Contract Process

Once the client gives the green light:

  • Send Finance an Email: Send an email to Finance@brandsom.nl (Floris) so they can send the Financial Form and draft the contract. You must include the following in your email:

    • Duration of contract

    • Type of contract

    • Marketplaces involved

    • Integrator details

    • Products and categories

    • Main issue/challenge

    • Logistics setup

    • Ad spend (if applicable)

    • Targets (if applicable)

Step 3: Finalize the contract

  • Finance finalizes the contract and sends the final draft to Sales.

    • Sales Confirmation: Sales confirms the details before the contract is sent out and informs Finance via the email thread.


Sales: Post-Contract Steps

Step 1: Handover from sales to consultant

After the client has signed the contract, Sales must ensure the consultant has everything they need to be successful. This includes:

  • Populating all Hubspot fields with the information mentioned in Step 1 and 2 in Pre-Contract Steps.

  • Make sure to set the company owner to the relevant consultant, and update the Brandsom Team field accordingly. 

  • Make a note on HubSpot, tagging the consultant and include:

    • A short summary of the company and why they have come to Brandsom

    • Who are the contact persons and their roles in the company

    • Project Scope/Marketplace information

    • Contract Info

    • Attach all relevant documents

Step 2: Great Customer Experience

Sales needs to get the consultant in touch with the new client ASAP. Please do the following:

  • Inform the consultant about their new client via Slack

  • Inform Imco about the new client (via email). Imco will assist the consultant where needed.

  • Introduce the consultant via email to the client and ask them to share their availability for a Kick Off meeting.

  • Schedule an extensive follow up meeting (before the kickoff meeting) to make sure the consultant has a good handover.

Data that needs to be transferred from Sales to Consultant - based on onboarding workshop:

  • Integrator access (see Hubspot Snippet)
  • Marketplace access (see Hubspot Snippet)
  • List with focus products
  • WIP

Sales are now done.


2. Finance: Pre-Contract and Contract Finalization

Step 1: Collect Billing Information

Once Sales has sent Finance an email about a new client wanting to sign a contract with the relevant information, Finance must send the Financial Form to the new client. This form will gather:

  • Contact/Billing Details:

    • Signee Name and Email

    • Company Name and Address

    • Chamber of Commerce Number

    • VAT Number

    • EPR Number (if applicable)

Step 2: Contract Formulation

Once the Financial Form has been completed by the new client, please:

  • Formulate Contract: Draft the contract based on the information provided by Sales.

  • Approval Process: Send the contract for approval to Thijs or Joris and have the Sales Manager perform a final check.

  • Pando Doc Email: Ensure sales (Livia@brandsom.nl)  is CC’d in the Pando Doc email to the client.

Step 3: Post-Signature Actions

The new client has signed! Please do the following actions to ensure a smooth onboarding for the consultant:

  • Attach Signed Contract to HubSpot: Upload a PDF copy of the signed contract to HubSpot for the consultant to access.

  • Notify Sales of Completion: Inform Sales that Finance’s role is complete via email.

Finance is now complete.



3. Vertical Consultant/Brandsom Team: Post-Contract Steps

Step 1: Introduction and Kickoff Scheduling

  • Respond to Introduction Email: Immediately reply to the introduction email from Sales to introduce yourself to the client and schedule the kickoff meeting. Please note that Sales is not included in the kick off meeting, unless stated otherwise.

  • Create Email Alias: The Vertical Manager should create the email alias when appropriate and inform the team. Please be sure to take charge and ask your Manager do this for you.

  • Ask for Access: Please ask for direct access to any seller accounts and integrators before the kick off happens. Then you are better able to prep for the meeting and ensure you have everything you need.

Step 2: Familiarization and Preparation

  • Review HubSpot Information: Familiarize yourself with all the information stored in HubSpot regarding the client.

  • Prepare Kickoff Presentation: Make sure to use the Welcome to Brandsom presentation for this. (Duplicate the template slides to create a new presentation)

Step 3: Set Up Phase 

  • Consultant to add specialist and Finance to Integrator
  • Consultant to add specialists to marketplace(s)

  • SET UP PHASE FOR A MANAGED CLIENT INCLUDES:

    • Account Analysis (including the integrator)

      • Amazon

        • Is the brand registry correctly done? 

        • Build international listing should be turned off

        • VAT settings

        • VAT B2B invoice settings

        • Register the brand with Amazon

      • Bol

      • Is the brand registry done correctly?

    • Brand analysis

    • Keyword analysis

    • Content analysis

    • Formulation of strategy

    • Design of advertisements

Step 3: Post-Onboarding Feedback

  • Provide Feedback: After the onboarding, leave feedback in HubSpot regarding the process. Tag Livia and Joris to help continuously improve the onboarding experience.

  • Thijs calls client after 4 weeks how the onboarding has been.

title: Introduction
body:
One of the most important parts of working at Brandsom is fulfilling our client's needs. This is simply because they are the reason we get to work. Therefore, you want to build a strong working relationship with the client. To achieve this,  good communication is necessary to figure out their goals and needs and to let them know what we can do to help. After all, you want to keep the client satisfied. How do we do this? And what exactly is good communication and how do we keep it businesslike?

One way of communicating is by e-mail. Another frequently used option is calling the client by phone. It is very important to write and talk in a formal way at the early stages of the business relationship.


When to call, e-mail or plan a Google Meet?

We believe that a periodic meeting structure is the core of good communication with the customer. Besides this, we use the Voys (internet phone) and e-mail.

  • Try to discuss all topics in the (bi)weekly, monthly and quarterly meetings. If you receive e-mails in the meantime discuss the also during these meetings.
  • Second way of communication method is e-mail because it's clearer, faster and more reliable for the customer.
  • Third way of communicating is calling the customer using Voys. Sometimes it's quicker to just call the customer, if you call the customer please use Voys.
  • The customer can call the generic phone number (please check the site for the latest number) and re-routed if needed.


Tone of voice 

When you communicate with a client, it is important to use a formal tone of voice. This indicates the way and the tone in which you write or speak to a client. Your tone of voice should be professional and friendly. This way of communicating will be explained in detail in this module.

title: Code of conduct
body:
At Brandsom, where numerous individuals collaborate, we have established common agreements to ensure a pleasant working environment for everyone at all times. These guidelines are detailed in the Brandsom Code of Conduct.

We expect you to adhere to these rules. If you have any questions, please contact your supervisor.

In the upcoming modules and lessons, we will explore the various elements of the code of conduct in greater detail.

title: Making your own automatic Brandsom signature
body:
An automatic email signature is a text, like your contact information or a favourite quote, that’s automatically added at the end of Gmail messages as a footer.

With Gmail you can do this very easily: 

  1. In Gmail click on the pen-icon to add a signature

  2. Click 'Manage signatures’

  3. Scroll down to 'Signatures' and click: 'Add' - give it a name, for example: ”Brandsom [your name/function]”

  4. Fill in the text box (see 'FORMAT' below)

  5. Under the text box, you can find the automatic settings for your signature - for both options set your Brandsom signature as standard

  6. Scroll down to the bottom of the page and click 'Save'

title: Zorgverlof (langdurend)
body:
Wanneer: Als u iemand uit uw omgeving wilt verzorgen die levensbedreigend ziek is, heeft u recht op langdurend zorgverlof.
U kunt ook langdurend zorgverlof aanvragen als iemand uit uw omgeving langere tijd ziek of hulpbehoevend is. Voorwaarde is dat de verzorging noodzakelijk is en dat u de enige bent die deze zorg kan geven.
Voor wie:

  • kinderen, partner, ouders, grootouders, kleinkinderen, broers en zussen (tweedegraads bloedverwanten);
  • anderen in uw huishouden (bijvoorbeeld een inwonende tante);
  • bekenden (iemand met wie u een sociale relatie heeft. Bijvoorbeeld schoonouders, een buurvrouw of een vriend. De relatie moet wel zo zijn dat het logisch is dat u voor diegene moet zorgen). Voorwaarde is wel dat u de enige bent die de zieke zorg kan geven.

Duur: in 12 maanden 6 keer uw werkuren per week opnemen.

Salaris: De werkgever hoeft het salaris niet door te betalen over de uren dat je langdurig verlof hebt.

title: What causes error 8059?
subcategory: Amazon Error Solving SOP Overview - internal
body:
What causes error 8059?

This error occurs when there are restriction to list these products on Amazon at item level. On some occasion we block certain item from being created automatically by sellers in order to prevent customer issues with those products (for example: products being recalled by the manufacturer, products where we detect that there is a high chance of it being a counterfeit, products in which customer are reporting a high number of issues, etc.)

Sample:


Error: The item you are attempting to list (SKU: <SKU number>) is restricted by Amazon for your locale, therefore this record was ignored.

How to solve and prevent error 8059?

Once you detect these types of errors on the feed for a seller, you should present a list of products or SKU to the sellers. Instruct the seller to contact Seller Support from their account in Seller Central to understand why those products are restricted and if it is possible for them to sell those products on Amazon.


Your application should block the SKUs with these errors from uploading until the seller confirms they have the approval to sell in those restricted categories. Alternatively, if you see SKUs with these errors you should inform the seller about this and program your application to retry these same products after 24 hours.

title: What causes error 90111?
subcategory: Amazon Error Solving SOP Overview - internal
body:
What causes error 90111?

This error occurs when you are using invalid values in a numeric field.

Samples:


ResultMessageCode: 90111


ResultDescription: The Message/Price/MaximumSellerAllowedPrice field contains an invalid value: 8.14. The value "8.14" is not a valid CURRENCY

How to solve and prevent error 90111?

To resolve this issue, review the ‘Data Definitions’ tab in the category specific flat file to determine accepted field value formatting. For example, Currency needs to be entered with decimal (.) or comma (,) depending on the marketplaces (8.5 or 8,5) when filling out the price field. To prevent this error, you should check ‘Data Definitions’ and ‘Valid Values’ tab in category specific flat files and provide values based on the same.

title: The Perfect Brandsom customer
body:

title: Stimulating a Prospect to take Action
body:
At this point, you understand your prospect's challenges, goals, and plans. Hopefully, you've found something that they want to do, but can't do on their own. At this point in the call, you should be ready to introduce the things you do that can help them with their goals and challenges. Ideally, these are things that you're uniquely positioned to help them with - things your competition can't do. If so, you're now in a great position to share some ways you can lessen their concerns.

This brings us to the TCI part of the CGP, TCI & BA framework that you can use to understand a prospect's entire context on an exploratory call. 


TCI stands for timeline, consequences, and implications. During this part of the conversation, you want to cover three things:

  1. The prospect's timeline and urgency
  2. The negative consequences of inaction
  3. The positive implications of taking action

Here are some examples of how you can start this conversation:

"Have you thought about trying to do Y in order to achieve goal Z?"

or

"Would it help if I showed you how other companies implemented X in order to eliminate challenge Y?"

or

"Most companies who struggled with this challenge found that solution X eliminates 95% of the problem. Do you think that would be an improvement for you?"

Make sure that you confirm that they are indeed interested in implementing several of your solutions. If they are not, figure out why not. Once you discuss this with them, ask the prospect if they're convinced that your suggestions and ideas will help them overcome their challenges, and achieve their goals more assuredly.


This brings us to the T in TCI: timeline.

Now that you've uncovered challenges and goals and started to reshape their plans, it's critical to understand timeline and urgency.

Many companies or individuals have goals that are time-bound. They might have been assigned a timeline or they may have set one for themselves.You could ask the prospect:

"When do you need to achieve this goal?"

or

"How long are you prepared to deal with this challenge?"

From there, you are in a position to help them think through the steps required between now and the achievement of the goal.


The next step in TCI is C for consequences.

Timeline conversations transition easily into conversations about consequences. As a prospect talks through their timeline and plan, you should begin to understand how eager they are to change. If they're engaged heavily in planning, that means they're usually pretty serious about doing something differently. 

Consequences are bad things that result from inaction or making the wrong decision. To explore these with the prospector, you could ask:

"If you don't achieve this goal, what happens to the company? What happens to you? What happens to others?"

or

"How important is overcoming this challenge to your company?"


The final part of TCI involves implications.

On the flip side of the negative consequences of inaction are the positive implications of making a change. Implications are often implied within goals. 

If you can get the prospect thinking about what they will do next when they achieve their goal, overcome a challenge and/or avoid a consequence, they'll start to visualize what success looks like. To explore implications, ask questions such as:

"When you achieve goal X, what will you do next?"

or

"When you no longer have to worry about consequence Y, how will you feel?"

Often times this will help you understand personal motivations of different decision makers.


To recap, after you've covered CGP, which is your prospect's challenges, goals, and plans, you need to look at TCI, which is their timeline, consequences, and implications. Once you've helped them define a timeline and you've drawn out of them the negative consequences of inaction and the positive implications of making a change, they'll see the urgency of their situation and be ready to get into the logistics of moving forward with your solution.

title: Bid by placement campaign
body:
A bid by placement campaign is a campaign in which you spend some extra budget on the top placements in Amazon. This option is only triggered when the advertisement is bidding for one of these top positions. 

It is possible to add bid by placement to an existing campaign, however, to make it easier to check and keep control over the campaign (it needs to be checked every few days, otherwise the cost could get out of hand) the standard practice is to create a new campaign for the products in the bid by placement campaign.


To create a bid by placement campaign follow these steps:

  1. Log in on ASC (for the client you are working for) → Click: ‘advertising’ → ‘campaign manager’.


  2. Make sure the campaign manager is set to the right country (you can find/change this in the top right corner of the screen)

  3. Click: ‘create campaign’ and choose ‘sponsored products’

  4. Choose manual targeting and add the keywords that have proven (based on the automatic and manual campaigns) to have a structurally low ACOS and a lot of sales. Use:
       -   Exact, (In order for the advertisement to show, a shopper’s search term must
           match the search term exactly)
       -   Phrase (optional), (The advertisement may appear when a shopper searches
           for either the exact phrase of search terms or sequence of words in your
           search terms) 

  5. Select at the ‘campaign Bidding Strategy’: Dynamic Up & Down/Fixed Bidding

  6. Click the blue link: ‘adjust bids by placement’

  7. Add the percentage that can be offered extra at ‘top of searches’. Start with 25-50% to prevent high costs at the beginning

  8. Create an ad group for the products you want to make the bid by placement campaign for and select the same settings as this product was successful within the manual campaign

  9. When using phrase matches, add negative keywords

  10. Quality check: Make sure everything is set correctly → Click: ‘Submit for review’ in the top right corner.

title: E-commerce platform
subcategory: WIP - Marketplace and E-commerce IT set-up 101
body:
An e-commerce platform is a software application where both parties, the seller and the consumer, come and play their role. Essentially, a consumer should be able to use an e-commerce platform to discover products, shop around using a cart, and then check out.

The three main types of e-commerce platforms are: 

  • SaaS (software-as-a-service) platforms
  • PaaS (platform-as-a-service) platforms
  • On-premises platforms

SaaS and PaaS platforms both deliver e-commerce solutions through the internet. SaaS platforms, like Shopify, involve software only. Once they add a hardware element as well, they become known as PaaS platforms. On-premise e-commerce solutions are hosted locally by the retailer and managed by their IT staff rather than being set up by another provider and accessed through the cloud.

Some of the most popular e-commerce platforms in the market today are Shopify, BigCommerce, Magento, WooCommerce, Wix, Big Cartel, Squarespace, Salesforce Commerce Cloud, and Volusion. No single e-commerce platform can offer everything to all customers. When choosing a platform to use, consider what specific needs you want the platform to address.

title: What causes error 90057?
subcategory: Amazon Error Solving SOP Overview - internal
body:
What causes error 90057?

This error occurs when you have submitted data in a field that is not one of the pre-determined set of valid values required for the field.

Samples:

ResultMessageCode: 90057


ResultDescription: The Message/Price/StandardPrice/@currency field contains an invalid value: EUR. To correct this error, choose from the valid set of values. 


ResultMessageCode: 90057


ResultDescription: The Country of Origin field contains an invalid value: Europe (exc. United Kingdom and Germany). To correct this error, choose from the valid set of values. 


ResultMessageCode: 90057


ResultMessageCode:90057


ResultDescription: The Product Type field contains an invalid value: Video Games. To correct this error, choose from the valid set of values.

How to solve and prevent error 90057?

To resolve this error, your solution should replace the invalid value with one of the accepted values provided in ‘Valid Values’ tab in category specific flat files. (Download category specific template from here


To prevent this error always provide valid values to fields based on ‘Valid Values’ tab in flat files.


Note: You might see different valid values for same field in different categories. For Ex: A valid variation theme in Beauty is ‘Size-Scent,’ however, ‘Size-Scent’ is not a valid variation theme in the Apparel category. Hence, you are advised to check ‘Valid Values’ tab on the category-specific flat file in which you are listing. The error will also occur for using non-marketplace specific values. I.e. the use of ‘GBP’ as the standard currency for a marketplace where ‘EUR’ is the Standard currency.

Please refer to http://docs.developer.amazonservices.com/en_UK/feeds/Feeds_EU_Global_Seller.html, before sending feeds to multiple marketplaces.

title: AI - Prompt Writing Tips
body:
Prompt Writing Tips

Effective prompt writing is crucial for getting the best responses from AI models like ChatGPT. Here are some tips to help you craft clear and useful prompts:

  1. Be Specific and Clear

    • Clearly state what you need. The more specific your prompt, the more accurate and relevant the response will be.

    • Example: Instead of saying, "Tell me about marketing," specify, "Can you explain the benefits of social media marketing for small businesses?"

  2. Provide Context

    • Include relevant context or background information. This helps the AI understand the situation and provide a more tailored response.

    • Example: "I'm drafting an email to a client who is concerned about recent delays in product delivery. Can you help me write a response that addresses their concerns and assures them of improved service?"

  3. Define the Desired Format

    • Specify the format of the response you’re looking for, such as bullet points, a list, a formal letter, or a conversational reply.

    • Example: "Can you provide a list of key strategies for improving SEO?"

  4. Ask for Examples or Elaborations

    • If you're asking for an explanation, consider requesting examples to clarify complex concepts.

    • Example: "What are the advantages of using influencer marketing? Can you give examples of successful campaigns?"

  5. Limit the Scope if Necessary

    • If the topic is broad, narrow it down to ensure the response is manageable and relevant.

    • Example: Instead of asking, "How can I improve my business?" ask, "What are some effective digital marketing strategies for a small online retail business?"

  6. Avoid Ambiguity

    • Be clear about what you want to avoid misunderstandings. Ambiguous prompts can lead to off-target responses.

    • Example: Instead of asking, "What's the best way to increase sales?" specify, "What are some cost-effective strategies for increasing online sales for an e-commerce store?"

  7. Use Open-Ended Questions for Exploratory Answers

    • Use open-ended questions to encourage detailed responses and insights.

    • Example: "What are some emerging trends in e-commerce that small businesses should be aware of?"

  8. Combine Related Questions

    • If you have multiple questions on the same topic, combine them into a single prompt to get a comprehensive answer.

    • Example: "What are the key benefits of email marketing, and how can I measure its effectiveness?"

By following these tips, you can improve the quality of the responses you receive, making your interactions with AI more productive and informative.

title: Introduction
body:
One of the most important parts of working at Brandsom is fulfilling our client's needs. This is simply because they are the reason we get to work. Therefore, you want to build a strong working relationship with the client. To achieve this,  good communication is necessary to figure out their goals and needs and to let them know what we can do to help. After all, you want to keep the client satisfied. How do we do this? And what exactly is good communication and how do we keep it businesslike?

One way of communicating is by e-mail. Another frequently used option is calling the client by phone. It is very important to write and talk in a formal way at the early stages of the business relationship.


When to call, e-mail or plan a Google Meet?

We believe that a periodic meeting structure is the core of good communication with the customer. Besides this, we use the Voys (internet phone) and e-mail.

  • Try to discuss all topics in the (bi)weekly, monthly and quarterly meetings. If you receive e-mails in the meantime discuss the also during these meetings.
  • Second way of communication method is e-mail because it's clearer, faster and more reliable for the customer.
  • Third way of communicating is calling the customer using Voys. Sometimes it's quicker to just call the customer, if you call the customer please use Voys.
  • The customer can call the generic phone number (please check the site for the latest number) and re-routed if needed.


Tone of voice 

When you communicate with a client, it is important to use a formal tone of voice. This indicates the way and the tone in which you write or speak to a client. Your tone of voice should be professional and friendly. This way of communicating will be explained in detail in this module.

title: Bol.com regulation
body:
What is not allowed:

  • Sending items directly to the customer from your own address or distribution center outside the European Union.

  • Sending items directly to the customer from a third-party address or distribution center outside the European Union.

  • Having your company based outside the European Union, even if you deliver orders from within the European Union.

What is allowed:

  • Delivering packages from (your own address or distribution center in) the Netherlands or Belgium.

  • Delivering packages from (your own address or distribution center in) another country within the European Union, such as France or Germany.

  • Clearing items from outside the European Union on your own behalf and at your own expense, storing them within the European Union, and then shipping them to the customer.

title: Starting an Exploratory call
body:
The way you start an exploratory call can have a huge effect on how the rest of the call goes. There are three things you need to do at the beginning of the call to set yourself up for success:

  1. Build rapport
  2. Recap what you learned in previous conversations
  3. Set an agenda for the call


Rapport building should start before you get on the call - spend some time researching your contact on LinkedIn and reviewing their history in your CRM. Once you get on the call, ask some open-ended questions to get the conversation started. It's important to keep this part of the call from going on too long. Too much rapport building can make you seem like an over-eager relationship seller.


In your initial call, you should have set some expectations and secured buy-in for how this call would go. To get your follow-up call off to a good start, the easiest thing to do is pick up where you left off with something like:

"The other day, when we spoke, you shared a few challenges that you're frustrated with. You mentioned that it's preventing you from achieving some important company goals. Can you expand a bit more on what you're dealing with?"

Many prospects will repeat what you discussed and expand on their challenges, giving you a bunch of details. Getting your prospect to open up right away is a great way to get started. After this, you should ask some follow-up questions and repeat some of these issues, so that they know you're listening effectively. By getting the prospect to talk first, you can ensure your agenda will be focused on the challenges they've acknowledged.


When setting an agenda, use what you discovered about the prospect from your research, your initial conversation, and anything they just shared with you during the beginning of the conversation.


Here's an example of how a conversation could go:

You:
We can definitely cover that today. But before we get too far into that topic, I suggest we set an agenda. I know we booked x minutes today. Does that still work for you? 

Prospect:
Yes

You:
Typically, a good goal for this call is to really figure out how I can best help you.

I've worked with (many) companies like yours who were struggling with the challenge you've acknowledged. I can certainly share some advice based on my previous work with them. But I find that everyone is a bit different.

So, it usually makes sense for me to understand more context about your goals, other challenges you might be facing or anticipate facing, any plans you have in place to address those goals and challenges, as well as timelines and other constraints that you might have. Are you comfortable having that conversation with me today? 

Prospect:
Yes, that sounds good

You:
I suggest we treat this conversation like a two-way dialogue. I have a bunch of questions for you. I'm sure you'll have some questions that I will certainly answer for you. 

Then, at the end of the call, we can decide whether it makes sense for us to continue discussing how we can formally help you.

title: Meetings
body:
Sales meeting 

For sales meetings, it is important that you know quite a lot about the environment Brandsom is situated in. So do not hesitate to ask questions about stuff you don’t know and gain as much knowledge prior to the meeting. Learn about the lead you are about to have a sales meeting with and make sure you know what you will ask him/her. What are their needs and how could Brandsom assist them? How do our services align with the needs of the customer? Present yourself confidently and do not be afraid to not know some stuff most sales meetings are really not as scary as you might initially think. Be transparent about what you know and if you do not know something just let the lead kindly know you will get back to them about that and you will consult an expert within the team regarding that specific question.


It is also highly recommended that you attend a meeting of, for example, Joris or Thijs. See how they go about it.They first try to have small talk with the lead, then they ask the lead if they can tell them anything about their company and the company's needs. Then they give a pitch about Brandsom (that Brandsom is a company that helps other companies to sell successfully on marketplaces and how we do this, for example with a consultant for strategic sessions, content optimization and advertisement). Then they ask questions to get a better idea of the lead, explain the proposal and put it in the mail.Try to have this process of the meeting clear, take good notes and really try to help the lead.

In addition, it is very important to make good agreements with the customer and always ends with a call to action. So always agree on what the next step is. 

Google calender 

Put the call to action in your calendar so you don't forget to call or email back.

title: Per customer
body:
For each customer, an advertising work file is created per marketplace. 

In this work file, you fill in the following items every week:

  • Ads analyse overzicht 
  • Wijzigingen 

Ads Analysis overview

In this overview, you fill in a number of details for each week. This information allows us to check how the advertisements are doing. 


Data:

  • GMV - total: Total turnover. This information is taken from the business report of Amazon, for example.  
  • GMV - paid: turnover generated by advertisements. This information is in the report of the ad.
  • GMV - non paid: GMV total Minus (-)
  • GMV paid

Ad spend: How much is spent on the ads. This information is included in the report of the ad. Views: How many times the ads were shown: This information is included in the report of the ad. Clicks: How many times were the ads clicked on: This information is displayed in the report of the ad.

Conversions: How many times were sales made via the ads. This information is included in the report of the ad.

ACOS: Ad spend / GMV - Paid

TACOS: Ad spend / GMV - total

Adjustments

For each marketplace and each country, you note what changes have been made for each campaign. Did you exclude keywords, or did you change bids? You also need to make a note of the reason for these changes. 
The document contains a drop-down menu for the possibilities.

title: Photoshop Tutorial for Beginners 2022
Bol Assortment Analysis
body:
The assortment analysis is mainly used to determine the products that have to biggest potential to succeed on Amazon. Before you start, you should discuss with the client if they have products they think have potential, for example via historical sales on a different marketplace are their own webshop.


In the assortment analysis you want to determine:

  • Is there sufficient search volume on the most important keywords?

  • Does the price point of the client fits with the market average?


Also it is important to discuss with the client a potential discount during launch phase. This drastically increases the potential success of the product launch.


The best way to do keyword research for Bol is via Bollify. This tool will show you directly multiple keywords and the search volume on the specific keywords.

  • Go to Bollify > Discover > Magnet

  • Type in the most important keyword, and Bollify will show you all related keywords

  • If there is sufficient demand and the median of the price is in line with the client’s price, you can focus on this product

title: Health and Safety
body:
Health and Safety Policy for Brandsom

At Brandsom, we are committed to providing a safe and healthy work environment for all our employees, clients, and visitors. Our health and safety policy is designed to prevent accidents and illnesses, promote well-being, and ensure compliance with all relevant health and safety regulations. This policy outlines our commitment to maintaining a safe workplace and the responsibilities of everyone involved.

1. Commitment to Safety

Brandsom is dedicated to:

  • Ensuring a safe and healthy workplace for all employees, clients, and visitors.
  • Complying with all applicable Dutch health and safety laws, regulations, and standards.
  • Continually improving our health and safety practices through regular review and assessment.

2. Responsibilities

Management Responsibilities:

  • Provide and maintain safe working conditions, equipment, and systems.
  • Investigate all incidents, accidents, and near misses, and take corrective action to prevent recurrence.
  • Promote a culture of safety awareness and encourage employees to participate in health and safety initiatives.

Employee Responsibilities:

  • Follow all health and safety policies, procedures, and guidelines.
  • Report any hazardous conditions, near misses, or accidents to your supervisor immediately.
  • Cooperate with management in the investigation of accidents and the implementation of corrective actions.

3. Health and Well-being

  • Promote the physical and mental well-being of all employees.
  • Encourage a healthy work-life balance.

4. Reporting and Investigating Incidents

  • Encourage prompt reporting of all health and safety incidents, including near misses.
  • Investigate all incidents thoroughly to determine root causes and prevent recurrence.
  • Maintain records of all incidents and corrective actions taken.

5. Continuous Improvement

  • Regularly review and assess health and safety policies and practices.
  • Seek feedback from employees on health and safety matters and incorporate their input into improvements.
  • Stay informed about advancements in health and safety practices and implement best practices as appropriate.

By adhering to this policy, we aim to create a safe and healthy work environment where everyone at Brandsom can thrive. If you have any questions or need further information about our health and safety practices, please contact HR.

title: Advertisement job profile
body:
Advertisement Specialist (Junior / Medior / Senior)

  1. The Function

Advertisement Specialist
The Advertisement Specialist plays a critical role in driving product visibility, increasing traffic, and improving search rankings on marketplaces through strategic advertising. This position provides valuable insights into consumer behaviour, enabling data-driven and targeted advertising strategies that maximize performance.

This role requires a balance of analytical skills, campaign management, and continuous optimization, utilizing AI-driven automation to enhance efficiency. Specialists are responsible for planning, executing, and refining advertising campaigns across various marketplaces.

    Reporting Line
    The Advertisement Specialist reports to the Senior Advertisement Specialist.


    Your Responsibilities

    The Advertisement Specialist is responsible for developing and executing marketplace advertising strategies, analyzing campaign performance, and driving continuous improvement. The role evolves from foundational execution to strategic leadership, AI implementation and innovation as experience grows.

    High-Level Responsibilities

    • Campaign Execution & Optimization: Set up, manage, and refine marketplace advertising campaigns to maximize performance.
    • Performance Analysis: Monitor key performance indicators (TACOS, ACOS, conversion rates) and implement data-driven optimizations. 
    • Keyword & Audience Targeting: Conduct keyword research and audience analysis to improve ad targeting and efficiency. 
    • AI & Automation: Leverage AI-driven tools to automate bidding, targeting, and reporting processes.
    • Budget Management: Monitor and allocate ad spend effectively to optimize return on investment. 
    • Process Improvement: Continuously enhance advertising strategies and workflows, implementing scalable solutions.

    Role-Specific Responsibilities

    Junior Advertisement Specialist

    • Set up and manage advertising campaigns across various marketplaces Like Amazon and Bol.
    • Monitor and optimize advertisements based on key performance indicators (e.g. TACOS, ACOS, conversion rates).
    • Conduct keyword analysis and optimization using tools like Helium 10.
    • Perform weekly advertisement checks to track campaign performance and ensure efficiency.
    • Analyze conversion rates to improve click-through rates, retargeting, and overall sales.
    • Support brand awareness efforts for product launches, rebranding, and promotions.
    • Utilize Google Sheets for client reporting, project management, and budget tracking.

    Medior Advertisement Specialist

    • Execute Junior-level responsibilities with full efficiency and expertise.
    • Take ownership of automation implementation and contribute to AI-driven enhancements.
    • Organize products into appropriate ad groups based on keywords, pricing, and performance metrics.
    • Analyze advertising reports to identify trends and propose data-driven optimizations.
    • Develop and refine advertising strategies, providing recommendations to senior specialists.
    • Provide insights to Senior Specialists on campaign efficiency and quality (E.g. Latest AI trends about advertising on marketplaces).
    • Estimate project timelines and align execution with strategic priorities.
    • Contribute to knowledge-sharing and workflow improvements within the advertising team.

    Senior Advertisement Specialist

    • Own and lead marketplace advertising strategies, ensuring best-in-class execution.
    • Oversee budget management for advertising campaigns, optimizing spend and efficiency.
    • Drive the continuous development and innovation of advertising methodologies within the company.
    • Drive AI-powered optimization strategies for advertising success on marketplaces.
    • Guide and mentor Junior and Medior Specialists, ensuring professional growth and performance improvements.
    • Develop and implement internal training programs (Academy/workshops) to enhance team expertise.
    • Advise consultants and clients on advertising best practices, trends, and performance strategies.
    • Identify and propose process improvements, ensuring seamless advertising operations.
    • Provide input to the COO for performance evaluations of Junior and Medior Specialists (senior specialist is responsible for doing the evaluations).
    • Lead strategic meetings with consultants to discuss performance insights, campaign enhancements, and promotional strategies.
    • Client-facing responsibilities: Includes quarterly strategic meetings with Brandsom’s largest clients, one -of the clients managed by the advertising team (e.g. clients that solely opt for the advertising services of Brandsom) and ad-hoc clarifications of implemented strategies and results. 

     

    1. How to Make Impact

    On the customer side

    Develop and execute advertising strategies that maximize product visibility, conversion rates, and overall marketplace success. Provide data-driven insights that enhance ad performance and drive brand growth.

    On the process side

    Implement AI-driven automation and analytical tools to streamline advertising campaign management, reducing manual workload while improving efficiency and scalability.

    On the quality side

    Ensure that campaigns are optimized for peak performance through continuous monitoring, trend analysis, and strategic adjustments, leveraging automation and data-driven insights.

    1. Your Competencies

    Core Knowledge & Skills

    • Strong understanding of marketplace advertising, including bid strategies, keyword optimization, and performance metrics, specifically related to Amazon and Bol.
    • Ability to analyze campaign data and implement performance-driven optimizations.
    • Proficiency in advertising tools such as Helium 10, Perpetua, and Google Sheets.

    Analytical & Innovation Abilities

    • AI-oriented approach to automate and optimize advertising strategies.
    • Data-driven decision-making to continuously improve campaign efficiency.
    • Continuous learning mindset, staying updated on marketplace advertising trends and automation advancements.

    Leadership & Management Skills (Senior Level)

    • Proven ability to mentor and guide a team of advertisement specialists.
    • Strong communication skills to align stakeholders on advertising strategies and performance improvements.
    • Budget management expertise to allocate and optimize ad spending effectively.

     

    5. Your Experience

    Junior Level

    • At least 1 year of hands-on experience in marketplace/e-commerce advertising.
    • Basic knowledge of campaign setup, KPIs (e.g., TACOS/ACOS), and reporting tools.
    • Experience with Excel or Google Sheets for data analysis (e.g. understanding how to use V-look up in day-to-day tasks).
    • Strong motivation to develop expertise in advertising automation and AI tools (e.g. using ChatGPT prompts).

    Medior Level

    • Minimum of 2-3 years of experience in marketplace advertising.
    • Proven ability to execute and optimize advertising campaigns independently.
    • Strong analytical skills for interpreting reports and making data-driven optimizations.
    • Familiarity with audience analysis techniques and Share of Voice metrics.
    • Good expertise in advertising automation and AI tools (e.g. using ChatGPT prompts).

    Senior Level

    • Minimum of 2-3 years of experience as a Medior Advertisement Specialist.
    • Proven track record in managing advertising projects and strategic decision-making.
    • Experience leading teams and mentoring junior professionals in advertising.
    • Strong understanding of budget allocation, bid strategies, and AI-driven automation.



    Future Growth of the Role

    As marketplace advertising continues to evolve, this role will transition toward AI-driven ad optimization, predictive analytics, and automated bidding strategies. The focus will shift from manual campaign adjustments to data-driven automation, audience personalization, and real-time performance optimization, ensuring scalable and efficient advertising solutions.

title: From presentation to asana tasks (only for consultants)
body:
Intro

We have won a new customer and you will be the new consultant for this customer. In this academy you will learn how to handle the process of getting a client to your name to scheduling the asana tasks. 


  • You should have received the powerpoint with all the information you need from the sales team. This contains, among other things, the goals the customer wants to achieve, so please discuss this with the customer as well. 
    You will have a meeting with the customer every fortnight or every other month, depending on the size of the customer. If you have a monthly meeting, make sure you send an update every two weeks about these goals, these goals will also be discussed in the meetings with the customer. Think about the Target KPI's per month, quarter, year (TCOS, Turnover, Returns, when which integration).
    From the powerpoint, try to get the tasks clear in asana. You can divide this into (example Lucardi):
  • Recurring tasks
  • Tasks for each marketplace per week planned in advance
  • Exclusive consultancy tasks
    Try to plan these tasks in Asana at least 3 months ahead to create a customer framework. This makes it clear for the customer and for us with the planning.

title: Zalando Advertising ZMS
body:
Zalando Advertising ZMS

ZMS Ad Manager is the tool created by Zalando and is used to manage your campaigns on Zalando. You can find all there is to know about the tool via the URLs at the bottom of this academy. 

When creating campaigns, keep in mind the following:

  • The minimum budget for a well-performing campaign starts at €500. According to Zalando, the minimum duration is 3 weeks. 
  • Zalando divides the budget by the number of days the campaign should run. You can increase or decrease bids by changing the campaign duration. For example: A high budget, with a short duration means an aggressive bidding strategy. 

  • Avoid putting the same product in multiple ad groups with the same ad placement type. For example: if you put SKU1 in 'co-curated products ad group 1' and  'co-curated products ad group 2' they will compete against each other. It is allowed (when future placements or through ZMS managed) to place SKU in 2 different ad placement types For example: SKU 1 in  'co-curated products ad group 1' and  'in category ads ad group 1' 

  • If your campaign is performing well: increase the budget and extend the duration of your campaign. 

  • According to Zalando, a good ROAS is 3.0+. Very good performing campaigns have a ROAS of 7.0+ 

Recommendations:

  • Create ad groups per country

  • Create ad groups per category

  • Create ad groups per price (discount, full price, EOSS)

How to optimize your Zalando Ad Campaigns?

TBD

Zalando Ad Payments

Clients will receive a separate invoice for their Zalando Ad Spend.

title: Choosing the reversed or traditional model.
body:
Now, at Brandsom, we convert the touch, tell, sell, care model into a product conversion focus by using the reversed or traditional touch, tell, sell, care method.

Traditional versus reversed funnel:



Choosing the reversed or traditional approach for aw brand or client, depends on different factors:


  • The price of the product: Generally, the more expensive the more likely you should work in the traditional touch, tell and sell order to convert traffic into happy customer orders.

  • Complexity of the product: Generally, the more complex the more likely you should work in touch, tell and sell order to convert traffic into happy customer orders.

  • For consumer products that are not too expensive or complex: The reversed strategy works best, meaning you can start at the Sell phase. Because:

    • You have time to optimize product, price and content to improve conversion even more while selling the product at the same time. By starting to sell, the brand collects data and insights about its customers and its products which is useful to optimize. Increasing the traffic in the touch and tell phase will then directly benefit from the conversion optimizations.

    • You relatively gain instant revenue compared to a complex more expensive product. 

      • Note: this does not necessarily mean that the ultimate turnover and profit is higher following the traditional or reversed strategy, since the extra invested budget in touch and tell can make up for the higher price of the product.

title: OTP for Amazon accounts
body:
To start using OTP, follow this step-by-step guide:

  1. Begin by installing the Google Authenticator app. It is available for Android, IOS, and BlackBerry.


2. After downloading the app, log in to Amazon Seller Central on the account you want to activate the OTP for.


3. By doing so, you should see the following screen:


4. In order to log in for the first time, you will need someone to give you the six-digit password.


5. After successfully logging in, navigate to Settings on the top right corner and click on login settings in the drop-down menu.


6. Manage your Two-Step Verification (2SV) by clicking on "Edit" 


7. Select "Add new app"



8. Now it's time to open the Google Authenticator app


9. Click on the '+' icon at the bottom of the screen and scan the QR-code that is shown on your computer screen.


10. The app will now generate an OTP. Fill in the password and click on verify OTP and continue.



11. After doing all this, the OTP will be permanently stored in Google's Authenticator app.

title: Amazon A+ Content
body:
What is Amazon A+ Content

With Amazon A+ content you can present products in a different way by uploading extra descriptions, images, and brand stories. With A+ content you basically have an extra advertisement on the product page. If A+ content is uploaded correctly then it can result in more traffic, higher conversion rates, and more sales. Some examples of products with A+ Content:

  • BRITA
  • Airmax
  • M Line

As you may notice, there is a difference between the image sizes of the Airmax content and the other A+ Content pages. This has to do that the other pages are using A+ Premium Content. Follow the below steps to receive A+ Premium Content:

  • Have a Brand Story for all products in your catalog
  • Have at least 15 submissions of 'regular' A+ content.
  • Amazon checks this once per month, so it can take some time before you get access.

Amazon A+ Content Strategies

Within Brandsom, we cover four types of strategies that can be achieved via A+ Content. We will briefly discuss these strategies below:

  • Up-Sell
  • Cross-Sell
  • Conversion
  • Branding

Conversion

Definition: A term used in marketing that is used to describe when a visitor achieves a marketing goal or completes another action. When used in online retail, conversion is generally used to describe the conversion that takes place when a visitor on a site makes a purchase. 

Target: Boosting sales through conversion.

About Conversion of A+ content - the three elements you must look at:

  • It provides potential customers with USP information (unique selling points) of products.
  • It provides potential customers with general information that gives further insights into the products that are advertised.
  • When possible, information regarding the product line (to provide customers with information regarding the product set) 
    Note: This does not relate to all products. If the product line isn't available, little information about the category (style, purpose etc.) can be provided.

Cross-selling and Upselling

Definition of Cross-selling: To sell related or complementary products to a customer.

Definition of Upselling: To encourage a customer to buy a higher-end version of a product than what they originally intended to purchase.

Target: Both upselling and cross-selling are methods of increasing sales to existing customers but use slightly different approaches in doing so.

Cross-selling and upselling with A+ content:

  • A+ content used for products that are part of a set of products should give general information, supported by bullet points with the most important features.
  • Provide customers with 4 different products to cross- or upsell; include a simple description for each product.
  • Make a comparison chart available that shows the main differences between the products to help customers decide which product suits them best.

Branding

Definition: The promotion of a particular product by means of advertising and distinctive design.

Target: Bind customers to a certain brand.

About branding and A+ content:

  • Provide general information about the brand. Especially useful for niche products that do not generate enough revenue.
  • Ensuring a positive experience with the product/brand to gain repeat customers/purchases.
  • This strategy can be used for all products that don't fit the other two strategies.


Useful URL's

  • Brandsom A+ Content Strategy Slidedeck
  • Generic A+ Content information
  • A+ Content Guidelines
  • How to create A+ content

title: Working from home
body: Working from Home: Freedom with Responsibility At Brandsom, we value the balance between freedom and responsibility. As part of our commitment to flexibility and work-life balance, we offer the privilege of working from home up to 3 days per week. This flexibility is designed to support you in achieving a better work-life balance while also empowering you to take responsibility for your work and performance. The Freedom to Work from Home: At Brandsom, we enjoy the flexibility of working both from home and in the office. If you're performing well, you have the freedom to work from home up to 3 days per week. However, if your performance is not meeting expectations, it is required that you work from the office daily to receive the support and guidance you need to improve. Working from home allows you the freedom to manage your time and environment in a way that best suits your needs. Whether it’s focusing on a big project, avoiding long commutes, or simply needing quiet time to think, we trust you to make the most of these days. The Responsibility Behind It: While working from home offers flexibility, it comes with the responsibility to ensure that your work continues to meet the high standards we expect at Brandsom. This includes: Delivering quality work on time and meeting deadlines. Logging your hours accurately and consistently, just as you would in the office. Maintaining communication with your team and manager to ensure that collaboration remains smooth. Adhering to any other relevant guidelines, such as availability for meetings and team check-ins. How It Aligns with Our Values: The freedom to work from home is a privilege, not a right. It aligns with our core values of freedom and responsibility: Freedom: You have the autonomy to manage your work environment and schedule. Responsibility: You are expected to be accountable for your performance, your hours, and your contributions to the team, even when working remotely. Making the Most of Working from Home: To ensure that remote work is as effective as possible for both you and the team, here are a few best practices: Create a Dedicated Workspace: Set up a space that allows you to focus and be productive. Stick to a Routine: Maintain a consistent work schedule to stay organized and meet your deadlines. Communicate Effectively: Use tools like Slack, email, and video calls to stay connected with your team and manager. Be Available for Meetings: Ensure that you are reachable during working hours, especially for scheduled meetings. If You Face Challenges: If you’re having trouble maintaining productivity or meeting expectations while working from home, we’re here to help. We’ll work with you to identify any challenges and find a solution to ensure that you can continue to thrive, both at home and at the office.

title: Brandsom's Verticals
body:
Brandsom is divided into vertical teams: Consumer electronics (CE), Home and Garden (HG), Consumable Products (CP) and Fashion. Each team is then divided into four specialists and are led by a (junior) consultant. There is always a senior consultant that is responsible for the entire team. The four specialists are a channel/integrator specialist, an advertisement specialist, a content specialist and an operational specialist.
Our clients, the brands that we support, fall into one of these categories, based on what they offer. The goal is to put our people in a specific team, on a specific specialism or area. This way someone can evolve and gain their own expertise about a certain marketplace, integrator or brand.

The channel/integrator specialist will mainly focus on the channel integrator software and is specialised in integrating and giving support on a specific integrator and channel.


The advertisement and content specialists will focus on anything content-related and will create advertisements and content for brands. These specialists are trained to deliver optimal content for a specific brand and/or platform and are up-to-date on all the latest developments or changes in policies.


Lastly, the operational specialist will concentrate on the logistic side of the process, by implementing and then supporting fulfilment, answering technical questions about stock or orders, and in some cases managing or supporting good customer service.


  • Consumer electronics: Brands that fall into this category sell consumer electronics; electronic equipment intended for everyday use, at home or at the office, such as laptops, cables, and tools. 
  • Home and Garden: All brands that sell home goods like lamps, mirrors, chairs or garden supplies, such as flowers and plants.
  • Consumable products: These are goods used by individuals and businesses that must be replaced regularly because they wear out or are used up, such as office supplies (paper, pens, ink cartridges etc.) or fast-moving consumer goods like food. Other examples of businesses that sell consumable products are VitaminFit and Herbie's Herbs.
  • Fashion: The fashion team is split up into three teams. All fashion and accessory related brands are put into this category. Team members of fashion have a different specialism than the other 3 teams because, as stated before, fashion is a separate branch within the realm of marketplaces.

title: Analysis of Brand for Project/Setup Scope
body:
Objective

Ensure Brandsom can successfully manage a new client’s marketplace performance by conducting an analysis of their product data and marketplace accounts, and determine the project or setup scope.

Overview

There are 3 parties involved:

  • Sales: Livia
  • Consultants: Matthijs and Lotte
  • Integration Specialist: Betul

Sales will be the one to ensure all information is collected, and access is provided for the Consultant. The Consultant will be the one performing the Analysis, and the Integration Specialist will only be needed if the Consultant deems it necessary (checking the brands marketplace integrator environment). 

Lead Types:

  • Fashion - Lotte will perform the analysis
  • Non-Fashion - Matthijs will perform the analysis

Step 1: Sales Team Actions

Information and Account Access   

The consultant will need to be able to have certain material to perform the analysis. Sales will need to do the following:

  • Integrator Access 
    • Ask the brand to grant the consultant direct access to their marketplace integrator (if applicable) by having them add either Matthijs@brandsom.nl or Lotte@brandsom.nl as a user. All rights will need to be granted. 
  • Marketplace Seller Accounts Access
    • Amazon: Ask the brand to add the consultant’s email as a user with all rights.
    • Bol: Create an alias (BrandName@brandsom.nl) first, then ask the brand to add this alias as a user with all rights.
      • Ask Thijs or Joris to make the alias for you.
    • Zalando: Ask the brand to add consultant’s email as a user with all rights.
    • AboutYou: Ask the brand to add the consultant’s email as a user with all rights.
    • Other Marketplaces: Confirm with Thijs whether to use an alias or direct email for access.
  • Request Product Data
    • Ask for the complete product feed/data the brand currently uses or plans to use for selling on marketplaces.
  • Clarify Logistics Details
    • Confirm their current logistics setup and inquire if they’re interested in additional opportunities like marketplace fulfilment in the future (if they are not utilizing this already).

Asana Task Creation

Once all relevant information and account access has been granted, Sales will need to create and assign an Asana task to the consultant. This involves:

  • Create an Asana Task for the Analysis
    • Go to the ‘Sales Analysis New Prospects’ project in Asana, and create a task titled, ‘[Brand Name] Analysis - [High or Medium] Priority’
      • High Priority: Brands with over 500K marketplace revenue or well-known brands.
      • Medium Priority: Brands not live on a marketplace and with less than 5M in total revenue.
  • Provide a clear summary in the notes section, including:    
    • Company background and current situation.
    • Challenges and current marketplace standing.
    • Reseller presence and logistics details.    •    Key focus areas (ads, content, buy box, etc.).
    • Attach any relevant files or documents.
  • Set a Due Date
    • High Priority: 1 day
    • Medium Priority: 2 days
  • Assign the task to the relevant consultant
    • Lotte for Fashion
      • Matthijs for Non-Fashion
  • Share any required alias details via LastPass.


Step 2: Consultant Actions

Reviewing

The Consultant will need to review everything that Sales has listed in the Asana Task. Please be sure to accept any invitations from the brand or Last Pass.

Conduct the Analysis

An Analysis can now be performed! The consultant will need to:

  • Review Product Data
    • Assess content quality and variations.
  • Review Marketplace Integrator Environment  
    • Check statistics, mappings, errors, etc.
    • Ask Betel for help if needed (for example, many error messages are coming up so she may need to help you determine how extensive it is)
  • Review Seller Accounts
    • Evaluate the health and performance of accounts and ads.

Key Outcomes

After the Analysis, the consultant will need to determine 3 things:

  • Determine if the account is worth pursuing as a client.
    • If not, ask Thijs for a final assessment.
  • Estimate the number of hours required for the project or setup.
  • Identify key deliverables during the setup phase.

Present Findings to Sales

  • Fill in and adjust the Presentation 
    • Go to the Brand Analysis & Timeline TEMPLATE, and duplicate it.
      • Be sure to title it ‘[Brand Name] Analysis & Timeline’.
    • Input all findings into the presentation and adjust as needed.

Handover to Sales

Now that the consultant has made their analysis and adjusted the presentation, a 15 minute call will need to be scheduled to ensure a smooth handover. This can also be a time where Sales and the Consultant can determine if anything in the presentation needs to be adjusted.

Mark the Asana Task as Complete

Once the handover is done, mark the task complete in Asana.

This process is now complete, and Sales can create the proposal.

title: Secondly, making an analysis
body:
Creating an analysis such as looking at how many fashion companies visited our website in a specific month is significantly important as these companies can potentially be targeted through LinkedIn cold-acquisition. 

  1. Select the specific month you want to have data from by clicking on the left side of the screen. 

  2. Then select a label of your choice to view all the categorized leads who visited the website in that particular month.

Similarly, you can export an Excel file with all the categorized companies within a specific month. You do this by;

  1. Go to “Exporteren” on the top of the screen.

  2. Then as mentioned above, select the specific month and label you wish you obtain an excel file of.

  3. As follows, click export and select the columns you wish to export. 

  4. Then create the file.

Additionally, it is important to look at which pages the client visits as this can become useful when one of Brandsom’s sales agents contacts one of the company representatives through LinkedIn and send a message related to their needs. It also provided an insight into Brandsom’s website as it demonstrates which pages on the website gain traction. Furthermore, as part of the LinkedIn cold-acquisition, it is important to mirror the activity of the lead based on the data obtained through Leadinfo. If for example, a lead is looking at a blog about selling on Zalando, it may be useful to use this in the message you are going to send on LinkedIn.

title: Amazon Plan Of Action - High Defect Rate
body:
Below you will find a POA to reinstate an Amazon account which is blocked due to exceeding the target of the Defect Rate. Please customize the plan and make adjustments where needed, DO NOT use this plan 1:1.

---------------------------------------------------------

Plan of action - High defect rate for CLIENT X

The root cause of the issue

What was the primary cause for your defect rate exceeding the target of 1%


The defect rate has been caused by defects in solely one parent listing: our bath bucket in different color variations. This is also the bestselling product in our catalog, and unfortunately there have been some defects in transport and in the product itself: Package is outside the dimensions of the conveyor belt and is processed manually. Unfortunately the product is large and also fragile in shipping. We ourselves failed to keep a closer eye on this and identify this issue before it caused problems, as the issue is primaly caused by the carrier and installation of the product.


The actions that you have taken to resolve the issue

 More details about the actions you have taken to resolve higher than expected defect rate.


  • We contacted the carrier to discuss the handling of the package: DHL. Instead of stacking the buckets, they will now pack the buckets with an extra outer box. 

  • Also discussed with the carrier: bath buckets will now be handled manually and taken extra care of with a fragile label attached to the box. 

  • Regarding the installation of the bath buckets, often the plug (water stop) didn’t fit or we noticed that consumers do not know how to install this. We have taken two actions here: 

    • 1. We discussed the matter with the manufacturer, and decided to replace the plug by a better fitting one. 

    • 2. We include an English installation manual in the box for the customer to instruct the customer better how to install the product. 

    • 3. We will also add the installation manual to the product content.


The steps that you have taken to prevent the issue going forward

 More details about the steps you have taken to prevent higher-than-expected defect rate in the future.

  • We improved transport: an extra outer box around the product, the product will be picked and handled manually, the box will get a fragility logo. 

  • We will keep in close contact with the carrier about the shipment of these specific products.

  • The improved water stop / plug and the manual how to install the product should lead to less questions /defects for the customer. We will keep a close eye on the a-z claims and consumer questions.

  • We started working with a specialized marketplace agency that consults us better in how to sell and how to operate on marketplaces to prevent this from happening in the future.


To summarize, we have invested in improving and streamlining our operational and logistical processes and will continue to keep an eye on quality of the product and shipment/packaging of the product. Above processes are already in place and therefore we are ready to sell on Amazon again with improved quality.

title: What causes error 8571?
subcategory: Amazon Error Solving SOP Overview - internal
body:
What causes error 8571?

You have reached the limit on the number of ASINs you are allowed to create. You can continue to match your products against existing ASINs. Please refer to the ASIN Creation Policy page for more information on how to avoid receiving this error message.


You might see this error if you have not yet established a sales history with Amazon. As you increase your sales, your capacity to create new ASINs will increase.

Sample:


ResultCode: Error


ResultMessageCode: 8571


ResultDescription: We have temporarily removed your ability to submit listings (creating new offers or new ASINs) because an unusually high number of listings have been created from your account. Please refer to the ASIN Creation Policy page for more information on how to avoid receiving this error message. If you believe your listing creation privileges have been removed in error, please contact Seller Support by using the following link: https://sellercentral-europe.amazon.com/cu/contact-us.

How to solve and prevent error 8571?

To resolve this error, educate seller to contact Seller Support by using the following link: https://sellercentral-europe.amazon.com/cu/contact-us.


Note: This error has an associative error with it i.e. 8566. Hence you should ignore 8566 error associated with the SKUs erred out with 8571.

title: Amazon AI Bullet Tool
body:
Amazon AI Bullet Tool

Within Brandsom we have developed an AI tool that can create bullet points in bulk for Amazon. This tool is typically used for a client's "non-focus" products. This way we can make sure we have quality content for this part of the assortment. To use this tool, you need to provide:

  • High-Quality Descriptions: Shit in is shit out. If the description only contains some bullet points, the AI can't create high-quality bullet points.
  • Bullet Template: To ensure the quality of the bullet points, provide the AI with a template for the bullet points. Ideally, a template per category is provided (E.g. a template for Bluetooth Speaker and a template for Airfryer). Please find an example below
  • Keywords: If you want to add keywords in the bullet points, make sure to provide a comma-separated list of keywords. Only provide highly relevant keywords, 3-5 keywords is the sweet spot!
  • Other information: Context of the product, this is usually the category. Make sure to provide an identifier (SKU, EAN) so you can "vlookup" the created bullet points and add them to the feed.

Example Bullet Template:

  • SIZES UP TO 3XL: Available in XS to 3XL, our Men's Modern fit tapered Scrub Trousers are designed for durability, style, and comfort, catering to all sizes.

  • ODOR FIGHTING, LONG WEARING: Featuring antimicrobial and moisture-wicking technologies, these Scrub Trousers stay wrinkle and stain-free, ensuring you remain fresh throughout your workday.

  • EXCEPTIONAL QUALITY: Crafted from a blend of 95% fabric and 5% Elastane poplin, our Trousers offer stretchability and lightweight comfort for medical professionals.

  • 6 POCKETS: Equipped with six convenient pockets, including a double cargo pocket with a bungee loop and a back patch pocket, to keep essential items secure.

  • FUNCTION AND FIT: Designed for medical professionals, these Cherokee Scrub Trousers include a fly zip with a drawstring waistband, rib-knit interior cell phone pocket, and side vents for optimal functionality and fit."

It is advised to create a template per category, this way you can instruct the AI tool to give more relevancy to the bullets.

Other information that is needed to create bullet points via AI:

  • Category (This is used to give the AI Tool context regarding the product type)
  • Keywords (The AI will try to use as many as possible, this can also be used for specific names. For example "Athletic Pillow" for M Line.

If you want to use the tool to create bullet points, send a request to Imco@brandsom.nl with a populated template. The template can be found in the attachments of this course.

NOTE: Do not change the headers in the template, they have to be identical for the tool to work!

At Brandsom, we are dedicated to fostering the growth and development of our employees. Our comprehensive training and development initiatives include the following:

Personal Development Plan:

This plan is reviewed and analyzed every three months to stimulate continuous growth, training, and education. title: Channable Amazon V2 Learnings
subtitle: In this knowledge artical you'll find all learnings that we have regarding the onboarding / migration of the Amazon V2 channel. This will hopefully save you some time in your onboarding process
body:
I am migrating to the Amazon V2 API, what steps should I take?

input Daniel/Lea

Error 100526 - Listing state - WARNING

This error is regarding the GPSR. Go to 'Manage Your Compliance' in Amazon.

On the right you'll find 'Add Compliance Information'. Here you need to add GPSR information. Make sure to discuss with the client.

300403 - Listing state - ERROR

This error is about your images. From my experience, you can ignore this error as it is most likely about your image not being compliant, which is mentioned in a different error.

title: Mails EN
body:
Mail 1
