title: Four pillars
body: Based on these four pillars it can be affirmed that to act on critical issues and achieve sustainability these principles need to be maintained. For businesses this means trying to incorporate as many pillars as possible within their business model in order to create a positive impact.

title: Working Conditions and Regulations
body:
Salary

When an employee joins Brandsom, they will be assigned to a job group with a salary based on the position within the company. This will be done in consultation, taking into account the employee’s previous knowledge and experience.
There is an annual possibility of a salary increase. The supervisor assesses the employee’s performance based on the job profile and the progress associated with it.


Holiday allowance

The employee is entitled to a holiday allowance of 8% of the gross annual salary. The allowance is accrued each month and paid out monthly. It is also possible to have the allowance paid out only once a year in May. The decision whether you want it paid out annually or monthly can be made one time a year before December 31. Talk to your supervisor about this.


Vacation days

Employees are entitled to 25 days of vacation per year, based on a 40-hour contract. Vacation days are taken in consultation with a supervisor. Regulations concerning vacation days can be found in the extra information regarding vacation days, at the end of this handbook.

These regulations may not apply to your position within the company. Make sure to check your employment contract to find out what arrangement applies to you.

National holidays:

On the days listed below, you are free as a full-time colleague. Are you scheduled as a flex worker? If so, you will work as normal. 

  • New Year's day
  • Christmas (first and second)
  • Easter
  • Pinksteren
  • Hemelvaart

Travel expenses

Employees that use public transport or a car to get to work receive reimbursement for commuting expenses if the distance between home and work is more than 10 kilometres. This compensation is € 0.23 per kilometer travelled, with a maximum of € 300 net per month. The employee must declare travel expenses on a monthly basis. Alternatively, you can also declare your direct public transport costs by uploading a receipt. 

An explanation of how to do this is explained in the extra information about ‘Declarations’ at the end of this handbook.

These regulations may not apply to your position within the company. Make sure to check your employment contract to find out what arrangement applies to you.


Leave

The standard rules of the Work and Care Act apply unless otherwise indicated. In addition to calamities and special leave, the Work and Care Act lays down a number of leave arrangements, which include:

  • Maternity and childbirth leave;
  • Parental leave;
  • Care leave (short and long term);
  • Adoption leave.


Visit rijksoverheid for up-to-date information on these arrangements.


Absence

Brandsom has an absence protocol that provides rules that an employee must follow during illness. The absence protocol can be found in the extra information about 'Absence protocol - Short absence' at the end of this handbook.


Progression

The employee has progress reviews with a supervisor

  • before the end of the probationary period;
  • before the end of the fixed-term contract (annually);
  • every 3 months focussing on personal development through your PDP. 


Company properties

Brandsom will make certain resources (laptop, monitor, keyboard, mouse) available for employees to help improve their performance. For some of these resources, a loan agreement is drawn up. At the end of the employment, these borrowed goods must be handed in. 


Expenses

Incurred expenses are always reimbursed through a declaration form. There is no fixed expense allowance. More information on declarations can be found under ‘Declarations’ in the extra information at the end of this handbook. All declarations must be discussed with your supervisor before the purchase has been made.


Confidentiality obligation

The employee shall be bound by strict confidentiality during and after the termination of employment. This applies to everything that comes to the employee’s knowledge in this position and profession. Both during and after the termination of employment, the employee is prohibited from directly or indirectly disclosing to third parties in any form and by any means, any knowledge related to matters and interests of the company, its customers or its business relations, such as acquired knowledge or information on procedures, know-how, marketplaces, accounts, strategies, administrative techniques (including, but not limited to Microsoft Office, Google, Apps etc.), integrators, goodwill, customer data (including, but not limited to sales data or any other analytical data) or any other relevant information of the business, its customers or its affiliates. This clause applies both to termination on the part of the employer and to termination on the part of the employee.


The employee is at all times prohibited from unlawfully obtaining, using or disclosing non-public know-how and business information, pursuant to the Trade Secrets Act. This clause applies both to termination on the part of the employer and to termination on the part of the employee.

title: Tooling Cost Overview
body:
1. Marketplace, Integrator, or Brandsom Academy Resources

Start by attempting to resolve the issue using available resources such as the Marketplace, Integrator, or Brandsom Academy (e.g., ChannelEngine Support). These platforms often have comprehensive guides and FAQs on most common errors and issues. 


2. Direct Colleagues

If the issue remains unresolved, consult with your direct colleagues within your team or with colleagues from other teams in a similar role to yours. 


3. Support Email

If further assistance is needed, reach out to the integrator support team via email (support@marketplace/integrator) or for an even quicker response, call them.


Important numbers:

Channelengine support: +31 715 288 795

Channable support: +31 (0)30 260 08 21

Vendiro support:  +31 (0)71 203 25 00


4. Consultant

Should the issue persist, escalate to a designated consultant. Ensure to include all relevant information and any guidance received from previous steps.


5. CSM or Contact Person at [Marketplace/Integrator]

If the consultant is unable to resolve the issue, contact your Customer Success Manager (CSM) in case of ChannelEngine or designated contact person at the marketplace or integrator. Provide a comprehensive report of the situation, including all previous communications and attempted solutions.


6. Lead

For issues that remain unresolved after following the previous steps, escalate to the lead.


7. Imco

As a last resort, if the issue is critical and has not been resolved through the above channels, escalate to Imco. This step should only be taken when all other avenues have been exhausted. If he can't solve it, nobody can. 


Important Notes

Always document each step of the escalation process, including dates, times, and individuals involved.

Urgent matters that have a significant impact on business operations may warrant a direct escalation to higher levels, bypassing certain steps as necessary.

title: Time Logging: Guidelines and Best Practices
body: At Brandsom, accurate and consistent time logging is crucial, not only for client billing but also to help us better understand where time is spent. This insight allows us to identify areas for improvement in our efficiency and the quality of our work. The following guidelines outline our expectations for time tracking within the team: Time Logging Requirements: Minimum Logging Standard: You must log at least 90% of your working hours. Of these logged hours, 85% must be billable (Hours that are logged on clients with the 'paid' field as: Managed, fixed fee, SKU fee or Retainer). Daily Logging: Time should be logged every day from Monday to Friday. This ensures that all hours worked are tracked and accounted for on a consistent basis. Using the Timer: Always use the Everhour timer to log your hours. This tool helps you accurately track the time spent on different tasks and provides detailed reports. If You’re Struggling to Log Hours: We understand that sometimes time logging can be challenging. If you're having difficulty logging your hours consistently or meeting the required standards, we’ll work together to find a suitable solution. Our goal is to help you improve your time tracking process so that it becomes an easy and natural part of your workday.

title: ChannelEngine Intro
body:
ChannelEngine's story began in 2013 when it became undeniable that marketplaces were going to be one of the most important elements of the global E-commerce market. We saw an opportunity to make these powerful platforms available to any business. Our 15+ years of E-commerce experience helped us build the most complete marketplace management suite and now, 8 years later, we facilitate the sale of over 3 million products from 1500 brands to over 100 marketplaces.
Source: https://www.channelengine.com/company/about-us/

Quality check by second translator/consultant

* Please keep in mind that the agency costs a lot more than in-house but is a lot quicker and more efficient.


Contact Agency

Francesca Zavettieri
francesca.zavettieri@translated.com

title: Declaration process (travel reimbursement + other expenses)
body:
This chapter provides detailed instructions on how employees can use Nmbrs to submit various expense declarations. If you have any questions regarding the declaration process, please contact your supervisor for more information.

Declaration of Travel Expenses

Employee:

You are eligible for a commuting allowance if the distance between your home and the office is more than 10 kilometers, and you use public transport or a car for commuting. The allowance is €0.23 per kilometer traveled, with a maximum of €300 net per month, or the direct costs of public transport. Please discuss the type of transport costs you can declare with HR beforehand.

  • Submission Timeline: Submit your travel expenses around the 15th of each month to ensure you receive the reimbursement within the same month.
  • Submission Steps:
    1. Visit Nmbrs and log in.
    2. If you don't have an account, email hr@brandsom.nl.
    3. Click on 'Declaratieformulier' (Declaration Form).
    4. Fill in the pop-up window. Ensure you provide a clear description for tax purposes. Review the overview and add any comments if necessary.
  • Important: Make a copy, fill in the number of kilometers traveled in the provided Excel file, and attach it to your declaration form.


Declaration of Other Expenses

Employee:

If you incur expenses on behalf of Brandsom, such as ordering office supplies or sending a card, you can claim these through Nmbrs as well. The process is similar to submitting travel expenses, but without the need to fill in the Excel sheet.

  • Submission Steps:
    1. Follow the same steps outlined for travel expenses in Nmbrs.


Approving Declarations

Supervisor:

When an employee submits an expense declaration, you will receive an automated email from Nmbrs.

  • Approval Steps:
    1. Visit Nmbrs and log in to your Brandsom account.
    2. If you don't have an account, email hr@brandsom.nl.
    3. In ‘Mutatieformulieren’ (Mutation Forms), you can see pending requests and those awaiting accountant approval.
    4. Review the declaration and attachments, then approve or request adjustments if necessary.

For further assistance, please contact HR.

title: Amazon Fulfillment types
body:
Fulfilment by Amazon (FBA) is a service that Amazon offers to sellers who want to outsource their fulfilment process. When choosing this option, Amazon will take care of the entire logistics process, including processing orders, customer service, packaging, shipping and returns. 

When opting for FBA, sellers have three options: 

  • European Fulfilment Network (EFN) 
  • Multi-Country Inventory (MCI)  
  • Pan-European FBA 
  • Multi-Channel Fulfillment 

In this course, we'll go over the advantages and disadvantages of each of the three options Amazon offers. In addition, we'll cover the related costs of each fulfilment option and explain how to get started with FBA.

Advantages of the European Fulfilment Network

  • You only need to store your products in one place.
  • Your stock management is well-organised.
  • You can focus all your marketing efforts on one country.
  • You will only need to apply for one VAT number, which is the country where your products are stored.
  • In addition to your Dutch tax return, you only have to file a return in the country you chose.


Disadvantages of the European Fulfilment Network

  • It is more difficult to serve other countries while using this fulfilment option. It is therefore particularly suitable if you want to focus on only one European country and less suitable if you want to really internationalise to multiple countries.
  • Shipments to countries where your products are not stored take longer, which could be a reason for customers to choose a competitor's offer over yours.
  • You pay cross-border fees when you receive orders from other countries.  

Advantages of Multi-Country Inventory

  • Sell more easily to customers in various countries in Europe as customers receive faster, local delivery when your products are located in fulfilment centres close to where they live. 
  • You do not pay any cross-border fees for sales in countries where your products are stored. This way, you save on transportation costs when you send inventory in bulk to local fulfilment centres rather than dispatching individual orders across borders. 
  • Your listings qualify for accelerated shipping options including Amazon Prime 1-Day delivery, FREE Delivery and Next-Day delivery (where available).


Disadvantages of Multi-Country Inventory

  • You still pay Amazon cross-border fees if you're selling to customers in a country where your products are not in stock.
  • Complex inventory management.
  • Selling in multiple countries will result in more marketing efforts.
  • You need to apply for a VAT number for each country in which you are storing products.
  • You have to declare VAT in all countries in which your products are stored.

Advantages of Pan-Europea FBA

  • Greatest sales potential - reach all European Amazon customers easier, faster and cheaper.
  • Warehouse in all FBA countries.
  • Fastest delivery to all customers in Europe.
  • Payments of local fees for FBA services only.
  • Amazon Prime makes the product more visible.


Disadvantages of Pan-European FBA

  • Higher initial investment is required to make sure you are ready to sell all over Europe.
  • Stock management will be more complex.
  • You need to apply for a VAT number in all six countries in which your products are sold. In addition, you'll need to file tax returns in these countries. 

You can find a cost overview of all fulfilment types here.

title: Demand fulfillment channel
subcategory: TBR - Channel Added Value marketplace strategy framework
body:
Demand fulfilment channels are used to 'fulfil' the demand for a certain product or brand in a specific region. This demand (for example brand awareness) has been created outside of the platform.


Amazon could be used as a demand fulfilment platform. The Amazon audience has a high buy intent and has often made their mind on the specific product they want to buy. 

 

Characteristics of a Demand fulfilment channel

We will classify every channel type using the topics below. These topics are roughly the same as the Brandsom marketplace pillars (strategy, operation, integration, content and traffic). Integration is not included because this framework focuses on marketing and sales.

  • Audience (part of the strategy)
  • Assortment (part of the strategy)
  • Marketing (part of traffic)
  • Content
  • Pricing (part of the strategy)
  • Operations


Audience

  • A high number of visitors

  • The platform has an organic audience

  • Direct relation with the consumer (Amazon Vine)

  • Conversion focus


Assortment

  • Fast movers

  • Basics

  • Bundles


Marketing

  • Pay-per-Click ads

  • Focused on profitability

  • Brand protection


Content

  • Product detail pages

  • Brand store


Goals of content:

  • Conversion

  • Traffic


Pricing

  • Seller management

  • Competitive pricing


Operations

  • Operational excellence

  • Marketplace fulfilment method

title: Lesson 1 (for consultants): How to setup your Voys account
body:
It's finally here. Voys! All consultants should have a Voys account. To make your Voys experience as seamless as possible, we recommend downloading the Voys App. The app is the best and easiest way to use Voys.

In the step-by-step guide below, I'll walk you through the simple process.


Starting now, we kindly request that you open Voys each morning when you commence work, similar to applications like Slack. Please remember to set your profile to "available" so that you can receive calls during your work hours. The app doesn't need to be open on your phone. It can just run in the background and you'll be able to receive calls.

Don't forget to switch it off again at the end of your workday.


The great news is that Voys already offers a fantastic guide on how to download the app. You can access it by clicking on the following link: https://help.voys.nl/voys-app


If you have any questions don't hesitate to ask Floris. He will be able to help you with any problems.

title: Brandsom Internal; the Way we Work
body:
To make the journey to selling on marketplaces as successful as possible, Brandsom has formulated 6 pillars: Strategy, Integration, Operation, Content, Advertising, and Organisation.

  • Strategy: The most important thing is to start with the correct marketplace strategy. A consultant will analyse a brand and create a strategy based on this. This will be the starting point and the following steps are based on the strategy. Choosing the right strategy will depend on which marketplaces you want to sell on and in which regions a brand is interested.


  • Integration: The next step is to choose the right integrator. An integrator is software that connects a brand's existing order and product systems like Exact, Magento or Shopify, to a marketplace or multiple marketplaces. It helps to keep track of everything marketplace-related. When selling on multiple platforms, an integrator is vital. There are different types of integrators that are compatible with different marketplaces. Some integrators offer repricing functionalities, product filtering and order management, which mostly includes complete order integration, shipment-tracking, international invoicing and fulfilment automation. This all depends on your current IT infrastructure and which e-commerce environment is already present.
    Brandsom utilises the following integrators: ChannelEngine, ChannelAdvisor, ProductFlow, Tradebyte and Vendiro. The marketplace strategy will determine which integrator should be used.


  • Operation: Operation entails the logistical side and customer service aspect of the process. It is important for a brand to decide whether they want to use a fulfilment option or if they want to fulfil orders themselves. This all depends on how a brand has organised its logistical process and in which country they want to sell.
    An integral part of running a business on marketplaces is to have an outstanding track record when it comes down to customer service. Platforms like Amazon and bol.com set very specific policies for customer service. If these are not upheld by the seller, they will suffer extensive consequences like product- or even account deactivation.


  • Content: Selling a product on a marketplace is very labour-intensive. It takes a lot of resources to create quality content. Every product needs a title, bullets, a description and search terms, which all should be optimised for the platform a product is placed on. This way, visibility on a particular platform is ensured. The content can then be translated to the language of the platform on which the brand is selling on.

Then there is A+ content for Amazon, which can be added in addition to the product detail page. Through detailed descriptions, photo’s and video’s, a brand can increase its conversion up to 5%. It gives a more in-depth insight into a product and makes the product page look more professional.

  • Advertising: You can start advertising to get the most amount of traffic on your products. This will organically boost your position in the search results. Also, advertising will give you valuable information on products and will give insight about the consumer, as ads generate data like search terms and keywords, which in turn will help you optimise your ads and content on the product page.


  • Organisation: It's important to look at resources that are already available and those that need to be insourced. Does a brand already have a marketplace manager in-house? And what skills and knowledge are necessary? Based on these factors we can adjust our service to the need of the client. Brandsom offers both external support and in-house support. The latter means that a Brandsom consultant will work from the company. This hands-on approach will ensure that a brand has direct access to our knowledge.

title: Introduction
body:
One of the most important parts of working at Brandsom is fulfilling our client's needs. This is simply because they are the reason we get to work. Therefore, you want to build a strong working relationship with the client. To achieve this,  good communication is necessary to figure out their goals and needs and to let them know what we can do to help. After all, you want to keep the client satisfied. How do we do this? And what exactly is good communication and how do we keep it businesslike?

One way of communicating is by e-mail. Another frequently used option is calling the client by phone. It is very important to write and talk in a formal way at the early stages of the business relationship.


When to call, e-mail or plan a Google Meet?

We believe that a periodic meeting structure is the core of good communication with the customer. Besides this, we use the Voys (internet phone) and e-mail.

  • Try to discuss all topics in the (bi)weekly, monthly and quarterly meetings. If you receive e-mails in the meantime discuss the also during these meetings.
  • Second way of communication method is e-mail because it's clearer, faster and more reliable for the customer.
  • Third way of communicating is calling the customer using Voys. Sometimes it's quicker to just call the customer, if you call the customer please use Voys.
  • The customer can call the generic phone number (please check the site for the latest number) and re-routed if needed.


Tone of voice 

When you communicate with a client, it is important to use a formal tone of voice. This indicates the way and the tone in which you write or speak to a client. Your tone of voice should be professional and friendly. This way of communicating will be explained in detail in this module.

title: What NOT to do
body:
It is very important that you only use LastPass to store your passwords. Avoid saving your passwords to Chrome directly or in other locations. The goal is to store all information in a safe and encrypted space. 

If you are using the general LastPass account, be mindful of the following:

  • Never store passwords from your private accounts in the general accounts. As said before this is a general account and everybody has access to this. For example, if you save your bank account information or the login details to your personal bol.com account, everybody will have access to this. This is not desirable and must be avoided at all times.

  • You will find that most of the passwords cannot be shared. This is a safety measure. Only certain people can share certain passwords. However, if you are able to share a login with someone always ask and check. Only share if really necessary. 


Never share the master password of general accounts. If someone needs access, a consultant will make sure they get it.

title: Amazon Plan of Action - Late Dispatch Rate
body:
Below you will find a POA to reinstate an Amazon account which is blocked due to Pre-Fulfilmentcancellation rate. Please customize the plan and make adjustments where needed, DO NOT use this plan 1:1.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Reactivate your account

A Plan of Action that includes:

The root cause(s) of confirming shipment of your orders after the expected ship date, which impacted your Late Dispatch Rate (LDR).

The actions you have taken to resolve orders not dispatched by the expected ship date.

The actions you have taken to ensure your orders are confirmed as shipped by the expected ship date in the future.

 

The root cause of the issue
What was the primary cause for your LDR to exceed 4% over 30 days?

Unfortunately, due to increasing pressure at our warehouse and the carriers, we were too often unable to deliver products on time. It was also increasingly the case that products were delivered damaged. Orders were also more often not automatically reported as shipped due to a technical problem at our integrator. Due to increasing pressure and activities, we ourselves have failed to keep a closer eye on this.

 

The actions that you have taken to resolve the issue
 What have you done to resolve the issues with the orders that caused your LSR to exceed 4% over 30 days?

  • Our fulfillment partner has expanded its warehouse and staff, they use a packing robot, which means that orders can be shipped faster.
  • We’ve invested a lot of money in a new product information management (PIM) system. Now we can better control our product information and make sure it’s up-to-date. By using this PIM system (Akeneo) the information is stated correctly and more capacity available to control the other parts of market place sales.
  • We have expanded our marketplace team and we have contracted the services of an agency who will provide support on managing our seller account, proactively reaching out to customers.
  • We switched from integrator (ChannelEngine to Vendiro) and we tested our connection first in our UAT. Ensuring availability and unmatched performance of our brands on amazon. 
    1. With this new partner, Vendiro, we receive twice a day a list with outstanding orders so we are closer on it. This allows us to act on issues before they’re experienced by the customer.
  • Before we outsourced the customer service department. Now, we have our own customer service team for over 8 months already, and we are able to get feedback & anecdotes directly from the customer. Enabling us to resolve customer complaints timely and increase both CX of amazon, customer happiness and attitude towards our brands. 
    1. If there are late orders now due to ascendancy, we have capacity to inform customers in a proactively way.
  • Over the past year, our company has experienced tremendous growth. This has also brought a big step in professionalism and we can now broaden our focus more.
  • We also experienced ODR issues regarding our oven products. For this, we made improvements to the primary packaging of the products has been improved at the factory level and have purchased improved secondary packaging for shipping. For example, oven products are better protected avoiding broken glass. We have tested this the last half year since the improvement and have not had any issues since. We now can say our packaging is ecommerce proof.

The steps that you have taken to prevent the issue going forward
 What actions will you take to ensure your orders are confirmed as shipped by the expected ship date in future?

  1. Our fulfilment partner expanded the capacity of the warehouse and their staff. They invested in a packing robot so we can pick and pack quicker and also better.
  2. Invested in the PIM system Akeneo, with help of a consultancy company who helped us during 12 months to set up the system well before we start using it.
  3. Scale-up of our content team so that they can keep a close eye on all this.
  4. Automated shipping status to Amazon by using a new integrator called Vendiro.
  5. Scale-up of our customer service team, so that they can determine more on amazon. This way we can also act faster when problems arise.
  6. When we see that it’s getting busier in our warehouse and/or at the carriers, we’ll respond more quickly by extending the delivery time and change expected ship dates in Amazon.
     We also prioritize market place above the B2B orders. This wasn’t the case in the past.
  7. We monitor more times a day the status of the orders take immediate action when there are some problems arising.
  8. Our warehouse has marked the amazon orders as urgent, so they are always shipped the same day

 Briefly, we are ready for the future and we are prepared better than ever.

title: From/for pricing rule
body:
Introduction:

On May 28, 2022, the new European Consumer Protection Modernization Directive (OMNIBUS) came into force. The new directive includes a provision on the so-called from/for price or as it is called in Dutch ‘Van/voor prijs’.

In brief,

This guideline indicates that for a discount promotion where you want to mention the 'from' price, you must use the lowest sales price that you have conducted within the last 30 days before the price reduction. An example of this includes:

Would you like to mention a starting price or a discount percentage? Then you have to count that from the lowest price you asked in all 30 days before.

 Examples include:

If the product was € 15 for a long time, it may not first be increased to € 25 for a short time and then lowered again to € 15, so that it can be said that there is a 40% discount. After all, the lowest price in those 30 days was already €15, so there is no question of a discount

Suppose on October 1st the price was $100, on October 5th you lowered it to $90, and on October 31st you lowered the price to $89. Then you cannot say that you are giving a €11 discount. After all, the lowest price within 30 days was €90. The discount is therefore only €1,-.

However,

!!!In the Netherlands, it remains permitted to refer to the original price if there is an unbroken succession of price reductions!!! For example:

Please note, this arrangement:

  • Only applies to products, not to (digital) services and digital content;
  • Applies to all distribution channels: physical, online, telephone, catalogue, etc.
  • Concerns notifications from the seller that he has reduced the selling price compared to the previous selling price.

What does this rule not cover?

  • This law does not apply to perishable products
  • This rule does not restrict the entrepreneur's freedom to determine the price himself.
  • The rule does not cover generic communication about all or about categories or ranges of products offered by the seller if no specific measurable price reduction is announced. For example: Lowest price, promotion, sale, outlet, etc.
  • The rule does not cover price comparisons in which the sales price is compared with a recommended sales price, the price charged by a competitor or any other reference price (where it must be clear which type of reference price is used, otherwise it is an unfair commercial practice).
  • The rule does not cover discount cards or vouchers that entitle the consumer to a discount on all or categories or ranges of products offered by the seller.
  • The rule does not apply to offers where the purchase of a product in combination with other products is cheaper. For example, 1+1 free or two for the price of one.
  • The rule does not apply to loyalty programs where consumers receive a discount on future purchases when they purchase a product

This link provides further information about this Law and other general price laws referring to price manipulation:

https://www.acm.nl/nl/onderwerpen/verkoop-aan-consumenten/verkopen-aan-consumenten/prijzen-vermelden/prijzen-vermelden

title: Buyer's Journey
body:
An outdated sales process has three parts, with minimal value to the buyer: prospect, demo, and close. However, buyers don't want to be prospected, demo'd or closed in your sale process. They want to be educated, supported, and guided through the buying process. If you look at your interactions from the buyer's perspective you'll better understand how to maximize the value you give to your buyers. Starting with the buyer's journey enables you to do that.

To define the buyer's journey, think about how buyers become aware of a problem, consider the available solutions, and ultimately decide to purchase your product or service.



Awareness stage

Buyers identify a challenge they're experiencing or an opportunity they want to pursue. The thing the buyer is becoming aware of in this stage is their problem, not your solution. In order to fully understand the awareness stage of your buyer, ask yourself:

  • How do buyers describe their goals or challenges?
  • How do buyers educate themselves on these goals or challenges?
  • What are the consequences of inaction by the buyer?
  • Are there common misconceptions buyers have about addressing the goal or challenge?
  • How do buyers decide whether the goal or challenge should be prioritized?

Consideration stage

Buyers have clearly defined the goal/challenge. They have given a name to it, and they've committed to addressing it. They evaluate the different approaches or methods available to solve their challenge or capitalize on the opportunity. Things to ask yourself regarding this stage in the buyer's journey are:

  • What categories of solutions do buyers investigate?
  • How do buyers educate themselves on the various categories?
  • How do buyers perceive the pros and cons of each category?
  • How do buyers decide which category is right for them?

Decision stage

Buyers have already decided on a solution category. They might create a list of specific offerings in their selected category and decide on the one that best meets their needs or they might go with the solution they find first. Either way, a key component of an inbound sales strategy is connecting with buyers before they enter the decision stage. You want to be there to guide them toward a decision. Questions to ask yourself during the decision stage are:

  • What criteria do buyers use to evaluate the available offerings?
  • What do buyers like about your offering compared to alternatives?
  • What common concerns do buyers have with your offering?
  • Who needs to be involved in the decision? And how does each person's perspective on the decision differ?
  • Do buyers want to try your offering before they purchase it?
  • Outside of purchasing, do buyers need to make additional preparations such as implementation plans or training strategies?

title: How to work focussed?
body:
Please find some useful tips below on how to work focussed. Working focussed has a lot of benefits:

  • It's more fulfilling, completing tasks is a satisfying feeling.
  • It's less stressful, having to much open tasks and interruptions makes work more stressful.
  • Following these tips makes you more comfortable when reaching out to teammates.
  • Remove slack and your business email from your private phone.
  • Remove/Mute WhatsApp, your private mail, Tinder, Instagram, TikTok, Facebook from your Brandsom laptop.


General

  • Use Google tasks as your to-do list. You can find it on in the right bar of every Google App. Moving things from your memory to a to-do list, keeps your mind clear. 
  • Work in blocks, avoid switching during tasks. It's super hard for the brain to switch tasks. For example, process your mail on twice a day.
  • Discuss with your team how you would like to communicatie and what to expect, for example. To keep it simple, please all use the same way of working.
    • We check emails everyday at 11am and 3pm and slack every 2 hours.
    • If there is anything urgent, we call and or sent a text.
  • Ownership: a problem is not solved when is has been forwarded it to a colleague. If you are the owner of the problem or task. Please take the lead in tracking the progress. For example: if you have a question on onboarding of products, the 'problem' is solved when the products are onboarded. Not when you have created a ticket.


Slack

  • Slack helps to avoid a lot of emails, however slack messages disappear. Use Slack for simple and quick (answered within the same day) questions.
  • Do not expect an immediate reply, people work in blocks. If you have something urgent, call or walk-over to your colleague.


Email

  • Work in blocks and discuss this with your team.
  • Work with labels, move customer emails to the right label. For example (all ASICS e-mails -> ASICS folder. This helps to work on all ASICS emails in the same block.
  • Customers do not expect an immediate reply. We can answer within 24 hours, it is more important to answer or clearify instead of being the quickest.


Questions and how to work independently

  • Make sure you have a good overview of your work in the morning. This way you can discuss them daily. You can prepare the daily in the evening before.
  • Keep a digital list for all your questions which pop-up during the work. Collect all questions until you cannot proceed then ask all your questions in one meeting.
  • Use manuals. All marketplaces, integrators and our internal academy contain a lot of information. Check these sources first.
  • Make sure that you have multiple tasks that you can work on, this way you are not stuck when one task is finished or when you have a question.

title: Demand creation channel
subcategory: TBR - Channel Added Value marketplace strategy framework
body:
Demand creation channels are used to create demand within a specific region. These platforms can be used to introduce products and brands in new regions. Zalando is often used as a demand creation platform. With their powerful marketing tools and their own and organic audience, they are able to create demand in the markets in which they are active.


Zalando is often used as a demand creation platform. With their powerful marketing tools and with their own organic audience, they are able to create demand in the markets in which they are active.

Characteristics of a Demand creation channel

We will classify every channel type using the topics below. These topics are roughly the same as the Brandsom marketplace pillars (strategy, operation, integration, content and traffic). Integration is not included because this framework only focuses on marketing and sales.

  • Audience (part of the strategy)
  • Assortment (part of the strategy)
  • Marketing (part of traffic)
  • Content
  • Pricing (part of the strategy)
  • Operations


Audience

  • The platform has a high number of visitors and an organic audience

  • Direct relation with the consumer (use of an app and creating special relations with consumers via Zalando Plus)

  • High or mid-funnel focus

  • At the beginning of the customer journey


Assortment

  • Hero products, which are usually the brand's best selling products

  • Wide assortment


Marketing

  • Upper funnel periodic campaigns

  • Always on Pay-per-Click ads


Content

Marketplaces can create demand by using:

  • Brand stores

  • Category banners 

  • Landing pages

  • Product detail pages


Goals of content:

  • Branding

  • Up-to-date


Pricing

  • Focused on periodic campaigns

  • Retail moments

  • In line with other distribution channels


Operations

  • Focused on availability

  • Time to market

body:
Welcome to the Brandsom Academy course and strategy framework: 'Channel Added Value'. With this strategy framework, you will be able to determine the added value per channel in the online distribution strategy of the brand. 

We will use 'Casall' (a Swedish sportswear brand) as an example. Let's imagine Casall wants to increase its brand awareness on the German market. How can they achieve this?

With this course, you will learn which channels are suitable to increase brand awareness per region, and how you can operate on these platforms using the different Brandsom pillars: strategy, integration, operation, content and traffic.

title: Online marketplaces explained
body:
What are online marketplaces?

An online marketplace is an e-commerce platform where third-party sellers advertise their products. E-commerce stands for 'electronic commerce'. It means electronically buying and selling products online over the internet. In short, a marketplace like Amazon gives these brands a space to run their own “store” online so they can advertise and sell their products. To do this successfully, each marketplace has its own unique strategy, algorithm and policies.

Why do online marketplaces offer opportunities for a lot of brands?

First of all, when selling on an international marketplace like Amazon, you reach a large number of people. This accessibility means that you have a large international audience with relatively low investments in return. Expanding to the global market will give a brand a major boost and will increase its sales. In addition, these online marketplaces provide payment options and fulfilment solutions, such as Amazon FBA (fulfilment by Amazon), Zalando ZFS (Zalando fulfilment solutions) or Bol.com LvB (logistics via Bol.com). They offer many tools to advertise products and optimise content.

Then there is the advertising side of the process. In a physical store, it is very hard to determine what people actually look for. Online, however, you can analyse search patterns and implement bidding strategies for your ads. This will increase your visibility and makes sure that as many people as possible click on a product and then actually buy it.

Different marketplaces

There are many different marketplaces out there. It is important to choose the right marketplace, which depends on the product a brand sells. Amazon for instance is multifaceted, making it possible to sell basically anything on there; it's a one-stop-shop. If a brand sells more of a niche product, it may be valuable to choose a marketplace based on this. The fashion branch has fashion marketplaces specifically designed for the different categories within the fashion world. Fashion is therefore a completely separate segment in the world of e-commerce.

Here's a short list of the most important and upcoming marketplaces:

One-stop shop marketplaces

  • Amazon: The largest e-commerce platform in the world. Amazon offers many solutions to selling online, giving brands instant access to the international marketplace. Amazon is truly the place to go. It gives you access to orders, stock customer service, advertisement, fulfilment solutions and payment options. However, selling on Amazon is not an easy thing to do. It requires a clear strategy and knowledge to successfully do so.


  • Bol.com: This is a Dutch marketplace that you have undoubtedly heard of. Bol.com is probably the biggest marketplace on the Dutch and Belgian markets, with more than 3 million visitors each day! Just like Amazon, Bol.com is a one-stop shop. This means that the platform offers over 20 different product categories, so almost every brand can sell its products here. It offers fulfilment solutions, payment options, and tools for content and advertisement optimisation. A brand can enlist through the partner platform, to get support from professionals at Bol.com. 

Amazon offer brand stores. These are stores within the platform where a brand can create their own page as if it's their personal webshop, facilitated by Amazon.



  • Kaufland: Formerly known as Real.de, Kaufland is a German platform and is comparable to Bol.com. From food to electronics, you can find basically anything on here. However, unlike Bol.com or Amazon, Kaufland does not offer fulfilment solutions or invoicing possibilities.


Fashion marketplaces


  • Zalando: The largest online fashion platform. Clothing brands will benefit from Zalando because of its popularity and international character, having access to 23 different countries. Zalando offers a partner program and fulfilment solutions. Brands can outsource their entire logistical process with Zalando ZFS. 


  • About You: An upcoming marketplace that belongs to the Otto Group. Just like Zalando, About You offers both a partner program and a fulfilment solution called fulfilment by About You. This marketplace is open to large brands, as well as smaller ones, and has a young target audience. There are two integrators that are compatible with About You: TradeByte and ChannelEngine.


Fashion marketplaces are specifically designed for the fashion branch. Unlike one-stop shops, they only focus on clothing, shoes and accessories. The different marketplaces can be divided into 4 separate categories: demand creation, demand fulfilment, category specialist, and discount. In general, the best integrators for fashion marketplaces are ChannelAdvisor, TradeByte and ChannelEngine.

title: Zalando's Shop cleaning
body:
Zalando has introduced, from October 15th, the Shop cleaning dates.

Those are dates, at the beginning of each month, in which Zalando permanently delists, with error ZABLO_02:

  • all the articles that have more than 1000 PDP views and 0 conversion rate. 
  • Articles with no PDP views despite high Product Listing Page (PLP) views of at least 5,400. In this context, the PLP is the catalogue page in a given Zalando sales channel.

The delisting is done by Zalando on a monthly basis.

As this is a permanent removal of the affected articles from the platform, it is very important to communicate to the clients before the Shop cleaning date which articles will be affected, in order to prevent the permanent deactivation of products that are relevant for the customer and they want to keep online.

If this is the case, we can suggest the customer to apply a discount or create a ZMS Selected Sponsored Products campaign to push sales and gain conversions, which will prevent the articles from being permanently deactivated. 

In order to check the current articles at risk, simply check on Zdirect Analytics > Article Performance > Stock > PDP views and Conversion Rate columns:


  • The specific Shop Cleaning Dates are here
  • More information on the Shop Cleaning process are here 

Finally, it is important to check if the articles which are blocked by ZABLO_02 have some leftover stock. If they do, we can request a Return to merchant to get it back.

title: Low Bid Campaign & Negative Keyword Targeting
body:
A low bid campaign enables you to find new keywords at a favourable cost-per-click (CPC). Keywords are the words consumers use to find a product. So finding the right keywords is important, and a low bid campaign is ideal to determine what keywords are the most useful for advertising a product.


In contrast to PPC (pay-per-click), where you pay every time someone clicks on your ad, CPC determines the cost of the Amazon product ads that sellers will have to pay. The cost varies depending on the number of clicks. If the ad placed, gathers more clicks than the initial bid, it is a success. So, the CPC is determined by the total amount of money spent divided by the total clicks.


Start by creating one automatic sponsored products campaign for all products available. Name this campaign: all products - low bid. Then set up a low bid, between 5 and 10 cents. The important thing is that the bid is lower than the usual bid in the corresponding categories of products that are being sold. For example, fashion products will use different bidding amounts than home and garden products.


When creating a Sponsored Products campaign, there are three different bidding strategies. Pick the strategy that works best for the campaign's objective. This setting applies to all bids within the campaign.


In this case select “dynamic bidding, up and down” as a bidding strategy. This will allow Amazon to increase the bidding automatically, the moment the algorithm expects you to have the highest possible conversion. It can also lower the bid if the algorithm predicts that your conversion will be next to none. Thus, making sure that you don’t waste your budget on auctions that will not generate conversion. Amazon will never increase a bid by more than 100%. So if your bid is €1,-  Amazon will never bid more than €2.-. On average this bidding strategy will have an ACoS between 3% and 10%.


In a low bid campaign, a low ACoS is prefered. You will want to keep the spending as low as possible. The objective is to discover long-tail keywords that drive cheap conversion. So ultimately the challenge is to find a low ACoS and CPC, while still being high enough to win auctions and get good conversion.


Negative keyword targeting

A negative keyword is a word or phrase that is the opposite of normal keywords. Where a normal keyword is used to showcase an ad, a negative keyword is used to prevent your ad from appearing. This will stop your ads from showing up on irrelevant searches and save you from spending unnecessary ad money.


Negative keywords are used in two different ways:

One way is to narrow down the possibility that your ad is being found on a broad search query. For example, if you sell handmade red leather shoes, you don’t want the ad to appear when a customer searches for shoes only. This will result in unnecessary clicks.

Another way is to use the keywords found in the automatic campaign, to then exclude these from the manual campaign. By entering negative keywords in the manual campaign, you can exclude low converting keywords. In general, the following keywords should be turned into negative keywords: 

  • Low CTR (click-through-rate) non-converters

  • High spend, non-converters

  • High click, non-converters


Without negative keywords, it's likely that your ads appear to thousands of searches that are:

  • Irrelevant
  • Unprofitable
  • Have low click-through-rates (CTR)
  • Have low conversion rates (CVR)


You can add negative keywords at campaign level or ad group level, depending on the products and the structure you use for your campaign. The more precise negative keywords are, the better this will work. When you add a negative keyword at campaign level, it is automatically added to all of the ad groups in that particular campaign.

Adding a negative keyword at the ad group level will only apply the negative matching to that one ad group in the campaign.


Negative keywords have two match types: phrase and exact.

Negative phrase match

A negative phrase match prevents your ad from appearing in search queries that contain the selected set of words in an exact sequence, allowing plurals and slight misspellings.

For example, if we had “junior tennis rackets” as a negative phrase, here are some queries that would show your ad and others that would block it.



The first two show the ad because they don’t contain the exact phrase, just parts of it. The last two contain the entire negative phrase and are therefore blocked.


Negative exact match

With negative exact, the search term must match your keyword exactly, allowing plurals and slight misspellings.

What would happen if “junior tennis rackets” was the negative exact?



While the complete match is typed in, your ad still shows because it also includes extra words outside of the negative exact. Only the last two search terms contain the exact negative match or a misspelling and are therefore blocked.


Note: Make sure to add all the keywords that are used in the manual campaign to the negative keywords in the automatic campaign. By doing this, you prevent these campaigns from driving up the price by bidding against each other.

title: What causes error 5000?
subcategory: Amazon Error Solving SOP Overview - internal
body:
What causes error 5000?

This error occurs due to all kinds of XSD violations i.e. due to malformed or invalid XML documents.

For example:

  • Strings with too many characters

  • Wrong sequence of attributes

  • Missing required attributes

  • Wrong values in enumerations

Sample:

ResultMessageCode: 5000


ResultDescription: XML Parsing Warning at Line 7, Column 57: Finished parsing 'AmazonEnvelope', but no 'Message' elements were found. The envelope appears to be empty. 

How to solve and prevent error 5000?

To solve this error, check the error message in the results i.e. feed processing report. This description will point to the exact location of the error in the XML and will also briefly explain the error. If the description is generic, please use XML validation tools together with the relevant XSD to find the errors in the file.


Note: If there are multiple occurrences of “Error 5000”, you might not have used the updated XSD. Please download the latest XSDs and submit the feed again.


Download the latest XSDs from Seller Central and validate your XML feed against these XSDs/ specific XSD as per error description. Make modifications in your API based on the required XSD.


As a good practice, we advise to download the latest XSDs from https://sellercentral.amazon.co.uk/gp/help/G1611 at frequent intervals.

  • Onbepaalde tijd contract 

Loonheffingen formulier 

Formulier voor toepassen van loonhefkorting. Dit kan alleen bij één werkgever aan staan.  Dit formulier moet altijd ingevuld worden, ook als er geen korting van toe passing is. Controleer goed of alle gegevens juist zijn ingevuld. 

Laptop bruikleenovereenkomst

Dit formulier kan pas ingevuld worden na aanschaf nieuwe laptop.

title: Cold acquisition
body:
Cold acquisition is a common term for unsolicited approaches to businesses where the seller and potential buyer do not yet know each other. The intent of the seller is to sell something to the other party. 

 

At Brandsom, this cold acquisition through Linkedin will be used to get in touch with a potential lead. The goal of this approach is to respond to the possible need of a company. 

 

Which companies are interesting to approach? 

Brandsom is a company that helps other companies to sell successfully on marketplaces. Therefore, it is best to approach companies that have a possible interest in this. For example, a cleaning company will have much less interest in this than a company with its own clothing brand. 

 

Here are a few examples of what kind of companies might be interested:

- Companies with their own brand and website 

- Retail companies 

- Companies that already sell on marketplaces 

 

So we want to approach these companies to respond to the possible need. If it turns out that there is actually interest from this company, try to schedule an introductory meeting. More about this later.

title: Search term builder
body:
When you have to create search terms via using the Search term builder, follow these steps:


  1. The first step is for the consultant to fill in the necessary data which the consultant has received from the client. This should have been placed in the sheet called “Bron data”. This data file will contain hopefully most of the data you will need for creating the bullets. Look through the sheet “Bron data” to get to know the products you will be working with a bit better.
     
     
  2. Next, you go to the sheet “Combined Search Terms”. Here you fill in the corresponding product information, often this is EAN, SKU, Title, Brand.
     
      
  3. If you are not already are familiar with the products you are working with you should get a sense of the product composition either via the data in “Bron data” and/or the customers' website. After having done that you can devise the category and subcategories. If you have trouble with creating them or have doubts of the categories you have created feel free to discuss with the consultant of the customer or your supervisor.
     
     
    The following section is divided into Category search terms, Specific search terms and Brand search terms. Depending on the products you are working with you create search terms for these sections. Having finished this you will combine the search terms, see Combining search terms for more info.  


Category search term

  1. After having taken a look at the “Bron data” you should already have seen some unique attributes of the products, this can be their colour, size etc. Fill these attributes in, with the use of “=VERT.ZOEK” in the sheet “Attributen” as done in the figure below. 

  2. If you have all the relevant attributes in the sheet “Attribute”, you can fill them in in the sheet “Combined Search Terms” with the use of “=VERT.ZOEK”. It should look similar to the figure below.  



Specific Search Terms

  1. First, you create categories and subcategories to split up the products into relevant product groups. This helps to create specific search terms for product groups. If you have trouble with creating them or have doubts about the categories you have created feel free to discuss with the consultant of the customer or your supervisor.
     
     
  2. After having created the categories and subcategories you go to “Category Search Terms Data” and “Sub Category Search Terms Data”. In these sheets, you use the function "=UNIQUE()" → Select the column within the sheet “Combined Search Terms” that contains either the categories or the subcategories. If done correctly you will get a list of all the unique categories and subcategories in the right sheet.


  3. It is recommended to first do some research on what is important for the product categories. This you can do via the website of the customer but also via looking up substitutes on amazon or other platforms.
     
     
  4. If you feel you are close to saturation you can start writing up search terms for the respective categories. You do this in the sheets “Category Search Terms Data” and “Sub Category Search Terms Data” for every category.

 

Brand Search Terms

  1. It may happen that the group of products for which you are creating search terms are from different brands. In this case, you use the same steps as discussed for category and subcategory search terms but use the sheet "Brand Search Terms Data"   



Combining Search Terms

  1. After you have collected search terms and have filled them in the sheet “Combined Search Terms”, the search terms are combined in the columns denoted by the title “Product level search terms”. The formulas you do not have to fill in yourself but should already be in the cells. The only thing you have to do yourself is possibly insert the formula "=REMOVE_DUPLICATES_RANGE()" which removes all duplicate words in the denoted cell. If the formula doesn’t work as your supervisor or the consultant for help.
     
      
  2. After you have removed all the doubles you check how many characters your list of search terms consists of via the function "=LENGTE". The maximum number of characters depends on the platform you are making the search terms for, hence check in the relevant academy page or ask your supervisor.
