title: Bullet builder
body:
When you have to create bullets via using the bullet builder follow these steps:

  1. The first step is for the consultant to fill in the necessary data which the consultant has received from the client. This should have been placed in the sheet called “Bron data”. This data file will contain, hopefully, most of the data you will need for creating the bullets. Look through the “Bron data” to get to know the products and the data you will be working with a bit better. 


  2. First off all make sure that the SKU - Parent SKU - EAN are filled in correctly in the sheet "Master data" (see figure below).


  3. In this sheet, fill in the corresponding identifiers or USPs (Unique Selling Points). The data of the USP you ought to find in the sheet "Bron data", you can use "=VERT.ZOEK" for this task. You should have about 4 to 5 identifiers per product. These identifiers say something unique about your product and can therefore be used for your bullets (more about this in Amazon module 3: Bullets).

     
  4. It may also be the case that your identifier consists of two variables. This can be useful if the product has many unique characteristics. You can put the variables/characteristics concerned next to each other under the respective bullet. Then you can merge the variables/characteristics by using "=TEKST.SAMENVOEGEN()" under a new column, this is then your Identifier (see figure above)!


  5. Now that you have the identifiers in the sheet “Master data”, go to "Bullet Identifiers". In this sheet, you will create the actual bullets based on the identifiers. Click on the top white cell of the column where you want your identifiers. In the example (see figure below), this would be the cell that contains "Gloves Heavy Duty". Then use the function "=UNIQUE()" → Select the column within the sheet “Master data” that contains the respective identifiers. After this, you get a row with all the unique values in your specified range and can now write the bullets for each identifier as shown in the figure below. Different platforms may have different requirements and rules for bullets, so make you sure you know these before writing bullets!!!
    Do this for all the other identifiers. 


  6. After you have written your bullets for the denoted identifiers and languages it is now time to collect the bullets per language category in one sheet. Commonly this sheet will be called “Builder [language]”, for example “Builder DE” which denotes a bullet builder for bullets in german. In this builder, you copy the columns with the SKUs and Parent SKUs. In the columns alongside these columns you search for the corresponding identifiers by means of '=VERT.ZOEKEN()'. Search for the identifiers in the sheet “Master data”, your formula will probably look like this: "=VERT.ZOEKEN(A6;'Master data'!A$3:J; 9;ONWAAR)".
    Do this for all the other identifiers.


  7. Now that you have the SKUs and identifiers in you builder sheet, you can add your bullets in the following columns with the use of "=VERT.ZOEKEN()". In this case you use the identifier as the search key.

    Quality check: make sure that you (Spell) check your bullets.

title: Forecasting and Replenishment strategy
body:
Presentation on replanishment

Forecasting and Replenishment strategy

Replenishment strategy is needed for:

  • Revenue for our clients and for us as Brandsom

  • Profitability for our clients and for us.

  • Visibility and branding by offering a complete assortment.

  • Customer contact and trust.


Replenishment strategy include:

  1. Strategy + Forecast = target per channel per country

    • Forecast total and monthly sales for the year

    • Use historical data, other clients as benchmark, market research and seasonality

    • Include  KPIs: discount%, return rate, marketing spend, and units needed to hit the sales

    • Forecast per EAN

      • Based on the information gathered, which styles are likely to be best-sellers, as well as the assortment chosen and the total year/month target

      • Divide the total target over the selected styles (/ unit price) to forecast per EAN the needed quantities.

  2. Pre-season Planning = entire process before manufacturing products.

    • How to successfully achieve that?  

      • Connect with the client!

      • Discuss lead times

        • What products can be re-bought, which can’t? 

        • Does the client offer seasonal (and when are new seasons launched)?

        • Or only NOS products?

      • Client input: what are the product categories, core products & price point and brand strength

      • Brandsom Merchandising input = Brandsom’s role is to advise, support, provide insights and create awareness of what the client should do and think of in order to reach targets the client wants to reach!

      • Useful tools:

        • Helium research

          • Market analysis (white-label vs brand, market size, top sellers, price)

          • Competitor analysis (visitors, top selling products, important KPIs, reviews)

          • Keywords analysis (what are customers looking for)

        • Marketplace backend (example Zalando)

          • Best selling categories

          • Highest return rate

          • Conversion rate per category

        • Other customer data

          • E-commerce market research data

          • Clients’ data per marketplace 

          • Other clients as a benchmark

  3. In-season Planning = Relates to the entire process from inbound / stocking the products to selling and replenishing

    • Discuss final forecast with client, when the goods are going to be in stock and planned ready for sales

    • Combine with Marketing Plan

      • Manage monthly/weekly/daily targets

      • (Bi-)weekly stock analysis

        • Incorporate and discuss in your (bi)weekly!

        • Analyze sales Last X days, current stock and return rate

        • Create an advisory report based on the analysis for the client

        • Discuss your advice to restock / discount with client

        • Make it a standard topic to discuss

        • Create new shipments

      • Commercial moments / discount strategy 

        • Start 1 month in advance to plan for a big commercial moment

      • Commercial feedback on merchandising


If the academy isn't clear enough for you, don't hesitate to use one of the following forecast templates created for our clients Cornucopia and IKKI.

  • TargetsCornucopia
  • IKKI_Forecasting_2023.xlsx

title: Amazon Attribution
body:
Amazon attribution

Amazon attribution is a free service that allows you to track visitors that come to your amazon page via social media and google ads campaigns. Where you can normally see how many clicks your campaign has generated, it is now also possible to track behaviour after the click. This includes clicks, conversion, add to cart, detail page views and views of other products. Amazon itself indicates that up to 300 different touchpoints can be tracked. In addition, data is stored for up to 14 days and linked. This also allows you to see if someone decides to buy your product at a later date. 

Register for Amazon Attribution


Before you register for Amazon attribution, it is necessary that you have an active brand in the Amazon Brand Registry. Without a brand registry, there is no attribution.

  1. Log in to Seller Central and go to sellercentral.amazon.com/attribution.

  2. Click "register" to get an email invitation.

  3. Accept the invitation in the email you receive from DSP support.

  4.  Add other users (or agencies) to the list.

Understanding Amazon Attribution Campaigns


Setting up the Amazon Attribution campaigns is pretty straightforward. However, there are some new terms used. Below is an explanation to understand the hierarchy and the specific terminology.  

Start creating the Amazon Attribution Campaign



  1. Select the right advertiser, this is the brand for which the attribution link will be created. Customers with multiple registered brands can have multiple advertisers here. 


  1. Create a "new order - The order is a group of line items that track conversion and activity for the same products. So anything that you want to track after a link is clicked. 


  1. Select how you want to upload the orders and tags - Select ‘Manually create order and tags’ for all types of campaigns except google search campaigns. For these campaigns, you need to upload a file and perform a bulk operation. 


  1. Select the corresponding products

Here you select the products that will be tracked. Find the product and select "add variations" to include it. Products from your portfolio that are not included, but were eventually purchased, can be found under the "total" in the metrics

  1. Fill in the order settings and click "continue to line items".

Enter a clear name for your order. For example, "product name - ad location (    FB/INST/GOOGLE) - ad goal".


  1. Create the line item

Line item name: Enter a clear name for the line item here, based on the strategy and location of the tag. Please note that attribution campaigns can never be deleted so the chance of a contaminated list is high. Therefore, always make sure you use clear names, especially for test campaigns. 

Publisher: Select the publisher that corresponds to the place where the link will be placed (e.g. Facebook, Instagram, Google shopping etc.). 

Channel: Once you have chosen the publisher, the channel option appears. Select the correct channel here.

Click-through URL: Enter here the amazon link where you want the visitors to end up. This can be a product, but also your store or product page.

  1. Copy the Amazon Attribution URL

Once you have created the line item, the Amazon Attribution tag appears. Click to copy this tag. This is the URL you will use in all relevant campaigns from now on. 

  1. Paste the tag into the relevant campaign manager if you're using it.

If you are using another campaign manager besides amazon ads manager, copy the URL as created in 7. 


  1. Start with Measurements - You have now finished setting up your Attribution campaign for this particular link. It may take up to 1-2 days for Amazon Attribution to display the data correctly.

title: Useful tools
body:
At Brandsom, we work with various helpful tools that allow for easy communication with colleagues, time tracking, password managing, and work management in general. As an employee, it's important to know how these tools can be used to work more efficiently and safely. In this lesson, we will introduce some of the tools we use on a daily basis. We'll briefly explain what they do and how they are used correctly.


Asana

Asana is a software-as-a-service (SaaS) designed to improve team collaboration and work management. It helps teams manage projects and tasks in one tool. Teams can create projects, assign work to teammates, specify deadlines, and communicate about tasks directly in Asana. For more information on how to start using Asana, read through the quick-start guide that can be found here.


Everhour

Everhour is a time management app that helps companies better organize their working hours. The time tracking software integrates directly into Asana, giving you a detailed log of your activities. This way, you can get the amount of time a team member spends on any task or project versus the original estimate. To start using this tool, download the extension for your browser here. This will automatically integrate the software into Asana. To learn more about the use of Everhour, read this article.


Slack

Slack is a messaging app that makes communicating with colleagues easy. With Slack, you can message anyone inside your organization and collaborate just like you would in person. People can work in channels that bring together the right people and information. The app helps keep teams stay aligned and make decisions more quickly by sharing information with everyone at once. Overall, Slack is a great tool for team communication as it is perfect for that quick response conversation with colleagues. Read this quick start guide on how to use Slack if you would like more information.


LastPass

LastPass is a password manager that stores all of Brandsoms usernames and passwords in one safe place called a Vault. After saving a password to your Vault, Lastpass always remembers it for you. It is one of the most highly secure password managers available. This is important because we work with sensitive client information that must be guarded against unauthorized access. Lastpass can be downloaded as an extension to your web browser. More information on the tool and how to download it can be found here.

title: Front-End check Amazon
body:
To check the front-end content on Amazon, we using a tool called Rainforest. Unfortunately it is not possible to create multiple accounts, therefore access should be requested via the team lead. Below you will find a step-by-step guide how you can use Rainforest to scrape Amazon’s front-end content. For this manual we are assuming that you are going to check a large amount of products.


Navigate to the “Collections” page via the menu on your left-hand side.



Here you will see an overview of all current collections that were created. A collection basically is a set of products and should be created seperately per Amazon country. You can create a new collection by clicking on the “+Add new” in the top menu.



In the screen that pops up you can submit the following details:

Name

  • Make sure this is recognizable see you know what kind of product selection it is if you ever need it again.

Allowed request type = product

Schedule

  • Here you need to select “manual”, or if you need it on a weekly basis for example, you can select “weekly”.

Priority

  • You can leave this at “Normal”.

Notifications

  • Here you have to make sure CSV is selected

  • Please remove the mailadres for the “send via email” sheet or submit your own mailadres here if you would like to receive the file via mail as well.


Once you are done, click save. We will select what fields to scrape later on in the process


In this step we will start adding the products we would like you to scrape in bulk. You can do this based on EAN code or based on ASIN. Preferabbly this is done by ASIN. The easiest way to to gather all the ASINs is via an “all listing report” which can be found in Seller Central via Inventory reports. 


Click “template” and a download will automatically start.



Open the CSV File in LibreOffice and use ‘text to columns’ function to make sure all headers are placed where they should be. Populate the sheet as follows:

  • In column A you need to place “product” as value

  • If you want to scrape based on ASIN, paste all ASINs in column F

  • If you want to scrape based on EAN, paste all EAN codes in column G

  • In column J you need to place the marketplace domain you are going to scrape, for example “amazon.de”. A list of allowed values can be found here.


If you followed all steps correctly, your spreadsheet should now look like this:


Now, save the file as CSV and select "Edit Filter Settings". Once you press save, you are able to select a delimiter. Here you will choose a comma.


In Rainforest, press “add in bulk”, a screen will appear on the right-hand side of your screen. Here you will select “upload populated template”. If you followed all steps correctly the file should now be imported into Rainforest. If not, check if you missed a step or reach out to one of your colleagues.


Go back to “Collections” via the menu on the left, look for the collection you just created and select “actions > edit collection” on the right.



Now you can click on choose CSV field, as you will see some fields already have been pre-selected. You can leave these as is. Depending on what you are looking to scrape, you can add fields via this menu. Let’s say you are looking to check the bullets on the front-end, then you should add this field to the export as shown below.



Once you added all the fields you want to export, press save and save again. You can now run this collection via “actions > run collection”. Once the collection is finished you can download the results via “actions > download results tests > download”


The easiest way to check if content was pushed to the front end, is to put a filter on the bullet tab and see if there are bullets which are not in line with the content that was most recently pushed. 


If the content was not overwritten on the marketplace, you can do the following:

  • Push content via Category Listing

  • Create a case to update the content


Above academies will be added soon.

title: Characteristics of integrators
subcategory: How to pick the right integrator?
body:
Now that we know that there are multiple integrators to choose from, it is important to describe the characteristics that define a brand's choice for a certain integrator.

1. Available channels

Every integrator has its own roadmap. Major marketplaces (Amazon, Bol.com) are often available while smaller more local or niche-oriented marketplaces are less accessible. The availability of channels determines whether to start integrating. It will be a dealbreaker if specific desired marketplaces or channels are not available.

Fashion

  • Tradebyte
  • ChannelAdvisor
  • ChannelEngine

Consumer Electronics

  • ChannelAdvisor
  • ChannelEngine
  • Vendiro
  • ProductFlow

Home & Garden

  • ChannelEngine
  • Vendiro
  • ProductFlow

Consumable products:

  • ChannelEngine
  • ChannelAdvisor
  • Vendiro
  • ProductFlow

Currently available

Integrators have marketplaces that are currently available. These marketplaces are often displayed on their website.

Available in the coming 6 - 18 months

If the currently available marketplaces are not sufficient for the brand. Please request the latest road map for the marketplace. 

2. Integration options

The integration options are just as important as having the road map of the integrator. Within integration, the integrator itself is often not useful. There are 3 ways of integrating, which we will discuss below.

Plug-ins

Plug-ins are often available for popular E-commerce and ERP systems. They are easy to install and configure but only support limited features. A plug-in integration works for small and simple integration directly to an ERP or E-commerce environment. Plug-ins are currently not always suitable for fashion brands and can be developed by third parties, the integrator or ERP developer.

Examples:

  • Phoenix media (third party) offers a plug-in to connect Magento and Tradebyte
  • ChannelEngine offers a WooCommerce and Shopify plug-in
  • ProductFlow offers a WooCommerce plug-in

Application Programming Interface (API)

Integrators always offer direct integration with their API. This often is a more difficult but complete integration.

Middleware

Not all integrators offer plug-ins (ChannelAdvisor does not). Sometimes partners develop so-called 'middleware', which connects the brand to the integrator. An example of this is 'Rosetta'.

3. Integrator features

The primary function of integrators is to connect the brand with several marketplaces. The core interfaces are:

  • Product content and images
  • Orders, shipments and return
  • Offers (stock & price)

Integrators offer additional features on top of this. They can be categorized in PIM, OMS, fulfilment, pricing and other features.

PIM

PIM stands for 'Product information management'. Integrators offer multiple PIM features. Some examples are:

  • Creation of attributes
  • Smart content rules
  • Translation
  • Image types
  • Import or export of content/data/attributes

OMS & Fulfilment

OMS stands for 'Order management system', and along with fulfilment, these are important features for the operational management of marketplaces. Integrators offer different features:

  • Distribution of orders to several warehouses
  • Warehouse management for picking and packing, and track & trace

Pricing

One of the core features for integrators is pricing. The most common features are:

  • Configuration of period promotion
  • Stripe through price
  • Dynamic pricing
  • Pricing rules
  • Competitor automated pricing
  • Seller/Buybox pricing

4. Price and operating costs

Along with features, roadmaps, and integrations options, pricing is an important factor when deciding which integrator to choose for your business. Prices for integrations consist of the following:

  • Set-up fee: between €2000 and €5000
  • Monthly recurring fee: between €100 and €1500
  • Variable fee: 0% and 2%

Costs of operating

  • UX: integrators with a complex, rigid and inefficient 'User experience' have a higher cost of operating because the brand needs more resources to perform the same tasks.

title: Picking the right integrator (checklist)
subcategory: How to pick the right integrator?
body:
The following questions and checklist will help you select the right integrator for your brand:

  1. Which marketplaces are currently important for the brand?
  2. Which marketplaces are important for the brand in the coming 6 months?
  3. Which ERP and/or E-commerce system does the brand have to connect to?
  4. Is the brand widely distributed? If this is the case, pricing is an important feature.

title: Channable Zalando Integration Checklist
body:
Questions for networking with Zalando

  • Is the customer part of the Zalando Partner Program? Channable only supports the partner program, not Wholesale or ZFS.
  • Do you plan to sell with your own fulfillment?  Channable only supports the use of its own fulfillment.
  • Can you use the Channable order connection?  There is no way to fulfill orders in the Zalando account, so customers need to use our order connection.

Check readiness
To start onboarding, the customer must be approved by Zalando to start selling through the Zalando Partner Program and must have completed the Brand Readiness phase and signed the contract. This includes having a zDirect account.

Do you have this? Useful links: Intro to getting started with Partner program and Brand readiness info Are you aware of Zalando's strict requirements, especially in terms of image

title: Amazon - Error Solving
body:
When trying to solve errors on Amazon there are a few key steps that you need to take, or check on, before escalating the error in case you are unable to solve it. The steps listen below are the low hanging fruit. In a lot of cases, following these steps will help you to either solve the problem directly or get a better understanding of where the error originates, if it is fixable by us or if an amazon account manager is needed. 

1. Is build international listings turned on

Build international listings is the bane of amazon existence. For every customer that is onboarded it should be checked if this is turned on or not. Unless a customer has a very specific reason for this to be turned on, it should always be turned off. The BIL system generates random problems, gives errors on country specific uploads and can do all sorts of things in the background that can go unnoticed. Especially in combination with integrators (that push on individual countries) this can cause a lot of problems. Even after being turned off, problems can persist on ASINS for a very long time.

How to check if BIL is turned on?

Two quick ways to see if this is turned on:

1. Go to inventory > Sell Globally 

Click the Build international Listings tool


If you can see a "get started" button as in the screenshot you are in luck, and BIL has never been enabled. 

If you see a screen similar to below international listings might be enabled. In this case it is wise the connection is removed, but only after clearing this with the customers consultant. 

2. Another quick way to check is by going to a product listing and checking the offer tab. If you see any mention of a BIL-source or BIL-enabled that means the Build International Tool is activated.




2. Brand Registry Check

Next step would be to check the brand registry to make sure that the brand is in the registry and that you are brand owner. Be careful in this check, as small differences (such as capitals or not or having B.V. at the end of the brand or not etc.) between the product brand and the brand in the brand registry can cause you to not have brand ownership over the product. This in turn will probably mean that any of the content changes you propose will not stick. 

Do we have the right persmissions?

If the brand is registered by another company but the customer in case is a reseller, make sure the correct permissions have been given. As you won't have access to the brand registry itself, you can't perform a check on your own. The customer in this case needs to check with the brand owner to make sure their account (and ours) is permitted to sell the product and make content changes. 


3. What values are in the category listing report

The best way to check which content is attached to an ASIN and to see which of the content that is send in actually reaches the backend according to Amazon. So download the category listing per country and check to see what is going on. This is also the first step for our next point:


4. Push new content based on ASIN instead of EAN

Most new content that is pushed via integrators is pushed based on the EAN number of the product. However, if content is not accepted by Amazon (and there is no clear error) pushing the content based on ASIN can help. In order to do this, download the category listing, adjust the attributes where necessary and change the barcode-type from EAN to ASIN. Adjust the hersteller-barcode to the product ASIN as well, and re-push the content. 


5. Check if multiple EAN's or ASIN's are connected to each other

Sometimes a problem such as "content doesn't match ASIN X" or content that is accepted but doesn't change is caused by multiple ASIN's or EAN's that are connected to each other. In a perfect world every EAN is only connected to one ASIN and the other way around. Unfortunately merging products and (customers) uploading product the wrong way created duplicate versions of these products. They can be a pain to get rid of, but knowing this is the case is the first step to trying to solve the issue. 

A good way to check for this is to try and add the product to the catalogue manually in Amazon. If you do this by searching for the EAN, or ASIN and you see multiple products popping up... you've just found the potential reason for your problems. Solving this usually involves creating cases and merge requests to amazon, so a good next step is to check in with the customer consultant with your newly gained knowledge in order to form some next steps.

title: Firstly, labelling the company
body: Go to the list of companies and click them individually to assess their information and in which category the company should be labelled. The labels include Fashion, consumer electronics, home and living, and consumable products. Carefully analyze which label suits which company. The easiest way to do this is by visiting the website of the company and seeing which sort of business it is. However, if it is not any of the 4 categories, please label it as “not interesting’’. Nonetheless, if there is a company offering a similar service as Brandsom please label it as “concurrent”. Likewise, if there is a company that is already within our sales funnel please label it as “Lead”.

title: Content job profile
body:
Content Specialist (Junior / Medior / Senior)

  1. The Function

The Content Specialist ensures brand visibility across marketplaces by optimizing product content for maximum reach and conversion. This role involves refining AI-generated content, enhancing product detail pages, and developing high-quality brand storytelling strategies.

This position is integral to executing content automation, marketplace adaptation, and performance optimization, driving efficiency through AI and data-driven content strategies.

Reporting Line
The Content Specialist reports to the team consultant

Your Responsibilities

The Content Specialist plays a key role in developing and optimizing content strategies tailored to marketplace requirements. Responsibilities range from foundational content execution to advanced AI-driven optimization and team leadership.


High-Level Responsibilities

  • Content Optimization: Ensure product content (titles, bullet points, descriptions) is optimized for marketplace search algorithms and conversion.
  • AI & Automation Integration: Utilize AI tools to enhance content creation and streamline optimization processes (e.g. using ChatGPT prompts).
  •  Marketplace-Specific Adaptation: Tailor content to meet marketplace requirements, ensuring compliance and visibility. 
  • Content Strategy & Brand Development: Develop and refine brand messaging, incorporating search term optimization and storytelling techniques. 
  • Design & Multimedia Content: Support visual content creation, including basic design tasks and video editing where applicable (using tools like Indesign and Photoshop).
  •  Performance Tracking & Reporting: Use data analytics to measure content impact, iterating for continuous improvement.

Role-Specific Responsibilities

Junior Content Specialist

  • Optimize AI-generated content for marketplace relevance and searchability.
  • Develop Brand Store and A+ Content to enhance brand presence.
  • Conduct keyword analysis to improve search rankings.
  • Ensure consistency and accuracy of product listings across marketplaces.
  • Support content automation initiatives and begin learning AI-based tools.
  • Perform basic design tasks, including image enhancement and simple video editing.
  • Gain insights into marketplace content features and best practices.

Medior Content Specialist

  • Execute Junior-level responsibilities with full efficiency and proficiency.
  • Develop data-driven content strategies to improve marketplace performance.
  • Independently manage content-related tasks, ensuring quality and efficiency.
  • Advise consultants on content optimization strategies and automation improvements.
  • Estimate project timelines and coordinate efforts with Senior Specialists and Consultants.
  • Enhance content workflows through automation, AI, and data analytics.
  • Contribute to team knowledge-sharing and strategic refinement.
  • Utilize advanced design and video editing tools (Photoshop, InDesign, Premiere Pro).

Senior Content Specialist

  • Own Brandsom’s content strategy and execution for marketplace brands.
  • Manage budgets and allocate resources for marketplace content initiatives.
  • Lead and mentor a team of content and design AI specialists.
  • Oversee AI-driven automation, ensuring scalable content solutions.
  • Research and integrate cutting-edge AI and automation tools into content workflows.
  • Optimize multimedia content production, including advanced video and image editing.
  • Drive innovation in content automation and workflow efficiency.
  • Provide strategic insights to enhance brand positioning and content performance.
  • Collaborate with senior consultants and management to drive content innovation.
  • Ensure adherence to evolving marketplace content trends and best practices.
  1. How to Make Impact

On the customer side

Develop and implement compelling content strategies that improve brand visibility, searchability, and conversion across marketplaces. Provide tailored solutions that enhance brand storytelling and customer engagement.

On the process side

Leverage AI and automation to streamline content creation, reducing manual workload and improving efficiency. Implement workflows that ensure consistency, accuracy, and performance-driven optimization.

On the quality side

Ensure content quality by integrating data-driven insights, continuously improving AI-generated content, and aligning with marketplace standards. Drive innovation through scalable, automation-first content solutions.

  1. Your Competencies

Core Knowledge & Skills

  • Expertise in marketplace content creation, optimization, and automation.
  • Strong understanding of AI tools and automation in content workflows.
  • Proficiency in data-driven decision-making for content strategy.

Analytical & Innovation Abilities

  • AI-driven approach to optimizing content creation and automation.
  • Ability to identify content trends and implement data-backed improvements.
  • Continuous learning mindset to stay ahead of industry developments in AI and content automation.

Leadership & Management Skills (Senior Level)

  • Proven ability to mentor, lead, and develop a content team.
  • Strong communication skills to align stakeholders and enhance content processes.
  • Ability to manage budgets and optimize content initiatives efficiently.



  1. Your Experience

Junior Level

  • Experience with Google Sheets and/or Excel required.
  • Basic understanding of marketplaces and e-commerce 
  • Strong motivation to learn AI tools, automation, and content optimization processes.
  • Familiarity with Photoshop, InDesign, or equivalent design tools is preferred.

Medior Level

  • 2-3 years of experience in content creation, marketplace optimization and automation.
  • Proven ability to manage and execute content tasks independently.
  • Experience using AI tools for workflow efficiency.
  • Proficiency in content design and editing tools (Photoshop, InDesign, Premiere Pro).

Senior Level

  • 5+ years of experience in content strategy, marketplace optimization and AI-driven automation.
  • Strong leadership experience in content team management and innovation.
  • Expertise in AI-powered content creation and automation.
  • Proven track record in budget management, process optimization, and operational efficiency.

Future Growth of the Role

As AI and automation continue to evolve, this role will shift from manual content management to AI-driven optimization, automated workflow creation, and intelligent content strategies. Specialists will play a key role in integrating AI models, leveraging predictive analytics, and enhancing content automation tools for scalable, long-term brand success on marketplaces.

title: Lesson 1: Essential Tools for Outbound Sales
body:
Welcome to the first lesson of the outbound sales learning process. To excel in outbound sales, having the right tools is critical for managing leads, personalizing outreach, and streamlining workflows. Below is an overview of the tools you’ll need:


1. HubSpot
HubSpot is a comprehensive Customer Relationship Management (CRM) platform. It helps you track and manage your interactions with leads and prospects efficiently. From logging email communication to setting up follow-up tasks, HubSpot ensures that your sales process stays organized and data-driven.

You can find outbound sequences here.


2. LinkedIn Sales Navigator
LinkedIn Sales Navigator is a powerful tool for identifying and connecting with potential prospects. It allows you to leverage LinkedIn’s extensive professional network to find leads based on specific criteria such as industry, job title, and location. This tool is essential for crafting targeted and meaningful outreach.


3. LinkedHelper
LinkedHelper is an automation tool designed to enhance your LinkedIn outreach efforts. It enables you to send personalized connection requests, follow-up messages, and more at scale. When used responsibly, it saves time and ensures consistency in your outbound process.

You can find message templates here.


4. Lusha
Lusha is a contact information enrichment tool that provides accurate email addresses and phone numbers for your prospects. It integrates seamlessly with other platforms and ensures that your outreach efforts are backed by reliable contact data.
What to Do If You Don’t Have These Tools


If any of these tools are not yet set up for your account, please contact Livia, and she will assist you in getting everything sorted.


What’s Next?
Each of these tools will be explained in greater detail throughout this module. You’ll learn how to maximize their features, integrate them into your workflow, and use them effectively to enhance your outbound sales efforts.


Let’s get started!

title: Bol.com description builder
body:
A product's description is very important on bol.com. It helps buyers make an informed decision and improves the likelihood of purchase. However, making a separate description for each product takes a lot of time and effort. With help of the bol.com description builder,  you are able to make this process more efficient. You can find the template here.


Make sure to make a copy of the template before using it. This way, everyone can keep using it without having to create a new builder every time. It's also important that the formulas in the template remain untouched. The builder is created for you to enter relevant information in the right places. Making changes to the (original) template itself will result in errors for anyone that uses it.


 How the builder works

There are 5 different tabs in the template. Each tab has a specific function. We'll go over each tab to explain what they do and how they should be used.

  • Brondata

This tab contains the already existing data on a particular product, usually supplied by a consultant. If you have to put in the data yourself, make sure that all information on a particular SKU stays organised in this tab.


  • Zoektermonderzoek (Search term research)

Research on keywords should be done before you start creating descriptions for products on bol.com. The intention is to incorporate the keywords that are found during this research into the product description. Keep this in mind before starting to write descriptions, as these keywords are essential for the findability of online products. Relevant keywords for certain products are noted in this tab.


  • Master data

In this tab of the description builder, the identifiers are determined. Identifiers say something about the product. Let's take "Pink sunglasses with polarized lenses" as an example. As you may notice, this product has 3 identifiers; sunglasses, polarized lenses, and pink. Discuss with a consultant which components of a product are important to include in your product description. This way, you can discover which identifier(s) to use.


To find identifiers, some general information about the product is needed. This information can be found and noted in the aforementioned Brondata tab. You can use vertical search to ensure that the right information goes to the right SKU in the right column.

  1. Start by filling in the SKU and/or EAN in the Master data tab. These can be retrieved from the Brondata tab with help of a vertical search.
  2. Repeat step 1 for the titles in the third column.
  3. Create an identifier for each product. Use Brondata and vertical search again.


The descriptions are structured in such a way that each description starts with 3 USP's and a short text. Every USP has a title that corresponds with the identifier. Next to the USP's, there are five short bullet points that summarize the most important characteristics of the product. Prior to creating the bullet points, make sure that you know what character to use for them(dots/ticks).


  • Identifiers

This tab is where you can enter your data. A formula that ensures that only unique values are shown is built-in for this tab. It is likely that that the Master data tab contains duplicate identifiers in every column. The idea of this builder is that you don't have to perform the same action multiple times. The UNIQUE function gets rid of the duplicate values in a column and leaves only unique values, which is important for later steps. Make sure to write an appropriate text for each identifier. Take into account the search terms you found during your search term research. The first 300 characters of the description are indexed by Google. Therefore, including the most important keywords in these 300 characters is advised.


  • Builder

All the data for each SKU comes together in the Builder tab. Whereas in the Identifiers tab you wrote text per identifier, now you are going to combine them into one description per SKU. To do this, follow these steps:

  1. Place the SKU's and EAN's, collected from the Master data tab, into the first two columns by using vertical search.
  2. Do the same thing for the titles and put that data in the third column.
  3. With the help of vertical search, put all identifiers for each product from the Master data tab in the corresponding columns D to N.


After doing this, the values coming from the Identifiers tab are now automatically merged into a structured description in the last column (AE).

Note: Always do a final check to make sure that descriptions are not too long. Also, check for (grammar) mistakes!


Tips regarding identifier

Make sure your identifiers are not too general. Using "Material" as the identifier for ten different products because you want to indicate which materials they are made of, will result in all ten products having the same identifier, which is not very practical. In the identifiers tab, the word "Material" will only appear once because there is only one unique value for that specific identifier. As you can imagine, not every product will be made of the same material. However, by describing the identifier too generally, you only get to enter one value for all ten products. This means that you can't differentiate between the different products, resulting in all products having the same sentence in the description regarding the material. It makes more sense to make your identifier more specific so that you can enter different values for each product. If you want to mention the material of a product in the description, then write "Material Steel" or "Material Copper" to be more specific.  


Example: 
10 products, of which 5 are made of steel and 5 of copper. You have used the corresponding material as an identifier for all 10 products, giving you two identifiers in the Identifier tab; steel and copper. This way, every product in the Builder tab that has the identifier steel or copper is given the correct sentence. 

If the Brondata tab is well-organised, this should not cause too many problems. It is easiest to look up a column of materials in this tab and then copy it into the Master data tab by using vertical search. Doing this will prevent you from having to manually insert a new identifier for each product.

title: Category specialist channel
subcategory: TBR - Channel Added Value marketplace strategy framework
body:
Category specialists are channels that operate within a niche category. Some examples are marketplaces that only sell premium products, sustainable wear, or specific sport categories. 



Breuninger only sells fashion brands in a high-value segment, making the brand a good example of a category specialist in the premium segment. 

 

Bever is an example of a category specialist in the outdoor category.


Avocadostore is an example of a category specialist in the sustainability category.


Characteristics of a Category specialist channel

We will classify every channel type using the topics below. These topics are roughly the same as the Brandsom marketplace pillars (strategy, operation, integration, content and traffic). Integration is not included because this framework focuses on marketing and sales.

  • Audience (part of the strategy)
  • Assortment (part of the strategy)
  • Marketing (part of traffic)
  • Content
  • Pricing (part of the strategy)
  • Operations


Audience

  • Early adopters

  • Niche market

  • High authority within the target group

  • Value-added services

  • Focused on the end of the funnel


Assortment

  • High-end

  • Exclusives

  • Collabs

  • Speciality (plus size)


Marketing

  • Mid funnel


Content

  • Category banners

  • Brand store


Goals of content:

  • Gain authority

  • Brand awareness with early adopters


Pricing

  • Full price


Operations

  • Drop shipment

  • Availability of long-tail

title: Amazon Launch Plan Instructions - Strategy
body:
Amazon Launch Plan

The Amazon Launch plan is developed to bring structure to the launch on the Amazon marketplace and to walk the client through the most important aspects of the marketplace. If we want to be successful in any marketplace, we need to work together very closely with our clients. This launch plan will help you achieve just that.

The launch plan is combined with the Kick-Off meeting, where in the first part we mainly focus on sales handover (not in this academy), the second part will mainly focus on how we are planning to have a successful launch of the brand on Amazon. This is done via the Brandsom Framework, we will discuss what is requested per step in this academy.

Strategy

In the strategy part, we will mainly focus on the following subjects:

  • Sales targets
  • Pricing Strategy
  • Reviews
  • Reseller Management

Sales targets have to be set in MerchantSpring, when the client is new to the marketplace you should keep in mind sales still have to take off in the early stages. This should also be reflected in your sales target to make sure we manage the client's expectations correctly.

To increase the chances of success on the marketplace, we advice the client to start with an introduction price. This is a lower price for a set amount of time to drive more sales in the early stages. This has to be discussed with the client, since they should maintain their profitability. You can advise on a price via two options:

  • Check the price in the category and try to match (or even go a bit lower) the average price.
  • Compare pricing with the most important competitors and set the price a bit lower

Please note that if you want to use vouchers (coupons) the product first has to be sold for the full price.

Reviews are important, they drive conversion and CTR. They are also quite hard to obtain, therefore it is wise to use Amazon Vine to gather reviews via their dedicated reviewing program. Costs for the Vine Program can be found here.

Other resellers may sell the products of our client on Amazon, this can impact the potential of having the buybox. It is very important to communicate to the client that this can have an impact on the sales targets. Some tips brands can follow to avoid resellers:

  • Create product bundles that are not being offered to the resellers
  • Create marketplace-specific SKUs, which are just a bit different than the regular SKUs
  • More complex are contracts that prevent resellers from being allowed to sell on Amazon, this is not something we can advise on.

title: Probation period
body:
At Brandsom, the probation period is the initial 30 days of your employment. This period serves as a time for you to familiarize yourself with our company, your role, and to ensure a mutual fit. After the completion of the 30 days, you will have a scheduled meeting with your manager to review and discuss your performance and experiences during the probation period.

If it is determined that you have not successfully completed your probation period, you will receive written communication outlining this decision. Additionally, you will receive an invitation from our HR department to discuss the next steps. This meeting will provide an opportunity to address any concerns and discuss possible paths forward.

title: What causes error 5501?
subcategory: Amazon Error Solving SOP Overview - internal
body:
What causes error 5501?

This error occurs when the product ID (EAN/UPC) provided by you is not available on Amazon. This could happen when you insert an invalid product ID or when you are trying to match products in bulk without already having checked if the product is available on Amazon.

Sample:


We did not find any products on Amazon using the product-id (XXXXXXXXXXXXX) provided by you. If you already know the Amazon Standard Id (ASIN), please provide it in the product-id field and set the product-id-type as ASIN.

How to solve and prevent error 5501?

The best way to prevent this error is to call the Products API for ‘GetMatchingProductforID’ before creating feeds. This will confirm that all the products that are in the list are available on Amazon. You can even check the corresponding ASIN for that product.


To know more about ‘GetMatchingProductforID’, click on the link below: http://docs.developer.amazonservices.com/en_UK/products/Products_GetMatchingProductForId.html

title: Definitions and abbreviations
subcategory: WIP - Marketplace and E-commerce IT set-up 101
body:
Definitions:

  • Middleware: System, software, script, dataset, mapping table or other software supporting the main systems it's connected to.
  • E-commerce platform: Back-end or platform on which e-commerce websites can be created. Examples are: Shopify, Woocommerce, Magento2
  • Marketplace integrator: Software that establishes a connection between the sellers systems (often e-commerce platform or ERP) to transmit content and images, orders and shipments, stock and prices.
  • Master data: Master data represents the consistent and uniform set of identifiers and extended attributes that describes the core entities of the enterprise including customers, prospects, citizens, suppliers, sites, hierarchies and chart of accounts.

Abbreviations:

  • ERP - Enterprise Resource Planning: One of the core systems for medium to big companies. Typically handling customer data, orders and shipment, metadata, company set-up, and warehouses. Exact, SAP (FMS), Microsoft Dynamics NAV (Navision).
  • PIM - Product Information Management: This is the process of managing all the information required to market and sell products through distribution channels. Examples are: Riversand, Informatica, Inriver, Akeneo (Magento2)
  • DOM - Distributed Order Management: DOM is a method used to optimize fulfilment so orders arrive at customers on time while incurring the lowest possible cost. This system provides the support brands need to deliver high-quality customer service, as well as boost brand trust and customer satisfaction.
  • MDM - Master Data Management: MDM is a technology-enabled discipline in which business and information technology work together to ensure the uniformity, accuracy, stewardship, semantic consistency and accountability of the enterprise's official shared master data assets.
  • DAM - Digital Asset Management: A system that stores, shares and organizes digital assets in a central location. Operations on a collection of digital assets require the use of a computer application implementing Digital Asset Management (DAM) to ensure that the owner, and possibly their delegates, can perform operations on the data files.

title: Ziek Melding - Team Lead
body:
Een medewerker dient zich minimaal een half uur voor aanvang werktijd of uiterlijk vóór 09.00 uur ziek te melden bij diens leidinggevende. 

Dit moet worden geregistreerd in Nmbrs (zie module Nmbrs voor de uitleg) 
Zorg ervoor dat je onderstaande informatie hebt: 

  • Telefoonnummer waarop de zieke medewerker te bereiken is; 
  • De verwachte duur van het verzuim; 
  • De lopende afspraken en werkzaamheden;
  • Spreek af hoe en met welke frequentie je contact houdt en wie daarvoor het initiatief zal nemen.

Je mag nooit informeren naar eventuele medische informatie van de medewerker. 

Gaat de ziekmelding over een medewerker met een min/maxcontract dan moeten de ingeroosterde uren voor 70% in Asana onder ziekte-uren worden geregistreerd. 

Geef de ziekmelding ook altijd door aan de persoon die verantwoordelijk is voor de planning. 

Bij een verzuim langer dan 3 dagen graag een kaartje sturen naar de medewerker. 


Herstelt

Zodra de medewerker zich hersteld meldt, dien je dit ook weer te registeren in Nmbrs.

title: Introduction
body:
One of the most important parts of working at Brandsom is fulfilling our client's needs. This is simply because they are the reason we get to work. Therefore, you want to build a strong working relationship with the client. To achieve this,  good communication is necessary to figure out their goals and needs and to let them know what we can do to help. After all, you want to keep the client satisfied. How do we do this? And what exactly is good communication and how do we keep it businesslike?

One way of communicating is by e-mail. Another frequently used option is calling the client by phone. It is very important to write and talk in a formal way at the early stages of the business relationship.


When to call, e-mail or plan a Google Meet?

We believe that a periodic meeting structure is the core of good communication with the customer. Besides this, we use the Voys (internet phone) and e-mail.

  • Try to discuss all topics in the (bi)weekly, monthly and quarterly meetings. If you receive e-mails in the meantime discuss the also during these meetings.
  • Second way of communication method is e-mail because it's clearer, faster and more reliable for the customer.
  • Third way of communicating is calling the customer using Voys. Sometimes it's quicker to just call the customer, if you call the customer please use Voys.
  • The customer can call the generic phone number (please check the site for the latest number) and re-routed if needed.


Tone of voice 

When you communicate with a client, it is important to use a formal tone of voice. This indicates the way and the tone in which you write or speak to a client. Your tone of voice should be professional and friendly. This way of communicating will be explained in detail in this module.

title: Promotional Calender
body:
Working with the promotional calendar

The promotional calender serves as a guide for planning client-focused promotions and in order to make sure customers are enrolled into all promotions, given how important they are for the sales results. 

  • The promotion calender

The calender consist out of multiple tabs, for now we will focus on the first tab "Marketing Calender".

In this calender you can find the information on upcoming holidays in the most important countries and marketplaces [1] as well as the weeks where action is required from consultants or advertising team [2]

Consultants: For big promotional moments a preparation document will be created that can be found at the link in this overview for the weeks that it applies to. This document will be created by the advertising team and needs to be filled in by the consultants with information on the promotions such as if customers will participate (or why not), if promotions are set and which products will be the main focus. The document will also be shared by the advertising team via email.

title: The approach to the potential lead
body:
In both cold acquisition and lukewarm acquisition, it is important to approach the right person within the company. This does depend on the size of the company. If the company has 3 employees you can approach the CEO. If there are more employees, it is wise to choose a person with enough authority within the company. Furthermore, the department in which the person works is also important. 

Experience has shown that this is often the E-commerce managers or marketplace managers, often it also makes a difference if it is the local manager in NL. This position you look for within the company on Linkedin. (In order to speed up the search process, you can create a filter on the function: E-commerce / marketplace)

When you have found the right person within the company, you try to mirror the potential lead as well as possible. If it is a lukewarm lead you can look up in lead info what the company is interested in. For example, if they have searched for selling on Zalando, you can/must include in your message that it might be interesting for the company to start selling on Zalando and that we at Brandsom can help them with this. 

So really try to mirror the (possible) need. 

The message via LinkedIn can be sent in two ways:

1. (free account) click on the three dots next to follow the appropriate employee. Then click on make connection and add the message.

2. (trial version of a pro account) if you purchase the free 90-day trial period with Linkedin you can send people direct messages without having a connection, these messages are called Inmail messages. 

In the messages, you try not to be coercive but to be thoughtful with the potential lead. The goal is to schedule an introduction meeting where we can convince the lead why Brandsom is THE solution to all marketplace-related problems.

Below is a document with template messages to send, and reminders.

Template document

title: MerchantSpring - Replenishment
body:
Replenishment

In this academy, we go over the process of replenishment via MerchantSpring. Replenishment is the process of restocking inventory that has been sold on the marketplace, to avoid products getting out of stock. More generic information regarding replenishment can be found here.

  • In general, you want to have about 4 weeks of stock in the warehouse
  • For best sellers, this can be 5-6 weeks to make sure the product doesn't get OOS
  • Fashion normally has a higher Stock in Weeks due to the freeze period for Zalando.
  • If the client is new to the marketplace, it can be difficult to do an initial forecast. Keep in mind the potential growth when replenishing. Out-of-stock on marketplaces has a huge negative impact on the sales rank.

How to manage Replenishment for Zalando

For Zalando you can use this template, it combines some exports and gives you a replenishment advice based on the desired stock in weeks. You will find the instructions per tab below:

  • Replenishment Sheet - Here you put all SKUs that are fulfilled by ZFS. You can get a list via MerchantSpring > Sales by Product > Filter on Marketplace fulfilled

  • Zalando EU - MerchantSpring - Make an export of “Sales By Product” for the Zalando EU channel. Import this in the designated sheet. 

  • ChannelEngine - Go to ChannelEngine, set the date range to 60 days and only select the returns. Import this file in the designated sheet

  • ZalandoErrors - Make an export of the errors in zDirect and import it in the designated sheet.

  • Shipments - Go to Shipments in zDirect and download the “Dispatched Quantity CSV” for all open shipments.

The template deducts the products that are already in a different shipment from the replenishment advice. 

How to manage Replenishment for Amazon

To manage replenishment for the Amazon marketplace, you can use this template. Always make a copy of the sheet and put it in your client's Google Drive. It is pre-populated with an example, you can replace the data and it will automatically do the calculations.

  • Put all your client's marketplace-fulfilled SKUs in the "Replenishment Sheet" tab, in Column A, you can find all FBA SKUs via "FBA Inventory" in MerchantSpring
  • Go to Sales By Product in MerchantSpring and click download the overview, make sure the date range is selected as "last 30 days"
  • Import the CSV file to the corresponding sheet in the template (File, import, upload the file here. Select "Replace data at selected cell")
  • If the client is selling on multiple marketplaces, add the exports to the corresponding sheets

In MerchantSpring, the stock shown is the EU stock if the client is selling on multiple marketplaces via FBA. The shown sales are on the marketplace level. Therefore we need to export the sales to combine the sales on the EU level and match it to the EU stock. 

How to manage Replenishment for Bol

To manage replenishment for the Bol marketplace, you can use this template. Always make a copy of the sheet and put it in your client's Google Drive. It is pre-populated with an example, you can replace the data and it will automatically do the calculations.

  • Put all your client's marketplace-fulfilled SKUs in the "Replenishment Sheet" tab, in Column A. You can find all marketplace-fulfilled SKUs via "Sales by Product", here you will find a column that shows the fulfillment type.
  • Go to "Sales By Product" in MerchantSpring and export the data, make sure the date range is selected as "last 30 days"
  • Import the CSV file to the corresponding sheet in the template (File, import, upload the file here. Select "Replace data at selected cell")

The template will give you a replenishment advice, communicate this with the client, and have them confirm the shipment.

title: Dominant Influential Steady Compliant (DISC)
body:
People skills are essential in contact with clients. Qualitative contact is essential to retain clients. DISC helps to understand and adapt to human behavior. Therefore it can help you in your type of goal setting with a client and to keep your client happier and meet their expectations. 



DISC explained.

What?

  • DISC is a universal language of behavior and emotion. 

  • Based on the work of William Marston.

  • Objective and therefore not right or wrong.

  • A tool to understand each other even better.



How? 

Winning teams are mixed teams of different character traits. A person is a mix of all character types and is never 100% a D or an I. 

  • High D

    • Recognized by: Dominant; decisive; impatient; insensitive; ad hoc; doesn’t respond well to personal criticism. 

    • Avoid: Telling him/her what to do; attacking them personally if they did or didn’t do something; avoid ultimatums.  

    • Needs and motives: Goaldrive: result-driven; feardrive: to lose control; power and authority are important; working environment with little limitations; personal challenges to take credit.


  • High I

    • Recognized by: The enthusiast; emotional; ad hoc; impatient; disorganized; doesn’t like details; likes challegences; talkative and communicatively strong.

    • Avoid: Negativity; conflicts; personal rejection; a dull and never changing environment.

    • Needs and motives: Goaldrive: social recognition; feardrive: not being liked; status and fun is important.


  • High S

    • Recognized by: Teamplayer; need for safety and security; resistant to sudden change; family-minded and people-focused; likely to postpone tasks.

    • Avoid: Sudden changes; competition instead of cooperation; conflicts and aggressive behavior; multiple tasks.

    • Needs and motives: Goaldrive: safe environment; feardrive: sudden changes; focus on security, family and work; sincerity, recognition, reliability and loyalty are important.


  • High C

    • Recognized by: Postponing decisions; reluctant; critical thinker; careful; accurate; conscientious. 

    • Avoid: Criticizing their work; personal questions; inaccurate or incomplete information.

    • Needs and motives: Goaldrive: perfection; feardrive: to make mistakes; works independently; needs clear rules, regulations and directions.

title: Create ZFS Shipment
body:
1. Log in on zDirect 
  2. Go to > Fulfilment > Shipping Notices  
  3. Create Notice

  4. You are now on a page where you create the actual shipment, which is based on a CSV file. Column A you call "EAN", column B you call "Quantity".
    EAN is, of course, the product you are going to send, and Quantity is the quantity of that specific product.








  5. Once you have saved the file as a CSV (separated by list separators), you can upload it under "Upload File".

  6. Here Zalando gives either an error message, if the products are not completely onboard, or about the number of products to be shipped.

  7. Then you fill in the Carrier's name, how you want to pack the products: Parcel or Palletized, and the number of boxes the delivery will consist of.

  8. Finally, you indicate the earliest date on which delivery can be made and the destination: Lahr, Monchengladbach or Erfurt. 

Updated 18-02-2022

Employees are entitled to a holiday allowance of 8% of their gross annual salary. This allowance is accrued monthly and can be paid out by:

Travel Expenses

Employees who commute to work using public transport or a car are eligible for reimbursement of commuting expenses if the distance between home and work exceeds 10 kilometers. The compensation rate is €0.23 per kilometer traveled, with a maximum reimbursement of €300 net per month.

To claim your travel expenses:

title: MarktMentor - Keyword Research
body:
How to use MarktMentor for Keyword Research?

MarktMentor enables us to do keyword research for Bol more efficiently. This is done via the "Termwijzer" tool in MarktMentor. Below we will discuss the most important metrics you can find in this tool.

  • Zoekvolume Exact
    • Shows the exact search volume for the specific keyword
  • Zoekvolume Breed
    • Show the total search volume where the keyword is included
    • For example; 
      • Keyword "rugtas" has 1000 search volume
      • Keyword "rugtas kinderen" has 200 search volume
      • Zoekvolume breed is 1200 search volume
  • Sales om te ranken
    • The number of sales you need to rank on the first page for this keyword
  • Gerelateerd
    • Shows 0-100% how related the shown keyword is, compared to the keyword you provided in the Termwijzer. The higher the percentage, the more related the keyword is.

How to perform Search Term research?

First, we will create a "Zoekwoordenlijst". When performing keyword research you can add keywords to this list, which can be exported once you are finished. You can make a split based on category for example. This way you can always track the overall performance of keywords on the Bol marketplace.

You can create a "Zoekwoordenlijst" via Winkel Optimaliseren > Zoekwoordenlijsten > Project aanmaken (This is client name, or category)

Once you created a project, you can add a list to the project. This usually is category but can also be specifically for one product.

Once you have created the list, we are going to do the actual keyword research.

How to find keywords with MarktMentor?

There are two ways to find keywords that are relevant for your product. You can find keywords that are relevant based on your seed keyword (initial keyword), or you can analyze your most important competitors and use keywords they perform well on. We will go over both possibilities in this academy.

First, we look into the "Termwijzer" (via Winkel Optimaliseren) which gives us keywords based on our initial keyword. For example, in this academy we use the example of researching baby carriers (baby draagzak).

  • In the "Termwijzer" I search for "Baby draagzak" which is my main keyword. The tool now shows me all kinds of variations on this keyword that can be relevant to my product. 
  • Now you select all keywords that are relevant for your product, (shown in the GIF below) and add them to my Zoekwoordenlijst.

You can also use MarktMentor to analyse competitors, you can then use the keywords they perform well on to use in your own content. This is also done via "Termwijzer", but now you select "Product" at the very top of the screen.

  • Find up to 5 of your top competitor products. Keep in mind price point while selecting the products.
  • Add the EANs comma separated in the search bar and press Search, you will know see the keywords your competitors are ranking on.
  • Now do the same as previously, select all the relevant keywords, and add them to your "Zoekwoordenlijst". 

You now have a full list of all keywords that are relevant for your product (or category) and you can use this input the create the content for Bol. How to write you best product title and description can be found in the Bol content academy.

title: About Enriched Images
body:
What are Enriched Images?

  • Enriched images are high-quality visuals that provide enhanced product details, features, and context to improve the shopping experience.
  • These images go beyond standard product photography by incorporating infographics, lifestyle shots, and text overlays to highlight key product benefits and uses.
  • Aim to convey a comprehensive view of the product, enabling customers to make informed purchasing decisions.

What does it mean?
Enriched images are images that you can use to make certain information, for example about your product, more visual and attractive. Creating a Rich Picture is used to make it easier for someone to convey a story or information through a visual drawing for example. For example, you can visually process USPs of your product in an image instead of just placing text. Research has shown that visual information is more attractive to read than textual information. People tend to look at images rather than text.

Where can you use it?
You can use this if you use your products on marketplaces such as Amazon or Bol. You then provide information about your product and add enriched images to the image of the product in question.

When do you deploy it?
You can use this if you want to clarify a product with visual content. For example, if you want to sell a piece of clothing, you can visually display the specifications.

Alcohol, Drugs, and Medication

The use or possession of alcohol and/or drugs is prohibited. Employees must report to work fit for duty, free from the adverse effects of illegal drugs or alcohol. Lawful use of prescribed medications is allowed. Even during breaks or overtime, the consumption of alcohol is not permitted.

title: Annual Leave
body:
Annual Leave

In this chapter, we provide additional information about vacation days. If you have any questions, please contact HR.

Vacation Days

Employee:

  • Entitlement: Employees are entitled to 25 vacation days per year based on a 40-hour work week.

  • Application Process: Submit your vacation request digitally via Nmbrs at least four weeks before the desired vacation date(s).

    1. Visit Nmbrs and log in.
    2. If you don't have an account, email team@brandsom.nl.
    3. Click on 'Verlof aanvragen' (Requesting leave).
    4. Fill in the pop-up form with the dates, hours, and any remarks.
    5. Once your days are approved, record them in the vacation overview.

Supervisor:

  • Response Time: Respond to vacation requests via Nmbrs within two weeks.
  • Approval Criteria: Requests can only be denied if they cause significant disruption to business operations. Always check the vacation overview to avoid conflicts with multiple employees taking leave simultaneously.

Regulations

Expired Vacation Days:

  • Statutory Vacation Days: These lapse six months after the calendar year in which they were accrued. For example, vacation days accrued in 2024 will expire on July 1, 2025.
  • Non-Statutory Vacation Days: These lapse five years after the last day of the calendar year in which they were accrued.

Paying Out Vacation Days:

  • Statutory Vacation Days: These cannot be paid out unless it is at the end of a contract.
  • Redemption: Employees who have not taken their statutory vacation days may choose to redeem them, but this cannot be enforced by either the employee or Brandsom.

For more information or assistance with vacation requests, please contact HR.

title: Decision Tree
What causes error 99038?
subcategory: Amazon Error Solving SOP Overview - internal
body:
What causes error 99038?

This error occurs when invalid (Ex: HTML Code) characters are used in Product Description or Name.

Sample:


ResultMessageCode: 99038


ResultDescription: The Message/Product/DescriptionData/Description field contains an invalid value. The product_description field contains an invalid value. Please provide a valid value for the field and try again. 

How to solve and prevent error 99038?

To resolve this issue, check ‘Data Definitions’ in category-specific flat files and provide values based on accepted values only.


Note: The Description can contain spaces, but it cannot contain a carriage return (#0D), a line break (#0A), or a tab (#09). Note on html escape codes—do not use.


For example, data description should not contain ASCII characters as mentioned in ‘Data Definition’

title: The 5 steps of Project management
body:
Working at a project-based company requires project management. By implementing this skill you not only track your own progress but also stay focused and organized. In this module, we will walk you through the entire process of good project management using examples of different projects. 

THE FIVE STEPS OF PROJECT-MANAGEMENT

  1. Project Initiation

  2. Project Planning

  3. Project Execution

  4. Project Monitoring and Controlling

  5. Project Closing

Step 1: Define the project goals and draw up a list of stakeholders. Who are they? What do they expect from the project and when does it need to be done? Make sure you have all plans, goals and expectations clear before you start. Imagine your task is to write bullet points for a client. Are you working on this content on your own, or with others? When does it need to be done? What tone of voice should you use for the content? 

 Step 2:  There are 5 notable strategies for when it comes to project planning and time management:

  • Be intentional: keep a to-do list and check off every task you complete

  • Be prioritized: rank your tasks in order of importance - if it is hard for you to prioritize, ask one of your team members for help

  • Be focused: manage distractions and take breaks when necessary

  • Be structured: time block your work

  • Be self-aware: track your time

There are some useful tools you can implement to track your tasks and that help you stay organized. For example, you can use the task list that Gmail provides to check off every task you have completed. Click here to learn how to use Google Tasks. Other programs like Todoist and ClickUp assist with tasks as well. Do not forget to make your goals and tasks S.M.A.R.T.: specific, measurable, attainable, realistic and timely. If you are writing content for different categories of the same client, divide the different categories into different time slots to help you stay focused and organized. 

Step 3: Time for the execution. This is the stage where you and your team do the actual work. Try to maintain effective collaboration and communication between everyone involved in the project to ensure that everyone is on the same page. While writing bullet points, for example, write down any questions you have regarding the project and your written content. Sometimes it also helps to ask for interim feedback to steer yourself in the right direction. 

Step 4: This step of project management is about project monitoring and controlling. This phase runs simultaneously with the project execution and thereby ensures that the project is carried out the way it’s supposed to. At Brandsom, project monitoring and controlling are done via Asana. Want to learn more about Asana? Check out the module on the Asana way of working here.  

Step 5: Last but not least is the project closure stage. It is time to finish up the project and reflect! How did it go? Did you meet the goals and expectations of the project, your team and the stakeholders? What went well and what can be improved next time? Do not forget to mark your task as ‘done’ on Asana!
