title: Scheduling translations
body:
Important:
All translations are recorded in this document: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1l2dzUij2eQYH933VtROEkerhtD4qKe_FrThYjLROxWs/edit#gid=0

Finance will regularly check the document and invoice accordingly. 

*Only leads can plan translation work and follow the process below:

Available hours

For translations, we work with freelancers or with an agency. The freelancers are scheduled through the transaltion lead. The agency can be scheduled directly through the Vertical Lead. 
Please note! The translation lead can tell you that a translation has to go to the agency because the freelancers are not available. 


  • In-house translators are freelancers, working for Brandsom as needed. Usually, they are only available up to 8h per week. 

  • Translation agency has two different settings, “professional” and “economy”:

    • professional: This is a translation that’s machine based and thoroughly checked by a translator. This setting is recommended for front end info, especially full sentences like for example descriptions and longer bullets.

      Costs €0.10/word; up to 1500 words/working day; possibility to pay speed up fee. 

    • economy: This is a machine translation with light editing, meaning there won’t be any mistakes, however things that sound weird but aren't wrong will not be changed. This setting is only recommended for back end info and very simple info like for example a list of colors.

      Costs €0.04/word; up to 5000 words/working day; possibility to pay speed up fee. 

Scheduling 

  • Step 1: Once consultants have content ready for translations, please create an Asana task with the link to the document and tag translation lead in it. If the lead is not available let Lea know.

  • Step 2: Any tasks that are received before Friday 11.00 will be checked on Fridays from 11.00-15.00 for estimation, and to contact translators. Consultants receive a finishing date until 17.00 that day. 

  • Step 3: Finishing dates for in-house translations will be Monday 14.00 for short (1-2h) translations and Friday 17.00 the upcoming week for longer (2-6h) translations. For very long translations, the deadline is TBD.

 

Assignment check-list

  • Source language(s), target language(s)

  • Deadline range (From “this date would be great” to “we need it then, even if we have to pay more”)

  • Which content is for front end, which is for back end? 

  • In case not the whole document needs translations (usually that’s the case) please mark clearly what needs translation!

title: Why Inbound Sales?
body:
In many cases, you (salesperson) will be the first person from your company that potential clients get to meet. The experience that you provide during your prospecting/sales effort will set the tone for the entire relationship. The problem with this is, most people don't trust salespeople. Why? Because most salespeople haven't adapted to the modern era.


Back in the day, buyers really needed salespeople in order to make a decision. Salespeople back then had the answers to the key questions in the buyer's mind, such as:

  • What's included in the offering?
  • What does it cost?
  • How does it compare to competitors?
  • Who else is using your offering, and do they like it?

Nowadays, the information buyers use to make a purchase decision is just a click away. The power in the buying and selling process has shifted from the seller to the buyer. The internet changed the buyer-seller relationship. In other words, the buying process has transformed.


The world has changed, and far too many salespeople are living in the past. It's time to take a new approach to sales, it's time to go inbound!

title: Where is Brandsom?
body:
Let's put Brandsom in perspective

  • The brand or retailer will have a Global (Brand) strategy: Usually global goals and strategy are set up by the (top) management of the company and add value to the CBI of the company. For example: New Edition needs to grow +30% in turnover and +10% in profit via all her online and offline activities.

  • For bigger companies the brand or retailer can have a Regional strategy: Regional headquarters can have sub goals, but will have to add value to the global strategy. For example: New Edition needs to grow +40% in Germany, because it’s their new focus market. They execute this by using for example local influencers in the German market. 

    • Note: Some brands or retailers stick to a more globalized and uniform brand strategy and some brands extend to a regional and local focus strategy.

  • These global or regional headquarters will have Brand, Sales, E-commerce and/or Marketing departments having their brand, sales and marketing strategy and goals.

    • We, Brandsom: create and  develop the brand’s or retailers’ online channels strategy. From strategy to operation and integration.

    • We continuously execute and optimize operation, integration, advertising and organization.

    • We reflect, evaluate and adjust the strategy and tactics to meet the brands’ and retailers’ expectations and goals.

title: Enriched Images - Best Practices and Examples
body:
The do's and don'ts of Enriched Images

When creating enriched images, you should keep in mind some best practices we have learned over the years. 

  • Start with a content plan, you will learn more about this in the next academy
  • Consistency is key, the USPs mentioned in your textual content should also be shown in your visual content and vice versa.
  • Enriched images are always secondary images, the main image is under strict guidelines and should be handled differently.
  • Try to highlight 1 USP per image, otherwise there is too much information for the client to process. 

Since enriched images are tailored for the specific product (every product type has its own needs), it is difficult to have in-depth templates. But more generally speaking, the following templates are used the most:

  • Highlight USPs - Show the most important USPs of the product on the image. Also explain what the benefit for the client is.
  • Comparison Table - Used to explain the difference between clients' products and competitor products. Very useful for higher-priced products to explain the price difference
  • Review Image - Shows a positive review as 'proof' of your product. Keep in mind there are strict guidelines for the usage of reviews and it should add to the story you are trying to tell. Don't use random reviews.
  • Dimensions - For lots of products dimensions are important. Instead of just using an image with numeric dimensions, try comparing the product to a different product that reflects the consumers' imagination. 

Some examples of enriched images can be found below:

 

We also did tests to prove to importance of enriched images. In this test it clearly shows that consumers prefer to see enriched images over lifestyle images.

title: The Feed
body: The Feed is, as it were, a large (excel/google sheets) file containing all product information from a specific customer collection. This includes product names, materials, country of origin, EANs, color names and other important attributes/information. This file forms the basis for the rest of the integration and is usually provided by the customer.

title: Introduction
body:
One of the most important parts of working at Brandsom is fulfilling our client's needs. This is simply because they are the reason we get to work. Therefore, you want to build a strong working relationship with the client. To achieve this,  good communication is necessary to figure out their goals and needs and to let them know what we can do to help. After all, you want to keep the client satisfied. How do we do this? And what exactly is good communication and how do we keep it businesslike?

One way of communicating is by e-mail. Another frequently used option is calling the client by phone. It is very important to write and talk in a formal way at the early stages of the business relationship.


When to call, e-mail or plan a Google Meet?

We believe that a periodic meeting structure is the core of good communication with the customer. Besides this, we use the Voys (internet phone) and e-mail.

  • Try to discuss all topics in the (bi)weekly, monthly and quarterly meetings. If you receive e-mails in the meantime discuss the also during these meetings.
  • Second way of communication method is e-mail because it's clearer, faster and more reliable for the customer.
  • Third way of communicating is calling the customer using Voys. Sometimes it's quicker to just call the customer, if you call the customer please use Voys.
  • The customer can call the generic phone number (please check the site for the latest number) and re-routed if needed.


Tone of voice 

When you communicate with a client, it is important to use a formal tone of voice. This indicates the way and the tone in which you write or speak to a client. Your tone of voice should be professional and friendly. This way of communicating will be explained in detail in this module.

title: Pension
body: At Brandsom, we provide a reliable and straightforward pension plan designed to ensure your financial security in retirement. Our pension scheme is distributed on a monthly basis and is aligned with Dutch government standards, ensuring compliance and stability. The amount you receive is directly tied to your bruto salary, offering a clear and predictable benefit that grows with your earnings. This system not only provides peace of mind but also a transparent and equitable way to prepare for your future, making retirement planning simple and effective with Brandsom.

title: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP)
subcategory: WIP - Marketplace and E-commerce IT set-up 101
body:
Enterprise resource planning (ERP) consists of technologies and systems companies use to manage and integrate their core business processes. Enterprise resource planning software offers single system solutions that integrate processes across the business. Such applications allow for users to interact within a single interface, share information, and enable cross-functional collaboration. Several ERP systems that are used: 

  • It'sPerfect (mainly focused on fashion)
  • Microsoft Navision
  • SAP (Fashion Management Solution)

ERP allow businesses to quickly access needed information for clients, vendors, and business partners, contributing to improved customer and employee satisfaction, quicker response rates, and increased accuracy rates. Associated costs often decrease as the company operates more efficiently.

Things to know when working with ERP software:

  • ERP systems are complex and often managed by third parties
  • ERP systems often lack enriched data (copy and images)
  • ERP systems interfaces are often limited in available fields.

title: Ending an Exploratory call
body:
At this point, you've used CGP to uncover your prospect's challenges, goals, and plans. You've also used TCI to help them express their timeline for making a change, the consequences of missing that timeline, and the implications of taking the right action.


Before you wrap up the call, you need to make sure they know the process for taking that action itself. If you and your prospect don't have a shared vision of where the necessary resources and approval are going to come from, then it is unlikely you'll be able to come to a final agreement to move forward.

This means that you've got to talk about BA from the framework: budget and authority. 



You should look at the budget from the prospect's perspective, not your own. To do this, consider any costs involved to execute their plan, not just the cost of your solution. Also, discuss time investments as well as financial investment requirements. 

It's critical that the budget conversations come after the challenges, goals, plan, and consequences sections of the conversation.

  • In the goal conversation, you should have determined how much money they stand to gain or to save. 
  • In the plan conversation, you can determine the cost of the alternative plans.

At this point, ask the prospect a direct question about their financial situation. For example:

"Based on our conversation, it seems like you're spending X dollars on a solution that's not helping you get where you want to go. What did you plan to invest so that you're 100% confident that you'll achieve your goals?"

Note: In the example above, there is no mention of discounting to close the deal.


As an inbound salesperson, you'll focus on guiding and advising prospects who value your differentiation. If you hastily cover goals, challenges, plans, and consequences, you'll find yourself in a position where prospects have the upper hand in negotiations on price. But, if you thoroughly explore the opportunities using the CGP, TCI & BA framework, you will find yourself in a position where you are the best choice for your prospect and their decision will be to pay your price, or continue to deal with their challenges, miss their goals, and face the consequences of a poor decision.


The final piece of the framework is A for authority.

With information at the fingertips of everyone, more junior people are tasked with doing research. They are key, but certainly not key people in making the decision itself. Therefore, the right time to ask about decision-making processes is later on, after you understand the goals, challenges, plans, timeline, consequences, and implications. Understanding all these things helps you better understand why different people are involved. 

To explore authority, you could, for example, ask questions such as:

"Are you convinced that we can help you better than anyone else?"

or

"How have decisions like this been made in the past?"

or

"Who needs to be involved in this decision?"


As you approach the end of the exploratory call, you should ask your prospect what they took away from the call:

"What were your takeaways from this call?"

or

"Was this call helpful for you? - Why"


Whatever the next step is, now is the time to get commitment from the prospect. This is another place the CGP framework will help. You can use it to summarize your understanding of their context. Once you understand their context, you're in a better position to start influencing their future. By suggesting new plans, helping them quantify consequences, thinking through decision-making processes and setting a budget, you're helping them plan for a better future. It is a service to them instead of a tool for you.

To get a commitment from the prospect, you could say something like:

"Today, we discussed a few alternative plans that might help you overcome all of your challenges and achieve your goals. Based on our discussion today, you seem confident that this new plan will get you where you need to go, help you avoid consequence C, and do it all within a timeline and budget that will be acceptable to the whole team."

title: Proactively Addressing Client Profitability Using the Brandsom P&L framework
subtitle:
Objective:
This module is designed to empower Brandsom consultants to take a proactive role in identifying and addressing profitability issues for our clients. As consultants, you are the strategic lead and trusted advisor for your clients. Financial success is a key component of your role. This guide will walk you through when, why, and how to introduce a P&L (Profit & Loss) discussion, how to involve the right internal stakeholders, and how to position the value of Brandsom’s financial insights to clients.
body:
Why Profitability Matters

Every successful marketplace strategy is grounded in financial viability. Clients may be expanding to new channels, adjusting pricing strategies, or facing increasing return rates. All of these have direct impact on profitability. It’s your responsibility as a consultant to spot these moments and proactively offer financial insights, using our marketplace- and country-specific P&L templates.

These templates break down performance per channel (e.g. Zalando NL, Bol BE) and provide clarity on margins, costs, returns, and more — enabling smarter decisions.

Key Principle: Every Client Should Be Offered a P&L

We expect every managed client at Brandsom to be informed of our ability to support them with a customized P&L. Whether or not they immediately request it, every client should be made aware that:

  • We have a useful, standardized P&L template that can be tailored per marketplace and per country.

  • This template can reveal cost structures, profitability per SKU, margin impact of returns, marketplace commissions, etc.

  • Brandsom proactively offers to build this when there is a strategic need — not only when the client asks for it.

Fashion clients, due to typically higher return rates and margin complexity, should always be offered a P&L.

When to Discuss Profitability with a Client (examples)

Be alert to these moments — they are opportunities to bring value:

Situation

Example

Returns are too high

Return rates >35% on Zalando

Financial targets aren’t met

Monthly or quarterly GMV/margin targets missed

Client mentions financial pressure

“We’re not seeing the ROI we expected”

Finance flags issues

Late payments, negative balance flags

Expansion discussion

The client is evaluating a new marketplace but is hesitant about profitability

Pricing strategy changes

Client is revising prices and unsure of impact

In each of these moments, you should proactively suggest a P&L as a tool to bring clarity and steer strategy.


Proactive P&L Offer: Always On

Even if none of the above signals are present, use fixed client meetings — especially quarterly/yearly strategy sessions — to remind clients that:

“We can provide a detailed profitability analysis per marketplace or country using our internal P&L framework. Would you find it valuable if we did that for [channel]?”

This helps normalize financial performance as a shared topic between consultant and client — and positions Brandsom as a strategic partner.



Requesting a P&L: The Consultant's Workflow

If you’ve identified a need — or if the client explicitly asks for a financial breakdown — here’s what to do:

1. Plan a Strategy Sync with Joris

  • Schedule a short internal meeting with Joris to explain:

    • The client's current situation

    • The reasoning for building the P&L

    • Which channels or countries to include

  • Come prepared with key metrics and marketplace context.

2. P&L Creation by Joris/Floris

  • Based on your input, Joris and Floris will set up the P&L.

  • You’ll receive the document with guidance on how to interpret and present it.

  • Make sure the financial assumptions (fees, return rates, etc.) are aligned with the client’s situation.

3. Present to Client

  • Schedule a dedicated session or include it in a planned client call.

  • Use the P&L to:

    • Illustrate current margins

    • Pinpoint profit drains (e.g. return fees, ad costs)

    • Align on next steps (price adjustments, SKU selection, channel shift)

    • Adjust strategy based on outcome of P&L

Note: If the P&L turns out to be very negative; a new plan will be communicated instead of sharing the negative P&L

4. Keep Joris in the Loop

  • Joris remains in CC or looped into the communication.

  • He can step in for deep-dive questions or additional iterations of the file.

  • Encourage transparency and collaboration.

Tips for Communicating P&L Value to Clients

  • "Let’s ensure we’re not just growing revenue, but growing healthy revenue."

  • "Our goal is to help you scale profitably — not just be present on more channels."

  • "We’ve created a tool that reveals which channels are truly contributing to your bottom line."

  • Use visuals — charts and scenario simulations (e.g., changes in return rate or price) resonate well.

  • Always tie the insights back to actionable decisions (e.g., exclude unprofitable SKUs, optimize ad spend).

Extra tips

  • Use MerchantSpring to stay up to date on your client’s profitability. It doesn’t sketch teh whole picture but it’s very useful to get an indication of how it’s going

Final Words

Offering financial clarity through P&L insights isn’t a nice-to-have — it’s core to delivering strategic, high-value consultancy. You are not expected to be a financial analyst, but you are expected to spot the need, initiate the process, and guide the conversation.

Take initiative. Bring value. Lead strategically.

title: Amazon Escalation Contact
subtitle: Some issues with Amazon can be very persistent and eventually you end up going back and forth with Seller Support. As one of the view Amazon Agency Partners we have the unique options to escalate this directly to our Amazon Agency Manager.
body:
We now have access to the so called 'Seller Issue Office Hour' where we can book a 15 minute timeslot with our Agency Manager. He can help you escalating long-lasting issues internally. Of course, we want you to keep in mind some specific guidelines.

Please, do NOT expect your issue to be solved during these 15 minutes. He will try his best to direct you in the right direction and will use his resources to push things at Amazon internally.

Before you escalate it, make sure you have tried every relevant troubleshooting option provided by Amazon, for example; Violation Appeals, Seller Support tickets, calling with the Account Health Team etc.

If you want to escalate your issue directly to our Amazon contact, please reach out to imco@brandsom.nl providing the following information:

  1. Account Number: The Merchant Token ID
  2. Company or Brand Name: The name of the Seller Account
  3. Discussion Topic: additional details about the case (maximum 280 characters). Important information includes at least:
  • Issue description:
  • Marketplace:
  • ASIN:
  • SKU:
  • Attempted fixes:

For now, we have decided to follow this route to make sure we have tried everything possible and are not missing something. Once you have double-checked with Imco, we can schedule an appointment with Amazon.

title: Amazon Content Automation
body:
You can submit the information via this form, which will automatically trigger the Amazon Content workflow. Once the document is ready, it will be sent to the provided email address. Currently working for NL, DE, FR.

What does to workflow do and how does it work?

To give you an idea of what is exactly happening in this workflow, we will shortly summarize below:

  • Based on the provided category, the workflow finds the most important competitors.
  • The workflow takes these competitors and uses them to perform keyword research (similar to Cerebro from Helium10)
  • Based on the category (keyword research) and product description, the AI creates content for the products
  • The content includes; Product Title, Bullets points (5) and search terms

How to request your Amazon content

  • Use the template provided - Download it as xlsx
  • Make sure all information is populated to avoid errors
  • Use this form, and it will automatically trigger the workflow.
  • The final file is automatically sent to your email address
  • Ideally request based on parent/grandparent and add color and sizing via a spreadsheet

Some things to take into consideration:

  • Category can not contain special characters (/, -, ‘ , etc)
  • The category must be in the language of the requested marketplace, as it is used to perform competitor and keyword research
  • Category has to be specific (e.g. Collagen Powder is correct, Supplements is incorrect)
  • The process can take up some time, this is due to some safety measures implemented (waiting times) - This is to make sure all data is available for the workflow.

How to handle errors:

  • If the workflow has an error, it will automatically send an error message into Slack
  • Double-check if all information is correctly populated in your template
    • All fields populated?
    • Less than 400 lines in the sheet?
    • No special characters in the category?
  • Re-upload your revised template to see if it is working correctly
  • If it is still not working, please email imco@brandsom.nl with the document.

Last but not least; ALWAYS check the output from the AI to see if it is correct. 


Known issues:

  • Search Terms are not capped at 250 bytes yet
  • Some categories can trigger errors, for now the exact reason is unknown and so far only in France.

If you have feedback, please share it with Imco!

title: Zalando Attribute Tool-How to submit?
body:
To submit data for your clients, please access the form here: Zalando Attribute Tool- Client Submission


Please read all the explanations within the form very carefully, as they contain important instructions for submission.

Once you submit the form, we will run the tool using your provided data. We will then share the generated attribute results back with you. You can then use this completed file to submit the attributes to Zalando Partner Care.


How to submit the file? 


  1. Generate and Download the Zalando Attribute Insights Report:
  • Go to the Zalando Partner Backend: https://zdirect.zalando.com/articles/attribute-insights
  • Before downloading the report, it is crucial that you select the specific attributes for which you need updates. We recommend choosing attributes that our tool can successfully recognize from images and text (such as neckline, sleeve length, pattern, fit, pockets, etc.).
  • Once you've selected your desired attributes, download this customized report.

    

  1. Review the Insights Report and Fill the Template:
  • Review the downloaded, customized Insights Report to identify the specific products that are missing data for the attributes you selected.
  • Download the Empty Zalando Attribute Template: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1RCaCDeahIKQ5jffsHTr23qhAfjxs5dc2C1iCKjwWKjk/edit?gid=1161785421#gid=1161785421 
  • Copy the relevant product data (SKU, Image Url, Model Image Url, Description) for these identified products into the downloaded template.
  1. Upload Your Completed Template:
  • Once your template is filled, upload it using the "Add file" button in the section below.

Important Notes for Submission:

  • Category Format: Please ensure the category names in your template (e.g., 'shirt', 'dress') are written exactly as they appear in Zalando's categories. This helps the tool find the correct attributes.
  • Data for Best Results: For each product, please provide as much information as possible in the template:
  •  At least one of these is needed: Image Url, Model Image Url, or Description.
  •  For the best results, please provide all three (Image Url, Model Image Url, AND Description) whenever possible. This gives the AI the most information for accurate tagging.
  • One form submission per client. If you have more clients, please submit the form again for each one.
  • File Naming: Please name your files clearly, for example: [ClientName]_ZalandoTemplate.xlsx

title: Q&A Follo Merger
subtitle: Question and answers about the Follo Merger
body:
Topic English Answers (EN)
Integratie What will happen to the systems and software we use? For the first few months everything will stay exactly as it is. During that time we’ll compile a full inventory of every tool in use. After the audit we’ll decide, system by system, whether to keep it, roll it out group-wide or switch to a better tool already used elsewhere in Follo. Throughout the transition our guiding principles are minimal disruption for internal and external users and, wherever possible, an improvement in ease of use.
Integratie What will happen to our office? Brandsom’s office, like Follo’s headquarters, is in Utrecht. From 1 September 2025 we will all work from the Follo Utrecht location. The lease on the current Brandsom office has been terminated.
Integratie Will we get a different email address? Email addresses remain unchanged until the rebrand on 01-01-2026; after that they will migrate to @folloagency.com.
Integratie What will the integration look like? We’re moving towards one brand, one team and one way of working while fully respecting both company cultures. Over the next few months we’ll keep operating as before the acquisition, with extra focus on cross- and up-sell. An integration team (including Finance and other key functions) is being formed now to tackle the various topics and set priorities. Everyone will receive regular progress updates in Slack.
Integratie Can I contribute to the integration? Absolutely. Let your manager know you’d like to help and we’ll plug you in where your expertise fits best.
Integratie Where can I go if my question isn’t on this list? You can—and should—ask anything! Email Thijs or Anouk; we’ll reply as fast as possible and treat your question confidentially if you ask us to. Your input will also help us expand this Q&A.
Klanten What will happen to my client portfolio? You keep your clients exactly as they are. The only exception is clients that currently buy services from both companies; for those we’ll set up joint meetings with the relevant account managers to agree the best set-up, ensuring the client experiences zero disruption and even better service.
Klanten Will any clients move to another location? No. Clients will not be reassigned to other labels or offices. Only shared clients will be reviewed case-by-case to decide the smoothest arrangement.
Klanten How will clients be updated? Key clients will be contacted individually on 16 and 17 July 2025. All other clients will receive a general email announcement on those same dates explaining the partnership and what it means for them.
Klanten How and when was the news shared within the group? Brandsom team was informed on 4 July 2025 and Follo employees on 4 July 2025. External communication to clients starts 16 July 2025 and the press, partners and suppliers were informed by email on 17 July 2025.
Klanten What should I say if my client asks what will change? Extensive briefing will folow, but in general: Reassure them that nothing significant changes in the coming months: same contacts, same contracts. Behind the scenes we’ll combine expertise so they benefit from a wider service offering and stronger results.
Klanten Can I offer services from other labels within the group to my clients? Yes, cross-selling is actively encouraged. We’re mapping the best internal options now and will share clear guidelines so you can propose them confidently.
People What will happen to my job? Every position is retained.
People What will happen to my role? Your role stays exactly the same throughout 2025. During that time we’ll work out the future org-structure that best supports our growth—always balancing client needs with employee development.
People Will we be able to make use of each other’s capacity in the short term? Yes. Sharing capacity will let us balance workload, tap specialist skills and ultimately deliver an even better service to clients.
People What impact will the merger have on my career path in the long term? The enlarged group means more specialised roles, bigger projects, international exposure and richer learning paths, giving you multiple ways to grow your career.
Rebranding Is our name going to change? From announcement to 31 Dec 2025 we operate as ‘Brandsom by Follo’. On 1 Jan 2026 the brand becomes Follo only.
Visie What is the vision of the new combined company? Follo aims to be the performance-marketing authority in Western Europe, delivering unrivalled expertise and innovation focused on measurable results for our clients.
Visie What are the strategic reasons behind the merger? Combining forces lets us accelerate innovation (AI) and take a leading position in data-driven performance marketing across Europe. And it gives the Brandsom team more internal carreer opportunities.
People/HR How are the employment terms arranged at Follo? Your current Brandsom contract stays exactly as is until 31 Dec 2025. From 1 Jan 2026 you’ll transition to a Follo contract, and Follo guarantees that no term will be worsened.
People/HR What secondary benefits does Follo offer? The benefits package matches Brandsom’s and improves in a few areas—such as longer remote working and extra vacation days. A detailed comparison will be shared later in 2025.
People/HR How many vacation days do I get at Follo? The current plan is 26 vacation days per year for a full-time contract; this figure is still subject to final confirmation.
People/HR What is Follo’s remote-work policy? Follo operates a flexible remote-work policy with no fixed cap at the moment. The policy is under review, but it is expected to allow longer remote periods than Brandsom’s current one-month limit.
People/HR How is the pension plan organised? Follo’s pension scheme mirrors the existing Brandsom plan; full documentation will be shared in Q4.
People/HR What home-office support do I receive? A home-office allowance of € 2 per remote-work day will be available; final terms will be confirmed later this year.
People/HR How is car parking arranged? Parking at the Follo Utrecht office is free. If the on-site lot is full you can use the Park-and-Ride 10 minutes’ walk away—also free for employees.
People/HR Can I apply for roles at other Follo offices? Yes. From 2026 you may apply for vacancies at any Follo office through the internal mobility programme.

title: Zalando discount analysis tool
body:
Zalando Discount Analysis Tool: User Guideline

This tool helps us automatically compare Zalando discount prices with client’s discount prices, identify discrepancies, and provides a summarized report.

Tool drive folder: Zalando price analysis tool

1. Required Templates

To use this tool, you need to prepare three templates as explained below. 

Template Name

Explanation

Key Columns Used

ZdirectReportTemplate

Zdirect report contains necessary headers only and filtered on live products

ean, country, regular_price, discounted_price, brand, status_cluster

CalculatedPriceTemplate

This is basically a list of EANs and a discount price list. You can create this file in different ways:

CE Price V2: exported calculated prices from  Pricing for the specific country

CE Price V1: go to offer mapping, export mapping and use Promo price and EAN for specific country 

If you want to use your internal file just copy EAN and discounted price to Base price

EAN, Base price(discount price)

ZalandoPriceReport

Zalando's price update error report 

ean, country, issue_codes




2. Preparing Your Templates

  1. ZdirectReportTemplate (Google Sheet):
    • Download your full Zdirect product report from Zalando for the specific country
    • Manually filter this raw report for desired products (please only use “Live” products)
    • Copy the filtered data (excluding headers) and paste it into the ZdirectReportTemplate 
    • Ensure to keep existing column headers and delete the rest of the data in the template
  2. CalculatedPriceTemplate (Google Sheet):
    • Ensure to keep headers and paste the report you received from CE V2 price export. 
    • If you want to fill out manually you can include EANs and Base price(desired discount price)
  3. ZalandoPriceReport (Google Sheet):
    • Download your Zalando Price Report from articles→Price updates→ download full report 
    • Filter the country you want to check prices, copy and paste the all data into ZalandpPriceReport but ensure to keep headers 

3. Uploading Templates to Google Drive

Place all three prepared template files in this Google Drive folder:

  • Your "Upload_Files" Folder Link: Upload_Files

4. Running the Analysis Tool

The tool is a Google Apps Script that automates the analysis.

  1. Access the Apps Script Project:
    • Open the Apps Script editor: https://script.google.com/u/0/home/projects/1QSH-nXnbY9rCaGTSsgppeZ2w7s6d6ew1P_WL6ktB6fXrNKRYrDJB1CuS/edit
    • (Consider creating a Drive shortcut to this script project for quick access.)
  2. Change Email Recipient (if needed):
    • In the Apps Script editor, look for the line: const RECIPIENT_EMAIL = 'betul@brandsom.nl'; (it's usually around line 14-15).
    • Replace betul@brandsom.nl with your own email address where you want to receive the report.
    • Save the script after making this change.
  3. Select & Run Function:
    • In the Apps Script editor, from the dropdown menu, select runDiscountAnalysis.
    • Click the "Run" button (▶).
  4. Authorize (First Run):
    • Grant permissions when prompted to allow the script access to Google Drive and Gmail

5. Reviewing the Report

Once the script completes:

  • Email Notification: You (or the email address configured in the script) will receive an email from "Zalando Discount Analysis Tool." The subject will include the analyzed brand and country (e.g., "Zalando Discount Analysis Report - Petrol Industries - CH - 2024/07/01"). The email contains a summary of results and a link to the report.
  • Generated Report Location: A new Google Sheet report is created in your "Generated_Files" folder:
    • Your "Generated_Files" Folder Link:
      https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/199z9oAgxZ97smBoVkUzN1WLASNNT9k67
    • Filename example: zalando_discount_analysis_report_[Brand]_[Country]_[Timestamp].
  • Report Contents:
    • "Discount Data Report" Sheet: All original columns from your ZdirectReportTemplate, plus Discount_Percentage, Price_Match_Status (color-coded for clarity), Base price, and Price_Report_Error_Status.
    • "Discount Summary" Sheet: A quick overview of total items analyzed and a breakdown of counts for both Price_Match_Status and Price_Report_Error_Status.
    • Conditional Formatting: The Price_Match_Status column will be color-coded for easy identification of correct (green), incorrect (red), or unmatched (yellow) entries.

Troubleshooting: Check the Apps Script's "Execution log" for detailed error messages if the tool doesn't run as expected.

title: Gemini - Custom Gems Academy
subtitle: This academy provides a guide for creating and using Custom Gems within Gemini. By creating personalized Gems for recurring tasks, you can significantly increase your efficiency and ensure a consistent output for your work.
body:
What is a Custom Gem? A Custom Gem is a personalized version of Gemini that you configure for a specific, repeatable task. Think of it as giving Gemini a permanent set of instructions and a specific role to play. Instead of typing the same detailed prompts every time you need to summarize meeting notes or format feedback, you can use a Gem that already knows exactly what to do.

The primary goals are:

  • Save Time: Automate the setup for recurring tasks.
  • Ensure Consistency: Produce outputs in the same format and tone every time.
  • Improve Quality: Leverage fine-tuned instructions to get better, more relevant results.

Keep in mind that at the time of writing Gems are not shareable across the team.

How to Create a Custom Gem

  1. Navigate to your Gemini dashboard.
  2. On the left-hand menu, select "Create a Gem."
  3. You will see a configuration panel. Fill in the following fields:
    • Title: Give your Gem a short, clear, and descriptive name. This is what will appear in your list of Gems.
      • Example: Meeting Notes Summarizer
      • Example: Constructive Feedback Analyzer
    • Description: Write a brief one-sentence description for your own reference so you remember what the Gem does.
      • Example: Takes raw meeting notes and formats them into a structured summary with action items.
    • Instructions (System Prompt): This is the most important part. The instructions are the "brain" of your Gem. You tell Gemini how it should behave, what its purpose is, the tone it should use, and the format for its response. Be as specific as possible. A good prompt often includes:
      • Role: Who should Gemini act as? (e.g., "You are an expert project manager.")
      • Task: What is the primary goal? (e.g., "Your task is to reformat text I provide.")
      • Context: What information will you provide? (e.g., "I will paste unstructured notes from my meetings.")
      • Format: How should the output look? Use headings, bullet points, and bold text to define the structure.
      • Tone: What should the tone of voice be? (e.g., "Professional, concise, and direct.")
      • Constraints: What should Gemini not do? (e.g., "Do not add any opinions or information that was not in the original text.")

Example Use Cases Here are a few practical examples you can build today.

1. The Meeting Notes Gem

  • Goal: To quickly bring order to messy meeting notes.
  • Title: Meeting Notes Formatter
  • Sample Instructions:


You are an efficient personal assistant. Your task is to take the raw, unstructured meeting notes that I provide and format them into a clean, professional summary.


Your output MUST use the following structure:


**Meeting Summary**

* **Date:** [Identify the date from the notes, or leave blank if not mentioned]

* **Attendees:** [List the attendees mentioned]


**Key Decisions:**

* [A bulleted list of the main decisions made during the meeting]


**Action Items:**

* **Owner:** [Person's Name] - **Task:** [Describe the action item] - **Due Date:** [Date]


**Brief Summary:**

A two-sentence summary of the meeting's purpose and key outcomes.


Your tone should be neutral and concise. Do not add any information not present in the original notes.


2. The Feedback Gem

  • Goal: To process feedback you've received and turn it into an actionable development plan.
  • Title: Constructive Feedback Analyzer
  • Sample Instructions:


You are a professional executive coach. Your role is to help me analyze feedback I have received in a constructive, objective, and forward-looking manner. I will paste the feedback I received.


Your task is to process this feedback and present it using the following format:


**Feedback Analysis**


**1. Key Themes:**

* [Summarize the 2-3 main recurring themes you identified in the feedback.]


**2. Categorized Points:**

* **Strengths to Continue:** [Bulleted list of positive points and strengths mentioned.]

* **Areas for Development:** [Bulleted list of constructive criticism or areas for improvement.]


**3. Actionable Next Steps:**

* [Suggest 2-3 specific, actionable steps I could take to address the areas for development. Frame these as positive opportunities for growth.]


Your tone must be supportive, objective, and encouraging. Do not be defensive. Focus only on personal and professional growth.

How to Handle Poor Results: If your Gem isn't giving you the desired output, try the following:

  • Check your input: Is the text you're providing clear enough for the Gem to work with?
  • Refine the Instructions: Your prompt is the most likely cause. Add more specific constraints or provide an example of a good output directly in the instructions.
  • Start a new chat: Sometimes, Gemini gets "stuck" in a certain conversational path. Starting a new chat with your Gem can reset it.
  • Iterate: The first version is rarely perfect. Tweak your instructions based on the output you get and try again.

Last but not least; ALWAYS check the output from your Gem for accuracy and context before sharing it or acting on it. The AI is a tool to assist you, not replace your judgment.

title: Why Gemini?
body:
Why Gemini?

Introduction

This module introduces Gemini, our new AI tool, and its benefits for Brandsom's internal operations. The focus is on Gemini's integration with Google Workspace and its reported performance enhancements, demonstrating how it can support more efficient and effective work.

Gemini: Key Benefits

Gemini offers distinct advantages designed to improve operational efficiency and content generation.

  1. Google Workspace Integration

Gemini's seamless integration with Google Workspace tools enhances daily workflows and minimizes context switching.

  • Gmail Integration: Facilitates efficient email management.
    • Summarize lengthy email threads.
    • Draft context-aware replies.
    • Identify key action items from multiple emails.
    • Conduct targeted email searches using natural language prompts.
  • Google Calendar Integration: Supports streamlined meeting management.
    • Identify optimal meeting times.
    • Generate meeting agendas.
    • Summarize meeting notes.
    • Draft follow-up emails with action points.
  • Google Docs, Sheets, and Slides Integration: Enhances content creation and data analysis.
    • Draft comprehensive reports in Google Docs.
    • Analyze data sets and identify trends in Google Sheets.
    • Generate presentation content and slides in Google Slides.
  1. Enhanced Output & Speed

Internal feedback from our AI Ambassadors indicates that Gemini delivers faster and higher-quality output compared to previous AI tools.

  • Accuracy: Improved precision in responses, reducing revision time.
  • Efficiency: Accelerated content generation and data processing.
Versatility: Ability to produce diverse content, from marketing copy to internal documentation, with reduced effort.

title: Custom Gems: Feedback Gem
body:
Feedback Gem: Senior Content Specialist Performance Review

You are a specialized assistant designed to provide constructive feedback to a Senior Content Specialist, aligning with their core responsibilities and recent performance observations. Your goal is to help them meet and exceed expectations in content automation and workflow efficiency, AI tool integration, and delivering strategic content insights.

The Senior Content Specialist's responsibilities are:

  1. Drive innovation in content automation and workflow efficiency.
  2. Research and integrate cutting-edge AI and automation tools into content workflows.
  3. Provide strategic insights to enhance brand positioning and content performance.

You will be given content (e.g., reports, project plans, meeting notes, content proposals) from the Senior Content Specialist. Your task is to analyze this content against the specific feedback points below and provide actionable, measurable suggestions for improvement.

Key Feedback Areas and Assessment Criteria:

1. Driving Innovation in Content Automation and Workflow Efficiency

  • Observation: The specialist has initiated automation (e.g., templates, standardization) but needs to scale these efforts.
  • Assessment Criteria:
    • Workflow Audit: Does the provided content demonstrate a structural audit of current manual workflows, identifying areas for automation and time savings? (Look for analysis of existing processes, pain points, and potential efficiencies.)
    • New Automated Workflows: Does the content propose or describe the implementation of at least one new automated workflow per quarter (e.g., automated keyword analysis, bulk-upload tools, template-generated A/B texts)? (Look for concrete examples or plans.)
    • Team Knowledge Sharing: Is there evidence of proactive sharing of improvements with the team (e.g., monthly knowledge updates, stand-up demos)? (Look for communication plans, documentation for team access.)

2. Researching and Integrating Cutting-Edge AI and Automation Tools

  • Observation: The specialist experiments with AI (e.g., ChatGPT, Jasper) but lacks structural integration into projects and processes.
  • Assessment Criteria:
    • Monthly "Tool Deep Dive": Does the content detail a systematic approach to testing and evaluating new AI tools (e.g., Claude, Perplexity, Zapier Agents, GPT integrations via n8n) for applicability? (Look for structured reviews, comparative analyses.)
    • Client Mini-Pilot with AI: Is there a proposal or report for a mini-pilot with a client, demonstrating the use of AI for content generation or analysis, including measured impact on quality/time? (Look for specific client projects, before-and-after metrics.)
    • Documentation: Is there evidence of documented findings (e.g., in Notion, Google Drive) serving as a reference for colleagues? (Look for structured documentation, shared resources.)

3. Providing Strategic Insights to Enhance Brand Positioning and Content Performance

  • Observation: Content is strong and SEO-proof, but insights often remain operational, lacking strategic recommendations for clients.
  • Assessment Criteria:
    • Strategic Reporting Structure: Do reports or analyses include a clear structure that explains why something works, linking it to buyer journeys and brand positioning, rather than just what works? (Look for strategic conclusions, market context.)
    • Deepening Strategic Framework: Is there evidence or a plan for personal development in brand strategy or conversion psychology (e.g., masterclasses via Coursera, Growth Tribe)? (Look for learning initiatives, application of new frameworks.)
    • Marketplace Positioning Pitch: Is there a pitch or proposal for a client focused on improving their brand positioning on marketplaces, based on competitor analysis, data, or content-gap research? (Look for proactive, high-level client proposals specific to marketplaces.)

Your Functionality:

  • I will provide you with content from the Senior Content Specialist.
  • Your primary task is to assess this content against the "Assessment Criteria" for each of the three feedback areas.
  • For each area, clearly state whether the criteria are met, partially met, or not met.
  • Provide specific, actionable, and constructive feedback that directly references the submitted content and guides the specialist toward meeting the detailed improvement points.
  • Highlight areas of strength where the specialist is already excelling.
  • Ask clarifying questions if necessary to fully understand the context of the provided content.

Please provide the content from the Senior Content Specialist you would like me to assess. Be sure to specify the context of the content (e.g., "This is a report on our Q1 automation initiatives," or "These are notes from a client strategy meeting").

title: FBA NL Warehouse Requirements
subtitle: Welcome to the academy on the specific requirements and restrictions for using the Fulfillment by Amazon (FBA) warehouse in the Netherlands (PNLD). Because this is a recently launched and closed program, it operates under several unique conditions that differ from standard FBA warehouses. This guide will walk you through the essential points you need to know.
body:
Key Restrictions & Onboarding

The FBA program in the Netherlands is exclusive. Keep the following in mind:

  • Onboarding via Account Manager Only: Sellers cannot sign up independently. Access must be requested through an Account Manager. While onboarding is generally straightforward, admission is not guaranteed and can be refused if warehouse capacity is limited.

  • No Partner Carrier Program: The Partner Carrier Program is not yet available for creating inbound shipments to the NL warehouse.

  • Boxes Only: Currently, only shipments in boxes are accepted. Pallet shipments are not supported at this time.

 

Unsupported Product Categories

Several product categories are not permitted at the NL FBA warehouse. These include:

  • Hazmat (Level 6 or higher)

  • Shoes & Apparel

  • Heavy & Bulky items

  • Beer, Wine, and Spirits

  • Pharmaceutical products

  • Any other products requiring special licenses for storage or sale

 

Important Operational Considerations

  • Transition to German FBA: After one year of using the NL FBA warehouse, selling partners are required to transition to using FBA in Germany (DE).

  • Not Part of Pan-EU: The PNLD warehouse is not integrated into the Pan-European FBA network.

  • Potentially Longer Delivery Times: The warehouse does not operate 24/7, which may result in slightly longer delivery times for customers compared to other fulfillment centers.

Summary

When considering the FBA NL warehouse for a client, it is crucial to communicate these limitations clearly. The program is accessible only via an Account Manager and has specific restrictions on shipments and product categories. Furthermore, the mandatory transition to DE FBA after one year and its exclusion from the Pan-EU network are key strategic points to discuss with the client.

title: SOP creation of branding elements, including Amazon Brand Stores, A+ Content, and Brand Story.
body:
Objective: Ensure consistent quality and alignment with client expectations during the creation of branding elements, including Amazon Brand Stores, A+ Content, and Brand Story.

1. Purpose

To define the standardized process for creating branding elements on Amazon—such as Brand Stores, A+ Content, and Brand Story—ensuring consistency in execution, alignment with client branding, and high-quality output that meets both internal and external expectations.

2. Scope

This SOP applies to all consultants and content specialists involved in the planning, briefing, creation, and finalization of branding elements on Amazon, including Brand Stores, A+ Content, and Brand Story.


3. General Guidelines

  • AI-generated images may be used if necessary, and must be billed separately.
  • The client receives three feedback rounds:
    • During Pre-creation stage > Mock-up
    • During Creation Stage > Home Page & Category Page
    • During Post Creation  (minor edits only) – no design revisions, text-only changes permitted.


4. Process Overview

A. Briefing Stage

Consultant

  1. Use the email template (attached at the end) to request the following from the client:
    • Brand Guidebook / Style Guide
    • Logo
    • Fonts (or indicate where fonts can be accessed)
    • USPs, certifications, tone of voice, slogans
    • Available assets (product/lifestyle images/ icons)
    • Preferred structure: 
      1. Brand Store: homepage, category page(s), etc.
      2. A+ content: Per Category, products or Collection
    • Top competitors (or perform a benchmark analysis if not provided)
      1. who are their competitors, 
      2. which do they look up to > any content they really like
  2. Inform the content team to ensure the Branding references and possible design styles align with the client’s hourly budget.
  3. Present Branding references to the client using a curated slide deck.
    • Show the client 3–5 possible design styles the client can choose from. 

Important:

If the client lacks sufficient imagery, inform them that additional design work (e.g., AI-generated or custom image creation) is billable.

B.  Pre-Creation Stage (~2 hour total)

  1. Create mock-ups based on competitors (no original images created yet). Use the Slide Deck.
  2. Present to client for approval.

C. Creation Stage

  1. Create homepage and one category page.
  2. Present to client for approval.
  3. Upon approval, proceed with remaining pages based on the agreed structure and feedback.

D. Post-Creation / Feedback Stage

Consultant:

  1. Share preview link with the client for final minor adjustments (text only).
  2. Process feedback
  3. Publish the content directly.




5. Responsibilities (RACI-model)

Legend:

  • R = Responsible (executes the task)
  • A = Accountable (owns the task)
  • C = Consulted (provides input)
  • I = Informed (kept updated)

6. Documentation & Resources

  • Slide Deck with Examples 
  • Mock-up Slide Deck
  • Email template (briefing request)

Email template

To begin the creation of your [Amazon Brand Store], we kindly ask you to share the following materials and information. This will ensure the store design aligns with your brand identity and meets your expectations.

Please provide the following:

  • Brand Guidebook / Style Guide
  • Logo files (preferably in .AI, .EPS, or high-res .PNG format)
  • Font files
    • If not available, please let us know where your fonts can be accessed (e.g. Google Fonts or Adobe Fonts)
  • Tone of Voice / Slogans / Taglines
  • USPs & Certifications
  • Available Assets
    • Product images
    • Lifestyle images or branded content
    • Videos (if applicable)
  • Preferred Store Structure [choose]
    • For example: Homepage, Category Pages, Product Pages (Brand Store)
    • For example: : Per Category, products or Collection (A+ content)
  • Top Competitors
    • If you don’t have a list, we can perform a benchmark analysis based on your category

Once we receive your materials, we will create a first mock-up for review and proceed through the design stages.

If you have any questions or need help collecting these materials, feel free to reach out.
